section_header: NEW 2030 CLIMATE EQUITY GOALS FOR AUSTIN — Sustainable Buildings
policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Sustainable Buildings

- Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified 2030 building-sector targets; followed by multiple numbered strategies in the Sustainable Buildings section.
mechanism_description: The policy aims for net-zero carbon in new buildings and lower emissions in existing buildings, including reducing emissions from natural gas use, which reduces building operational energy-related emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Ensure benefits of building decarbonization programs flow to low-income communities and communities of color through comprehensive energy poverty mitigation and partnerships.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Ensure benefits flow to low-income communities and communities of color

Pursue a comprehensive energy poverty mitigation strategy by partnering with trusted community organizations, affordable housing developers, and schools in equitable outreach and program development.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (energy poverty mitigation strategy and partnerships) tied to the buildings goal.
mechanism_description: Partner with trusted community organizations, affordable housing developers, and schools to support equitable outreach and program development that mitigates energy poverty and helps benefits reach low-income communities and communities of color.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy consumption via outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanded benchmarking for existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Enhance understanding of energy consumption

Enhance resident and building owner understanding of energy savings opportunities, benefits, and climate impacts of energy consumption. This will be done through direct outreach, culturally relevant communications, expanding benchmarking requirements for all existing buildings, and better access to energy and water data.
extraction_rationale: Concrete programmatic actions (benchmarking expansion; data access; outreach).
mechanism_description: The city will expand benchmarking for existing buildings and improve outreach and access to energy and water data so residents and building owners better understand consumption and are encouraged to pursue energy-saving actions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon building milestones through new building energy codes/amendments and incentives/education for existing building decarbonization, in collaboration with utilities and with equity focus.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Achieve energy-efficient, net-zero carbon buildings

Achieve goal milestones for net-zero carbon buildings through new building energy codes, amendments, and other methods. Engage owners and operators of existing buildings to decarbonize through incentives and education for contractors and occupants. Collaborate with local utilities to implement equitable emission reduction strategies.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy mechanisms (codes, amendments, incentives, education, utility collaboration).
mechanism_description: The strategy uses new building energy codes and amendments and engages existing building owners and operators through incentives and education to cut building energy use and related emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Net-zero carbon new buildings; 25% existing; 30% gas emissions reduction
policy_statement: Create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging building-related technologies by partnering with local unions and education/advocacy organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Achieve net-zero carbon for all new buildings and reduce emissions by 25% for existing buildings while lowering all natural gas-related emissions by 30% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Ensure equitable workforce development for emerging technologies

Prioritize investment in local emissions reduction and create equitable workforce development and training opportunities for emerging technologies by partnering with local unions, education, and advocacy organizations that serve low-income communities and communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Defines a workforce development intervention with specified partners and equity focus.
mechanism_description: The strategy prioritizes investment and partnerships with unions and community-serving education and advocacy groups to create equitable training opportunities that build the workforce capacity needed to deploy emerging emissions-reducing technologies.

policy_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target with a deadline; followed by a list of strategies.
mechanism_description: The policy sets a goal to cut greenhouse gas emissions by reducing refrigerant leakage, lowering fugitive emissions from cooling and refrigeration systems.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting and high-GWP refrigerants to increase capture and safe destruction.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Capture and destroy old refrigerants

Develop a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high ozone-depleting substances and global warming potential (GWP) refrigerants.
extraction_rationale: Specific program intervention (destruction program with pricing mechanism).
mechanism_description: Developing a refrigerant destruction program that places a price on older high-GWP refrigerants increases their capture and safe destruction, reducing fugitive emissions from leaks and disposal.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Move forward with building code amendments and other local actions to allow/encourage low and no-GWP refrigerants as soon as feasible.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Improve building codes to encourage cleaner refrigerants

Closely follow developments in revised building codes that allow the use of low and no GWP refrigerants... and move forward with code amendments and other local action as soon as feasible.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/code change commitment related to refrigerants.
mechanism_description: Update building codes and take local actions to allow and encourage low- and no-GWP refrigerants, reducing climate impacts from refrigerant leaks.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Create incentives for refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair in partnership with food retail, restaurants, warehouses, and HVAC companies, prioritizing outreach to small businesses owned by people of color.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Create incentives for leak detection and repair

Partner with grocery stores, convenience stores, restaurants... to create an incentive for designing and tracking refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair.
extraction_rationale: Concrete incentive-based policy with named partner sectors.
mechanism_description: Create incentives with businesses to improve refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair, reducing refrigerant leakage and associated fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Create an awareness, education, and training campaign for HVAC service providers and related stakeholders; lead by example with refrigerant leak detection/repair at City-owned facilities.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Awareness and training for HVAC service providers

Create an awareness, education, and training campaign... Lead by example by implementing a refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair program at City-owned facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific campaign plus a City operational commitment (lead by example).
mechanism_description: The city creates awareness and training for HVAC service providers and implements refrigerant leak detection, prevention, and repair at city-owned facilities to reduce fugitive refrigerant emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce refrigerant leakage emissions 25%
policy_statement: Reduce the volume/use of refrigerants, particularly high-GWP refrigerants, by expanding and prioritizing codes and incentives favoring passive design, reduction, and efficiency.
parent_statement: Reduce community-wide greenhouse gas emissions from refrigerant leakage by 25% by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Reduce the volume of refrigerants

Emphasize the link between design and refrigerant use by reducing and preventing the use of refrigerants... particularly those with high GWP.

How we'll get there:

- Ensure codes and incentives that favor passive design, reduction, and efficiency are expanded and prioritized.
extraction_rationale: Concrete policy direction to use codes/incentives to reduce refrigerant use.
mechanism_description: Expanding and prioritizing codes and incentives that favor passive design and efficiency reduces the use of high-GWP refrigerants, lowering refrigerant-related climate pollution.

policy_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
By 2030, reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline*.

*The embodied carbon associated with the construction and materials must not exceed 500 kg CO2e/m2 (~100 lbs. CO2e/sf) per project.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target plus specific performance threshold; followed by multiple strategies.
mechanism_description: The policy sets a limit and reduction target for embodied carbon in construction materials so new projects use lower-carbon materials, reducing construction-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to deliver healthy, low-carbon buildings, prioritizing community facilities serving low-income communities and communities of color first.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Lead by example through design and construction standards

In partnership with other cities and states, develop City of Austin design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements to result in healthy, low-carbon buildings.
extraction_rationale: Specific government procurement/standards action (lead by example).
mechanism_description: The city develops design and construction specifications and purchasing agreements so that new public projects deliver healthier, low-carbon buildings, reducing building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs (e.g., expedited permitting and Austin Energy Green Building) and transition voluntary guidance into planning/development agreements over time.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Incentivize lower-carbon materials

Enhance and integrate lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices into City incentive programs, like the expedited permitting process and Austin Energy's Green Building program, to transition voluntary design guidance into planning and development agreements over time.
extraction_rationale: Clear incentive/program modification commitment.
mechanism_description: The city expands incentive programs to encourage lower-carbon building materials and deconstruction practices, reducing emissions associated with producing and disposing of construction materials.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Create a performance framework and educational programming on low-carbon materials best practices for industry professionals and the public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Educate stakeholders on materials best practices

Create a performance framework and educational programming for industry professionals and the general public, focusing on low-income communities and communities of color, to reduce the lifecycle and negative health impacts of building materials and construction practices.
extraction_rationale: Specific education/programming intervention tied to embodied carbon and health impacts.
mechanism_description: Create a performance framework and educational programming to help industry professionals and the public adopt lower-impact building materials and construction practices, reducing lifecycle emissions and associated health impacts.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Reduce embodied carbon 40%
policy_statement: Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact building materials (e.g., glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, carpet).
parent_statement: Reduce the embodied carbon footprint of building materials used in local construction by 40% from a 2020 baseline by 2030 (with project embodied carbon not exceeding 500 kg CO2e/m2).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Stimulate decarbonization with local producers installation

Prioritize partnerships within local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact materials, specifically glass, steel, aluminum, concrete, drywall, insulation, and carpet.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention focusing on decarbonizing named material categories via partnerships.
mechanism_description: The strategy prioritizes partnerships with local materials markets to decarbonize high-impact building materials, lowering embodied emissions from producing and installing construction products.

policy_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text: By 2030, equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified water-demand target with timeframe and implementation basis; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: The city plans to implement Water Forward Plan strategies to reduce community-wide water demand.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Enhance community engagement and partnerships to advance Water Forward equity strategies (e.g., My ATX Water, incentives, ordinances) and implement targeted outreach to improve participation.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Engage residents in water efficiency technological transitions, and conservation programs

Enhance community engagement strategies and create partnerships with community organizations to advance equity through Water Forward strategies, including My ATX Water (Advanced Metering Infrastructure), incentives, and ordinances.
extraction_rationale: Specific engagement and partnership actions to implement water conservation strategies with equity focus.
mechanism_description: Enhance community engagement, partnerships, and targeted outreach to increase participation in water efficiency technologies and conservation programs, leading to lower per-capita water use.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Evaluate water conservation and customer assistance program criteria to remove structural barriers and expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and other low-income-serving programs.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Evaluate water conservation, customer assistance, and workforce development program participation criteria

Evaluate program criteria to identify opportunities to address structural barriers that prevent program participation... explore strategies to expand enrollment in the Customer Assistance Program and increase participation in other programs serving low-income customers.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program evaluation and expansion commitment for equity in water assistance and conservation.
mechanism_description: The policy evaluates participation criteria and addresses structural barriers to expand enrollment in water conservation and customer assistance programs, increasing uptake of water-saving actions and reducing per-capita water use.

section_header: SUSTAINABLE BUILDINGS — Goal: Water demand ~152,000 acre-feet/year
policy_statement: Assess and pursue water conservation and energy management optimization at the water-energy nexus, including evaluating Austin Water facility energy use and developing methods to quantify emissions reductions from water-demand reduction.
parent_statement: Equitably achieve a community-wide water demand of approximately 152,000 acre-feet per year by 2030 by implementing strategies in the Water Forward Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce emissions at the water-energy nexus

Assess how water demand reduction is associated with energy consumption in residential and commercial buildings and Austin Water facilities. Identify and pursue synergistic water conservation and energy management optimization efforts through programs and partnerships.
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment and optimization actions linking water conservation to emissions reductions.
mechanism_description: The strategy assesses the relationship between reduced water demand and energy consumption in buildings and water facilities, then pursues water conservation and energy management optimization through programs and partnerships to reduce emissions.

policy_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 80% of new non-residential development is located within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
extraction_rationale: Quantified land-use development siting target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.
mechanism_description: It sets a target to concentrate new non-residential development in activity centers and corridors, supporting compact growth that reduces car travel and related emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Adopt Complete Communities plans to reverse historical and racist impacts, address displacement, and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Plan for complete communities

Adopt Complete Communities plans that reverse historical and racist impacts on areas experiencing displacement and preserve neighborhoods in communities of color.
extraction_rationale: Specific planning instrument commitment (adopt plans) tied to equity and land use.
mechanism_description: Adopt Complete Communities plans to help address displacement pressures, preserve neighborhoods in communities of color, and support more equitable community resilience outcomes.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Work with medium and large employers to locate places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Work with employers on location and amenities

Work with medium and large employers to locate their places of business in growth centers and along corridors.
extraction_rationale: Explicit intervention to influence employer location decisions toward target areas.
mechanism_description: The strategy works with employers to concentrate job locations in growth centers and corridors, supporting shorter trips and greater use of transit and other lower-carbon travel options, which reduces transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops with first/last-mile options.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3 : Create mobility hubs

Create community mobility hubs, including park-and-ride facilities, adjacent to transit stops to offer a complete trip experience. These hubs should offer various first- and last-mile mobility options.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/program concept to improve multimodal access.
mechanism_description: Creating mobility hubs with park-and-ride and first/last-mile options next to transit stops improves transit access and multimodal connectivity, supporting shifts away from single-occupancy driving and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 80% non-residential development in centers/corridors
policy_statement: Phase out free employee parking at City facilities and other large employers within 1/2 mile of the transit priority network, replacing it with cash-out, carpool/vanpool, transit passes, telework, or other commuter benefits.
parent_statement: Locate 80% of new non-residential development within the city's Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Phase out free parking

Phase out the practice of providing free parking spaces to employees at City of Austin facilities and other large employers located within 1/2 mile of the Austin Strategic Mobility Plan's transit priority network. Instead, offer a parking cash-out, carpool and vanpool options, flex schedules, transit passes, support for teleworking, or other commuter benefits programs.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy change with defined scope and alternative measures.
mechanism_description: Phasing out free employee parking and offering cash-out, transit passes, carpool/vanpool options, telework support, and other commuter benefits shifts commute behavior away from driving alone, reducing vehicle travel and transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text: By 2027, preserve and produce 135,000 housing units, including 60,000 affordable housing units*, with 75% of new housing located within ½ mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
extraction_rationale: Quantified housing production/preservation target with timeframe and location share; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: The policy plans to preserve and add housing with most new units located near activity centers and corridors, supporting more compact, transit-supportive development that reduces driving and associated emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 135,000 housing units by 2027
policy_statement: Engage communities vulnerable to displacement for immediate affordable housing assistance and proactively monitor at-risk affordable properties to extend affordability.
parent_statement: Preserve and produce 135,000 housing units (including 60,000 affordable units) by 2027, with 75% of new housing located within 1/2 mile of Imagine Austin activity centers and corridors.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Offer immediate affordable housing assistance

Engage directly with communities that are vulnerable to displacement and connect them with services. Proactively monitor affordable housing properties at risk of losing their affordability status to extend the period of affordability.
extraction_rationale: Direct programmatic commitment for anti-displacement and affordability preservation.
mechanism_description: The strategy engages communities vulnerable to displacement, connects them with affordable housing services, and monitors at-risk affordable properties to extend affordability and reduce displacement risk.

policy_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, 50% of trips in Austin are made using public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided altogether by working from home*.
extraction_rationale: Quantified mode-shift target with deadline; followed by multiple strategies.
mechanism_description: It sets a target to increase trips by transit, biking, walking, carpooling, and telework-related trip avoidance, reducing driving and transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Expand and improve public transportation

Work with the Austin Transit Partnership to implement Project Connect and expand and improve public transportation services.
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation commitment to deliver transit improvements through Project Connect.
mechanism_description: Implementing Project Connect expands and improves public transportation services, supporting shifts from private car travel to transit and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options and expand free transit options to increase access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Promote free transportation options

Create comprehensive, user-friendly resources connecting community members with free transportation options.

How we'll get there:

- Expand free transit options, including through Capital Metro, to provide increased transportation access for low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities.
extraction_rationale: Concrete access policy (resources + expanding free transit).
mechanism_description: The strategy expands free transit options and provides user-friendly information about them to increase access and use of public transportation, supporting lower transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Engage impacted communities to improve transit stops/stations and enforce ADA and accessibility guidelines around transit and public spaces.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Enhance transit stations and stops

Partner with Capital Metro, developers, and community organizers to engage low-income communities, communities of color, and people with disabilities to improve transit stops, stations, and access to these facilities.

How we'll get there:

- Enforce the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Public Rights-of-Way Accessibility Guidelines regulations...
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation and enforcement commitments for equitable, accessible transit facilities.
mechanism_description: It improves transit stops and stations and enforces ADA/accessibility guidelines to make transit easier to use, supporting higher transit use and reducing transportation emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Implement 2020 Proposition B bonds for transportation infrastructure (sidewalks, bikeways, trails, safety, safe routes to school, substandard streets) in low-income communities and communities of color through meaningful engagement.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Prioritize bicycle networks

Implement the 2020 Proposition B voter-approved general obligation bonds for transportation infrastructure... in low-income communities and communities of color through a meaningful community engagement process.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure investment implementation tied to a named funding mechanism (Proposition B).
mechanism_description: Implementing transportation infrastructure like sidewalks, bikeways, trails, and safe routes to school supports shifts toward walking and biking, which can reduce car travel and transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Provide access to free or reduced-price bicycles and bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve safety and comfort for riders of color.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 5: Enhance bicycle education and training

Provide access to free or reduced-priced bicycles and basic bicycle training for communities of color and train police officers on bicycle laws and racial profiling to improve the comfort and safety of people of color riding bicycles.
extraction_rationale: Specific program/training interventions with equity and safety focus.
mechanism_description: Providing free or reduced-price bicycles, basic bicycle training, and police training on bicycle laws and racial profiling increases rider comfort and safety, supporting more cycling in place of higher-emitting trips.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement, and build priority segments while addressing ADA barriers and gaps.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 6: Improve sidewalks, urban trails, and crossings

Update the City's Sidewalk Plan and Urban Trails Plan, emphasizing equity and meaningful community engagement.

How we'll get there:

- Build all high- and very-high-priority sidewalk and trail segments and address ADA barriers and gaps...
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning update and build-out commitments with accessibility requirements.
mechanism_description: The city plans to update its sidewalk and urban trails plans and build priority sidewalk/trail segments while addressing ADA barriers and gaps, improving active transportation access and enabling more walking and biking in place of car trips to reduce transport emissions.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION AND LAND USE — Goal: 50% mode shift by 2030
policy_statement: Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies, including a City telework policy encouraging at least 85% of eligible City employees to participate and supporting a goal of 15% residents working from home by 2039.
parent_statement: Ensure 50% of trips in Austin are made by public transit, biking, walking, carpooling, or avoided by working from home by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 7: Promote remote and flexible work policies

Actively promote remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to maintain and encourage further reductions in single-occupancy vehicle trips.

How we'll get there:

- ...all new City of Austin telework and remote work policies contribute towards the goal of achieving 15% Austin residents working from home by 2039. This should include a strategy to implement a City of Austin telework policy encouraging no less than 85% of eligible City of Austin employees to participate in telework.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy approach with quantifiable participation targets/timeframe.
mechanism_description: The city promotes remote work, telework, and flexible commute policies to reduce single-occupancy vehicle commute trips, which lowers transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text: By 2030, 40% of total vehicle miles traveled in Austin are electrified, and electric vehicle ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse. This translates to approximately 460,000 electric vehicles on the road.
extraction_rationale: Quantified electrification target with timeframe and scale estimate; followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: The city sets a target to electrify a substantial share of vehicle travel and broaden electric vehicle ownership, reducing transportation emissions by shifting miles from combustion vehicles to EVs.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment to inform an EV adoption growth plan, using community partners and hiring residents from the communities served.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Conduct an EV Community Needs Assessment

Complete an Electric Vehicle Community Needs Assessment... The assessment will inform an EV adoption growth plan...
extraction_rationale: Specific planning/assessment commitment tied to equity and EV adoption.
mechanism_description: Completing an EV community needs assessment helps identify barriers and priorities so an EV adoption growth plan can better enable increased EV uptake.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing EVs, prioritizing low-income communities and communities of color and areas with limited transit access.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Create equitable incentives for buying and leasing EVs

Collaborate with community partners to create inclusive and easily accessible incentives for buying or leasing electric vehicles. This strategy would... prioritize low-income communities and communities of color...
extraction_rationale: Specific incentive program commitment with equity prioritization.
mechanism_description: The city plans to create inclusive incentives for buying or leasing electric vehicles to increase EV adoption and reduce transportation emissions, prioritizing low-income communities and communities of color.

section_header: TRANSPORTATION ELECTRIFICATION — Goal: 40% VMT electrified by 2030
policy_statement: Partner with mobility authorities to reduce or eliminate toll rates for low-income communities and communities of color who drive EVs on toll roads from the Eastern Crescent to central Austin.
parent_statement: Electrify 40% of total vehicle miles traveled (VMT) in Austin by 2030 and ensure EV ownership is culturally, geographically, and economically diverse (about 460,000 EVs).
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Reduce tolls for EVs in the Eastern Crescent

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: 50% tree canopy by 2050
policy_statement: Create a tree, water, and soil management/resilience guide for private property and provide City technical and financial assistance for tree planting/care for residents and small businesses in low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Achieve at least 50% citywide tree canopy cover by 2050, focusing on increasing canopy cover equitably.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Promote tree health and resilience on private and non-City public lands

Create a tree, water and soil management, and resilience guide for various types of private property. Provide City technical and financial assistance for tree planting and care for residents and small businesses in low-income communities and communities of color...
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance plus assistance commitments targeting equity groups.
mechanism_description: The City creates a tree/water/soil resilience guide and provides technical and financial assistance to support tree planting and care on private property, expanding healthier tree canopy that helps reduce heat impacts and sequester carbon.

policy_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text: By 2030, include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound City land management requirement with emissions outcome (neutral/negative); followed by strategies.
mechanism_description: The city will put its publicly owned lands under a management plan designed to achieve carbon-neutral or net-negative outcomes, lowering net emissions from land management while improving community co-benefits.

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
policy_statement: Prioritize carbon neutrality and community benefits in public land acquisition and management, including completing land management database and using carbon-negative/low-carbon practices.
parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 1: Prioritize carbon neutrality for public lands

Prioritize carbon neutrality and community benefits in land acquisition and management practices for public lands.
extraction_rationale: Specific management and acquisition prioritization commitment.
mechanism_description: The city prioritizes carbon neutrality in public land acquisition and management practices to preserve and enhance ecosystem carbon storage and avoid associated emissions.

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
policy_statement: Reclaim underused public spaces to increase access and ecological function through green infrastructure, and conduct a study to convert an ambitious percentage of City surface parking lots to functional green uses where feasible.
parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 2: Reclaim public space and prioritize green infrastructure

Identify and reclaim mono-use, underused, and unconventional public spaces... with a focus on green infrastructure.

...A study should be conducted to examine the use of surface-level parking lots on City property to develop a plan to convert an ambitious percentage of these to functional green uses when feasible.
extraction_rationale: Concrete land-use/green infrastructure actions including a specified study and conversion planning.
mechanism_description: The city identifies underused public spaces and studies converting surface parking lots to green uses, expanding green infrastructure that improves ecological function and increases heat and stormwater resilience.

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
policy_statement: Promote community stewardship of neighborhood public lands by establishing Neighborhood Stewardship Councils/Ambassadors, providing paid training/compensation, and creating grant assistance and staff positions to support excluded communities.
parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 3: Promote community stewardship

Promote community stewardship and management of neighborhood public lands to ensure higher levels of care and maintenance.

How we'll get there:

- Establish Neighborhood Stewardship Councils or Ambassadors...
extraction_rationale: Specific governance/program design including paid roles and funding support mechanisms.
mechanism_description: It promotes community stewardship councils/ambassadors and provides support so neighborhood public lands receive higher levels of care and maintenance, strengthening local climate resilience.

section_header: NATURAL SYSTEMS — Goal: City-owned lands neutral/negative emissions management plans
policy_statement: Explore carbon farming via food forests and community gardens on public lands using low-carbon practices, including supporting local research in partnership with institutions and organizations serving low-income communities and communities of color.
parent_statement: Include all City-owned lands under a management plan that results in neutral or negative carbon emissions and maximizes community co-benefits by 2030.
verbatim_text:
## Strategy 4: Promote carbon farming

Explore the ability of food forests and community gardens on public lands to use low-carbon and carbon farming practices.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to explore and support carbon farming practices on public lands.
mechanism_description: The strategy explores using food forests and community gardens on public lands to apply low-carbon and carbon farming practices that increase carbon stored in soils and vegetation while improving ecosystem conditions.

policy_statement: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
verbatim_text: Austin will achieve net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions by 2040.
extraction_rationale: A quantified, time-bound citywide emissions target (net-zero by 2040).
mechanism_description: The city will pursue net-zero community-wide greenhouse gas emissions, creating an economy-wide pathway to reduce emissions across sectors.

policy_statement: Lebanon commits to unconditionally generate 18% of electricity (power demand) and 11% of building-sector heat demand from renewable energy sources by 2030.
verbatim_text: Moreover,  Lebanon  commits  to  unconditionally  generate  18%  of  the  power  demand  (i.e.  electricity demand) and 11% of its heat demand (in the building sector) from renewable energy sources in 2030, compared to a combined 15% in 2015.
extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy shares with a deadline (2030).
mechanism_description: Lebanon commits to increase the share of electricity generation and building-sector heat supplied by renewable energy, displacing fossil energy and lowering emissions.

policy_statement: Lebanon commits to reduce power demand through energy-efficiency measures by 3% under BAU in 2030 unconditionally, and by 10% under BAU in 2030 conditionally.
verbatim_text:
3. A 3% reduction in power demand through energy-efficiency measures in 2030 compared to the demand under the BAU scenario.
...
3. A 10% reduction in power demand through energy-efficiency in 2030 compared to the demand under the BAU scenario.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified energy-efficiency targets tied to BAU and a target year (2030).
mechanism_description: It commits to lowering power demand by using energy-efficiency measures, reducing the amount of electricity that needs to be generated.

section_header: An exemplary energy policy and building management
policy_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Energy efficiency in buildings and adapting to the effects of climate change

## 2. Producing and consuming green, local energy

## 3. Improving the sustainability of construction and renovation
extraction_rationale: Named thematic chapter followed by multiple numbered objective lists; serves as umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The strategy aims to improve energy efficiency in buildings, which reduces building energy use and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Reduce the energy consumption of City of Brussels buildings by 40% compared with 2008 (by 2030).
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
## 1. Energy efficiency in buildings and adapting to the effects of climate change

-  Reduce the energy consumption of City of Brussels buildings by 40% (compared with 2008).
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target with baseline year; clear policy objective.
mechanism_description: The city will reduce energy consumption in its buildings through energy-efficiency improvements, lowering overall building energy use.

policy_statement: Set intermediate building-stock targets: city/CPAS public buildings to reach average EPC C+; SISP social housing renovation plan to meet regional average EPC C+ by 2040 with priorities met by 2030; CPAS/Régie homes rated EPC F/G to be downgraded by 2033.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
To achieve the regional targets of energy neutrality for public buildings by 2040 and energy performance for private buildings by 2050, three intermediate targets have been set for the city's entire building stock:
- o Public heritage buildings owned by the city and the Public Social Assistance Centre (CPAS) will achieve an average Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) of C+ ...
- o For  Social  housing  (SISP),  there  will  be  a  renovation  plan ... by 2030 ... to meet the regional objective of achieving an average EPC level of C+ by 2040.
- o The most energy-intensive homes (EPC F and G) owned by the CPAS and the Régie Foncière will have been downgraded by 2033.
extraction_rationale: Contains multiple specific, time-bound building performance commitments.
mechanism_description: The city sets intermediate EPC improvement targets and a renovation plan for public buildings and social housing, leading to building upgrades that reduce overall building energy use.

policy_statement: Reduce by at least 55% (vs. 2008) all emissions linked to the energy consumption of City Council/Régie/CPAS/SISP public buildings by 2030.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  Compared  to  2008,  reduce  by  at  least  55%  all  emissions  linked  to  the  energy consumption of the public buildings of the City Council, the Régie, the CPAS and the SISPs by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions reduction target with baseline and deadline.
mechanism_description: The city aims to reduce emissions from energy consumption in public buildings by lowering the energy used to operate those buildings.

policy_statement: Enhance city flat roofs for climatic functions using the “Living Roofs” tool at design stage and implement “Living Roofs” recommendations for priority sites.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
-  Enhance  the  city's  flat roofs where  this  is possible with  a climatic function (photovoltaic solar panels, solar thermal panels, vegetation, bleaching, etc.):
- o Right  from the  design  stage  of  each  renovation  or  roofing  project,  the  City Council uses the "Living Roofs" tool...
- o The City Council will implement the recommendations of the "Living Roofs" study for the priority sites identified...
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention with named tool and implementation commitment.
mechanism_description: The City Council uses the “Living Roofs” tool during project design and will implement its recommendations to add climate functions (e.g., vegetation, bleaching, solar PV/thermal) to flat roofs, helping reduce urban heat and increase clean energy production that displaces fossil electricity.

policy_statement: Propose to the region a 10% reduction in energy consumption by 2030 compared with 2008.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council is proposing to the region to reduce its energy consumption by 10% in 2030 compared with 2008.
extraction_rationale: Explicit (non-binding/proposed) quantified target addressed to the region.
mechanism_description: The City Council is proposing a regional target to reduce overall energy consumption, lowering energy demand and related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Propose doubling regional photovoltaic solar production to 100 GWh/year, with City Council contributing 6 GWh.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
## 2. Producing and consuming green, local energy

-  Increase photovoltaic solar panel production in the region: ... proposing to the region that photovoltaic solar panel production be doubled, reaching 100 GWh per year, with the City Council contributing 6 GWh.
extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy production target including a specific City contribution.
mechanism_description: Increasing photovoltaic solar electricity production raises renewable generation, which displaces fossil-fuel electricity and lowers power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Ensure 100% of electricity consumed by the city's public buildings comes from renewable energy produced in Belgium.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  Increase the proportion of energy consumed by the city's public buildings, the Régie, the CPAS and the SISP that comes from renewable energy produced in Belgium: 100% of the electricity  consumed  by  the  city's  public  buildings  comes  from  renewable  energy produced in Belgium.
extraction_rationale: Clear binding procurement/consumption commitment with 100% target.
mechanism_description: The city increases the share of electricity used in public buildings that comes from renewable energy, helping displace fossil-based power generation and reduce associated emissions.

policy_statement: Develop at least one “Positive Energy Neighbourhood” in the city.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  Develop one or more "Positive Energy Neighbourhoods": The city has at least one Positive Energy Neighbourhood.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention with minimum count target.
mechanism_description: Developing positive energy neighbourhoods increases district-scale energy efficiency and local clean energy generation, which displaces fossil energy use and reduces emissions.

policy_statement: Develop an urban heating network in the city in collaboration with Brussels Energy.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  Develop an urban heating network in the city in collaboration with Brussels Energy.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure development commitment.
mechanism_description: Developing an urban heating network shifts buildings to a shared heat supply that can use more efficient and lower-carbon heat sources, reducing emissions from space and water heating.

policy_statement: Develop energy communities: set up at least one energy community and one collective self-consumption project in each neighbourhood.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  Develop energy communities: At least one energy community and one collective selfconsumption project have been set up in each of the city's neighbourhoods.
extraction_rationale: Specific, minimum-coverage target across neighbourhoods.
mechanism_description: Developing energy communities and collective self-consumption projects increases locally shared renewable electricity use, reducing reliance on fossil-based grid generation.

policy_statement: Facilitate collective self-consumption projects in privately rented housing to combat energy poverty.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  The  City  of  Brussels  is  facilitating  the  implementation  of  collective  self-consumption projects in the private housing it rents out, in order to combat energy poverty.
extraction_rationale: Specific enabling action linked to social objective (energy poverty).
mechanism_description: The city is facilitating collective self-consumption projects in rented private housing so more on-site renewable electricity can be used locally, displacing fossil-based grid electricity and helping reduce energy poverty.

policy_statement: Systematically use an environmental impact analysis tool (e.g., TOTEM) for construction/renovation projects by the City, Régie, CPAS and SISPs by 2030.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
## 3. Improving the sustainability of construction and renovation

-  By 2030, with regard to the environmental impact of construction/renovation projects, an impact analysis tool such as TOTEM will be systematically used by the City, the Régie, the CPAS and the SISPs to integrate the principles of sustainability and reversibility.
extraction_rationale: Mandated systematic tool use with deadline.
mechanism_description: The City and related public entities will systematically use an environmental impact analysis tool for construction and renovation projects to integrate sustainability and reversibility principles and reduce life-cycle impacts such as embodied emissions.

policy_statement: Adopt measures to reduce construction site environmental impacts and routinely analyse environmental impact of two variants for construction/renovation projects across the region.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  The  City  Council  is  proposing  an  ambitious  target  for  the  environmental  impact  of construction/renovation projects throughout the region: The City Council will be adopting measures  to  reduce  the  environmental  impact  of  the  worksites  and  will  be  routinely analysing the environmental impact of the two variants.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to adopt measures and routine analysis; framed as regional target proposal.
mechanism_description: The City Council will adopt measures to reduce construction/renovation worksite impacts and will routinely analyze environmental impacts of project variants to lower emissions from construction activities.

policy_statement: Develop a cross-cutting checklist of sustainability criteria (including circular economy) to be integrated into upstream design of each renovation/construction project.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text:
-  Incorporate the circular economy dimension (reversibility, flexibility, pooling, reuse, etc.) into construction and renovation projects right from the design stage. : The City Council
is committed to developing a cross-cutting checklist of sustainability criteria, including the circular economy dimension, which will be integrated into the upstream thinking of each renovation/construction project.
extraction_rationale: Specific instrument (checklist) to be developed and applied.
mechanism_description: The city council commits to developing a sustainability checklist that is integrated into early project design so renovation and construction projects incorporate circular-economy practices that reduce embodied emissions from materials and construction.

policy_statement: Incorporate water management and biodiversity protection systems (e.g., green roofs, cisterns, permeability, nesting boxes, urban agriculture) from the design stage where conditions allow.
parent_statement: An exemplary energy policy and building management (2030 strategic objectives) for energy efficiency, renewable/local energy, and sustainable construction/renovation.
verbatim_text: -  From the design stage of construction and renovation projects, incorporate systems for managing water  and  protecting  biodiversity  where  conditions  allow  (e.g.  green  roofs, cisterns, valleys, permeability, nesting boxes, urban agriculture, etc.).
extraction_rationale: Concrete design requirement with examples of measures.
mechanism_description: Incorporating green roofs, cisterns, permeable surfaces, and habitat features into construction and renovation designs helps manage water and protect biodiversity, strengthening urban resilience.

section_header: Public space, a living space served  by  low-carbon,  peaceful and active forms of transport
policy_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

1. Rethinking public space as a place to live

## 2. Encouraging  a  modal  shift  towards  low-carbon,  active  and  shared transport: walking, cycling, public transport, etc.

3. Becoming a pioneer city in sustainable freight transport

## 4. An exemplary employer
extraction_rationale: Thematic chapter with multiple numbered objective blocks; functions as parent for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The strategy encourages a modal shift toward low-carbon, active, and shared transport (walking, cycling, public transport), which reduces transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Incorporate the 10-Minute City ambition into urban planning and development to improve quality of life by 2030.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:  Urban planning and development incorporate the 10-Minute City ambition to improve the quality of life of the people of Brussels.
extraction_rationale: Specific planning commitment tied to defined concept (10-Minute City).
mechanism_description: Incorporating the 10-Minute City ambition into urban planning and development supports closer access to daily needs and more walking, cycling, and transit use, which can reduce car travel and related emissions while improving quality of life.

policy_statement: Routinely apply the STOP principle prioritising walking, cycling/active micro-mobility, public transport, then shared private transport and private cars.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:  The  City  of  Brussels  routinely  applies  the  STOP  principle (prioritising  the  different modes of transport and favouring them in the following order: walking, bicycles and active micro transport ...), public transport, collective private transport ... and finally individual private transport...
extraction_rationale: Defined policy principle with commitment to routine application.
mechanism_description: The city applies a transport prioritization approach that favors walking, cycling/active micromobility, and public transport ahead of private cars, supporting a shift to lower-emission travel and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Increase by 50% the public space dedicated to public transport, active transport, living/meeting spaces and green spaces by 2030.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:  The public space of 2030 is improved by increasing by 50% the public space dedicated to public transport, active transport, living/meeting spaces and green spaces.
extraction_rationale: Quantified land-use/public-space reallocation target.
mechanism_description: It improves public space by increasing space for public transport and active transport, enabling a shift from car travel to lower-emission modes and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Achieve modal share targets by 2030: cycling ~15% of journeys; walking 30%; public transport 30%; private motor vehicle trips down to 25%.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:
-  Walking, cycling and public transport are encouraged, easy and safe... Cycling accounts for around 15% of journeys made in the City of Brussels. Walking
- accounts for 30% of all journeys.
-  Public transport ... accounting for 30% of journeys in the City of Brussels.
-  Car traffic is reduced: Trips made by private motor vehicle now account for only a quarter of all journeys...
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified modal share targets clearly stated.
mechanism_description: Encourage higher shares of walking, cycling, and public transport while reducing private car trips to lower transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Reduce traffic from conventional thermal combustion vehicles by 50% in favour of electric and hybrid vehicles by 2030.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text: -  Traffic from conventional thermal combustion vehicles has fallen by 50% in favour of electric and hybrid vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Quantified vehicle technology transition target.
mechanism_description: Shifts traffic away from conventional thermal combustion vehicles toward electric and hybrid vehicles, reducing road transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Reduce heavy goods vehicle presence in residential areas and around schools/crèches/sports halls and increase environmentally-friendly, pooled last-mile logistics.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:
3. Becoming a pioneer city in sustainable freight transport
-  Fewer heavy goods vehicles in residential areas and around schools, crèches, sports halls, etc.
-  Gradual increase in environmentally-friendly modes of transport and pooling of lastmile logistics.
extraction_rationale: Concrete freight measures (restriction + modal/pooling shift).
mechanism_description: It aims for fewer heavy goods vehicles in residential and school-adjacent areas and a gradual shift to environmentally friendly, pooled last-mile logistics, reducing freight-related transport emissions.

policy_statement: Increase the number of sustainable logistics hubs and encourage operators to offer sustainable logistics solutions (pilot schemes).
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:
-  Logistics hub: Support sustainable freight transport and distribution logistics through pilot schemes:
- o The  City  Council  has  set  itself  the  objective  of  increasing  the  number  of sustainable logistics hubs...
- o The  City  Council  encourages  commercial  and  transport  operators  to  offer sustainable logistics solutions.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (pilot schemes) plus objective to increase hubs.
mechanism_description: The City Council supports pilot schemes to increase sustainable logistics hubs and encourages operators to offer sustainable logistics solutions, helping shift freight distribution toward lower-emission operations and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, ensure over 90% of public authority home-to-work journeys use low-carbon modes (vs. 80% in 2019).
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:
## 4. An exemplary employer

-  A local authority that uses low-carbon modes of transport: By 2030, more than 90% of the public authority's home-to-work journeys will be made using low-carbon modes of transport, compared with 80% in 2019.
extraction_rationale: Quantified commuting modal shift target with baseline.
mechanism_description: It sets a target for public authority home-to-work journeys to shift to low-carbon transport modes, reducing commuting-related transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, establish a shared and co-managed vehicle fleet across City Council, CPAS and para-communal non-profits, and reduce/optimize fleet use (20% fleet reduction vs 2018; share 70% of non-utility vehicles internally; 100% low-carbon engines where alternatives exist).
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text:
-  Based on similar needs and uses, streamline and pool vehicles ...:    By  2030,  the  City Council, CPAS and the para-communal non-profit organisations will have a shared and co-managed fleet of vehicles.
-  The City Council is committed to reducing its fleet of vehicles by 20% compared with 2018, and to sharing 70% of non-utility vehicles internally.
-  100% of the car fleet uses a low-carbon engine, provided that the alternative exists.
extraction_rationale: Multiple specific fleet management targets and commitments with dates/baselines.
mechanism_description: The City Council, CPAS and para-communal non-profits will pool and optimize vehicle use through a shared fleet and shift cars to low-carbon engines where alternatives exist, reducing emissions from fleet travel.

policy_statement: Adopt a business travel policy prioritising rail over air for trips under 8 hours, and prefer environmentally-friendly modes for journeys under 5 km where feasible.
parent_statement: Public space and transport strategy (2030 strategic objectives) to support low-carbon, active and shared mobility, sustainable freight, and exemplary employer mobility.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council has a business travel policy and will give preference to rail over air for all train journeys of less than 8 hours. In addition, for every journey of less than 5 km, where logistics allow, the City Council will give preference to environmentally-friendly modes of transport.
extraction_rationale: Clear operational travel rules (mode preference thresholds) are specific policy measures.
mechanism_description: The City Council will give preference to rail over air for trips under 8 hours and to environmentally-friendly modes for journeys under 5 km, shifting travel to lower-emission options and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: Zero waste and circular resource management
policy_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Consuming differently by reducing the quantity of waste

## 2. Reusing the resources around us

## 3. Recycling through better organisation of waste sorting

## 4. Composting bio-waste from the city and third parties
extraction_rationale: Thematic chapter with four numbered strategic objective groups; umbrella for multiple sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The city plans to compost bio-waste and improve reuse and recycling so less organic material is landfilled, reducing methane emissions and recovering nutrients.

policy_statement: Achieve an overall 20% reduction in waste produced annually per inhabitant and by professionals (aligned with regional targets) by 2030.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: -  Overall 20% reduction in the quantities of waste produced annually per inhabitant and by professionals, in line with regional targets.
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target.
mechanism_description: The policy sets a target to reduce the amount of waste produced per person and by professionals, lowering overall waste generation and the emissions associated with managing and disposing of that waste.

policy_statement: Reduce waste sent to incinerator (white bags) by 30% by 2030.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: -  30% reduction in waste sent to the incinerator (white bags).
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste-stream reduction target.
mechanism_description: It aims to reduce the amount of residual waste sent to incineration, lowering emissions from waste combustion.

policy_statement: Reduce waste produced by City Council departments by 30% by 2030.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: -  30% reduction in waste produced by the City Council departments.
extraction_rationale: Quantified internal waste reduction commitment.
mechanism_description: The City Council aims to cut waste generated by its departments, reducing disposal needs and avoiding emissions associated with landfilling waste.

policy_statement: From July 2023, stop serving food/drink prepared in single-use catering equipment and serve only mains water for City Council activities and City-organised events (zero-waste strategy).
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: In  line  with  the  Brussels-Capital  Region's  BRUDALEX  2.0,  from  July  2023  the  City Council will no longer serve food and drink prepared in single-use catering equipment, and will only serve mains water as part of its own activities and at events it organises.
extraction_rationale: Clear operational restriction with start date; concrete zero-waste measure.
mechanism_description: The City Council will stop serving food and drink prepared in single-use catering equipment and will only serve mains water at its activities and organised events, reducing single-use items and the waste they create.

policy_statement: Label 500 'welcome container' businesses in the city to promote zero-waste solutions in shops.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text:
-  Shops offer 'zero waste' solutions:

Labelling of 500 welcome container businesses in the city.
extraction_rationale: Quantified programmatic action (labelling) to drive waste reduction.
mechanism_description: The city labels “welcome container” businesses to promote zero-waste shopping options that increase reuse and reduce single-use packaging waste.

policy_statement: Promote sustainable businesses via 5 zero-waste routes around the city.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: -  The City  of  Brussels  is  committed  to  promoting  sustainable  businesses  via  5  zerowaste routes around the city (see existing routes).
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention with quantifiable count (5 routes).
mechanism_description: The city is committed to promoting zero-waste routes for businesses to increase waste diversion and reduce landfill disposal, avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Ensure each new City Council contract is geared towards a Zero Waste policy.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: -  The  City  Council  is  committed  to  ensuring  that  each  new  City  Council  contract  is geared towards a Zero Waste policy.
extraction_rationale: Procurement requirement stated as commitment.
mechanism_description: The City Council commits to gearing new contracts toward a Zero Waste policy to increase waste diversion from landfill, avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Set up a waste recovery and reconditioning method at one of the city's markets.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council undertakes to set up a waste recovery and reconditioning method at one of the city's markets.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program action with defined location type (market).
mechanism_description: The City Council undertakes to set up a waste recovery and reconditioning method at a market to divert waste from landfill, reducing methane emissions from disposal.

policy_statement: Ensure 70% of bulky-collection waste is recycled/upgraded/recovered directly by social economy actors and 90% of bulky-collection waste is sorted.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text:
-  Increase the recycling and reuse of waste collected during bulky goods collections...:  70%  of  the  waste  collected during bulky goods collections is recycled, upgraded or recovered...
-  Increase  the  sorting  of  waste  collected  during  bulky  goods  collections:  90%  of  the waste collected during bulky goods collections is sorted.
extraction_rationale: Two quantified targets for bulky waste management.
mechanism_description: The policy increases sorting and recycling/reuse/recovery of bulky-collection waste so less material is sent to landfill, avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Improve sorting so that 60% of household waste is ready for reuse/recycling; 70% of non-household (non-construction) waste suitable; 95% of construction waste ready for reuse/recycling.
