parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text:
-  Improved sorting of household waste: 60% of the weight of household waste is ready for re-use and recycling  1 .
-  Increase  in  the  proportion  of  non-household  waste ...: 70% ... is suitable for re-use and recycling.
-  Increase in the proportion of construction waste ready for re-use and recycling: 95% of construction waste is ready for re-use and recycling.
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified sorting/recycling readiness targets by waste stream.
mechanism_description: Improved waste sorting increases the share of materials ready for re-use and recycling, diverting waste from landfill and avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Recover 100% of biowaste produced by the city and set up individual and collective composting facilities wherever possible, including community composting accessible to all residents under the 10-Minute City initiative.
parent_statement: Zero waste and circular resource management strategy (2030 strategic objectives) covering waste reduction, reuse, recycling/sorting, and biowaste composting.
verbatim_text:
-  Increase the recovery of bio-waste produced by the city: Recover 100% of the biowaste produced by the city.
...
-  Set up individual and collective composting facilities wherever possible:

As part of the 10-Minute City initiative, the City Council aims to set up community composting  facilities  accessible  to  all  Brussels  residents...
extraction_rationale: Quantified recovery target plus specific composting facility rollout commitment.
mechanism_description: The city aims to increase biowaste recovery by setting up individual, collective, and community composting facilities, diverting organics from disposal to composting to avoid methane emissions and recover nutrients.

policy_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

1. Water, a precious resource to be consumed sustainably

## 2. Combatting the growing risk of flooding

## 3. Blue  mesh:  Using  water  as  a  source  of  freshness...

## 4. Preserving water quality
extraction_rationale: Water chapter introduces multiple numbered strategic objective lists; acts as umbrella.
mechanism_description: The plan aims to manage water more sustainably by reducing water consumption and protecting water systems (including flood risk reduction, cooling benefits from the blue network, and water quality preservation).

policy_statement: Reduce mains water consumption by 40% for building use and outdoor maintenance by identifying/treating leaks in all buildings and equipping public buildings (where possible) with water storage capacity matching needs; also deploy municipal rainwater storage covering 20 days’ use and equip renovated public buildings similarly (if conditions allow).
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text:
 Reduce our consumption of mains water by 40% in the use of buildings and in the maintenance of outdoor areas: The City Council has set itself the target of identifying and treating every leak in all of its buildings, as well as equipping all public buildings, where technically possible, with a water storage capacity...
-  Equip the municipality with a rainwater storage capacity. Together, these installations will cover the equivalent of 20 days' use...
-  During renovation, equip public buildings with rainwater storage capacity equivalent to 20 days' use, if conditions allow.
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target plus concrete measures and storage-capacity targets.
mechanism_description: The City Council sets a target to identify and treat leaks in its buildings and to equip public buildings and the municipality with rainwater storage capacity, reducing consumption of mains water for buildings and outdoor maintenance.

policy_statement: Increase access to drinking water by making public drinking fountains easily accessible in dense areas and near every sports ground and children’s play area.
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text: -  Increase access to drinking water: Public drinking fountains will be easily accessible in densely  built-up  areas  and  in  the  immediate  vicinity  of  every  sports  ground  and children's play area.
extraction_rationale: Specific service-access commitment with defined locations.
mechanism_description: Public drinking fountains will be made easily accessible in dense areas and near sports grounds and children’s play areas, improving access to drinking water and helping people stay hydrated during heat.

policy_statement: Ensure 100% of roadworks in priority intervention zones and upstream of flood-prone areas incorporate Integrated Stormwater Management (ISWM).
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text: -  Generalise  Integrated  Stormwater  Management  (ISWM)  during  public  works  in priority areas and upstream of them: The City Council is committed to ensuring that 100% of roadworks in priority intervention zones and upstream of flood-prone areas incorporate integrated rainwater management.
extraction_rationale: Binding-scope commitment with 100% coverage requirement.
mechanism_description: The City Council commits to incorporating integrated stormwater (rainwater) management into roadworks in priority areas to reduce flooding risk in flood-prone zones.

policy_statement: Permeabilise or disconnect from sewers at least 250,000 m² of impermeable surfaces in the Molenbeek catchment priority intervention zone.
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council is committed to permeabilising or disconnecting from the sewers at least  250,000m²  of  impermeable  surfaces  in  the  priority  intervention  zone  of  the Molenbeek catchment area.
extraction_rationale: Quantified area-based flood management target.
mechanism_description: The City Council is committed to making hard surfaces permeable or disconnecting them from sewers to reduce stormwater runoff entering the sewer system and improve drainage resilience.

policy_statement: Ensure 100% of City Council projects in priority flood control zones incorporate integrated rainwater management measures.
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council's projects in priority flood control zones include integrated rainwater management system projects: The City Council is committed to ensuring that 100% of its projects incorporate integrated rainwater management system measures.
extraction_rationale: 100% integration requirement for own projects in defined zones.
mechanism_description: The City Council commits to incorporating integrated rainwater management measures in its projects in priority flood control zones to reduce stormwater runoff and flood risk.

policy_statement: Encourage local stakeholders to implement ISWM in priority intervention zones with a target of at least 50 voluntary ISWM projects supported via awareness campaign and grants.
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council will encourage the integration of integrated rainwater management system  intervention  for  all  projects  by  local  interested  parties...: At least 50 integrated rainwater management system projects by local interested parties in priority intervention zones are carried out on a voluntary basis ... via  the  City  Council's  flood  control  awareness campaign, with the help of municipal and regional grants.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target plus mechanism (campaign + grants) directed at third parties.
mechanism_description: The City Council will encourage voluntary integrated rainwater management projects, supported by awareness campaigns and grants, to improve stormwater handling and reduce flood risk in priority zones.

policy_statement: Implement the blue network to create permeable/infiltrating water surfaces linked to the green network; ensure cool/recreational water access within 10 minutes in every neighbourhood; and provide an accessible natural open-air bathing area in the City of Brussels region.
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text:
-  The blue network will be implemented, creating a network of permeable, infiltrating water surfaces throughout the area, linked to the green network.
-  Increase the number of cool areas or recreational access to water in every City of Brussels neighbourhood, accessible in less than 10 minutes from any point in the city.
-  A natural open-air bathing area will be accessible in the City of Brussels region.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/access commitments including a quantified accessibility standard.
mechanism_description: It will implement a connected network of permeable, infiltrating water surfaces linked to green spaces and expand access to cool recreational water areas, improving urban cooling and stormwater resilience.

policy_statement: Maintain good water quality in ponds and semi-natural water areas via constant monitoring and a defined/applied intervention procedure; and work with other authorities to reduce sewage discharges into the canal and the Seine river.
parent_statement: Towards satisfactory water management (2030 strategic objectives) covering water consumption reduction, flood risk reduction, blue network cooling/access to water, and water quality protection.
verbatim_text:
-  Good water quality ... will be maintained in all the ponds and (semi-) natural water areas: constant monitoring will be carried out and an intervention procedure will be defined and applied.
-  The City Council undertakes to do its utmost, in cooperation with the Regional Council and the municipalities in the same catchment area, to reduce the discharge of sewage into the canal and the Seine river.
extraction_rationale: Operational commitments (monitoring/procedure; intergovernmental action to reduce discharges).
mechanism_description: The city will carry out constant monitoring of ponds and semi-natural water areas, define and apply an intervention procedure, and work with other authorities to reduce sewage discharges into the canal and river to maintain good water quality.

policy_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Relocating sustainable production

## 2. A city that meets the demand for sustainable, accessible food

## 3. Reducing food waste
extraction_rationale: Food chapter has multiple numbered strategic objective lists; acts as umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The strategy aims to reduce food waste, which lowers emissions from producing, transporting, and disposing of wasted food and can reduce methane if less food ends up in landfill.

policy_statement: Set aside 10 new hectares for agro-ecological open-ground professional farming on city land/property and set up 4 pilot projects in city green spaces.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text:
-  The City Council promotes the use of surfaces ... to encourage agro-ecology...
- o 10  new  hectares  have  been  set  aside  for  agro-ecological,  open-ground professional farming on city land and/or property.
- o 4 pilot projects will be set up in the city's green spaces.
extraction_rationale: Quantified land allocation and pilot project commitments.
mechanism_description: The city promotes agro-ecology by setting aside land and setting up pilot projects for agro-ecological farming, supporting practices that can build soil carbon and restore ecosystems.

policy_statement: Propose doubling the number of professionals in urban agriculture and explore developing qualifying training in horticulture/market gardening.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text:
-  Increase the number of professionals working in the field of urban agriculture:
- o The City Council is proposing to double the number of professionals working in the field of urban agriculture.
- o The city will look into the possibility of developing the provision of training leading to qualifications in horticulture and market gardening.
extraction_rationale: Quantified workforce proposal plus concrete training exploration commitment.
mechanism_description: The city proposes to increase the number of urban agriculture professionals and look into developing horticulture and market-gardening qualification training to support expanded urban food production and local resilience.

policy_statement: Eliminate the use of synthetic pesticides in the city.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  Synthetic pesticides are no longer used in the city.
extraction_rationale: Clear prohibition-style commitment.
mechanism_description: Stopping the use of synthetic pesticides reduces toxic chemical exposure and associated harm to local ecosystems.

policy_statement: If procurement rules allow, source at least 50% of fruits and vegetables purchased by the City of Brussels from local production channels aligned with EU Farm to Fork objectives.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  With  a  view  to  reterritorialization,  and  if  current  European  regulations  on  public procurement allow, at least 50% of the fruit and vegetables purchased by the City of Brussels come from local production channels and contribute to the objectives of the European Farm To Fork strategy.
extraction_rationale: Quantified procurement target with conditionality explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: The City of Brussels plans to source a large share of its purchased fruits and vegetables from local production channels aligned with the EU Farm to Fork strategy, shifting demand in ways that reduce food-related supply-chain emissions.

policy_statement: Double the number of local producers and artisans at the city's markets and prioritise permits for local producers/artisans.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  Develop local sources for the city's markets: Double the number of local producers and artisans at the city's markets. The town gives priority to providing permits to local producers and artisans.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target plus administrative mechanism (permit priority).
mechanism_description: The town develops local sources for the city's markets by prioritising permits for local producers and artisans, increasing local sourcing and reducing transport-related emissions from supplying goods.

policy_statement: Propose regional targets: 50 Good Food shops and 100 Good Food label restaurants.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text:
-  Increase the number of Good Food and bulk products on offer in the city: The city is proposing that the region should have 50 Good Food shops.
-  Increase the number of Good Food restaurants: The city is proposing that the region reach 100 restaurants with the Good Food label.
extraction_rationale: Quantified proposed targets addressed to the region.
mechanism_description: The city is proposing regional targets to increase the number of “Good Food” shops and “Good Food” label restaurants, supporting a shift toward lower-emissions food options and reduced food-system emissions.

policy_statement: Propose launching at least 5 neighbourhood kitchens in the city.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  Encourage  "neighbourhood  kitchens"  throughout  the  city:  The  City  Council  is proposing to the region to launch at least 5 "neighbourhood kitchens" in the city.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention with minimum number.
mechanism_description: The city council is proposing to launch neighbourhood kitchens to support community food provision, which can reduce food waste and the amount of organic waste going to landfill, lowering methane emissions.

policy_statement: Set up at least one logistics hub within the city for pooling deliveries and/or waste management and support logistics tools for pooling sustainable food operators in shopping districts.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text:
-  Setting up logistics hubs in the city:
- o At least one logistics hub within the city, with the aim of pooling deliveries and/or waste management.
- o The City Council undertakes to support logistical resources or tools dedicated to the pooling of sustainable food operators in shopping districts.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure/tool commitments with minimum count.
mechanism_description: The city sets up logistics hubs and supports pooling tools so deliveries (and/or waste logistics) are consolidated, reducing delivery traffic and associated transport emissions.

policy_statement: Promote flexitarian diets with a target that 50% of Brussels residents eat meat 4 days a week or less (vs. 36% in 2020).
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  Promoting the flexitarian diet to the people of Brussels: 50% of Brussels residents eat meat 4 days a week or less (36% in 2020).
extraction_rationale: Quantified behavioural/diet target with baseline.
mechanism_description: Promoting flexitarian diets reduces meat consumption, which lowers demand for high-emission livestock products and cuts food-related greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Ensure food served in city facilities meets Good Food criteria: meals served by Cuisines Bruxelloises are 100% organic; meat reduced to 60% (as stated), without increasing red meat; vegetarian menus 3 days out of 5.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  The food served in the city's facilities fully complies with Good Food criteria: ... the meals served by the Cuisines Bruxelloises to the City (schools, crèches, etc.) are 100% organic, have reduced the amount of meat to 60% (previously 50%), without increasing the proportion of red meat, and the menus are vegetarian 3 days out of 5.
extraction_rationale: Operational meal/procurement standards with quantified requirements.
mechanism_description: The city serves food in its facilities that follows Good Food criteria, using organic meals and more vegetarian menus with reduced meat, which lowers the climate impact of food consumption.

policy_statement: Host three zero-waste markets by 2030 and run awareness campaigns to encourage bulk and organic products in markets.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text: -  Encourage the development of "bulk" and "organic" products in the city's markets: The city is committed to hosting three zero-waste markets in its region by 2030, and to conducting awareness-raising campaigns.
extraction_rationale: Quantified commitment with timeframe and accompanying measure.
mechanism_description: The city commits to hosting zero-waste markets and running awareness-raising campaigns to encourage bulk and organic products, reducing single-use packaging and overall waste generated.

policy_statement: Reduce food waste by 50% by 2030 within city infrastructure and call on the region to reduce food waste by 50% by 2030; recover at least 100 tons/year of surplus/unsold food.
parent_statement: Towards more sustainable food systems (2030 strategic objectives) covering agro-ecological production, Good Food availability, procurement/meal standards, and food waste reduction.
verbatim_text:
-  Reduce food waste within the city's  infrastructure:  Reduce food  waste by  50% by 2030...
-  Operate a business within the City of Brussels. The city is calling  on  the  region to reduce food waste by 50% by 2030.
-  Increased food recovery from food surpluses/unsold food: At least 100 tons of surplus food are recovered every year.
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste reduction and recovery targets with deadline.
mechanism_description: The city plans to cut food waste in its infrastructure and increase recovery of surplus/unsold food, reducing organic waste disposal and avoiding landfill methane emissions.

section_header: A  green  city  that  welcomes biodiversity
policy_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Protecting and developing local and urban biodiversity

## 2. Preserving biodiversity on a global scale

## 3. Using nature as a solution to combat heat islands
extraction_rationale: Biodiversity chapter provides multiple numbered strategic objective lists; serves as umbrella.
mechanism_description: The policy aims to protect and develop urban biodiversity and use nature-based solutions (e.g., greener, connected green spaces) to reduce heat-island impacts while supporting carbon storage.

policy_statement: Label 100% of the city's green spaces as “Réseau Nature” by 2030 under differentiated management criteria including absence of synthetic pesticides and prioritising indigenous plants.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text:
-  Develop a differentiated management system for green spaces, meeting the criteria of  the  "Réseau  Nature"  label... absence  of  synthetic  pesticides...
- o 100% of the city's green spaces to be labelled Réseau Nature by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified coverage target tied to defined label/management criteria.
mechanism_description: The city will develop a differentiated green space management system so green spaces meet “Réseau Nature” criteria (including no synthetic pesticides and prioritizing indigenous plants), improving urban ecosystem condition and resilience.

policy_statement: Map and develop the city's green network by 2030 to complement the regional network (new green spaces/greening incl. trees, shrubs, climbing plants, green roofs) and identify priority migration corridors leading to biodiversity measures.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text:
-  Develop  the  City  of  Brussels'  green  network ...
- o By  2030,  the  city's  green  network  will  be  mapped  and  developed  to complement the regional network... Priority migration corridors will have been identified,  giving  rise  to  new  measures  to  promote  biodiversity  and  species migration.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound mapping/development commitment with specific actions.
mechanism_description: The city plans to map and develop an interconnected green network and priority migration corridors, leading to new biodiversity measures that support species movement and preserve ecosystem functions (with associated carbon and resilience benefits).

policy_statement: Ensure every resident has access to a local green space or park within 10 minutes.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text: -  Complete the green network so that every resident has access to a local green space or park within 10 minutes.
extraction_rationale: Clear accessibility/service standard.
mechanism_description: Completing the green network expands access to parks and green space, which helps reduce heat impacts and improves stormwater resilience.

policy_statement: Develop 2 pilot projects to protect nocturnal biodiversity while guaranteeing a sense of security.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text: -  Preserving  biodiversity:  developing  2  pilot  projects  aimed  at  protecting  nocturnal biodiversity while guaranteeing a sense of security.
extraction_rationale: Specific pilot project commitment with count.
mechanism_description: Developing pilot projects to protect nocturnal biodiversity helps preserve ecosystems while maintaining a sense of security.

policy_statement: Continue the Neder-Over-Hembeek Urban Forest project to create an extensive biodiversity conservatory.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text: -  Continuing  the  Neder-Over-Hembeek  Urban  Forest  project  to  create  an  extensive biodiversity conservatory.
extraction_rationale: Named ongoing project continuation commitment.
mechanism_description: Continuing the urban forest project expands urban tree and habitat cover, supporting biodiversity while increasing carbon uptake and reducing urban heat impacts.

policy_statement: Adopt and enforce a tree charter for all local operators to ensure appropriate species selection/planting/management, integrating principles throughout project and tree life cycle.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text: -  Adopt and ensure compliance with a tree charter aimed at all local operators, so that the right species can be planted in the right places and in the right way, and these trees can be managed with respect. Integrate these principles as early as possible in the project and throughout the life of the trees...
extraction_rationale: Governance instrument (charter) with compliance/enforcement intent.
mechanism_description: Adopt and ensure compliance with a tree charter so operators choose suitable species, plant them appropriately, and manage trees over their life, supporting a healthier urban tree canopy that mitigates heat and sequesters carbon.

policy_statement: Plant 8 trees for each newborn child registered, in deforestation-sensitive areas in UN-recognised developing countries, to increase carbon sinks.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text: -  To increase carbon sinks, the City of Brussels is committed to planting 8 trees for each new-born child registered in its population registers in deforestation-sensitive areas in countries recognised by the UN as "Developing Countries".
extraction_rationale: Quantified tree-planting commitment tied to registry events.
mechanism_description: The city is committed to planting trees in deforestation-sensitive areas to increase carbon sinks through added biomass that sequesters carbon.

policy_statement: Incorporate biodiversity criteria into sustainable procurement for goods/services and Cuisines Bruxelloises food contracts to combat global ecosystem erosion.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text: -  The  city  is  helping  to  combat  the  erosion  of  ecosystems  on  a  global  scale  by incorporating biodiversity criteria into its sustainable procurement strategy for goods and services, and into the Cuisines Bruxelloises food contracts.
extraction_rationale: Procurement policy mechanism explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: The city incorporates biodiversity criteria into its sustainable procurement strategy and food contracts to help reduce global ecosystem erosion and support biodiversity preservation.

policy_statement: Carry out 3 pilot projects to reduce urban heat island effects and use vegetation as the first solution to combat heat.
parent_statement: A green city that welcomes biodiversity (2030 strategic objectives) to manage green spaces without synthetic pesticides, develop a connected green network, improve access, and enhance tree governance and global biodiversity actions.
verbatim_text:
## 3. Using nature as a solution to combat heat islands

-  The city is combatting heat islands in public spaces and buildings.
- o The city is committed to carrying out 3 pilot projects to reduce the urban heat island phenomenon.
- o The city is committed to using vegetation as the first solution to combat heat.
extraction_rationale: Specific pilot count plus prioritised intervention approach.
mechanism_description: The city is committed to piloting vegetation-based nature solutions in public spaces and buildings to reduce urban heat island impacts.

section_header: Brussels, a benchmark for sustainable events, culture, sport and tourism
policy_statement: Brussels, a benchmark for sustainable events, culture, sport and tourism (2030 strategic objectives) requiring action plans and sustainability standards for cultural venues, events, and tourism.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. An environmentally-responsible cultural policy

## 2. An environmentally-responsible events policy

## 3. An environmentally-responsible tourism policy
extraction_rationale: Thematic chapter with multiple numbered objective lists; umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: It sets objectives for environmentally-responsible culture, events, and tourism so that sustainability standards and action plans reduce the environmental impacts and associated emissions from these activities.

policy_statement: Ensure all City-funded/directly dependent cultural venues have an action plan aligned with climate objectives (and raise awareness for other venues), including low-carbon transport solutions, Good Food catering, local sourcing (including local currency), and reduced energy use.
parent_statement: Brussels, a benchmark for sustainable events, culture, sport and tourism (2030 strategic objectives) requiring action plans and sustainability standards for cultural venues, events, and tourism.
verbatim_text:
-  The city undertakes to ensure that all cultural venues (museums, theatres and cultural centres) that are funded and directly dependent on the City Council have put in place an action plan designed to meet the area's climate objectives...
- o Routinely  proposing  transport  solutions...
- o An increase in the catering offering that meets the "Good Food" criteria.
- o Promoting local sourcing ... through  the  use  of  a  local  Brussels currency.
- o Reduced energy consumption.
extraction_rationale: Mandated action plan requirement with specific components.
mechanism_description: The city undertakes to ensure City-funded cultural venues put in place climate-aligned action plans that reduce energy consumption and promote lower-carbon transport and catering/sourcing, supporting emissions reductions from venue operations and associated activities.

policy_statement: Ensure 100% of City-organised events comply with BME sustainability action plan; require events on City territory to meet mandatory eco-event charter criteria and have 50% go further via non-mandatory recommendations, focusing on transport, vegetarian food, waste sorting/reusable crockery, and reduced energy use.
parent_statement: Brussels, a benchmark for sustainable events, culture, sport and tourism (2030 strategic objectives) requiring action plans and sustainability standards for cultural venues, events, and tourism.
verbatim_text: -  The City Council undertakes to ensure that 100% of the events organised by the City Council  comply  with  the  BME's  sustainability  action  plan,  and  that  the  events organised on the City's territory comply with the mandatory criteria of the sustainable event charter and 50% propose to go further via non-mandatory recommendations...
extraction_rationale: Clear compliance requirements with quantified shares (100%, 50%) and specified focus areas.
mechanism_description: The City Council undertakes to ensure City-organised events comply with a sustainability action plan and that events on City territory comply with sustainable event charter criteria, reducing event-related transport, energy use, and waste and thereby lowering associated emissions.

policy_statement: Double the number of tourists arriving by train and double the number of cycle tourists through promotion/facilitation measures; double the number of tourist accommodations with Green Key certification.
parent_statement: Brussels, a benchmark for sustainable events, culture, sport and tourism (2030 strategic objectives) requiring action plans and sustainability standards for cultural venues, events, and tourism.
verbatim_text:
increase,  through  promotion  and  facilitation  measures,  the  proportion  of  tourist travel  using  a  low-carbon  solution  to  reach  twice  as  many  tourists  who  come  to Brussels by train and twice as many cycle tourists.

-  Double the number of tourist accommodations with "Green Key" certification.
extraction_rationale: Quantified tourism transport and accommodation certification targets.
mechanism_description: The policy increases tourist arrivals by train and cycling through promotion and facilitation measures, shifting travel to lower-carbon modes and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: Towards an inclusive economic transition for the region
policy_statement: Towards an inclusive economic transition for the region (2030 strategic objectives) to develop shared services/economy of use, second-hand promotion, local currency uptake, certifications, and transition jobs support.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

1. Supporting economy of use, the circular economy and shared services

2. Promoting and supporting the local economy through local sourcing

## 3. Local businesses contribute to climate objectives

## 4. Preparing for the emergence of new jobs linked to the transition
extraction_rationale: Thematic chapter with multiple numbered objective lists; umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The policy supports shared-use and circular economy activities (including second-hand and local sourcing), which can reduce demand for new goods and lower lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, develop pooling of municipal public buildings for complementary functions (after feasibility study) and routinely consider pooling options in new construction/renovation projects.
parent_statement: Towards an inclusive economic transition for the region (2030 strategic objectives) to develop shared services/economy of use, second-hand promotion, local currency uptake, certifications, and transition jobs support.
verbatim_text:  Between now and 2030, the City Council is committed to developing the  pooling  of  municipal  public  buildings  for  other  complementary functions  during  the  periods  when  they  are  not  in  use,  following  a feasibility study on all of its assets. In addition, new (construction/renovation) projects will routinely take into account the options for pooling.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound commitment with prerequisite feasibility study and ongoing design requirement.
mechanism_description: The city council commits to pool municipal public buildings for complementary uses when they are otherwise unused, which can reduce the need for additional buildings and associated construction and operating emissions.

policy_statement: Promote second-hand initiatives and certify 5 second-hand stores each year.
parent_statement: Towards an inclusive economic transition for the region (2030 strategic objectives) to develop shared services/economy of use, second-hand promotion, local currency uptake, certifications, and transition jobs support.
verbatim_text:
 Promoting and supporting access to second-hand products:
- o The City Council is committed to promoting second-hand initiatives (certification, sales route).
- o Certify 5 second-hand stores each year.
extraction_rationale: Specific recurring quantified certification target.
mechanism_description: The city council commits to promoting and certifying second-hand initiatives to increase product reuse, lowering demand for new goods and the emissions from producing and distributing them.

policy_statement: Propose tripling the number of environmentally certified businesses in the region compared with 2019 and promote these certifications and awarded companies.
parent_statement: Towards an inclusive economic transition for the region (2030 strategic objectives) to develop shared services/economy of use, second-hand promotion, local currency uptake, certifications, and transition jobs support.
verbatim_text:
-  Increase in the number of companies with environmental certification in the region...
- o The City Council is proposing that the region should achieve a target of three times as many businesses with environmental certification in the region by 2019.
- o The City Council undertakes to promote these certifications and the companies awarded them.
extraction_rationale: Quantified regional target proposal plus City commitment to promote certifications.
mechanism_description: The City Council proposes a target to increase environmentally certified businesses and undertakes to promote the certifications and awarded companies, supporting wider adoption of improved environmental practices that reduce emissions.

policy_statement: Organise job placement into new professions required for the climate transition in collaboration with regional bodies (Regional Council, COCOF, VGC).
parent_statement: Towards an inclusive economic transition for the region (2030 strategic objectives) to develop shared services/economy of use, second-hand promotion, local currency uptake, certifications, and transition jobs support.
verbatim_text: -  In  collaboration  with  the  Regional  Council,  the  COCOF  and  the  VGC,  organise  job placement in the new professions required for the climate transition.
extraction_rationale: Specific programmatic action (job placement) with named partners.
mechanism_description: The city plans to organize job placement into new climate-transition professions so more skilled workers are available to deliver emissions-reducing projects.

section_header: Children and young people are key parties in the climate change transition
policy_statement: Children and young people transition strategy: extend and strengthen the City of Brussels eco-crèches model and implement exemplary school environment and youth education measures (2030 strategic objectives).
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Extending and strengthening the City of Brussels eco-crèches model
...
2. An exemplary school environment
...
## Raising awareness and equipping young people to tackle climate change issues
extraction_rationale: Thematic chapter with structured objective lists (infrastructure/mobility/waste/awareness; school environment); umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The city plans to expand eco-crèches and improve school environments while raising awareness and equipping young people on climate issues, supporting pro-climate behavior and long-term institutional capacity for climate action.

section_header: 2030 Strategic objectives - Extending and strengthening the City of Brussels eco-crèches model
policy_statement: Eco-crèches: generalise washable nappies in 100% of eco-crèches; continue replacing plastic crockery with durable materials in 100% of eco-crèches; and use eco-responsible care/cleaning approaches across 100% of eco-crèches.
parent_statement: Children and young people transition strategy: extend and strengthen the City of Brussels eco-crèches model and implement exemplary school environment and youth education measures (2030 strategic objectives).
verbatim_text:
-  ... generalising the use of washable nappies in 100% of eco-crèches.
-  Extend the use of washable nappies to 100% of eco-crèches.
-  Continuing the gradual replacement of plastic crockery ... in 100% of eco-crèches...
-  Care  and  cleaning  products: ... use eco-responsible products/diaper-changing techniques in 100% of the city's eco-crèches.
extraction_rationale: Multiple explicit 100% implementation commitments for eco-crèche practices.
mechanism_description: The city expands reusable nappies, replaces plastic crockery with durable materials, and uses eco-responsible care/cleaning practices in eco-crèches to reduce single-use waste and the emissions associated with producing and disposing of those materials.

section_header: 2030 Strategic objectives - Extending and strengthening the City of Brussels eco-crèches model
policy_statement: Eco-crèches: strengthen food sustainability with 100% organic, local and seasonal food; increase non-meat meals; reduce waste; and extend Good Food certification to all host environments.
parent_statement: Children and young people transition strategy: extend and strengthen the City of Brussels eco-crèches model and implement exemplary school environment and youth education measures (2030 strategic objectives).
verbatim_text: -  Strengthen food sustainability by offering 100% organic, local (including from Brussels agriculture)  and  seasonal  food,  increasing  the  frequency  of  non-meat  meals  and drastically  reducing  waste.  On  this  basis,  extend  Good  Food  certification  to  all  host environments.
extraction_rationale: Specific operational standards (100% organic/local/seasonal) and certification extension.
mechanism_description: It strengthens food sustainability by shifting meals toward organic/local/seasonal and more non-meat options while reducing food waste, which lowers food-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: 2030 Strategic objectives - Extending and strengthening the City of Brussels eco-crèches model
policy_statement: Eco-crèches: provide at least one eco-training per year for all service staff and at least one per year for all childcare staff; and organise at least one eco-crèche-related activity per year with parents in 100% of eco-crèches.
parent_statement: Children and young people transition strategy: extend and strengthen the City of Brussels eco-crèches model and implement exemplary school environment and youth education measures (2030 strategic objectives).
verbatim_text: -  Organisation of at least one eco-training session per year for all service staff, organisation of at least one eco-training session per year for all childcare staff... and organisation of at least one activity linked to the  eco-crèche  project per  year  with parents  in  100%  of  eco-crèches...
extraction_rationale: Quantified training/activity frequency requirements with full coverage.
mechanism_description: The policy organizes annual eco-training for staff and eco-crèche activities with parents to build knowledge and encourage lower-impact daily practices that reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: 2030 Strategic objectives - An exemplary school environment
policy_statement: School infrastructure: install water fountains in all school buildings and install rainwater recovery systems in schools with vegetable gardens; develop sustainable water management initiatives in schools.
parent_statement: Children and young people transition strategy: extend and strengthen the City of Brussels eco-crèches model and implement exemplary school environment and youth education measures (2030 strategic objectives).
verbatim_text:
-  Develop sustainable water management initiatives in schools (drinking fountains, water bottles, rainwater tanks, infiltration and flooding zones in courtyards, etc.).
- o The  City  Council  is  committed  to  installing  rainwater  recovery  systems  in schools with vegetable gardens.
- o The City Council is committed to equipping all its school buildings with water fountains.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure commitments with clear scope.
mechanism_description: The City Council is committed to equipping school buildings with drinking fountains and adding rainwater recovery and infiltration measures so schools can rely less on potable water and better manage stormwater and flooding.

section_header: 2030 Strategic objectives - An exemplary school environment
policy_statement: School mobility: ensure 100% of schools have a School Travel Plan by 2030; support primary schools with cycle passes; and provide cycle parking in all renovation/construction projects.
parent_statement: Children and young people transition strategy: extend and strengthen the City of Brussels eco-crèches model and implement exemplary school environment and youth education measures (2030 strategic objectives).
verbatim_text:
-  Continue  to  develop  School  Travel  Plans  within  our  schools...
- o 100% of schools will have a School Travel Plan by 2030, with the support of the regional council.
- o The city is committed to supporting all its primary schools by organising cycle passes for pupils.
... In all renovation and construction projects, the city undertakes to provide indoor and/or outdoor cycle parking.
extraction_rationale: Quantified school coverage target with supporting measures.
mechanism_description: The city will develop School Travel Plans and support cycling (cycle passes and cycle parking) to increase low-carbon school travel and reduce transport emissions.

section_header: Transforming the public authority to ensure a successful transition
policy_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Human resources management to meet future challenges

## 2. The City Council's finances and tax policy, tools for transition

## 3. Making public procurement more sustainable

## 4. Climate planning and the City Council's internal monitoring methodology

## 5. Technology at the service of climate planning
extraction_rationale: Thematic chapter with multiple numbered strategic objective lists; umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The city plans to improve internal climate planning, monitoring methodology, and supporting technology so it has better data and tools to guide climate action over time.

policy_statement: Train, empower and raise awareness of climate issues among City Council staff by function/department, and inform all new employees of relevant climate issues upon starting work.
parent_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text:
## 1. Human resources management to meet future challenges

-  The departments are developing skills to implement actions that meet the objectives of the climate plan:
- o Training,  empowering  and  raising  awareness  of  climate  issues  among  City Council staff...
- o All new employees are informed of the climate issues relevant to their role as soon as they start work.
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional capacity-building measures.
mechanism_description: The city provides training and awareness-raising on climate issues for staff and informs new employees at onboarding, strengthening internal capacity to implement climate actions.

policy_statement: Apply for maximum available European/regional/FWB subsidies for climate-related investments and strengthen/diversify ethical, socially responsible and sustainable investments.
parent_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text:
## 2. The City Council's finances and tax policy, tools for transition

-  For  its  investments  linked  to  climate  objectives,  the  City  Council  is  applying  for  a maximum of European, regional and FWB subsidies, etc.
-  Strengthen and diversify ethical, socially responsible and sustainable investments.
extraction_rationale: Explicit financial/resource mobilisation commitments.
mechanism_description: The City Council applies for available subsidies and strengthens sustainable investments to increase funding for climate-related investments, accelerating delivery of climate actions.

policy_statement: Use taxation to encourage virtuous behaviours contributing to the transition and discourage harmful behaviours.
parent_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text: -  The  City  Council  is  committed  to  using  taxation  as  a  tool  to  encourage  virtuous behaviour  that  contributes  to  the  transition  and  to  discourage  behaviour  that  is harmful to the climate.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to use fiscal policy as a lever.
mechanism_description: The City Council commits to using taxation to encourage behaviors that support the transition and discourage behaviors that are harmful to the climate, shifting activity in ways that reduce emissions.

policy_statement: Implement sustainable procurement by ensuring contracts include ecological, social and ethical clauses; develop a sustainable procurement strategy; monitor contract effectiveness; and train/raise awareness among purchasers/users.
parent_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text:
## 3. Making public procurement more sustainable

-  Public procurement contracts meet sustainable procurement criteria, incorporating ecological, social and ethical clauses.
- o The City Council, via the Procurement & Facilities department, will develop a sustainable  procurement  strategy...
- o The  city  is  committed  to  monitoring  its  public  procurement  contracts  to measure their effectiveness...
- o Public purchasers and/or users will be trained or made aware...
extraction_rationale: Concrete procurement policy actions and institutional commitments.
mechanism_description: The city will develop and apply sustainable procurement criteria in public contracts, monitor effectiveness, and train purchasers so purchasing shifts toward lower-impact goods and services, reducing lifecycle emissions.

policy_statement: Carry out an annual carbon assessment and develop a regional resilience diagnosis; continuously monitor Climate Plan objectives and report annually to the College and publicly every two years; and monitor 100% of climate objectives via a single platform linked to City plans.
parent_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text:
## 4. Climate planning and the City Council's internal monitoring methodology

-  The City Council has carried out an annual carbon assessment and has developed a regional  resilience  diagnosis...
-  ... presented  annually ... and every two years publicly...
-  100% of the  climate  objectives  are  monitored  via  a  single  platform...
extraction_rationale: Specific governance/monitoring system commitments with frequencies and 100% platform coverage.
mechanism_description: The City Council carries out annual carbon assessments, develops a regional resilience diagnosis, and monitors and reports climate objectives through a single platform to support climate planning and tracking progress.

policy_statement: Implement Green IT strategy to reduce the carbon footprint of digital activity by 20% compared to 2019.
parent_statement: Transforming the public authority (2030 strategic objectives) to strengthen HR capacity, sustainable finance/tax policy, sustainable procurement, climate monitoring, and Green IT.
verbatim_text:
## 5. Technology at the service of climate planning

-  Developing a Green IT strategy to enable the development of a digital business.
- o The City Council's Green IT strategy has reduced the carbon footprint of digital activity by 20% compared to 2019.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions/footprint reduction target tied to Green IT strategy.
mechanism_description: The city develops a Green IT strategy that reduces the carbon footprint of digital activity, lowering emissions associated with ICT services and operations.

section_header: Towards shared governance of the Climate Plan
policy_statement: Towards shared governance of the Climate Plan (2030 strategic objectives) including Local Climate Assembly, support for local initiatives, Future Generations Council, and strengthened external collaboration.
verbatim_text:
## 2030 Strategic objectives

## 1. Setting up the Local Climate Assembly

## 2. Support for local initiatives

## 3. Future Generations Council

## 4. Boosting external trade
extraction_rationale: Governance chapter contains multiple numbered strategic objective lists; umbrella for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: Sets up a Local Climate Assembly and a Future Generations Council, supports local initiatives, and boosts external collaboration to strengthen coordination and capacity to deliver the climate plan.

policy_statement: Set up a Local Climate Assembly to steer achievement of Climate Plan objectives by 2030; link it to the regional Citizens' Climate Assembly for transparent monitoring; and entrust it with roadmap completion, monitoring/evaluation, and uniting stakeholders.
parent_statement: Towards shared governance of the Climate Plan (2030 strategic objectives) including Local Climate Assembly, support for local initiatives, Future Generations Council, and strengthened external collaboration.
verbatim_text:
-  In 2030, the strategic objectives of the Climate Plan will be achieved thanks to the steering  of  a Local  Climate  Assembly...
... the Local Climate Assembly ensures transparent monitoring...
-  To meet these objectives, the Brussels City Council will entrust the Assembly with 3 main tasks:
- o ... complete the Climate Plan's roadmap...
- o Monitoring and evaluating the Climate Plan...
- o Unite  as  many  players  as  possible...
extraction_rationale: Institutional governance mechanism with defined tasks and timeline.
mechanism_description: Establishes a Local Climate Assembly to steer the climate plan, complete the roadmap, provide transparent monitoring and evaluation, and unite stakeholders to support delivery of the plan’s objectives.

policy_statement: Extend the Call for Climate Projects throughout the year and adapt green grants to accelerate climate projects; support projects aligned with the Climate Plan and raise the profile of local stakeholders’ actions.
parent_statement: Towards shared governance of the Climate Plan (2030 strategic objectives) including Local Climate Assembly, support for local initiatives, Future Generations Council, and strengthened external collaboration.
verbatim_text:
-  The City Council is extending the Call for Climate Projects throughout the year, and adapting the types of green grants to meet the climate challenges and speed up the implementation of climate projects in the region.
-  The City Council supports the development of projects in line with the Climate Plan.
-  ... the City Council is raising the profile of the actions carried out by local interested parties.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program funding/support and visibility measures.
mechanism_description: The City Council extends the call for projects year-round and adapts green grants to speed up implementation of climate-plan-aligned projects and raise the profile of local actors’ actions.

policy_statement: Establish a Future Generations Council of local youth to anchor the Climate Plan in future generations’ vision and produce concrete proposals/actions in dialogue with the Brussels Climate Assembly.
parent_statement: Towards shared governance of the Climate Plan (2030 strategic objectives) including Local Climate Assembly, support for local initiatives, Future Generations Council, and strengthened external collaboration.
verbatim_text: -  The city is home to a "Future Generations Council" made up of young people from the area, whose role is to ensure that the city's Climate Plan is permanently anchored in  the  vision  of  future  generations... this  Council  brings  out  concrete  proposals  and  actions...
extraction_rationale: Explicit institutional arrangement with defined role and outputs.
mechanism_description: Establishes a Future Generations Council of local youth to keep the climate plan anchored in future generations’ vision and to develop concrete proposals and actions that support implementation and long-term adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: Strengthen collaboration with federal/regional/municipal authorities and international networks, and work long-term with universities/higher education to bridge research and innovation for the transition.
parent_statement: Towards shared governance of the Climate Plan (2030 strategic objectives) including Local Climate Assembly, support for local initiatives, Future Generations Council, and strengthened external collaboration.
verbatim_text:
-  The City Council has strengthened its collaboration with the Federal Government, the Brussels-Capital Regional Council and the 18 Brussels municipalities...
-  The City Council has strengthened its international links to share best practice...
-  The City Council is working with universities and higher education establishments over the  long  term  to  bridge  the  gap  between  research  and  innovation...
extraction_rationale: Concrete governance/collaboration commitments with named counterparts.
mechanism_description: The City Council strengthens collaboration with other authorities, international networks, and universities to share best practices and connect research with innovation, improving institutional capacity to support the climate transition.

policy_statement: Adopt and implement the City of Brussels Climate Plan targeting at least a 55% reduction in GHG emissions by 2030 (vs. 2008), carbon neutrality by 2050, and energy carbon neutrality for public buildings by 2040.
verbatim_text: Today, inview of the climate change challenges that lie ahead, the City of Brussels is taking action by adopting a new Climate Plan with an even more ambitious long-term objective: a 55%reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2030, and carbon neutrality for the region by 2050. This objective of energy carbon neutrality has even been brought forward to 2040 for public buildings.
extraction_rationale: Plan-level quantified targets with timelines (2030/2040/2050) constitute an explicit policy commitment.
mechanism_description: The city is taking action by adopting a Climate Plan that sets greenhouse gas reduction and carbon neutrality objectives, guiding an overall pathway to reduce emissions.

section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
policy_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text: Objective Má -1         Improving the energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and facilities of the tertiary sector and increasing the ratio of renewables
extraction_rationale: Named umbrella objective that is followed by multiple numbered measures (M1–M7) in the Emission Reduction Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Improve energy efficiency in buildings and industrial/tertiary facilities and increase the share of renewable energy to reduce energy use and displace fossil energy.

policy_statement: M1: Refurbish and operate energy-consciously municipal buildings and facilities and those of public service providers, including planned refurbishments of multiple institutions by 2023 and continued fundraising for further modernization.
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Refurbishment and energy conscious operation of the buildings and facilities of the Municipality M1

The Municipality of Budapest maintains numerous institutions and companies... and may directly influence their energy consumption and energy efficient renovation and operation.

Provisions have been made for the refurbishment of several, mainly cultural and social institutions maintained by the Municipality by 2023...
extraction_rationale: Concrete action measure (M1) specifying refurbishment/energy-conscious operation with timeframe and implementation scope.
mechanism_description: The municipality plans refurbishment and energy-conscious operation of its buildings and facilities to reduce energy consumption.

policy_statement: M2: Upgrade Budapest’s public lighting by replacing worn-out/outdated luminaires with LED technology, aiming for electricity savings of 18,242 MWh/year and CO2 mitigation of 4,200 tCO2/year by 2027 (implementation timeframe noted as 2026/2027).
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Reconstruction and energy upgrading of the public lighting system

M2

...about 110 thousand are worn-out... and will need to be replaced in the next 5 years... replaced by LED luminaries... the target value of the savings on electricity can be 18,242 MWh/annum.

Target year: 2027 ... Target year value: 18,242 MWh/annum 4,200 tCO2/annum
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention with quantified energy/CO2 savings and target year.
mechanism_description: The city will replace worn-out public lighting luminaires with LED technology to reduce electricity use and related CO2 emissions.

policy_statement: M3: Implement a residential building refurbishment program (insulation, heating modernization, renewables) targeting renovation of one third of Budapest’s residential buildings by 2030, with estimated savings potential of 4,809,105 MWh/year and 981,977 tCO2/year.
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Refurbishment of residential buildings

M3

...The measure comprises the following elements: thermal insulation of external walls; modernisation of the heating system; installation of renewable energy technologies...

The renovation of one third of the residential buildings in Budapest has a saving potential of 4,809,105 MWh energy and 981,977 t CO2 per annum.

Timeframe: 2030
extraction_rationale: Defined program measure with concrete intervention types, quantified savings, and timeframe.
mechanism_description: The program refurbishes residential buildings through insulation, heating system upgrades, and renewable energy installations to reduce building energy use and associated CO2 emissions.

policy_statement: M4: Promote photovoltaic deployment in Budapest to reach a potential total installed solar capacity of about 1,500 MW by 2030, with an estimated CO2 savings potential of about 467,224 tCO2/year (as per the measure’s indicator).
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Facilitation of solar panel development projects

M4

...the portion of the buildings and roofs in Budapest... are sufficient for the installation of solar panels of a total installed capacity of 1500 MW (1750 MWp)...

Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 2,031,409 MWh/annum 467,224 t CO2 /annum
extraction_rationale: Specific renewable energy deployment measure with quantified capacity and target-year indicators.
mechanism_description: The city facilitates solar panel development on buildings and roofs to expand photovoltaic generation, which displaces fossil electricity generation and reduces CO2 emissions.

policy_statement: M5: Implement a district heating development program to refurbish and expand district heating assets and increase renewable/waste heat/cogeneration shares to qualify as 'efficient district heating and cooling', with multiple quantified component projects through 2030.
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Development of the district heating system (reconstruction, increasing efficiency and the ratio of renewables, etc) M5

The purpose of the district heating development program is to refurbish and develop the district heating system... and to increase the ratio of renewable energy resources... and to realize 'efficient district heating and cooling'... using at least 50% renewable energy, 50% waste heat, 75% cogenerated heat or 50% of a combination of such energy and heat.

Timeframe: 2030
extraction_rationale: Concrete program with defined standard/threshold for efficient district heating and explicit implementation timeframe.
mechanism_description: Implementing a district heating development program refurbishes and expands the district heating system while increasing renewable, waste-heat, and cogenerated heat shares, which lowers fossil heat use and reduces heating-related emissions.

policy_statement: M6: Conduct surveys and research (including detailed building modelling and databases, renewable potential assessment, and life-cycle analyses) to support planning for emissions reduction and renewable deployment, by 2022.
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Conducting basic surveys and researches...

M6

...it is important to survey and model the existing buildings in detail, to create a database... as well as to explore administrative requirements and restrictions.

Life-cycle analyses... are also missing...

Timeframe: 2022
extraction_rationale: Time-bound, specified intervention (research/survey work program) with defined outputs (models, database, LCA).
mechanism_description: Conduct surveys, detailed building models and databases, renewable potential assessments, and life-cycle analyses to provide data that supports planning for emissions reduction and renewable deployment.

policy_statement: M7: Support industrial and tertiary-sector decarbonisation by providing information on best available technologies and assistance in finding financing, through 2030.
parent_statement: Má-1 objective: Improve energy efficiency of buildings, industrial production and tertiary-sector facilities and increase the share of renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Enhancing mitigation and decarbonisation activities of industrial production and facilities of the tertiary sector

M7

The municipality plays a role... primarily in providing information to businesses, making them acquainted with state-of-the-art technologies and providing assistance in finding the necessary financing...

Timeframe: 2030
extraction_rationale: Defined municipal support action for decarbonisation with timeframe (though outputs are partly qualitative).
mechanism_description: The municipality provides businesses with information on state-of-the-art decarbonisation technologies and assistance in finding financing, helping enable adoption of lower-carbon industrial and tertiary-sector technologies and reducing emissions.

section_header: 4.1.   REDUCTION OF THE EMISSION OF GREENHOUSE GASES (MITIGATION)
policy_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text: Objective Má -2         Improving the energy efficiency of transport infrastructures, supporting and developing environmentally friendly forms of transport
extraction_rationale: Named umbrella objective followed by multiple numbered transport measures (M8–M12).
mechanism_description: It supports and develops environmentally friendly transport modes and improves the energy efficiency of transport infrastructure, which reduces energy use and transport-related emissions.

policy_statement: M8: Develop and modernize public transport and related systems to increase public transport modal share to 50% by 2030 (distance-based), including continued fleet modernization and service/infrastructure improvements.
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Development of public transport with more attractive vehicles and services and better infrastructure

M8

According to the Budapest Mobility Plan (BMP), the share of public transport shall be increased to 50% by 2030 with consideration to the distances covered...

The modernisation of the vehicle fleet needs to be continued.
extraction_rationale: Specific measure with quantified modal split target and explicit action scope (fleet, services, infrastructure).
mechanism_description: It plans to modernize public transport vehicles and improve services and infrastructure to raise public transport use, which reduces transport emissions by shifting trips away from higher-emitting modes.

policy_statement: M9: Expand and improve cycling and pedestrian infrastructure to increase cycling share to 5% and walking share to 15% by 2030 (distance-based), including core network development and supporting facilities (bike stands, B+R, MOL Bubi expansion).
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Development of the cycling and pedestrian infrastructure

M9

By means of the preferred development of cycling traffic, the share of cycling should be increased to 5% by 2030 based on the distances covered.

...giving priority to pedestrian traffic... Target year value: walking 15%, cycling 5%
extraction_rationale: Concrete transport infrastructure measure with quantified modal split targets and timeframe.
mechanism_description: Expanding and prioritizing cycling and pedestrian infrastructure increases walking and cycling mode share, shifting trips away from motorized travel and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: M10: Facilitate uptake of electric/low-emission vehicles by supporting incentives (e.g., free parking initially) and establishing/supporting public charging infrastructure, targeting 30% (225,000) vehicles of environmental class 5 by 2030.
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Facilitation of the use of electric driven or low emission motor vehicles

M10

...The Municipality of Budapest can provide support... by offering free parking... and by establishing charging stations...

Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 30% (225,000 pcs)
extraction_rationale: Specific e-mobility support measure with quantified target share and actions (parking incentives, charging stations).
mechanism_description: The municipality supports incentives such as free parking and establishes charging stations to make electric or low-emission vehicles easier to use, enabling greater adoption and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: M11: Promote car-sharing and car-pooling, including establishing rules for an integrated community car rental/sharing system and encouraging providers to replace conventional vehicles with electric vehicles; also create car-pooling meeting points.
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of the use of car-sharing and car-pooling systems

M11

...establish the rules of a uniform... integrated community system of car rental or car sharing...

Service providers need to be encouraged to gradually replace the vehicles using traditional fuels by electric vehicles.

...promote the use of car-pooling systems... by... establishing car-pooling meeting points...
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention package describing concrete institutional/regulatory and infrastructure actions.
mechanism_description: It promotes car-sharing and car-pooling through an integrated community system and designated meeting points, and encourages providers to replace conventional vehicles with electric vehicles, which reduces transport emissions.

policy_statement: M12: Implement traffic control measures including low-emission/climate protection zones and introduce a congestion charge for passenger traffic in 2021–2025 per the Budapest Mobility Plan, alongside P+R expansion (target 22,000 P+R spaces by 2030) and related parking reforms.
parent_statement: Má-2 objective: Improve energy efficiency of transport infrastructure and support and develop environmentally friendly transport modes.
verbatim_text:
## Traffic control to reduce emission, designation of low-emission zones and construction of the related infrastructure

M12

...The designation of climate protection zones...

As prognosticated in the BMP, the congestion charge would be introduced for passenger traffic in the period of 2021-2025.

...Target year: 2030 ... Target year value: 22,000 pcs
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/economic instruments (zones, congestion charging) with stated introduction period and quantified P+R target.
mechanism_description: It designates climate protection (low-emission) zones and introduces a congestion charge for passenger traffic to discourage higher-emitting driving and reduce transport emissions.

policy_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text: Objective Aá -1         Development of the green - blue infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Named umbrella objective followed by multiple letter/numbered measures (A1–A7) in the Adaptation Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Developing green-blue infrastructure supports adaptation by improving urban cooling and stormwater management, with potential mitigation co-benefits.

policy_statement: A1: Increase and improve green areas and water surfaces, including creating/expanding support instruments (e.g., Environmental Protection Fund) for green community areas, green roofs, roof gardens, and green walls, with a target green area intensity value increase (52.5% to 57%) by 2030.
parent_statement: Aá-1 objective: Develop Budapest’s green-blue infrastructure to support adaptation (and co-benefits for mitigation).
verbatim_text:
## Increasing the ratio of green areas and water surfaces

A1

...ratio of green areas needs to be increased... alternative green area elements, e.g. green roofs and green façades can be established...

...recommend to widen the scope and enhance the volume of tender funds which support the establishing of green community areas, green roofs, roof gardens, green walls (e.g. Environmental Protection Fund).

Base year value: 52.5% ... Target year value: 57%
extraction_rationale: Specific green-blue infrastructure measure with concrete instruments and quantified indicator/target.
mechanism_description: The city recommends expanding funding support to establish more green areas, water surfaces, and green roofs/walls, which increases urban green/blue cover and helps reduce heat while providing some carbon sequestration.
