- -Publicity on heat disorder, information provision of heat disorder prevention
extraction_rationale: Concrete public-health adaptation actions.
mechanism_description: The city will publicize heat disorder risks and provide prevention information to help residents reduce heat exposure and related health impacts during high temperatures.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 2) Building resilience against climate change
policy_statement: As heat/heat-island adaptation measures, Hiroshima City will create a water and green network through conservation/creation of public open spaces and promote roof and wall greening of buildings (including via the green promotion system).
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.

【 Countermeasures against heat (heat island phenomenon, etc.) 】

extraction_rationale: Specific urban cooling/heat-island interventions with clear actions.
mechanism_description: The city will create a connected water-and-green network and promote roof and wall greening to reduce urban heat and heat-island impacts.

section_header: Chapter 6: Adapting to the effects of climate change (Adaptation) > 3. Concrete measures > 3) Building of a system to learn and evaluate the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will cooperate with the state and universities to consider a system to learn and evaluate climate change effects to time and advance adaptation measures appropriately.
parent_statement: Hiroshima City will implement an Adaptation Plan with two pillars: improving acknowledgement/understanding of climate change and improving regional comprehensive ability to deal with climate risks, and will comprehensively develop three measures under these pillars.
verbatim_text:
## 3) Building of a system to learn and evaluate the effects of climate change

In order to prepare for the estimated effects and advance 'adaptation measures' appropriately at a suitable time, the city will cooperate with the state and universities and consider a system to learn and evaluate.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to establish an evaluation/learning system with identified partners.
mechanism_description: The city will cooperate with the state and universities to consider a system that learns and evaluates climate change effects, helping it time and advance adaptation measures appropriately.

section_header: Chapter 5: Promotion of counteractions against global warming (Mitigation) > 2. Reduction target for greenhouse gas emissions
policy_statement: Hiroshima City will set greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets of 80% by FY2050, 30% by FY2030, and 5% by FY2020 compared to FY2013 (with FY2020 also equivalent to 7% compared to FY2005).
verbatim_text:
To establish the reduction target of greenhouse gas emissions, the city will set the long-term target based on the national government's target, keeping in mind the city Hiroshima aims to become.

| Timeframe            | Target Year   | Reduction Target (Total Target Amount)            |
| Long-term Target     | FY2050        | Compared to FY2013 ▲ 80%                          |
| Mid-term Target *1   | FY2030        | Compared to FY2013 ▲ 30%                          |
| Short-term Target *2 | FY2020        | Compared to FY2013 ▲ 5% (Compared to FY2005 ▲ 7%) |
extraction_rationale: Clear, quantified citywide mitigation targets with target years and base year.
mechanism_description: The city will set long-term and interim greenhouse gas emissions reduction targets to guide an overall pathway for cutting emissions over time.

policy_statement: For government operations, Hiroshima City sets targets to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by FY2020 by 5% in the city area and 5.1% for the city office (vs FY2013), and by FY2030 by 30% for both city area and city office (vs FY2013).

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text:
## Net-zero Electricity Generation

## Long-term target : Net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050

At  present,  fossil  fuels,  including  natural  gas and coal, account for over 70% of Hong Kong's fuel mix for electricity generation.  Under the overall strategy of achieving carbon neutrality, we  must  progressively  increase  the  use  of zero-carbon  energy  for  electricity  generation before 2050.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy section with long-term target and multiple specific sub-actions/targets presented underneath in subsequent paragraphs.
mechanism_description: Progressively increasing the use of zero-carbon energy for electricity generation reduces fossil-fuel generation and drives grid decarbonization.

section_header: Medium-term targets: Phasing out coal for electricity generation · Developing RE
policy_statement: By 2035, Hong Kong will cease using coal for daily electricity generation and keep coal only for backup; coal will be replaced by natural gas and zero-carbon energy (including renewables and nuclear).
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text: By  2035,  we  will cease using coal for daily electricity generation and  will  only  keep  it  for  providing  backup support. Coal will be replaced by natural gas with lower carbon emissions and zero-carbon energy (e.g. RE and nuclear energy) by then.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound fuel switching commitment under the net-zero electricity strategy; clearly a specific sub-measure.
mechanism_description: The plan is to stop using coal for daily electricity generation and replace it with natural gas and zero-carbon energy such as renewables and nuclear, lowering emissions from power generation.

policy_statement: Hong Kong will increase the share of renewable energy in the electricity generation fuel mix from less than 1% to 7.5–10% by 2035, and to 15% before 2050.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text:
we are committed to increasing the share  of  RE  in  the  fuel  mix  for  electricity generation from less than 1% at present to 7.5% to 10% by 2035.

Our goal is to increase the share of RE in the fuel mix for electricity  generation  from  the  existing  less than 1% to 15% before 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified RE share targets with clear deadlines; presented as part of the net-zero electricity pathway.
mechanism_description: The government is committed to increasing the share of renewable energy in the electricity generation fuel mix, which displaces fossil-based generation and lowers power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will incorporate renewable energy technologies in all capital works projects, update the Green Government Buildings circular, and increase required roof space for installing RE systems in new government buildings from 10% to 25%, while continuing RE installation in existing government buildings where possible.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text: The Government will continue to take the lead in developing RE and strive to incorporate RE technologies  in  all  capital  works  projects. We  will  update  the  Government  circular  on Green  Government  Buildings  and  raise  the requirements of applying RE technologies in new government buildings. For instance, the proportion of available roof space for installing RE systems will be increased from the current 10% to 25%. We will also continue to install RE systems in existing government buildings (including outdoor parking lots) as far as possible.
extraction_rationale: Specific government-led implementation measures with a quantified design requirement (10% to 25%).
mechanism_description: The government will incorporate and expand renewable energy installations on new and existing government buildings and capital works so that on-site clean generation displaces fossil electricity generation and reduces emissions.

policy_statement: WSD plans to install larger floating solar systems (5–10 MW each) at multiple reservoirs and is studying a >100 MW floating solar system at Plover Cove Reservoir, targeted to generate over 100 million kWh/year.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text:
the  Water  Supplies  Department  (WSD)  plans  to  install  larger  floating solar energy generation systems with a generating capacity of 5-10 megawatts (MW) each at  various  reservoirs,  including  Plover  Cove Reservoir,  Shek  Pik  Reservoir  and  Tai  Lam Chung Reservoir.

WSD  has embarked on a study on installing a floating solar energy generation system with a generating capacity of over 100 MW at Plover Cove Reservoir. The project is estimated to generate more than 100 million kWh of electricity a year
extraction_rationale: Concrete deployment plan with capacities and expected annual generation.
mechanism_description: The Water Supplies Department plans to install and study floating solar power systems on reservoirs to generate renewable electricity that displaces fossil-fuel generation.

policy_statement: The Government and power companies will enhance regional cooperation to identify and invest in zero-carbon energy sources/projects in neighbouring regions through joint investment and development.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Net-zero electricity generation (long-term target of net-zero carbon emissions in electricity generation before 2050) by increasing use of zero-carbon energy and pursuing suitable supply options.
verbatim_text: The  Government,  together  with  the  power companies, will also explore ways to enhance regional  cooperation  on  zero-carbon  energy and identify sources of zero-carbon energy in neighbouring regions, including seeking joint investment and development opportunities for participating in and operating zero-carbon energy projects near Hong Kong.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (regional cooperation and joint investment) to increase zero-carbon energy supply.
mechanism_description: The government and power companies will explore regional cooperation to identify and pursue joint investment and development of zero-carbon energy projects, increasing zero-carbon supply and displacing fossil electricity generation.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text:
## Energy Saving and Green Buildings

Through regulation as well as promotion and public education in energy saving, we can reduce the overall electricity consumption of  buildings,  and  hence  lower  the  cost  of adopting  new  zero-carbon  energy.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy section that introduces a package of measures and multiple explicit targets/actions in subsequent paragraphs.
mechanism_description: Through regulation, promotion, and public education on energy saving, the policy aims to reduce buildings’ electricity consumption, lowering energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: Medium-term targets: Reduce the electricity consumption of commercial buildings by 15% to 20%, and that of residential buildings by 10% to 15% by 2035
policy_statement: Hong Kong will reduce electricity consumption of new and existing commercial buildings by 15–20% and residential buildings by 10–15% by 2035 (baseline: 2015 operational conditions).
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text: we  have  also  set  a  medium-term target of reducing the electricity consumption of new and existing commercial buildings by 15% to 20%, and that of residential buildings by 10% to 15% by 2035, using the operational conditions  of  2015  as  the  comparison  basis.
extraction_rationale: Quantified medium-term building energy reduction targets with baseline year.
mechanism_description: The government has set a medium-term target to cut electricity consumption in commercial and residential buildings, reducing overall building energy use.

policy_statement: The Government plans to tighten statutory OTTV standards by 2025 and complete RTTV reviews by 2023 and 2030; new government buildings will have OTTV at least 10% above statutory standards.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text: The  Government  is  conducting  a review on the OTTV, and plans to complete the  review  for  tightening  up  the  statutory standards  by  2025. The  Government  also plans to complete two reviews on the Residential  Thermal  Transfer  Value  (RTTV) by 2023 and 2030. To lead by example, the OTTV of new government buildings will be at least 10% above the statutory standards.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory tightening with explicit deadlines and a quantified government-building requirement.
mechanism_description: The Government plans to tighten OTTV/RTTV thermal performance standards and have new government buildings exceed those standards, which reduces heat transfer through building envelopes and lowers building energy use.

policy_statement: The Government will study adding district cooling systems (DCS) in more new development areas (including Hung Shui Kiu/Ha Tsuen and artificial islands in Central Waters).
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text: We will work on the infrastructure by integrating energy efficient facilities in development projects.  We  will  study  the  feasibility  of incorporating DCS in more new development areas, such as Hung Shui Kiu/Ha Tsuen New Development Area and the artificial islands in the  Central  Waters
extraction_rationale: Concrete planned action (feasibility study) to expand a named energy-efficiency infrastructure solution.
mechanism_description: The government will study incorporating district cooling systems into new development projects to lower the electricity needed for building cooling.

policy_statement: The Government will consider more frequent energy audits and mandating implementation of identified energy management opportunities, strengthen promotion of retro-commissioning, and consider requiring large commercial buildings to regularly commission key systems.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text:
we will consider conducting energy audits more frequently and mandating the implementation of identified energy management opportunities. At the same time, we will strengthen the promotion of RCx

We will consider requiring large commercial buildings to carry out regular commissioning of air-conditioning systems and major building services installations
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/mandatory mechanisms under consideration are specific policy interventions (audits, mandated actions, commissioning requirements).
mechanism_description: The Government will consider more frequent energy audits and mandating implementation of identified energy management opportunities, strengthen promotion of retro-commissioning, and consider requiring large commercial buildings to regularly commission key systems to reduce building energy use.

policy_statement: The Government will keep the Mandatory Energy Efficiency Labelling Scheme (MEELS) under review, consider setting minimum energy efficiency requirements for specified appliances before supply in Hong Kong, and consider expanding MEELS to non-domestic/commercial appliances.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Energy saving and green buildings, with targets to cut building electricity consumption and strengthen regulations and tools to improve energy performance.
verbatim_text:
We will keep the scheme under review.  Apart from upgrading  the  grading  standards,  we  will  also consider  setting  a  minimum  energy  efficiency requirement  so  that  certain  appliances  must meet the minimum energy efficiency standards before they can be supplied in Hong Kong

we  will consider expanding the scope of the scheme to cover non-domestic or commercial appliances.
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy intent to introduce minimum standards and expand scope of an existing mandatory labeling scheme.
mechanism_description: The government will keep the appliance efficiency labelling scheme under review and consider expanding it and setting minimum efficiency standards so appliances use less electricity, reducing emissions from electricity generation.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
## Green Transport

## Long-term target: Zero carbon emissions from vehicles and transport sector before 2050

We expect to achieve the long-term target of zero carbon emissions from  vehicles and the transport  sector  before  2050  through  the electrification of vehicles and ferries, development of new energy transport and implementation of measures to improve traffic management.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with long-term sectoral target and multiple distinct measures described underneath.
mechanism_description: The strategy expects to reach zero-carbon transport by electrifying vehicles and ferries, developing new-energy transport, and implementing traffic management measures that reduce transport-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Hong Kong targets ceasing new registration of fuel-propelled and hybrid private cars in 2035 or earlier.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text: the  Hong  Kong Roadmap on Popularisation of Electric Vehicles announced by the Government in 2021 cover various  aspects,  including  ceasing  the  new registration of fuel-propelled and hybrid private cars in 2035 or earlier
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory/market access commitment with a specific year.
mechanism_description: It pursues ceasing new registration of fuel-propelled and hybrid private cars, accelerating a shift to electric cars and lowering transport emissions.

section_header: Medium-term target: Set a concrete timetable for adopting new energy public transport
policy_statement: The Government will partner with sectors to test electric and new energy public transport and commercial vehicles and aims to set a more concrete way forward and timetable around 2025; it will review EV charging installation targets around 2025.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
the Government will play an active role  in  developing  new  energy  transport,  and will partner with different sectors in the coming years  to  actively  test  the  technological  and commercial viability of various electric and new energy public transport and commercial vehicles, with a view to setting a more concrete way  forward  and  timetable  in  around  2025.

In  around  2025,  we  will  review  this year's target for the installation of charging facilities
extraction_rationale: Time-referenced implementation commitment (tests leading to timetable by ~2025; review of charging targets by ~2025).
mechanism_description: The Government will partner with different sectors to test electric and new-energy public transport and commercial vehicles and will review charging-facility installation targets, helping enable wider EV adoption and associated transport emissions reductions.

policy_statement: The Government plans to collaborate with franchised bus companies and stakeholders in the next three years to test hydrogen fuel cell electric buses and heavy vehicles, supported by an interdepartmental working group on hydrogen energy.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text: Following the release of the Plan, the Government has set up an Interdepartmental  Working  Group  on  the  application  of hydrogen energy in Hong Kong. We plan to collaborate with the franchised bus companies and other stakeholders in the next three years to test out hydrogen fuel cell electric buses and heavy vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned trials with a defined timeframe and enabling governance action (working group).
mechanism_description: The government plans to work with bus companies and stakeholders to test hydrogen fuel cell electric buses and heavy vehicles, supporting a shift away from diesel and lowering transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will subsidise ferry operators to trial electric and hybrid ferries and will explore progressively replacing traditional ferries with new energy ferries before 2035, subject to trial results and technology.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
As  regards  ferries,  the Government  will  subsidise  ferry  operators  to conduct  trials  for  electric  and  hybrid  ferries.

the  Government will  explore  with  the  ferry  operators  the possibility of progressively replacing traditional ferries with new energy ferries before 2035.
extraction_rationale: Concrete financial mechanism (subsidies) plus a time-bound transition intention (before 2035).
mechanism_description: The Government will subsidise trials of electric and hybrid ferries and explore progressively replacing traditional ferries with new-energy ferries, reducing emissions from ferry operations.

policy_statement: The Government will roll out a Free-flow Tolling System at government tolled tunnels and press ahead with congestion charging, with preliminary proposals to be drawn up in 2022.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Green transport, aiming for zero carbon emissions from vehicles and the transport sector before 2050 through electrification, new energy transport, and traffic management measures.
verbatim_text:
the Free-flow Tolling System will be rolled out at government tolled tunnels and the Tsing Sha Control Area, while 'congestion charging' will be  pressed  ahead  actively

We commenced  a  traffic  survey  in  September 2021  to  collect  the  latest  data  and  draw  up preliminary proposals for 'congestion charging' in 2022.
extraction_rationale: Specific transport demand management measures with an implementation/planning milestone (proposals in 2022).
mechanism_description: The government will roll out free-flow tolling and press ahead with congestion charging to discourage car trips and reduce traffic, lowering transport fuel use and emissions.

policy_statement: Parent strategy: Waste reduction, aiming to move away from reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal by 2035 and support carbon neutrality in waste management.
verbatim_text:
## Waste Reduction

The Government promulgated the Waste Blueprint for Hong Kong 2035 (the Waste Blueprint) early this year, advocating  the  vision  of  'Waste  Reduction · Resources Circulation · Zero Landfill' .

The  Waste  Blueprint sets out targets for per capita MSW disposal and  recovery  rate,  and  the  goal  of  developing adequate waste-to-energy facilities, with the aim to  move  away from the reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal  b y   2 0 3 5 .
extraction_rationale: Named strategy section linked to a specific plan (Waste Blueprint 2035) with clear goal and multiple actions/targets described thereafter.
mechanism_description: The Waste Blueprint sets targets to reduce municipal solid waste disposal and increase recovery so less waste goes to landfills, avoiding landfill methane emissions.

section_header: Medium-term target: Enhance waste reduction and recycling
policy_statement: Hong Kong aims to reduce per capita municipal solid waste disposal by 40–45% and raise the recovery rate to about 55% through MSW charging and other waste reduction and recycling initiatives.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Waste reduction, aiming to move away from reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal by 2035 and support carbon neutrality in waste management.
verbatim_text: Through  implementing  MSW  charging  and other waste reduction and recycling initiatives, and encouraging the whole community to work together,  we  aim  to  progressively  reduce  the per capita MSW disposal by 40% to 45% and raise the recovery rate to about 55%.
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste reduction and recycling targets tied to specific instruments (MSW charging and initiatives).
mechanism_description: By implementing MSW charging and other waste reduction and recycling initiatives, the policy aims to reduce per-capita waste disposal and increase recovery, diverting more waste from landfill and avoiding methane emissions.

policy_statement: The Government will enhance recovery of landfill gases from the three operating landfills to provide thermal energy and electricity for landfill operations and export to gas company/power grids.
parent_statement: Parent strategy: Waste reduction, aiming to move away from reliance on landfills for municipal waste disposal by 2035 and support carbon neutrality in waste management.
verbatim_text: we will enhance the recovery of landfill gases from the three landfills currently in operation for providing thermal energy and electricity  to  support  landfill  operation  and export to the gas company and power grids to provide energy for the public.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure/energy recovery action stated as a future measure.
mechanism_description: The government will enhance recovery of landfill gas and use it to generate thermal energy and electricity for landfill operations and export, capturing methane and displacing fossil energy.

policy_statement: The Government has set a Green Energy Target to improve the energy performance of the Government by 6% by 2024-25.
verbatim_text: We have also set  a  new  Green  Energy  Target  to  further improve the energy performance of the whole Government by 6% by 2024-25.
extraction_rationale: Quantified government operational energy target with a deadline.
mechanism_description: The government sets a Green Energy Target to improve energy performance across government operations, reducing overall energy use.

section_header: Combating Extreme Droughts and Safeguarding Water Supply
policy_statement: WSD targets reducing average fresh water per capita consumption by 10% in 2030 (base year 2016) and containing fresh water demand at around 1 billion m³ per annum before 2040, via water conservation, water loss management, and expanded use of seawater/recycled water for non-potable purposes.
verbatim_text: To  achieve  the  goal  of  reducing  the  average fresh water per capita consumption by 10% in 2030,  using  2016  as  the  base  year,  and containing the fresh water demand at the level of around 1 billion m³   per annum before 2040, WSD  is  taking  forward  three  major  water demand management initiatives, namely water  conservation,  water  loss  management and expansion of the use of lower grade water (viz. seawater and recycled water) for non-potable purposes.
extraction_rationale: Quantified adaptation/water management targets with timeframes and named initiative pillars.
mechanism_description: WSD is taking forward water conservation, water loss management, and expanded use of seawater/recycled water for non-potable purposes to reduce per-capita freshwater consumption and contain overall freshwater demand.

policy_statement: London is committed to being a zero carbon city by 2050, with the Mayor setting out actions within his powers to achieve this as soon as possible.
verbatim_text: The Mayor's London Environment Strategy (May 2018) was one of the first plans of any world city to be compatible with the highest ambition of the Paris Agreement. It commits London to being a zero carbon city by 2050. It sets out the actions the Mayor will take, within his powers, to get there as soon as possible.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to an economy-wide outcome (zero carbon) with a deadline (2050) and reference to actions the Mayor will take.
mechanism_description: The strategy commits the city to reaching zero carbon and sets out actions the Mayor will take to drive an economy-wide emissions reduction pathway.

policy_statement: London will follow an emissions pathway aligned with IPCC recommendations to reduce emissions by 60% on 1990 levels by 2030 and by nearly 80% by 2040.
verbatim_text: Understanding these different pathways has enabled us to set an ambitious emissions pathway in line with the IPCC recommendations. It will see London reduce its emissions by 60 per cent on 1990 levels by 2030 and by nearly 80 per cent by 2040.
extraction_rationale: Quantified targets with clear baselines and deadlines constitute an extractable mitigation commitment.
mechanism_description: London sets an emissions pathway aligned with IPCC recommendations to guide reductions in overall citywide emissions over time.

policy_statement: At least 70% of London's buildings need to reach energy performance level EPC C by 2030.
verbatim_text: Only 35 per cent of homes currently achieve adequate energy efficiency performance (EPC C or above) and many will still be in use by 2050. At least 70 per cent of London's buildings need to reach EPC C by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified building-efficiency target (share of buildings to EPC C) with deadline (2030).
mechanism_description: Setting a target for buildings to reach EPC C or above drives energy-efficiency improvements that reduce building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: 8. Decarbonising transport and increasing use of active and public transport remains a key objective for London.
policy_statement: By 2050, all of London's motorised road transport will need to be battery electric or fuel cell electric vehicles.
verbatim_text: By 2050, all of London's motorised road transport will need to be battery electric or fuel cell electric vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Clear technology/transition target for the road transport fleet with a deadline (2050).
mechanism_description: Transitioning motorised road transport to battery electric and fuel cell electric vehicles reduces transport greenhouse gas emissions by displacing fossil fuel combustion in vehicles.

verbatim_text: Action ES5.2 - Implement the 2045 CAP consistency review checklist for new development to demonstrate consistency with the 2045 CAP's strategies, measures, and actions.
extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation tool for development review and CEQA streamlining.
mechanism_description: Implementing a CAP consistency review checklist for new development helps align project decisions with climate plan strategies, supporting progress along an emissions-reduction pathway.

policy_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
verbatim_text:
Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings
Measure E1: Transition Existing Buildings to All- Electric
Measure E2: Standardize All-Electric New Development
Measure E3: Other Decarbonization Actions
extraction_rationale: Named Strategy with multiple measures and actions underneath, forming a clear umbrella policy.
mechanism_description: The strategy transitions existing buildings to all-electric and standardizes all-electric new development to reduce building-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 (CONTINUED) SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS
policy_statement: Measure E2: Standardize all-electric new development by adopting an ordinance requiring applicable new buildings be fully electric (no natural gas hookups), adopting ZNE ordinances by specified years, and adopting CALGreen Tier 1 standards and potential Tier 2 amendments.
parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
verbatim_text:
Measure E2: Standardize All-Electric New Development
Energy: • Action E2.1 - Adopt an ordinance requiring all applicable new buildings to be fully electric with no natural gas hookups... • Action E2.2 - Adopt a ZNE ordinance for all new residential buildings built after 2025 and all new nonresidential buildings built after 2030... • Action E2.3 - Adopt CALGreen Code Tier 1 green building standards and identify which Tier 2 standards could be adopted as code amendments.
extraction_rationale: Measure contains clear regulatory commitments (ordinances, code adoption) and timelines.
mechanism_description: The city adopts ordinances and code standards requiring new buildings to be all-electric and meet zero net energy/CALGreen tiers, which reduces building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 (CONTINUED) SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS
policy_statement: Adopt an ordinance requiring all applicable new buildings to be fully electric with no natural gas hookups, with affordability considerations and supporting measures to defray first costs.
parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
verbatim_text: Action E2.1 - Adopt an ordinance requiring all applicable new buildings to be fully electric with no natural gas hookups. Include affordable housing considerations in these requirements, and develop supporting measures (financial support, technical assistance, or other incentives) to defray potential additional first costs in order to maintain housing affordability.
extraction_rationale: Explicit ordinance adoption with specified building electrification requirement.
mechanism_description: Adopt an ordinance requiring applicable new buildings to be fully electric with no natural gas hookups, avoiding on-site fossil fuel combustion and associated emissions while supporting affordability through measures that defray first costs.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 (CONTINUED) SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS
policy_statement: Adopt a ZNE ordinance for all new residential buildings built after 2025 and all new nonresidential buildings built after 2030, including renter protections and affordable housing set-aside to offset first cost.
parent_statement: Strategy 5: Decarbonize Buildings through measures to electrify existing buildings, standardize all-electric new development, and other decarbonization actions (including embodied carbon and refrigerants).
verbatim_text: Action E2.2 - Adopt a ZNE ordinance for all new residential buildings built after 2025 and all new nonresidential buildings built after 2030. Include renter protections for affordable housing. Provide affordable housing set-aside to offset first cost.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound ordinance commitment with specified scope and protections.
mechanism_description: Adopt a zero-net-energy ordinance for new residential and nonresidential buildings to improve new-building energy performance and reduce operational energy use and related emissions.

section_header: TABLE ES-1 (CONTINUED) SUMMARY OF DRAFT 2045 CAP MEASURES AND AFFECTED RESOURCE AREAS

verbatim_text: Action E2.3 - Adopt CALGreen Code Tier 1 green building standards and identify which Tier 2 standards could be adopted as code amendments.
extraction_rationale: Concrete code adoption/identification action to raise building standards.
mechanism_description: Adopting CALGreen Tier 1 green building standards (and identifying Tier 2 standards for possible code amendments) improves building performance, reducing energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: 2.6.2.2 Strategy 2: Increase Densities and Diversity of Land Uses near Transit
policy_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
verbatim_text:
## 2.6.2.2 Strategy 2: Increase Densities and Diversity of Land Uses near Transit

Strategy 2 focuses on coordinating land use development that leads to outcomes associated with reduced vehicle miles traveled, such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses that can reduce travel distances for many trip purposes.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with defined intervention focus (land use coordination to reduce VMT); serves as umbrella for listed measures T1 and T2.
mechanism_description: Coordinate higher-density, mixed-use development near transit to shorten travel distances and reduce vehicle miles traveled, lowering transportation emissions.

section_header: Measure T1: Increase Density near High-Quality Transit Areas.
policy_statement: Measure T1 will increase density near High-Quality Transit Areas (HQTAs) by setting minimum and maximum dwelling unit density ranges, locating most residential and employment centers within 1 mile of an HQTA, and increasing dwelling units within HQTAs.
parent_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T1: Increase Density near High-Quality Transit Areas.

The performance objectives for Measure T1 are to: (1) achieve a minimum of 20 dwelling units (DUs) per acre (maximum of 30-150 DUs per acre) for HQTAs; (2) locate a majority of residential and employment centers in the unincorporated Los Angeles County within 1 mile of an HQTA; and (3) achieve a 27 percent increase in dwelling units within HQTAs.
extraction_rationale: Specific measure with quantified performance objectives for density and location, linked under Strategy 2.
mechanism_description: The measure sets density objectives and concentrates housing and jobs near high-quality transit areas to support more transit use and less driving, reducing transportation emissions and associated land-use impacts.

section_header: Measure T2: Develop Land Use Plans Addressing Jobs-Housing Balance and Increase Mixed Use.
policy_statement: Measure T2 will develop land use plans to address jobs-housing balance and increase mixed use, including achieving job density targets for new nonresidential projects and requiring community plans to identify strategies to reduce jobs/housing imbalance below 20%.
parent_statement: Strategy 2 will increase densities and diversity of land uses near transit to reduce vehicle miles traveled by coordinating land use development outcomes such as increased densities near transit, jobs-housing balance, and strategically located land uses.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T2: Develop Land Use Plans Addressing Jobs-Housing Balance and Increase Mixed Use.

The performance objective for Measure T2 is to achieve a job density of 300 jobs per acre for all new projects with nonresidential development by 2030. For communities with an imbalance of jobs/housing (±20 percent), community plans will identify and quantify strategies for bringing that imbalance below 20 percent.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target and planning requirement stated as a measure under Strategy 2.
mechanism_description: Developing land use and community plans to increase mixed use and improve jobs–housing balance reduces commute distances and vehicle miles traveled, which lowers transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## 2.6.2.3 Strategy 3: Reduce Single-Occupancy Vehicle Trips

Strategy 3 focuses on development of transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes to help reduce trips made in single-occupancy vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy functioning as umbrella for measures T3–T5.
mechanism_description: Develop transportation networks that make active travel more accessible and convenient to reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips and cut transport emissions.

section_header: Measure T3: Expand Bicycle and Pedestrian Network to Serve Residential, Employment, and Recreational Trips.
policy_statement: Measure T3 will expand the bicycle and pedestrian network, including increasing bikeway miles by 300% by 2035 and implementing the County Bicycle Master Plan and updating pedestrian/bicycle/active transportation plans every five years.
parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T3: Expand Bicycle and Pedestrian Network to Serve Residential, Employment, and Recreational Trips.

The performance objective for Measure T3 is to increase bikeway miles by 300 percent by 2035. This measure also requires implementing the County Bicycle Master Plan and completing updates to County's Pedestrian Action Plan, Bicycle Master Plan, and Active Transportation Plans every five years.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target (300% by 2035) and specific implementation requirements; listed under Strategy 3.
mechanism_description: The measure expands bicycle and pedestrian networks and implements/updates active transportation plans to support more trips by biking and walking, reducing vehicle travel and transportation emissions.

section_header: Measure T4: Broaden Options for Transit, Active Transportation, and Alternative Modes of Transportation.
policy_statement: Measure T4 will broaden options for transit and alternative modes by 2030 by doubling transit service hours, installing bus-only lanes and signal prioritization on major transit thoroughfares, and ensuring at least 75% of unincorporated County residents live within one-half mile of a bus or active transportation option.
parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T4: Broaden Options for Transit, Active Transportation, and Alternative Modes of Transportation.

The performance objectives for Measure T4 are by 2030 to: (1) double transit service hours from 560,000 to 1.12 million; (2) install bus-only lanes and signal prioritization on all major transit thoroughfares; and (3) have a minimum of 75 percent of unincorporated Los Angeles County residents live within one-half mile of a bus or active transportation option;
extraction_rationale: Contains concrete, quantified performance objectives and infrastructure actions; listed under Strategy 3.
mechanism_description: The measure will expand and prioritize bus and other transit/active transportation options, improving access and service so more trips shift away from driving and transportation emissions decrease.

section_header: Measure T5: Limit and Remove Parking Minimums.
policy_statement: Measure T5 will limit and remove parking minimums through parking supply reductions, unbundling parking costs, and implementing parking pricing to encourage park-once behavior.
parent_statement: Strategy 3 will reduce single-occupancy vehicle trips by developing transportation networks that increase the accessibility, comfort, and convenience of active travel modes.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T5: Limit and Remove Parking Minimums.

The performance objectives for Measure T5 are to: (1) reduce parking stipulations to reduce parking supply and encourage transit use; (2) unbundle parking costs to reflect the cost of parking; and (3) implement parking pricing to encourage 'park-once' behavior.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention set (parking reforms) framed as performance objectives; listed under Strategy 3.
mechanism_description: The measure reduces parking supply, unbundles parking costs, and implements parking pricing to encourage transit use and park-once behavior, which reduces driving and related transport emissions.

policy_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## 2.6.2.4 Strategy 4: Institutionalize Low-Carbon Transportation

The County also would endeavor to install EV charging stations (EVCSs) at County properties and in the public right-of-way, require new development to install EVCSs, and develop incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EVCSs.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with explicit interventions; umbrella for measures T6–T9.
mechanism_description: The County would install EV charging stations at County properties and in the public right-of-way and require new development to install EV charging, enabling wider EV adoption.

section_header: Measure T6: Increase ZEV Market Share and Reduce Gasoline and Diesel Fuel Sales.
policy_statement: Measure T6 will increase zero-emission vehicle (ZEV) market share and EV charging stations, including targets for fleetwide ZEV penetration, ZEV sales shares, total new EV charging stations, and EV charging stations at County facilities by 2030/2035/2045.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T6: Increase ZEV Market Share and Reduce Gasoline and Diesel Fuel Sales.

The performance objectives for Measure T6 are to: (1) increase the fleetwide percentage of light-duty vehicles in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 30 percent by 2030, 50 percent by 2035, and 90 percent by 2045; (2) increase the sales of new light-duty vehicles in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 68 percent by 2030 and 100 percent by 2035; (3) install 37,000 total new public and private shared EVCSs (including EVCSs at County facilities and properties) by 2030, 74,000 by 2035, and 140,000 by 2045; and (4) install 5,000 total new EVCSs at County facilities and properties by 2030, 10,000 by 2035, and 25,000 by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified targets with timelines; listed as a measure under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: The measure sets objectives to expand EV charging stations and increase ZEV fleet and sales shares, enabling wider EV adoption and reducing gasoline and diesel vehicle emissions.

section_header: Measure T7: Electrify County Fleet Vehicles.
policy_statement: Measure T7 will electrify County fleet vehicles by electrifying bus and shuttle fleets by 2035, increasing County-owned light-duty ZEV shares by 2030/2035/2045, and supporting the state's goal that new light-duty fleet purchases (with exceptions) will be ZEVs.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T7: Electrify County Fleet Vehicles.

The performance objectives for Measure T7 are to: (1) electrify the County bus and shuttle fleets by 2035; (2) increase the fleetwide percentage of light-duty vehicles in the Countyowned fleet that are ZEVs to 35 percent by 2030, 60 percent by 2035, and 100 percent by 2045; and (3) support the state's goal that all new light-duty vehicle fleet purchases, with certain exceptions, will be ZEVs.
extraction_rationale: Concrete electrification commitments and quantified targets; under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: The County plans to electrify its bus, shuttle, and light-duty fleets by increasing the share of zero-emission vehicles, reducing tailpipe transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Measure T8 will accelerate freight decarbonization with ZEV targets for medium- and heavy-duty fleets and drayage, ZEV sales targets, and requirements for EV charging stations at new and existing warehouse loading docks by 2030.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T8: Accelerate Freight Decarbonization.

The performance objectives for Measure T8 are to: (1) increase the fleetwide percentage of medium-duty and heavy-duty vehicles in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 40 percent by 2030, 60 percent by 2035, and 90 percent by 2045; (2) increase the fleetwide percentage of medium-duty and heavy-duty trucks in the County-owned fleet that are ZEVs to 50 percent by 2030, 70 percent by 2035, and 95 percent by 2045; (3) ensure that 100 percent of the drayage truck fleet is ZEV by 2035; (4) ensure that 100 percent of sales of medium- and heavy-duty trucks are ZEVs by 2045; (5) require that all new warehouse loading docks have EVCSs by 2030; and (6) require that all existing warehouse loading docks have EVCSs by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified ZEV targets and explicit charging infrastructure requirements; measure under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: The measure increases adoption of zero-emission medium- and heavy-duty and drayage trucks, reducing freight transport tailpipe emissions, and it requires EV charging stations at warehouse loading docks to support ZEV operation.

section_header: Measure T9: Expand Use of Zero-Emission Technologies for Off-Road Vehicles and Equipment.
policy_statement: Measure T9 will expand use of zero-emission off-road vehicles and equipment by increasing ZEV shares for off-road fleets and for construction/agriculture/manufacturing equipment by 2030/2035/2045.
parent_statement: Strategy 4 will institutionalize low-carbon transportation by increasing access to charging infrastructure, installing EV charging stations, requiring new development to install EV charging, and developing incentives and requirements for existing buildings to install EV charging stations.
verbatim_text:
## Measure T9: Expand Use of Zero-Emission Technologies for Off-Road Vehicles and Equipment.

The performance objectives for Measure T9 are to: (1) increase the fleetwide percentage of off-road fleet and equipment in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 20 percent by 2030, 50 percent by 2035, and 95 percent by 2045; and (2) increase the fleetwide percentage of construction, agriculture, and manufacturing equipment in the unincorporated Los Angeles County that are ZEVs to 50 percent by 2030, 75 percent by 2035, and 100 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Specific quantified targets for off-road equipment electrification/zero-emission transition; under Strategy 4.
mechanism_description: The measure increases the share of zero-emission off-road vehicles and equipment, which reduces combustion fuel use and associated transportation emissions.

section_header: Measure E1: Transition Existing Buildings to All-Electric.
policy_statement: Measure E1 will transition existing buildings to all-electric by electrifying shares of existing residential and nonresidential buildings and requiring zero net energy for major renovations by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E1: Transition Existing Buildings to All-Electric.

The primary performance objectives for Measure E1 are to: (1) electrify 25 percent of the existing residential buildings by 2030, 40 percent by 2035, and 80 percent by 2045; (2) electrify 15 percent of the existing nonresidential buildings by 2030, 25 percent by 2035, and 60 percent by 2045; and (3) require zero net energy (ZNE) for 50 percent of all major renovations by 2030, 75 percent by 2035, and 100 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Concrete measure with quantified electrification and renovation performance requirements; not shown in prior summary.
mechanism_description: The measure electrifies existing residential and nonresidential buildings and require zero net energy for major renovations, reducing building energy-related emissions by shifting away from fossil fuel use and improving efficiency.

section_header: Measure E3: Implement Other Decarbonization Actions.
policy_statement: Measure E3 will implement other building decarbonization actions by increasing biomethane share in utility gas, using low/zero/negative-carbon concrete for all new construction, and replacing high-GWP refrigerants at specified rates by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E3: Implement Other Decarbonization Actions.

The performance objectives of Measure E3 are to: (1) increase the proportion of biomethane in the utility natural gas mix to 20 percent by 2030, 30 percent by 2035, and 80 percent by 2045; (2) use low-carbon, carbon-neutral, or negative-carbon concrete for all new construction; and (3) replace high-global-warming-potential refrigerants with lowglobal-warming-potential refrigerants 15 percent by 2030, 25 percent by 2035, and 50 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions with quantified targets and material requirements; standalone measure text in this chunk.
mechanism_description: The measure aims to cut building-related emissions by increasing biomethane in utility gas, using lower-carbon concrete in new construction, and replacing high-global-warming-potential refrigerants with lower-GWP refrigerants to reduce fugitive climate pollution.

section_header: Measure E4: Improve Energy Efficiency of Existing Buildings.
policy_statement: Measure E4 will improve energy efficiency of existing buildings by reducing building energy use intensity relative to 2015 by sector-specific percentages by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E4: Improve Energy Efficiency of Existing Buildings.

The performance objective of Measure E4 is to reduce building Energy Use Intensity (thousand British thermal units per square foot) below 2015 levels by 20 percent for residential, 15 percent for industrial, and 25 percent for commercial by 2030; 25 percent for residential and industrial and 35 percent for commercial by 2035; and 50 percent for residential, industrial, and commercial by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Quantified efficiency targets over time; clear performance standard.
mechanism_description: Measure E4 sets performance objectives to reduce existing buildings’ energy use intensity below 2015 levels, lowering overall building energy consumption.

section_header: Measure E5: Increase Use of Recycled Water and Gray Water Systems.
policy_statement: Measure E5 will increase use of recycled water and gray water systems by meeting specified shares of total and agricultural/industrial water demand with recycled/gray/potable reuse by 2025/2030/2045 and implementing a direct potable reuse project by 2025.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E5: Increase Use of Recycled Water and Gray Water Systems.

The performance objectives of Measure E5 are to: (1) meet 25 percent of total unincorporated Los Angeles County water demand with recycled water, gray water, or potable reuse by 2025, 50 percent by 2030, and 90 percent by 2045; (2) meet 30 percent of water demand for agricultural and industrial uses with recycled water or gray water by 2025, 50 percent by 2030, and 80 percent by 2045; and (3) implement a successful direct potable reuse project by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Water-sector measure with quantified demand shares and a dated project commitment.
mechanism_description: The measure sets objectives to increase use of recycled water, gray water, and potable reuse (including implementing a direct potable reuse project), increasing the share of demand met through reuse and improving water supply resilience.

section_header: Measure E6: Reduce Indoor and Outdoor Water Consumption.
policy_statement: Measure E6 will reduce indoor and outdoor water consumption by setting per-capita water use targets and reducing outdoor landscaping and municipal water consumption by specified percentages by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure E6: Reduce Indoor and Outdoor Water Consumption.

The performance objectives of Measure E6 are to: (1) reduce total water use to less than 110 gallons per capita per day (GPCD) by 2030, 100 GPCD by 2035, and 85 GPCD by 2045; (2) reduce outdoor landscaping water use to 10 percent by 2030, 20 percent by 2035, and 50 percent by 2045; and (3) reduce municipal water consumption 10 percent by 2030, 20 percent by 2035, and 50 percent by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Quantified conservation targets with dates; concrete performance objectives.
mechanism_description: The measure sets per-capita water-use objectives and reduction targets for outdoor landscaping and municipal water use to lower overall per-capita water consumption.

section_header: Measure W1: Institutionalize Sustainable Waste Systems and Practices.
policy_statement: Measure W1 will institutionalize sustainable waste systems and practices by increasing waste diversion rates to 85%/90%/95% by 2030/2035/2045, reducing single-use plastics disposal, increasing construction and demolition diversion to 70%, and increasing reuse of construction and demolition debris in new projects.
verbatim_text:
## Measure W1: Institutionalize Sustainable Waste Systems and Practices.

The performance objectives of Measure W1 are to: (1) increase the total unincorporated Los Angeles County waste diversion rate to 85 percent by 2030, 90 percent by 2035, and 95 percent by 2045; (2) reduce the disposal of single-use plastics in landfills; (3) increase the Construction and Demolition Ordinance to 70 percent diversion; and (4) increase the percentage of construction and demolition debris reused in new projects (private and public).
extraction_rationale: Waste policy with quantified diversion targets and specific ordinance-related actions.
mechanism_description: The measure increases diversion of waste (including construction and demolition materials) and reduces disposal in landfills, which lowers landfill methane emissions.

section_header: Measure A1: Conserve Forests, Woodlands, Shrublands, Grasslands, Desert, and other Carbon-Sequestering Wildlands and Working Lands.
policy_statement: Measure A1 will conserve and restore carbon-sequestering wildlands and working lands by reducing natural land conversion, conserving/restoring forest lands, and managing wildland acreage for wildfire risk reduction and carbon stock savings by 2030/2035/2045.
verbatim_text:
## Measure A1: Conserve Forests, Woodlands, Shrublands, Grasslands, Desert, and other Carbon-Sequestering Wildlands and Working Lands.

The performance objectives of Measure A1 are to: (1) reduce the average annual amount of natural land converted for urbanized uses 25 percent by 2030 (53 hectares conserved annually), 50 percent by 2035 (106 hectares conserved annually), and 75 percent by 2045 (159 hectares conserved annually); (2) conserve and restore 2,000 acres of natural forest lands by 2030, 4,000 acres by 2035, and 6,000 acres by 2045; and (3) manage 10,000 acres of wildland for wildfire risk reduction and carbon stock savings by 2030, 20,000 acres by 2035, and 50,000 acres by 2045.
extraction_rationale: Land conservation/restoration measure with quantified acreage/hectare targets and timelines.
mechanism_description: The measure aims to reduce natural land conversion, conserve and restore forests, and manage wildlands for wildfire risk reduction to preserve ecosystems and protect carbon stocks.

section_header: Measure A3: Expand Unincorporated Los Angeles County's Tree Canopy and Green Spaces.
policy_statement: Measure A3 will expand the unincorporated County's tree canopy and green spaces by planting 130,000/200,000/270,000 total new trees by 2030/2035/2045 and developing and implementing an Urban Forest Management Plan.
verbatim_text:
## Measure A3: Expand Unincorporated Los Angeles County's Tree Canopy and Green Spaces.

The performance objectives of Measure A3 are to: (1) plant 130,000 total new trees by 2030, 200,000 total new trees by 2035, and 270,000 total new trees by 2045; and (2) develop and implement an Urban Forest Management Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified tree-planting targets and planning requirement.
mechanism_description: The county will plant new trees and implement an urban forest management plan to expand tree canopy, which helps cool urban areas and increase carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: Require mitigation for oak woodland loss at a minimum 1:1 in-kind replacement via a County-approved habitat mitigation plan, including monitoring for at least five years and consistency with the County Oak Woodland Conservation Management Plan and 2014 Guide (prioritizing acquisition over restoration and in-lieu fees where applicable).
verbatim_text:
Mitigation Measure 3.5-5: Proponents of projects resulting in the loss of oak woodlands shall mitigate with in-kind replacement habitat at a minimum of 1:1 mitigation ratio documented through a County-approved habitat mitigation plan... The plan shall include monitoring for a minimum of five years, with annual reports submitted to the County.

For oak woodlands impacts, project mitigation shall be consistent with recommendations in the County's Oak Woodland Conservation Management Plan and its 2014 Guide.
extraction_rationale: This is a specific compensatory mitigation requirement with a defined minimum ratio and monitoring duration, plus required consistency with named County guidance.
mechanism_description: The measure requires in-kind replacement of oak woodland habitat (with an approved mitigation plan and monitoring), helping maintain ecosystem functions and preserve stored carbon by avoiding net loss of oak woodland area.

policy_statement: Draft 2045 CAP sets a 2045 target to reduce unincorporated Los Angeles County greenhouse gas emissions 83% below 2015 baseline levels.
verbatim_text:
The unincorporated County's emissions with Draft 2045 CAP implementation are compared to the following future targets:

- The Draft 2045 CAP's target of 83 percent below 2015 levels by 2045, which aligns with the statewide 2045 target of 85 percent below 1990 levels as codified in AB 1279 and included in the 2022 Scoping Plan.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions-reduction target with a deadline (2045) stated as a CAP target.
mechanism_description: The Draft 2045 CAP sets a target to reduce unincorporated County greenhouse gas emissions below a baseline level, creating an overall pathway for emissions reductions across sectors.

section_header: 4.1. Action on Governorate buildings and services
policy_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text: The first priority of the Governorate and the City Council should be to act on its direct perimeter of responsibility: Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management). It is only while being exemplary on its own perimeter that the City Council will be able to promote the mobilisation of all stakeholders...
extraction_rationale: Defines an action area with multiple subsections and specific measures that follow.
mechanism_description: The Governorate and City Council will act first on their own buildings and municipal services (e.g., street lighting, water/sanitation, waste management) to reduce operational emissions and demonstrate leadership that supports wider stakeholder mobilisation.

section_header: Municipal buildings – 4.1.1.2 Energy savings / short-term actions
policy_statement: For municipal buildings, the Municipality will implement a staff awareness campaign and behavioural measures to reduce electricity use (targeting a 10% cut) and promote an internal challenge supported by the Environmental Awareness Unit (EAU).
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text:
More efforts should be done in raising staff awareness on simple change of behaviour to save energy without significant investment... 

It would be interesting to promote a challenge between services/units to invite all workers from the Governorate to make significant efforts in saving energy.

The best performing service would be celebrated as 'energy saver/climate saver'' and would get a special gratification. The Environmental Awareness Unit (EAU) within the Governorate Communication Department will promote these activities.
extraction_rationale: Concrete intervention (awareness/behaviour measures, competition) explicitly planned for municipal buildings.
mechanism_description: The municipality plans to raise staff awareness and run an internal challenge to encourage behaviour changes in municipal buildings that reduce electricity use and related emissions.

policy_statement: For municipal buildings, the Governorate will implement a long-term programme to replace equipment with energy-efficient systems, including switching to LED lighting and installing motion sensors, and improving heating/cooling management systems.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text:
Beyond promoting awareness and behavioural change, it is necessary to develop a clear and comprehensive plan to replace old devices and equipment and install energy efficient systems...

- -Switch from AC/Heating reverse systems to central management of heating and cooling; and
- -Switch to LED lighting and install motion sensors in offices.
extraction_rationale: Specific technology interventions are explicitly proposed as a programme for municipal buildings.
mechanism_description: The Governorate will implement a plan to replace old equipment with energy-efficient systems (such as LED lighting, motion sensors, and improved heating/cooling management) to reduce energy use in municipal buildings.

section_header: Street lighting – 4.1.2.2 Revolving fund to replace old lamps
policy_statement: The City of Luxor will create a revolving fund to replace old street lighting lamps with LED, reinvesting savings to achieve full replacement over time.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text: The City of Luxor looks for an initial financial support to feed in a revolving fund dedicated to old lamps replacement. The City should set up a specific budget monitoring mechanism and put aside financial resources preserved due to more efficient lamps replacing old devices. Resources saved will be invested again in lamp replacement.
extraction_rationale: Defines a financing mechanism (revolving fund) and reinvestment approach for LED street lighting replacement.
mechanism_description: The city plans to use a revolving fund to replace old street lighting lamps with more efficient LEDs, reducing electricity consumption and reinvesting the resulting savings to fund additional replacements over time.

section_header: Street lighting – 4.1.2.2 Street lighting strategic plan
policy_statement: The City of Luxor will develop a street lighting strategic plan with differentiated lighting levels and technical solutions (e.g., sensors, midnight reduction) supported by public consultation and monitoring of energy and budget savings.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text:
The City of Luxor would gain from developing a street lighting strategic plan, identifying areas of differentiated usage... 

Such an improvement... should lead to designing a lighting system combining qualitative lighting and reduced energy consumption. ...

1. Based on the city development plan identify 3 or 4 types of streets and areas...
2. Organise public consultation...
... 4. Implement step by step the plan... Such monitoring must measure energy consumption reduction and highlight what it means in budget cuts.
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning instrument with implementation steps and monitoring requirements.
mechanism_description: The city plans to implement a street-lighting strategy (e.g., differentiated lighting levels, sensors, and nighttime reduction) and monitor results to reduce electricity use and the emissions associated with that power use.

policy_statement: For water delivery, Governorate services will conduct a detailed assessment of the water distribution network and wastewater treatment to identify energy-saving investments (e.g., variable speed pumps, high-efficiency equipment) and consider establishing a SCADA monitoring and control system.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text: The Governorate services should conduct a detailed assessment of the current status of the water distribution network and the wastewater treatment process. ... switching from classical pumps to variable speed devices and high efficiency equipment might result in cutting electricity consumption by 30% or more. Likewise establishing a SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system for monitoring and control could help...
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions and assessment commitment for reducing water-sector electricity use.
mechanism_description: Governorate services plan to assess water distribution and wastewater treatment operations and pursue efficiency upgrades (e.g., variable speed pumps, high-efficiency equipment) and possibly SCADA monitoring/control to cut electricity consumption.

policy_statement: For solid waste management, the Governorate will undertake a comprehensive waste management assessment to enable separate collection and recycling, optimize collection, explore biodigesters, and explore landfill methane recovery; and will implement public awareness to reduce waste and improve recycling.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text:
The Governorate would gain realising a comprehensive assessment of its waste management, with the following objectives:

- -Get a good understanding of the breakdown in different type of waste...
- -Optimize the service...
- -Structure recycling' chains...
- -Specifically explore the benefit of installing bio-digesters units...
- -Explore the benefit or recovering methane form the landfill...

Additionally... would benefit from designing and implementing a plan to raise public awareness on waste management...
extraction_rationale: Enumerated concrete actions and assessments for waste system reform and methane mitigation.
mechanism_description: The Governorate plans to assess and improve waste services to enable separate collection and recycling, while exploring biodigesters and landfill methane recovery, which reduces waste sent to landfill and avoids or captures methane emissions.

policy_statement: The City of Luxor will implement a fleet plan to improve fleet management and awareness to reduce fuel consumption and progressively replace old vehicles with new ones (including using leasing to avoid heavy investments).
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text:
The City of Luxor could engage in a comprehensive plan to:

- -Continue upgrading the fleet management tool and invest more in people awareness in order to reduce energy consumption of the fleet as its actually stands, and
- -Replace old vehicles with new ones, looking for progressive replacement through leasing to avoid too heavy investments.
extraction_rationale: Clear intervention plan for municipal fleet management and replacement.
mechanism_description: The city could pursue upgraded fleet management tools, staff awareness, and gradual replacement of older vehicles with newer ones to reduce fleet fuel/energy consumption and associated transport emissions.

policy_statement: The Governorate of Luxor will set up a dedicated Environmental Awareness Unit (EAU) to raise awareness across key publics on energy, water, waste, and climate issues, and will launch actions including materials, an annual energy festival/day, and targeted campaigns (e.g., positive energy schools, mosque campaigns, tourism operators).
parent_statement: Luxor will implement actions on Governorate buildings and services (street lighting, water distribution & sanitation, waste management) as the first priority within its direct responsibility to demonstrate leadership and enable wider stakeholder mobilisation.
verbatim_text:
The Governorate of Luxor will set up a dedicated unit, focusing on raising awareness in different publics...

The EAU will launch its mission focusing on 3 priority targets - Governorate staff; children & mothers; tourist operators...

The unit will develop its activities... including:
- -Spreading information and training material...
- -Develop an annual event ' Energy festival or Energy day '...
- -Unroll specific awareness campaigns... ' Positive energy schools '... 
... Promoting an awareness raising campaign in mosques ...
... Mobilise all tourism' operators...
extraction_rationale: Institutional creation ('will set up') plus a list of concrete awareness actions.
mechanism_description: The Governorate sets up an Environmental Awareness Unit to spread information, training materials, events, and targeted campaigns to influence public and operator practices on energy, water, and waste in ways that support lower emissions.

section_header: 4.2. Action plan on the City of Luxor
policy_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text: Beyond its direct scope of responsibility (on buildings and services), the Governorate of Luxor also has to mobilize all stakeholders acting in its boundaries. It is only through the overall commitment of stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism' operators, local groups, farmers, etc.) that a significant reduction in energy consumption and development of renewable energy will be achieved
extraction_rationale: Introduces a broad action plan area under which multiple sectoral actions are detailed.
mechanism_description: Luxor plans to mobilize residents and local economic actors so their collective commitment leads to reduced energy consumption and increased renewable energy development.

policy_statement: For transport, Luxor will promote a short-term negotiation process with transport providers to adopt a common charter to improve transport services (routes, stops, timing) and reduce inefficient competition.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text: It seems that the only option would be to promote a negotiation process with all transport providers to adopt a common charter for global improvement of transport services aiming at clarifying routes, stops, timing, in order to promote synergies and avoid competition...
extraction_rationale: Specific short-term intervention (common charter) with defined scope.
mechanism_description: The city promotes negotiations with transport providers to adopt a common charter that coordinates routes, stops, and timing, improving service synergies and helping reduce transport emissions.
