policy_statement: Luxor will develop and implement a Sustainable, Clean and Green Urban Mobility Master Plan, including prioritising public transport and walking/cycling, creating/empowering a public transport authority, promoting new engine technologies, adopting a rapid transit bus system from the airport to the city centre, and establishing emissions monitoring.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
Ideally... Luxor would implement a Sustainable, Clean and Green Urban Mobility Master Plan (SCGUMMP) that could entail the following:

- -Integrating transport and city planning policies, while prioritising public transportation... and active modes...
- -Structuring the urban transport sector by empowering a public transport authority...
- -Developing communication and participation strategies...
- -Encouraging the rollout of new engine technologies...
- -Promoting active modes... and developing the use of electric bicycle,
- -Adopt Rapid Transit Bus (RTB) system from the airport to the city centre of Luxor,
- -Implementing a common methodology to measure GHG emissions...
extraction_rationale: Detailed multi-component plan with specific interventions and governance elements.
mechanism_description: Luxor plans to implement an urban mobility master plan that prioritises public transport and walking/cycling (including a rapid bus service), shifting travel to lower-emitting modes and reducing transport greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: For tourism, the Governorate will establish a 'Green tourism support unit' to provide advice and audits to hotels/resorts investing in energy efficiency and renewable energy, and may set up an investment fund providing attractive loans to support these investments.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
To shift behaviour and speed up the shift, the Governorate should establish a ' Green tourism support unit ' providing advices to tourism operators willing to invest in energy efficiency and renewable energy. This technical unit will ensure audits to any resort and hotel willing to make necessary investments...

The technical unit could also set up an investment fund that would help resorts and hotels doing the right choice, providing loans at an attractive rate...
extraction_rationale: Creates specific institutional and financing mechanisms to drive tourism-sector mitigation actions.
mechanism_description: The Governorate plans to establish a green tourism support unit to provide advice and audits and could offer attractive-rate loans, helping hotels and resorts invest in energy efficiency and renewable energy to cut energy use and displace fossil energy.

policy_statement: For tourism transport, Luxor will support upgrading cruise boats and docks, including establishing a 'Green boat refurbishment fund', using solar PV on boats and along docks, improving waste management on boats, and supplying docking boats with renewable electricity to avoid diesel use while docked.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
The Governorate of Luxor could facilitate the establishment of ' Green boat refurbishment fund '... 

Some on board functions and services could also be supplied only from renewable electricity produced with solar PV installed on the rooftop of cruise boats.

... the fleet improvement programme should extend to redesigning the docks... promote solar PV equipment as a major source of renewable energy supply... renewable energy will supply docking boats avoiding unnecessary use of diesel motors when cruise boats and dhows are not sailing.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions and a proposed fund tied to a defined tourism transport sub-sector.
mechanism_description: Luxor plans to support solar PV and renewable electricity for cruise boats and docks so vessels can use renewable power (including while docked) instead of running diesel motors, reducing fossil fuel use and associated emissions.

policy_statement: For residential buildings, Luxor will run a public awareness campaign to reduce household electricity use, including promoting 24°C thermostat settings, efficient lighting/appliances, and other behavioural measures.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
There is a lot to do to raise public awareness on energy issues, inviting inhabitants to reduce their consumption... Such awareness campaign to promote efficient behaviour should focus on few measures:

- -Promoting the proper temperature at home... thermostats to be always at 24 degrees Celsius in summer would save up to 25 per cent...
- -Promoting behavioural changes at home... replacing classical bulbs with efficient devices... emphasises on buying green label appliances...
extraction_rationale: Defined campaign with concrete measures and quantified potential savings.
mechanism_description: The city plans to run an awareness campaign encouraging efficient household behavior (e.g., 24°C thermostat settings and efficient lighting/appliances) to reduce residential electricity use.

policy_statement: For residential buildings, Luxor will promote installation of solar water heating (SWH) systems to replace electric water heaters, including training companies to produce/sell/install SWH and exploring monthly-fee business models for provision and maintenance.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
The most obvious plan to develop on the medium term is the promotion of Solar Heating System...

- -Promote the use of solar water heating (SWH) system instead of the current electric water heating (EWH) systems.
- -Involve and train companies that will produce, sell and install SWH systems, and
- -A possible model to market SWH would be to ask for a monthly fee...
extraction_rationale: Specific technology shift (EWH to SWH) plus market/training measures.
mechanism_description: The plan promotes installing solar water heating systems to replace electric water heaters, reducing electricity use for hot water and displacing fossil-fueled power generation.

policy_statement: For residential buildings, Luxor will develop an energy-retrofitting programme including mapping housing energy performance, training local companies, partnering with a bank as third-party investor to finance retrofits repaid from savings, and promoting high-performance standards for new buildings.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
To design and implement an adequate energy-retrofitting plan for residential buildings, the Governorate of Luxor should engage in the following steps:

- -Assess the need through a detailed mapping of housing...
- -Train small local companies...
- -Develop a partnership with a bank acting as a 'third party investor'...
- -Promote retrofitting in selected targets...

The Governorate should also work on promoting energy high-performance references for new building to be constructed.
extraction_rationale: Concrete multi-step programme with financing mechanism and standards orientation.
mechanism_description: The Governorate plans to develop and implement a residential energy-retrofitting plan (including assessment/mapping, local company training, and bank-backed financing) to increase retrofit activity and reduce household energy use.

policy_statement: For tertiary buildings, Luxor will implement an action plan including mapping tertiary buildings’ energy use, conducting awareness and training for private entrepreneurs, providing technical support, and developing showcase partnerships with non-commercial service providers (e.g., hospitals, mosques, churches).
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
It is necessary to add a first step to properly assess the need through a detailed mapping of tertiary buildings... 

Private entrepreneurs and services
- -Unroll a vast awareness raising campaign...
- -Offer them technical support...

Non-commercial service' providers
- -The Governorate should develop partnerships... to develop showcases of efficient building also using renewable energy,
- -Promote energy efficiency in hospitals...
- -Engage a partnership with priests and imams...
extraction_rationale: Contains a defined plan with concrete actions (mapping, campaigns, partnerships) for the tertiary sector.
mechanism_description: The plan maps tertiary buildings’ energy use and provides awareness, training, technical support, and partnerships to help service-sector buildings improve efficiency (including showcase efficient buildings using renewable energy), reducing overall energy use.

policy_statement: For industry, Luxor will conduct energy audits and raise awareness among business owners about reducing energy consumption and developing renewable energy solutions, as a short-term step toward a comprehensive industrial action plan.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
Three options could be promoted on the short term...

- -Get a better understanding of energy consumption and GHG emission of the industry in Luxor...
- -Raise awareness among business owners...
- -Raise awareness among the business sector on the benefit of developing renewable energy solutions...

One practical option would be to develop energy audits for companies willing to address energy issues.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions (audits, awareness, analysis) aimed at industry energy reduction.
mechanism_description: The city plans to develop energy audits and raise awareness among businesses so companies better understand their energy use and take actions that reduce industrial energy consumption.

policy_statement: Luxor will establish a 'Green Industry Unit' to provide advice and technical support for industrial process efficiency, rooftop renewable energy, and waste reduction/recycling, and later extend support to the service sector.
parent_statement: Luxor will implement the action plan on the City of Luxor by mobilizing stakeholders (residents, shops, businesses, tourism operators, farmers, etc.) to achieve energy reduction and renewable energy development.
verbatim_text:
The 'Green Industry Unit' initially set up to offer services to Industry' operators could progressively expend its support to the service sector (tertiary buildings) and the industry. This technical unit would provide advices and technical support...

The GIU will support companies in different projects:

- -Improving industrial processes to reduce energy consumption;
- -Promoting renewable energy units on the roof tops of factories and workshops; and
- -Improving production models to reduce waste generation and improve reuse and recycling.
extraction_rationale: Institutional measure ('unit') with explicit project support scope.
mechanism_description: Luxor will set up a Green Industry Unit that provides advice and technical support to companies to improve process energy efficiency, promote rooftop renewables, and reduce waste through reuse and recycling, leading to lower emissions from industrial energy use and materials handling.

section_header: Section V: Project Fiches — Priority Action #1 for SECAP
policy_statement: Priority Action #1: Urban Sustainable Mobility Master Plan (SCGUMMP) for Luxor to improve mobility while reducing energy consumption and GHG emissions from transport, with a project lifetime of 1 year (Oct 2017–Dec 2018).
verbatim_text: 1. Urban Sustainable Mobility Master Plan ... Location: City of Luxor, Governorate of Luxor Start date: October 2017 Project lifetime: 1 year ... End: December 2018
extraction_rationale: Named priority action/project with clear scope and timeline; has multiple components/deliverables beneath.
mechanism_description: Developing an urban sustainable mobility master plan supports shifts to more sustainable transport options, lowering transport energy use and GHG emissions.

section_header: Section V: Project Fiches — Priority Action #1 for SECAP
policy_statement: Develop an Integrated Sustainable Mobility Master Plan for a period of 15 years, including costing and an implementation roadmap with short/medium-term priorities.
parent_statement: Priority Action #1: Urban Sustainable Mobility Master Plan (SCGUMMP) for Luxor to improve mobility while reducing energy consumption and GHG emissions from transport, with a project lifetime of 1 year (Oct 2017–Dec 2018).
verbatim_text:
Deliverables should be as follows:

- Integrated Sustainable Mobility Master Plan for a period of 15 years (the best mobility scenario for the city) to improve mobility in the City Luxor and attain Egypt's vision, SDS 2030 and SDG 7, 9, 11, 12, and 17,
- Costing of these different components,
- Implementation road map, including priorities to be addressed on the short and medium terms,
extraction_rationale: Specific mandated deliverables (master plan, costing, roadmap) under the Priority Action #1 project.
mechanism_description: Developing an integrated sustainable mobility master plan and implementation roadmap supports shifting travel toward more sustainable transport options, which reduces transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Section V: Project Fiches — Priority Action #1 for SECAP
policy_statement: Implement a common methodology to measure and report GHG emissions and monitor benefits from sustainable urban mobility, including Nile river clean transportation and sustainable cruising.
parent_statement: Priority Action #1: Urban Sustainable Mobility Master Plan (SCGUMMP) for Luxor to improve mobility while reducing energy consumption and GHG emissions from transport, with a project lifetime of 1 year (Oct 2017–Dec 2018).
verbatim_text: - Implementing a common methodology to measure GHG emissions, report on them and monitor all other benefits deriving from the development of sustainable urban mobility, including river Nile clean transportation and sustainable cursing.
extraction_rationale: Concrete monitoring/reporting intervention explicitly listed as part of the master plan components.
mechanism_description: Implement a common methodology to measure and report GHG emissions and monitor benefits from sustainable urban mobility, supporting decision-making and tracking progress.

section_header: Section V: Project Fiches — Priority Action #1 for SECAP
policy_statement: Achieve a 30% reduction in transport energy consumption by 2030 through the mobility master plan (including contributions from new engine technologies, active mobility, urban planning/traffic optimization, and public transport/RTB).
parent_statement: Priority Action #1: Urban Sustainable Mobility Master Plan (SCGUMMP) for Luxor to improve mobility while reducing energy consumption and GHG emissions from transport, with a project lifetime of 1 year (Oct 2017–Dec 2018).
verbatim_text: Providing the Sustainable, Clean and Green Urban Mobility Master Plan implementation would have started in 2020 the latest and would demonstrate a 50% progress in implementation in 2030 the expected impact of such a plan should generate a 30% reduction in energy consumption by 2030, including: • New engine technologies will allow securing à 10% reduction, • Active mobility development (cycling and walking) allow 5% reduction, • Urban planning allows traffic optimization that will reduce consumption by 10%, • Public transport system and RTB service to airport result in another 5%
extraction_rationale: Quantified target (30% by 2030) with specified contributing measures; clearly a policy commitment within the project fiche.
mechanism_description: It implements an urban mobility master plan that expands active mobility, improves public transport, and optimizes traffic (alongside cleaner engine technologies) to reduce transport energy use and related emissions.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 2 for SECAP
policy_statement: Priority Action #2: Sustainable and Green Boats to convert tourist cruise boats from diesel to LPG or CNG/NG, with project lifetime Dec 2017–Dec 2020.
verbatim_text: 2. Sustainable and Green Boats ... Start date: December 2017 ... Project lifetime: 3 years ... End: December 2020 ... consists mainly in converting all these boats using fossil fuel to operate with LPG (Liquid Petroleum Gaz) or CNG (Compressed natural gas)
extraction_rationale: Named priority action with defined technology shift and timeline; supports multiple sub-commitments and numeric targets.
mechanism_description: The project converts tourist cruise boats from diesel to LPG or CNG/natural gas, lowering the emissions intensity of boat operations and reducing transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 2 for SECAP
policy_statement: Convert 110 tourist cruise boats and 155 small passenger boats to run by natural gas.
parent_statement: Priority Action #2: Sustainable and Green Boats to convert tourist cruise boats from diesel to LPG or CNG/NG, with project lifetime Dec 2017–Dec 2020.
verbatim_text: The ambition is to convert 110 cruise boats and 155 small passengers boats.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified conversion target for fleets under the priority action.
mechanism_description: The plan is to convert cruise and small passenger boats to run on natural gas, which shifts marine fuel use and reduces vessel emissions.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 2 for SECAP
policy_statement: Develop a study to install solar PV onto cruise boats for supplying electricity from renewable energy.
parent_statement: Priority Action #2: Sustainable and Green Boats to convert tourist cruise boats from diesel to LPG or CNG/NG, with project lifetime Dec 2017–Dec 2020.
verbatim_text: - Develop a study to install Solar PV onto cruise boats for supplying electricity from renewable energy.
extraction_rationale: Specific technical study commitment linked to renewable electricity supply for boats.
mechanism_description: The policy develops a study to install solar PV on cruise boats so renewable electricity can replace fossil-based electricity generation.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 2 for SECAP
policy_statement: Optimize overall cruise fleet management with an objective to improve overall efficiency by 30% mainly by improving occupancy rates.
parent_statement: Priority Action #2: Sustainable and Green Boats to convert tourist cruise boats from diesel to LPG or CNG/NG, with project lifetime Dec 2017–Dec 2020.
verbatim_text:
Short and medium terms priorities:
- -Optimize  the  overall  cruise  fleet  management  with  an  objective  of  improvement  its  overall efficiency by 30% mainly working on occupancy rate improvement.
extraction_rationale: Quantified operational efficiency objective (30%) stated as a priority deliverable.
mechanism_description: It optimizes cruise fleet management by improving occupancy rates, increasing transport efficiency and reducing emissions per passenger served.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 5 for SECAP
policy_statement: Priority Action #5: Green Governorate Buildings Plan (GGBP/GMBP) to reduce energy consumption and GHG emissions in Governorate buildings through energy efficiency measures and renewable energy (solar PV and solar water heating).
verbatim_text: The City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor has the desire to move forward to holistically solve these challenges by promoting sustainable energy in Governorate (municipal) buildings in the city of Luxor through the development of a comprehensive plan to reduce energy consumption and consequently GHG emissions. ... 'Green Municipal Buildings Plan - GMBP.'
extraction_rationale: Named plan with multiple concrete measures and quantified investments and outputs.
mechanism_description: The city plans to develop a comprehensive green municipal buildings plan that promotes energy efficiency and solar energy in Governorate buildings to reduce energy consumption and consequently reduce GHG emissions.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 5 for SECAP
policy_statement: Replace lighting in public buildings with LED lamps and incorporate motion sensors (about 8,500 LED lamps), with an estimated €40,000 investment and expected savings of 283 MWh/year.
parent_statement: Priority Action #5: Green Governorate Buildings Plan (GGBP/GMBP) to reduce energy consumption and GHG emissions in Governorate buildings through energy efficiency measures and renewable energy (solar PV and solar water heating).
verbatim_text:
Switch to LED lighting installation and incorporate motion sensors in offices of Governorate buildings. ...

Replacing lamps in all public buildings with LED devices, would require installing 8,500 LED lamps ... This would cost ... a total investment of 40,000 € (822,000 EGP).

Energy  saved  from  such  new  equipment  would  represent  283  MWh/y
extraction_rationale: Specific quantified intervention (LED+sensors), investment amount, and expected energy savings.
mechanism_description: Switch to LED lighting and incorporate motion sensors in public building offices to reduce electricity use from lighting.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 5 for SECAP
policy_statement: Install solar PV on Governorate building roofs via a staged approach, with a first round installing 7,500 kWp (estimated €10,000,000 investment) producing about 14,238,560 kWh/year.
parent_statement: Priority Action #5: Green Governorate Buildings Plan (GGBP/GMBP) to reduce energy consumption and GHG emissions in Governorate buildings through energy efficiency measures and renewable energy (solar PV and solar water heating).
verbatim_text:
The priority  action  #5 recommends  a  staged  approach  with  a  first  round  tapping  on  50%  of  the  entire potential, meaning installing 7,500 kWp: for example, 50 units of 100 kWp and 10 units of 250 kWp.

| Nb. of 1 kWp unit | Investment | Prod in kWh |
| 7 500 | 10 000 000 | 14 238 560 |
extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable capacity target with staged deployment, investment and generation figures.
mechanism_description: Install solar PV on government building roofs to generate renewable electricity that displaces fossil-based power generation.

section_header: City of Luxor - Governorate of Luxor Priority Action # 5 for SECAP
policy_statement: Equip 100 Governorate buildings with solar water heating by installing five SWH units (3 collectors) per building (total cost €292,000; production 1,380,000 kWh).
parent_statement: Priority Action #5: Green Governorate Buildings Plan (GGBP/GMBP) to reduce energy consumption and GHG emissions in Governorate buildings through energy efficiency measures and renewable energy (solar PV and solar water heating).
verbatim_text:
The priority action #5 propose to equip develop 100 buildings with 5 SWH units of 3 collectors.

| Cost in € for 100 x 5 units | Production in KWh |
| 292 000 | 1 380 000 |
extraction_rationale: Specific quantified rollout of solar water heating across buildings with cost and production.
mechanism_description: The action proposes equipping buildings with solar water heating units to generate renewable hot water energy and reduce fossil-based energy use for water heating.

section_header: City of Luxor (Governorate of Luxor) - Priority Action # 4 for SECAP
policy_statement: Priority Action #4: Sustainable and Green Tourism Plan (SGTP) to green hotels and resorts by promoting energy conservation/efficiency and renewable solutions (solar PV and solar water heating), starting Dec 2017 for 5 years.
verbatim_text: 4. Greening Hotels and Resorts ... Start date: December 2017 ... Project lifetime: 5 years ... The Sustainable and Green Tourism Plan (SGTP) will focus on hotels and resorts to promote energy conservation, energy efficiency and develop renewable solutions to cover energy needs.
extraction_rationale: Named priority action with defined interventions and timeframe; includes multiple components (SWH fund, awareness, incentives).
mechanism_description: The plan focuses on hotels and resorts to promote energy conservation and energy efficiency, reducing building energy use (and associated emissions).

section_header: City of Luxor (Governorate of Luxor) - Priority Action # 4 for SECAP
policy_statement: Set up a revolving fund to support investment in solar water heating in hotels and resorts, implemented in phases of 10 hotels per year with €500k/year investment capacity for the first two years.
parent_statement: Priority Action #4: Sustainable and Green Tourism Plan (SGTP) to green hotels and resorts by promoting energy conservation/efficiency and renewable solutions (solar PV and solar water heating), starting Dec 2017 for 5 years.
verbatim_text: The priority action #4 proposes to set up a revolving fund to support investment in hotels. This investment could be done in phases of 10 hotels per year with an investment capacity of 500K€ per year for the first two years (1m€) and another round on investment as soon as the fund has been replenished.
extraction_rationale: Specific financing mechanism (revolving fund) with phased implementation and budget numbers.
mechanism_description: It sets up a revolving fund to support hotel investment in solar water heating, reducing emissions by displacing conventional energy used to provide hot water.

section_header: City of Luxor (Governorate of Luxor) - Priority Action # 4 for SECAP
policy_statement: Supply natural gas to hotels/resorts in Luxor's eastern bank, saving about 8,157 MWh/year of electricity and reducing GHG emissions by 2.8 tCO2eq/y (as stated).
parent_statement: Priority Action #4: Sustainable and Green Tourism Plan (SGTP) to green hotels and resorts by promoting energy conservation/efficiency and renewable solutions (solar PV and solar water heating), starting Dec 2017 for 5 years.
verbatim_text: This priority action plans to supply natural gas to hotels/resorts in the eastern bank of the City of Luxor, which will save 8,157 MWh/year of electricity (8 GWh/year) from the total use 27 GWh/year (2015 reference) and will reduce GHG emissions by 2.8 tCO2eq/y.
extraction_rationale: Explicit planned intervention (gas connections) with quantified energy saving and emissions reduction figures.
mechanism_description: The action plans to supply natural gas to hotels and resorts, reducing their electricity consumption and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Egypt’s sustainable energy policy roadmap targets 22% of total electricity consumption from renewables by 2022 and 80% of electricity demand from local generation by 2020.
verbatim_text:
Egypt developed its sustainable energy policy road map with the aim to increase the operating and technical efficiency of distribution utilities, improve energy conservation and load management and diversify the sources of the regional electricity supply.

The goals of this roadmap are three folds:

1. 22% of total electricity consumption sourced from renewables by 2022.

... 

4. 80% of electricity demand from local generation (of all fuels) by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified national targets with deadlines (renewables share by 2022; local generation share by 2020).
mechanism_description: Egypt’s roadmap aims to increase the share of electricity sourced from renewables, which shifts the power mix away from fossil generation and lowers power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Egypt plans to increase the share of renewable energies in electricity supply from 9% to 20% by 2020, supported by laws, regulations, and decrees.
verbatim_text: The Egyptian Government has set plans to increase the share of Renewable Energies (RE) in its electricity supply from the current 9 per cent to 20 per cent by 2020. Many laws, by-laws, regulations and decrees have been developed and endorsed.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target (20% by 2020) and reference to enabling legal instruments.
mechanism_description: The government sets plans to increase the share of renewable energy in electricity supply, which displaces fossil-based generation and reduces power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Egypt’s Energy Efficiency Plan in the Electricity Sector targeted savings of 5,566 GWh between 2012 and 2015 via efficient household lighting (distribution of 60 million bulbs), appliance standards and labelling, and street-lighting energy savings (1,200 GWh).
verbatim_text:
Egypt has developed an Energy Efficiency Plan in the Electricity Sector saving 5,566 GWH between 2012 and 2015, mainly by measures in the lighting sector, including:

- -The first phase: Change to high efficiency lighting in the household sector. The planned distribution of 60 million bulbs shall save 3,320 GWH,
- -The second phase: programme of energy efficiency standards and labelling for household appliances. This shall save 1,663 GWH, and
- -Energy saving in street lighting of 1,200 GWH; supported by the Association of Energy Efficiency Engineers (AEEE).
extraction_rationale: Quantified energy-saving commitments with specific interventions and timeframe.
mechanism_description: The plan develops and implements efficient lighting, appliance standards and labelling, and street-lighting energy savings to cut electricity use and thereby reduce power-sector emissions.

section_header: 3.5. City of Luxor vision and objectives
policy_statement: Luxor’s SECAP sets short-term (2020) targets to reduce energy consumption by around 10–15% and reduce GHG emissions by 10% versus the 2015 BAU scenario.
verbatim_text:
Short-term objectives 2020:

- -Reduce energy consumption across the board by around 10% to 15%, and
- -Reduce GHG emission by 10% in 2020, compared to the business as usual scenario (2015 baseline).
extraction_rationale: Quantified mitigation targets with a defined year and baseline.
mechanism_description: The plan sets short-term targets to cut overall energy consumption and reduce GHG emissions relative to a business-as-usual baseline, guiding actions that lower emissions.

section_header: 2.2.2. Template — Proposed Communication or Awareness Raising Action related to Specific/Pilot Project: Environment
policy_statement: Conduct an awareness and guidance campaign (twice a week for 7 months, Feb–Sept) to promote switching microbuses to liquefied natural gas (LNG), including training from GASCO and preparing to switch 7 minibuses as initial demonstration vehicles.
verbatim_text:
The  campaign  will  raise  awareness  and  provide  guidance:  twice  a  week  for  7  months  from February till September

Design the campaign, presentations and publications, and switch 7 minibuses to work as follows:

One microbus to operate on the line El-Zeneya, another microbus on the line of El-Bayadeya, and five microbuses in the main lines with their processing and paint supported or free of charge as a mobile-display in the governorate.
extraction_rationale: Time/frequency specified and includes a concrete initial conversion action (7 minibuses).
mechanism_description: An awareness and guidance campaign, along with switching a small set of minibuses as demonstration vehicles, supports a shift of microbuses to LNG fuel, reducing transport emissions compared with conventional fuels.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure

- ... Priority Action E.1 Implement Green Building Standards and Incentivize Innovative Green Building Design
- ... Priority Action E.2 Improve Low-Income Housing Energy Efficiency
- ... Priority Action E.3 Enhance and Expand Energy Outreach and Education Programs
- ... Priority Action E.4 Retrofit Outdoor Streetlights to LED
- ... Priority Action E.5 Expand Financing Options for Residential Energy Efficiency Retrofits
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple specified priority actions listed beneath it; functions as an umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Implementing green building standards, improving low-income housing efficiency, expanding energy education, retrofitting streetlights to LED, and expanding retrofit financing lowers energy use in buildings and related infrastructure.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Implement green building standards by 2022 with 100% compliance by 2025, including requiring new municipal buildings and major renovations to meet LEED Silver or Net Zero Energy standards and adopting improved green building/energy codes.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
E.1 Implement Green Building Standards and Incentivize Innovative Green Building Design | Codify and implement green building standards by 2022, with 100% compliance by 2025

Require that all new municipal buildings and major renovations for the City and County meet LEED Silver or Net Zero Energy standards.
extraction_rationale: Contains clear deadlines and compliance target plus specific regulatory/standards actions; listed as Priority Action E.1 under the action area.
mechanism_description: Codifying and implementing green building standards that require new municipal buildings and major renovations to meet LEED Silver or Net Zero Energy standards reduces building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Expand weatherization/retrofit programs for low-income housing to increase the number of low-income residences served annually by 500% over 10 years and target a 30% reduction in household energy use.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text: E.2 Improve Low-Income Housing Energy Efficiency | Increase the number of low- income residences served by weatherization and retrofit programs annually by 500% over 10 years, with a 30% reduction in energy usage target per household
extraction_rationale: Quantified scale-up and performance target; listed as Priority Action E.2.
mechanism_description: Increase participation in weatherization and retrofit programs for low-income homes to lower household energy use.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Enhance and expand energy outreach and education programs to achieve a 10% or greater reduction in average electricity consumption in the residential and commercial sectors.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.3: Enhance and Expand Energy Outreach and Education Programs

Objective: Attain a 10% or greater reduction in average electricity consumption for residential and commercial sectors
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target; explicitly framed as a priority action under the action area.
mechanism_description: Enhancing and expanding energy outreach and education programs helps households and businesses change practices and adopt efficiency measures that reduce electricity consumption.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Improve Energy Efficiency of Buildings And Key Infrastructure
policy_statement: Replace all existing non-LED streetlights and leased outdoor lighting with LED bulbs by 2030 or sooner, beginning in 2025.
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.4: Retrofit Outdoor Streetlights to LED

Objective: Replace all existing non-LED streetlights and leased outdoor lighting with LED bulbs by 2030 or sooner, starting in 2025
extraction_rationale: Clear infrastructure retrofit commitment with timeline; listed as Priority Action E.4.
mechanism_description: Replace non-LED streetlights and outdoor lighting with LED bulbs to reduce electricity use and the emissions associated with electricity generation.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.5: Expand Financing Options for Residential Energy Efficiency Retrofits
policy_statement: Expand financing options for residential energy efficiency retrofits so that by 2025, 15% of all residences have energy-efficient space heating/cooling equipment and major appliances (including tariffed on-bill financing and advocacy for residential PACE and rebates).
parent_statement: Improve energy efficiency of buildings and key infrastructure through a package of priority actions (green building standards, low-income efficiency, outreach/education, LED streetlights, and residential retrofit financing).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.5: Expand Financing Options for Residential Energy Efficiency Retrofits

## OBJECTIVE

By 2025, 15% of all residences will have energy-efficient space heating and cooling equipment and major appliances

- Work with MLGW and other partners to develop a tariffed on-bill financing program for residential energy retrofits.
- Advocate for state legislation that allows the implementation of a local Residential Property Assessed Clean Energy (PACE) program.
extraction_rationale: Quantified adoption target with specific policy/program mechanisms; explicitly a priority action.
mechanism_description: The city will work with partners to develop on-bill financing and support a residential PACE program to help more homes install efficient heating/cooling equipment and appliances, reducing residential energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 2: Transform Our Energy Supply
policy_statement: Transform the community energy supply through priority actions, including decarbonizing the electric grid with renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 2: Transform Our Energy Supply

- ... Priority Action E.6 Decarbonize the Electric Grid with Renewable Energy
extraction_rationale: Named action area introducing a priority action; serves as an umbrella heading in the plan’s structure.
mechanism_description: Decarbonizing the electric grid with renewable energy increases renewable electricity supply and displaces fossil generation, reducing power-sector greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.6: Decarbonize the Electric Grid with Renewable Energy
policy_statement: Increase the percentage of carbon-free energy in the electricity supply to 80% by 2035 and 100% by 2050, focusing on renewable sources such as solar and wind, including implementing at least one community shared solar project with low-income subsidies by 2022.
parent_statement: Transform the community energy supply through priority actions, including decarbonizing the electric grid with renewable energy.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.6: Decarbonize the Electric Grid with Renewable Energy

Objective: Increase the percentage of carbon-free energy in our electricity supply to 80% by 2035 and 100% by 2050, focusing on renewable sources such as solar and wind

Implement at least one local community shared solar project that includes subsidies for low-income household participation in the Memphis area by 2022
extraction_rationale: Contains explicit quantified targets and a dated implementation commitment; listed as Priority Action E.6.
mechanism_description: Increase the share of carbon-free renewable electricity (including community shared solar with low-income subsidies) so fossil-based power generation is displaced and grid emissions decline.

section_header: ## Action  Area  3:  Increase  Green  Infrastructure  and Community Resilience
policy_statement: Increase green infrastructure and community resilience through priority actions, including expanding the urban tree canopy and aligning mitigation and adaptation efforts.
verbatim_text:
## Action  Area  3:  Increase  Green  Infrastructure  and Community Resilience

- ... Priority Action E.7 Nurture and Expand the Urban Tree Canopy
- ... Priority Action E.8 Ensure Alignment of Climate Change Mitigation and Adaptation Efforts
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions beneath it; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Nurturing and expanding the urban tree canopy increases green cover that helps reduce heat exposure and supports carbon storage in urban biomass.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.7: Nurture and Expand the Urban Tree Canopy
policy_statement: Increase urban tree canopy coverage countywide to 60% by 2050 through tree planting/replanting and related program and code measures.
parent_statement: Increase green infrastructure and community resilience through priority actions, including expanding the urban tree canopy and aligning mitigation and adaptation efforts.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.7: Nurture and Expand the Urban Tree Canopy

Objective: Plant or replant trees with the intention of increasing urban tree canopy to 60% coverage countywide by 2050
extraction_rationale: Quantified canopy target with deadline; explicitly a priority action under the action area.
mechanism_description: Planting or replanting trees increases urban tree canopy, which helps store carbon and reduce heat exposure through added shade and evapotranspiration.

section_header: ## Priority Action E.8: Align Climate Mitigation and Climate Adaptation Efforts
policy_statement: Identify and prioritize cross-cutting strategies that address climate change mitigation and adaptation, including implementing Mid-South Regional Resilience Plan projects upon completion and adoption and developing neighborhood-scale demonstration projects.
parent_statement: Increase green infrastructure and community resilience through priority actions, including expanding the urban tree canopy and aligning mitigation and adaptation efforts.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action E.8: Align Climate Mitigation and Climate Adaptation Efforts

Objective: Identify and prioritize cross-cutting strategies for climate change mitigation and adaptation

Implement projects and practices recommended in the Mid-South Regional Resilience Plan upon completion and adoption.
extraction_rationale: Priority action with concrete implementation commitments (implement resilience plan; develop demonstration projects).
mechanism_description: The city will identify and prioritize cross-cutting mitigation and adaptation strategies and implement Mid-South Regional Resilience Plan projects after completion and adoption to strengthen institutional capacity for climate resilience action.

section_header: ## Action  Area  1:  Shift  to  Low-Carbon  Transportation Modes and Reduce Reliance on Motor Vehicles
policy_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
## Action  Area  1:  Shift  to  Low-Carbon  Transportation Modes and Reduce Reliance on Motor Vehicles

- ... Priority Action T.1 Implement Land Use Patterns that Support Active Transportation and Transit
- ... Priority Action T.2 Create Complete Streets that Prioritize Walking and Bicycling
- ... Priority Action T.3 Enhance Public Transit
- ... Priority Action T.4 Reduce Commute Trips through Transportation Demand Management Strategies
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions listed; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: The policy implements land use, complete streets, public transit enhancements, and transportation demand management to shift travel away from motor vehicles toward walking, bicycling, and transit, reducing transportation emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.1: Implement Land Use Patterns that Support Active Transportation and Transit
policy_statement: Implement land use patterns that support active transportation and transit by implementing the Memphis 3.0 growth strategy and updating zoning/development codes to support walkable infill, mixed-use development and transit-oriented development.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
T.1 Implement Land Use Patterns that Support Active Transportation and Transit

Implement the Memphis 3.0 vision and growth strategy focused on building density and mixed uses around community and citywide anchor areas.

Update zoning and development codes to reflect the Memphis 3.0 vision and growth strategy, particularly focusing on regulations that support walkable, infill, mixed-use development and the implementation of Transit Oriented Development (TOD).
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (code updates and implementation of a named comprehensive plan); presented as Priority Action T.1.
mechanism_description: Implementing a density- and mixed-use-focused growth strategy and updating zoning/development codes to support walkable infill and transit-oriented development helps shift trips toward walking and transit, reducing vehicle travel and related emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.2: Create Complete Streets that Prioritize Walking and Bicycling
policy_statement: Create Complete Streets to convert 10% of vehicle trips to walking/bicycling by 2030 (starting in 2020) and increase to 40% of vehicle trips by 2050, including establishing a dedicated annual funding source of $20 million for pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
T.2 Create Complete Streets that Prioritize Walking and Bicycling | Starting in 2020, convert 10% of vehicle trips to bicycle and pedestrian modes by 2030 and then increase this percentage linearly to 40% of vehicle trips by 2050.

Establish a dedicated annual funding source of $20 million for pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified mode-shift targets with dates and a specific funding commitment; listed as Priority Action T.2.
mechanism_description: Create Complete Streets and fund pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements to shift a portion of vehicle trips to walking and bicycling, reducing transportation emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.3: Enhance Public Transit
policy_statement: Enhance public transit by fully implementing the Memphis 3.0 Transit Vision by 2022, increasing ridership/frequency to meet long-range objectives, and converting MATA’s entire fleet to electric by 2050, supported by increasing immediate annual funding by $30 million.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action T.3: Enhance Public Transit

Objective: Fully implement the Memphis 3.0 Transit Vision by 2022. Increase ridership and improve frequency to meet long- range (2040/2050) ridership objectives. Convert MATA's entire fleet to electric by 2050.

Provide dedicated annual funding to MATA and increase immediate annual funding by $30 million to implement the Memphis 3.0 Transit Vision.
extraction_rationale: Multiple explicit deadlines/targets plus funding amount; presented as Priority Action T.3.
mechanism_description: The city will implement the transit vision with added funding to improve service frequency and increase ridership, lowering transportation emissions by shifting trips to public transit, while also converting the transit fleet to electric to cut vehicle tailpipe emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action T.4: Reduce Commute Trips through Transportation Demand Management
policy_statement: Reduce drive-alone commute trips by 10% by 2022 and 40% by 2050 by expanding Transportation Demand Management programs at major employers and implementing smart parking and related measures.
parent_statement: Shift to low-carbon transportation modes and reduce reliance on motor vehicles through a package of priority actions (land use, complete streets, public transit, and demand management).
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action T.4: Reduce Commute Trips through Transportation Demand Management

## Objective: Reduce drive-alone commute trips 10% by 2022 and 40% by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction targets with dates; clearly a priority action.
mechanism_description: Expand transportation demand management programs and implement smart parking to reduce drive-alone commute trips, lowering transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 2: Set the Stage for Vehicle Electrification
policy_statement: Set the stage for vehicle electrification through a priority action to encourage electric vehicle adoption and develop charging infrastructure.
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 2: Set the Stage for Vehicle Electrification

- ... Priority Action T.5 Encourage Electric Vehicle Adoption and the Development of Charging Infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Named action area introducing a priority action; used as parent for hierarchy.
mechanism_description: The city plans to encourage electric vehicle adoption by developing EV charging infrastructure, which helps enable a shift from combustion vehicles to electric vehicles and reduces transport emissions.

section_header: ## PRIORITY ACTION  T.5 Encourage Electric Vehicle Adoption and the Development of Charging Infrastructure
policy_statement: Increase passenger EV travel to 5% by 2025, 30% by 2035, and 50% by 2050; and increase freight EV travel to 3% by 2025, 20% by 2035, and 50% by 2050, including developing a comprehensive community-wide EV strategy and committing to transition local government fleets to electric with charging infrastructure.
parent_statement: Set the stage for vehicle electrification through a priority action to encourage electric vehicle adoption and develop charging infrastructure.
verbatim_text:
## PRIORITY ACTION

T.5 Encourage Electric Vehicle Adoption and the Development of Charging Infrastructure

## OBJECTIVE

Increase passenger vehicle travel using electric vehicles to 5% by 2025; 30% by 2035; and 50% by 2050.

Increase freight vehicle travel using electric vehicles to 3% by 2025; 20% by 2035; and 50% by 2050.

To lead by example, Memphis and Shelby County government should commit to transitioning their fleets to electric and begin building necessary charging infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Contains explicit quantified adoption targets with timelines and specific implementation actions; presented as Priority Action T.5.
mechanism_description: The policy encourages EV adoption and builds charging infrastructure, including committing to transition local government fleets to electric, which shifts travel from combustion vehicles to electric vehicles and reduces transportation emissions.

section_header: ## Action Area 1: Reduce Waste and Move Toward a ZeroWaste Future
policy_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Action Area 1: Reduce Waste and Move Toward a ZeroWaste Future

- ... Priority Action W.1 Divert Greater Amounts of Organic Waste from Landfills
- ... Priority Action W.2 Reduce the Overall Amount of Waste Generated
- ... Priority Action W.3 Divert Greater Amounts of Inorganic Materials from Landfills
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions listed; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: The policy prioritizes diverting organic waste and inorganic materials from landfills and reducing overall waste generation to avoid landfill emissions, including methane from decomposing organics.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.1 Divert Greater Amounts of Organic Waste from Landfills
policy_statement: By 2035, cut the portion of yard and wood waste in municipal solid waste destined for landfills in half relative to 2016, including implementing municipal curbside composting for yard waste and advocating for updated state landfill regulations on organic waste.
parent_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.1 Divert Greater Amounts of Organic Waste from Landfills

Objective: By 2035, cut the portion of yard and wood waste in municipal solid waste destined for landfills  in half relative to 2016.

Implement a municipal curbside composting program for yard waste that includes fee changes to incentivize composting.

Advocate for updated state-level landfill regulations that discourage and/or phase out disposal of organic waste in landfills.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline and specific program/policy actions; listed as Priority Action W.1.
mechanism_description: Implementing curbside composting and promoting landfill rules that discourage disposing organic waste in landfills shifts yard/wood waste to composting, reducing methane from landfilled organics and recovering nutrients.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.2 Reduce the Overall Amount of Waste Generated
policy_statement: By 2030, reduce landfill disposal from commercial/institutional/industrial sectors by achieving a 20% reduction in the share of paper/cardboard and a 10% reduction in the share of food waste in the municipal solid waste stream, including considering regulations requiring private waste haulers to offer recycling and food-waste composting services.
parent_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.2 Reduce the Overall Amount of Waste Generated

Objective: By 2030, achieve a 20% reduction in the share of paper and cardboard in the municipal solid waste stream from commercial, institutional, and industrial (cii) sectors sent to landfills. By 2030, achieve a 10% reduction in the share of food waste in the municipal solid waste stream sent to landfills from cii sectors.

Consider new regulations that require private waste hauling companies to offer recycling and food waste composting services.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified waste reduction targets with date and a regulatory measure; listed as Priority Action W.2.
mechanism_description: The policy aims to reduce paper/cardboard and food waste sent to landfills, including considering regulations that require haulers to offer recycling and food-waste composting services, which increases waste diversion and avoids landfill methane emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.3 Divert Greater Amounts of Inorganic Materials from Landfills
policy_statement: Increase diversion of inorganic materials (construction and demolition waste and plastics) from municipal solid waste landfills, including revising the building code so permitting requires reduction, reuse, and recycling of materials and advocating for state policies on single-use plastics and recycling/reuse incentives.
parent_statement: Reduce waste and move toward a zero-waste future through priority actions focused on organic waste diversion, overall waste reduction, and inorganic materials diversion.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.3 Divert Greater Amounts of Inorganic Materials from Landfills

Objective: Increase the diversion rate of inorganic materials, namely construction and demolition waste as well as plastics, from disposal in municipal solid waste landfills

Reduce construction and demolition waste by revising the building code to require the reduction, reuse, and recycling of materials.
extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory change (building code revision) and defined objective; presented as Priority Action W.3.
mechanism_description: The policy increases diversion of construction and demolition waste and plastics from landfills (including a building code change that requires reduction, reuse, and recycling), which reduces landfill disposal and avoids methane emissions.

section_header: ## Action  Area  2: Promote  a  Cultural  Shift in  Our Community's Approach to Waste
policy_statement: Promote a cultural shift in the community’s approach to waste through priority actions on tire management/collection and waste education/outreach.
verbatim_text:
## Action  Area  2: Promote  a  Cultural  Shift in  Our Community's Approach to Waste

- ... Priority Action W.4 Improve Tire Management and Collection Practices
- ... Priority Action W.5 Expand Education and Outreach Efforts to Encourage Behavior Change
extraction_rationale: Named action area with multiple priority actions listed; umbrella program.
mechanism_description: Improve tire management and expand waste education and outreach to encourage behavior change that increases waste diversion and reduces landfill methane emissions.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.4 Improve Tire Management and Collection Practices
policy_statement: Increase the use of recycled tire materials in building projects and reduce improper tire disposal through targeted programs, including reworking pickup practices, incentivizing recycling, and considering more stringent tracking requirements for tire handling/disposal companies.
parent_statement: Promote a cultural shift in the community’s approach to waste through priority actions on tire management/collection and waste education/outreach.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.4 Improve Tire Management and Collection Practices

Objective: Increase the use of recycled tire materials in building projects and reduce improper tire disposal through targeted programs

Reduce improper tire disposal through reworking current pickup practices and incentivizing recycling programs.

Consider implementation of more stringent and traceable tracking requirements for tire handling and disposal companies.
extraction_rationale: Clearly stated objective and concrete measures; presented as Priority Action W.4.
mechanism_description: The action reworks tire pickup practices, incentivizes tire recycling, and considers stronger tracking for tire handlers to reduce improper disposal and increase diversion of tires into recycling and reuse.

section_header: ## Priority Action W.5 Expand Education and Outreach Efforts to Encourage Behavior Change
policy_statement: Launch waste outreach and education campaigns to inform and support residents about waste management (programs available and proper disposal), including conducting needs assessments in 2020 and monitoring and reassessing campaigns.
parent_statement: Promote a cultural shift in the community’s approach to waste through priority actions on tire management/collection and waste education/outreach.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.5 Expand Education and Outreach Efforts to Encourage Behavior Change

Objective: Launch outreach and education campaigns to inform and support residents about waste management in their community, including programs they can take part in and information regarding how to properly dispose of waste.

Conduct informal needs assessments with area stakeholders to determine marketing or social behavior change campaigns most needed in the region in 2020.
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to launch campaigns plus a dated implementation step (2020 needs assessment); presented as Priority Action W.5.
mechanism_description: Launch outreach and education campaigns (informed by needs assessments) to encourage resident behavior change on proper waste disposal and participation in waste programs, increasing diversion and reducing landfill impacts.

section_header: Priority Action W.6 Enhance Methane Recovery and Landfill Gas Destruction
policy_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text:
## Priority Action W.6 Enhance Methane Recovery and Landfill Gas Destruction

Objective: Extend the use of biogas controls to Class III and Class IV landfills. Improve the biogas capture rate at Class I landfills from 75% to 85% by 2020, and promote greater methane recovery over biogas flaring.
extraction_rationale: This is a named priority action with explicit targets (85% capture by 2020; controls/installation timelines) and multiple implementation steps listed below it, supporting classification as a parent policy.
mechanism_description: The action extends biogas controls and improves landfill biogas capture, increasing methane recovery (instead of flaring) to reduce methane emissions from landfills.

policy_statement: Starting in 2020, DPD staff will assess Class I landfill biogas capture performance versus the 75% national average and, if feasible, launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve an 85% landfill biogas capture rate by 2020.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Starting in 2020, Division of Planning and Development (DPD) staff aim to inquire with Class I landfill operator(s) how their biogas capture rate compares with the national average rate of 75%. Provided that incremental capture rates are feasible, DPD staff will coordinate the conversations to launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve a landfill biogas capture rate of 85% by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation commitment with a start date (2020) and a quantified target (85% capture rate by 2020) tied to the W.6 action.
mechanism_description: DPD staff plan to assess landfill biogas capture performance and, if feasible, implement and monitor improvements to increase capture, reducing methane emissions from the landfill.

policy_statement: Starting in 2020, City of Memphis Public Works and DPD will initiate discussions to install biogas controls at Class III and IV landfills, targeting completion by 2035.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Starting in 2020, City of Memphis Public Works and DPD staff will initiate the conversations surrounding installation of controls at Class III and IV landfills with targeted completion by 2035.
extraction_rationale: Specific action (installation of controls) with responsible parties and a target completion year (2035), presented as an implementation step under W.6.
mechanism_description: City staff will initiate conversations about installing controls at landfills, which would reduce methane emissions from landfill gas.

policy_statement: The City/partners will implement and support new legislation to lower the threshold for mandatory installation of biogas controls.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Implement and support new legislation that lowers the threshold for mandatory installation of biogas controls, which can be used as a complementary implementation mechanism.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative/regulatory mechanism is a binding policy instrument and is listed as an implementation step under W.6.
mechanism_description: New legislation lowers the threshold for mandatory installation of biogas controls, leading to wider use of biogas capture/control and reduced methane emissions.

policy_statement: WWTP operators will plan to install methane recovery systems in all Shelby County facilities by the end of 2035, with feasibility evaluation beginning in 2020 and an implementation and monitoring plan launched if feasible.
parent_statement: Priority Action W.6 will enhance methane recovery and landfill gas destruction by extending biogas controls to Class III/IV landfills, improving Class I landfill biogas capture from 75% to 85% by 2020, and installing methane recovery systems in all Shelby County wastewater treatment plants by 2035 while promoting methane recovery over flaring.
verbatim_text: Wastewater treatment plant (WWTP) operators can plan to install methane recovery systems in all facilities within Shelby County by the end of 2035 with support from DPD and City government personnel. Starting in 2020, staff can evaluate whether methane recovery or biogas flaring is feasible at uncontrolled WWTPs. Provided that installation of controls is feasible, launch an implementation and monitoring plan to achieve this goal.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure deployment commitment with a deadline (end of 2035) and near-term action (starting in 2020 feasibility evaluation), framed as implementation under W.6.
mechanism_description: WWTP operators plan to evaluate and, if feasible, install methane recovery systems (or biogas flaring) at wastewater facilities to cut methane released from treatment processes.

policy_statement: Adopt local government operations (City of Memphis and Shelby County) GHG reduction targets of 20% (buildings) and 15% (fleet) by 2020; 55% (buildings) and 45% (fleet) by 2035; and 80% for both buildings and fleet by 2050.
