mechanism_description: It sets a target to reduce per-person landfill waste, increasing diversion from landfill and avoiding methane emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Reduce water consumption per person 30% by 2030.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: - Reduce water consumption per person 30% by 2030
extraction_rationale: Quantified per-capita water reduction target with deadline.
mechanism_description: The policy sets a goal to reduce per-person water consumption, leading to lower per-capita water use.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Create a community-wide food rescue plan in collaboration with community-based organizations, businesses, and farmers.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: -   Create a community-wide food rescue plan in collaboration with community-based organizations, businesses, and farmers
extraction_rationale: Specific plan commitment to reduce food waste (a methane/emissions source).
mechanism_description: Create a community-wide food rescue plan so edible surplus food is recovered and redirected from disposal, reducing organic waste sent to landfill and avoiding methane emissions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Complete a Countywide solid waste characterization study and complete regular updates.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: -   Complete a Countywide solid waste characterization study and complete regular updates
extraction_rationale: Concrete data/assessment action with ongoing updates to support waste reduction policy.
mechanism_description: The county completes and regularly updates a solid waste characterization study to improve data for planning and targeting waste-related climate actions.

section_header: APPROACH 7: REDUCE WASTE AND WATER USE
policy_statement: Require waste reduction plans for all large events.
parent_statement: Approach 7: Reduce waste and water use.
verbatim_text: -   Require waste reduction plans for all large events
extraction_rationale: Regulatory requirement ('Require') applying to large events.
mechanism_description: Require waste reduction plans for large events to reduce waste sent to landfill and avoid landfill methane emissions.

section_header: LETTER FROM MAYOR DANIELLA LEVINE CAVA
policy_statement: Implement the Climate Action Strategy (CAS) to cut Miami-Dade communitywide greenhouse gas emissions 50% by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
verbatim_text: Officially launched on Oct. 27, 2021, this plan describes work taking place on multiple fronts to cut MiamiDade's greenhouse gas emissions 50 percent by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified emissions-reduction targets with deadlines (50% by 2030; net zero by 2050) tied to an implemented strategy.
mechanism_description: The plan describes work across multiple fronts to cut communitywide greenhouse gas emissions and pursue a net-zero goal over time.

policy_statement: Reduce California greenhouse gas emissions to 1990 levels by 2020 as required by AB 32.
verbatim_text: By requiring in law a reduction of greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions to 1990 levels by 2020, California set the stage for its transition to a sustainable, clean energy future.
extraction_rationale: Legally binding statewide emissions target with a clear deadline (2020) anchored in AB 32.
mechanism_description: A law requires reducing statewide greenhouse gas emissions to a defined baseline level, creating an economy-wide pathway that drives emissions reductions.

section_header: A. The Role of State Government:  Setting an Example
policy_statement: Set a California State government operations target to reduce GHG emissions by at least 30% below estimated business-as-usual emissions by 2020 (about 15% below current levels).
verbatim_text: Therefore, California State government has established a target of reducing its greenhouse gas emissions by a minimum of 30 percent below its estimated business-as-usual emissions by 2020 - approximately a 15 percent reduction from current levels.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with a deadline for state government operations.
mechanism_description: The state government establishes a target to reduce its operational greenhouse gas emissions, guiding actions that lower emissions over time.

policy_statement: Develop and adopt a Low Carbon Fuel Standard to reduce the carbon intensity of California transportation fuels by at least 10% by 2020, with regulation for Board consideration in March 2009.
verbatim_text: ARB is developing a Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS), which would reduce the carbon intensity of California's transportation fuels by at least ten percent by 2020... ARB identified the LCFS as a Discrete Early Action item, and is developing a regulation for Board consideration in March 2009.
extraction_rationale: Specific performance standard target (10% by 2020) and near-term regulatory milestone.
mechanism_description: ARB is developing a Low Carbon Fuel Standard regulation to lower the carbon intensity of transportation fuels, reducing lifecycle transportation fuel emissions.

policy_statement: Implement the Solar Hot Water and Efficiency Act incentive program with a goal of installing 200,000 solar water heating systems by 2017.
verbatim_text: The Solar Hot Water and Efficiency Act of 2007 (SHWEA) authorized a ten year, $250-million incentive program for solar water heaters with a goal of promoting the installation of 200,000 systems in California by 2017.
extraction_rationale: Funded incentive program with quantified deployment goal and date.
mechanism_description: An incentive program supports installing solar water heaters, reducing conventional water-heating energy use and related emissions.

policy_statement: Set a target of an additional 4,000 MW of installed combined heat and power (CHP) capacity by 2020 (displacing about 30,000 GWh).
verbatim_text: This measure sets a target of an additional 4,000 MW of installed CHP capacity by 2020, enough to displace approximately 30,000 GWh of demand from other power generation sources.
extraction_rationale: Quantified capacity target with a 2020 deadline for CHP deployment.
mechanism_description: It sets a target to expand combined heat and power capacity, displacing demand from other power generation sources and reducing associated emissions.

policy_statement: Achieve a statewide Renewables Portfolio Standard of 33% renewable electricity by 2020.
verbatim_text: Based on Governor Schwarzenegger's call for a statewide 33 percent RPS, the Plan anticipates that California will have 33 percent of its electricity provided by renewable resources by 2020
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified renewables share target with date.
mechanism_description: The plan anticipates increasing the share of electricity supplied by renewable resources, displacing fossil generation and lowering power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Implement the Million Solar Roofs Program goal to install 3,000 MW of new solar capacity by 2017 under existing solar programs.
verbatim_text: California has set a goal to install 3,000 megawatts (MW) of new solar capacity by 2017... Created under Senate Bill 1 (Murray, Chapter 132, Statutes of 2006), the Million Solar Roofs Program includes CPUC's California Solar Initiative and CEC's New Solar Homes Partnership
extraction_rationale: Specific capacity goal with year and named program.
mechanism_description: The program sets a goal to add new solar capacity, increasing solar PV generation that displaces fossil-fueled electricity generation.

policy_statement: The Government of Armenia approved the Nationally Determined Contributions (2021-2030) with an unconditional target to reduce emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030.
verbatim_text: On  April  22,  2021  the  Government  of Armenia  approved  Armenia's  Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) for a tenyear  implementation  period  (2021-2030), setting  the  new  unconditional  mitigation target of 40% reduction below 1990 emissions levels, to be achieved in 2030.
extraction_rationale: Contains a quantified national mitigation target with a deadline (40% below 1990 by 2030) approved by government.
mechanism_description: The government approved an NDC that sets an unconditional national mitigation target, creating an economy-wide pathway to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to construct about 1000 MW of solar power plants (including autonomous ones) by 2030 to support reaching solar generation targets.
verbatim_text: According  to  'The  Republic  of  Armenia Energy Sector Development Strategic Program (till 2040)", 2021, it is planned to increase the share of solar energy generation  in  total  to  at  least  15%  or  1.8 billion  kWh  by  2030.  To  this  end,  solar power  plants  with  the  capacity  of  about 1000 MW, including autonomous ones, are planned to be constructed.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure plan with quantified capacity (~1000 MW) and timeline (by 2030).
mechanism_description: The plan is to construct solar power plants to increase solar electricity generation, which displaces fossil-fueled generation and lowers power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to commission the Masrik-1 utility-scale solar PV plant (55 MW peak) by 2022 and hold tenders for additional solar PV plants totaling about 520 MW (including two 200 MW plants).
verbatim_text: It is planned by 2022 to commission Masrik-1 utility scale solar PV with a peak capacity  of  55  MW.  Further,  new  tenders will be held for the construction of another 7 solar PVs with a total installed capacity of about 520 MW, whereby the capacity of two of these plants will be 200 MW each.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned actions with clear capacities and a commissioning date plus procurement/tenders.
mechanism_description: The government plans to commission and tender new utility-scale solar PV plants, increasing renewable generation and displacing fossil electricity generation.

policy_statement: Armenia aims to reduce electricity distribution losses to 6.4% in 2027 under Electric Networks of Armenia's 2016-2027 investment program.
verbatim_text: Reduction of distribution losses to 6.4% in 2027 (ENA 2016-2027 Investment program).
extraction_rationale: Quantified performance target (losses to 6.4%) with a deadline under an investment program.
mechanism_description: An investment program to reduce electricity distribution losses lowers the amount of electricity that must be generated to deliver the same end-use consumption, reducing associated power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Armenia plans to reach total small hydropower plant (SHPP) capacity of 430 MW by 2023, supported by the Hydro Energy Development Concept and 15-year Power Purchase Agreements under the Energy Law.
verbatim_text:
In 2016 the GoA approved the Hydro Energy Development Concept of the RA, which envisages commissioning of new SHPPs. The Energy Law supports the development of SHPPs through the Power Purchasing Agreement for the 15- year period.

Quantitative goal: To reach the total capacity of SHPPs of 430 MWby 2023.
extraction_rationale: Clear policy support instrument (15-year PPA) and quantified capacity target with timeline.
mechanism_description: Armenia supports commissioning new small hydropower plants and 15-year power purchasing agreements to expand renewable electricity supply and displace fossil generation.

policy_statement: Under the Strategy and Action Plan, Armenia will construct medium utility-scale solar PV power plants totaling 175 MW by end-2024, including Masrik-1 (55 MW) in 2022 and additional 120 MW by end-2024.
verbatim_text: According to the Strategy and Action Plan, medium utility-scale solar power plants with total capa- city of 175 MWwill be constructed by the end of 2024. It includes: • PV 'Masrik-1', with a peak capa- city of 55 MW(about 110 GWh of annually produced electricity) in 2022; • Additional solar PVs with total capacity of 120 MWwill be cons- tructed by the end of 2024 (about 192 GWh of annually produced electricity).
extraction_rationale: Specific construction commitment with quantified capacities and deadlines tied to named Strategy/Action Plan.
mechanism_description: Armenia will construct medium utility-scale solar PV power plants to generate electricity, increasing renewable supply and displacing fossil-based power generation.

policy_statement: Under the Strategy and Action Plan, Armenia envisages construction of two 200 MW solar PV plants (400 MW total) in 2023 and 2024, including the 200 MW 'Ayg-1' plant.
verbatim_text:
According to the Strategy and Action Plan construction of 2 Solar PVs with the capacity of 200MW each is envisaged in 2023 and 2024.

Request for Qualification was announced in 2020 seeking private developers for a pro- ject to design, finance, build, own, and operate the first of considered 2 plants, which will be grid-connected 200MW solar PV power plant called 'Ayg-1'.
extraction_rationale: Planned utility-scale build-out with quantified capacities, years, and a named project with procurement step.
mechanism_description: Armenia plans construction of grid-connected solar PV plants to add renewable generation that displaces fossil electricity generation.

policy_statement: Armenia will support installation of solar PV plants up to 5 MW through tariff policy and power purchase agreements, targeting 315 MW of such PV capacity by 2030.
verbatim_text:
According to the Strategy and Action Plan, the construction of solar PV power plants with an installed capacity of up to5MW (inclusively) will be supported through tariff policy and power purchase agreement.

The quantitative goal is to support the installation of the total of 315 MWof solar PV power plants with an installed capacity of up to5MW (inclusively) by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Explicit support mechanism (tariff policy and PPA) plus a quantified capacity goal with deadline.
mechanism_description: The strategy supports building solar PV plants using tariff policy and power purchase agreements, increasing renewable electricity supply and displacing fossil-based generation.

policy_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Launched in 2017, the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan lays out 22 a comprehensive set of initiatives to increase energy efficiency potential in Bahrain. The Plan identifies 22 new initiatives across all sectors to achieve a reduction in energy use of 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over the period 2009-2013.
extraction_rationale: Named national plan with quantified target and multiple initiatives listed below (building codes, standards, district cooling, etc.).
mechanism_description: The National Energy Efficiency Action Plan sets out initiatives across sectors to increase energy efficiency, leading to reduced overall energy use.

policy_statement: Integrate energy efficiency objectives into building codes/regulations for design, construction, and maintenance of structures (NEEAP initiative).
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Twenty two main initiatives have been established to realize this target, including: - Building codes: This involves the integration of energy efficiency objectives into regulations governing the design, construction, and maintenance of structures.
extraction_rationale: A specific NEEAP initiative described as regulatory integration into building codes.
mechanism_description: Integrating energy efficiency objectives into building codes for the design, construction, and maintenance of structures reduces building energy use over time.

policy_statement: Introduce demand-side minimum efficiency standards for lighting, appliances, motor vehicles, and street lighting (NEEAP initiative).
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Twenty two main initiatives have been established to realize this target, including: - Demand-side efficiency standards: This involves new minimum standards for lighting, appliances, motor vehicles, and street lighting;
extraction_rationale: Concrete policy instrument (minimum standards) applied to specified end-uses.
mechanism_description: New minimum efficiency standards for lighting, appliances, motor vehicles, and street lighting improve end-use efficiency and reduce electricity and fuel use, lowering associated emissions.

policy_statement: Improve supply-side efficiency in electricity generation including transmission and distribution efficiency enhancements and installation of smart meters (NEEAP initiative).
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Twenty two main initiatives have been established to realize this target, including: - Supply-side efficiency standards: This focuses on electricity generation, including efficiency enhancements for the transmission and distribution of electricity, and the installation of smart meters;
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention package (T&D efficiency + smart meters) within a named plan.
mechanism_description: The initiative focuses on supply-side electricity efficiency improvements (generation and transmission/distribution) and installing smart meters to reduce losses and fuel use, lowering emissions from electricity supply.

policy_statement: Introduce district cooling using highly efficient industrial-grade equipment and insulated underground piping networks (NEEAP initiative).
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Twenty two main initiatives have been established to realize this target, including: - District cooling: This involves the introduction of highly efficient, industrial-grade equipment to produce space cooling for buildings through an insulated underground piping network;
extraction_rationale: Specific technology/infrastructure intervention clearly described.
mechanism_description: The initiative introduces an efficient district cooling network to provide building space cooling, reducing energy use for cooling and the emissions associated with that energy demand.

policy_statement: Retrofit government buildings and pursue other green building initiatives (NEEAP initiative).
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Twenty two main initiatives have been established to realize this target, including: - Green building initiatives: This involves retrofitting of government buildings; and other initiatives; and
extraction_rationale: Specific action (retrofitting government buildings) under NEEAP.
mechanism_description: The policy involves retrofitting government buildings through green building initiatives to reduce building energy use.

policy_statement: Reform power subsidies and transport-related subsidies to offset perverse incentives for wasteful consumption (NEEAP initiative).
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Twenty two main initiatives have been established to realize this target, including: - Subsidy reform: This involves reforms to both power subsidies and those that affect transport to offset perverse incentives for wasteful consumption.
extraction_rationale: Clear policy reform mechanism (subsidy reform) with purpose stated.
mechanism_description: Reforms power and transport-related subsidies to remove incentives for wasteful consumption, reducing energy and fuel use and associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Bahrain is gradually strengthening electricity tariffs as part of electricity subsidy reform, increasing residential tariffs for the first 3,000 kWh/month from US$0.008/kWh to US$0.08/kWh by 2019 and increasing industrial tariffs above 5,000 kWh/month from US$0.04/kWh to US$0.08/kWh by 2019.
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Electricity  Subsidy  Reform:  This  initiative  aims  to gradually increase electricity tariffs to reflect the actual costs of  power generation and promote more efficient electricity  consumption. ... For the first 3,000 kWh per month, the residential rate will be gradually increased from  US$  0.008/kWh  to  US$  0.08/kWh  by  2019. For  consumption  above  5,000  kWh  per  month,  the industrial  rate  will  be  gradually  increased  from  US$ 0.04/kWh  to  US$  0.08/kWh  by  2019.
extraction_rationale: Specific tariff reform initiative with quantified tariff changes and deadlines; listed as an initiative within NEEAP cross-sectoral actions.
mechanism_description: The initiative gradually increases electricity tariffs to reflect generation costs, which promotes more efficient electricity consumption and reduces electricity demand.

policy_statement: Bahrain is reforming transport fuel subsidies by increasing retail gasoline prices (regular 91 octane from US$0.21 to US$0.30 and premium 95 octane from US$0.26 to US$0.42) to target a 4% reduction in annual fuel use in the light-duty vehicle fleet.
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Transport Subsidy Reform: This  initiative  aims  for  a  4% reduction in annual fuel use in the light duty vehicle fleet. ... The price of regular grade gasoline (91 Octane) is  being  increased  by  42%,  from  US$  0.21  to US$ 0.30,  while  the  price  of   premium  grade  gasoline (95  Octane)  is  being  increased  by  60%,  from US$ 0.26 to US$ 0.42.
extraction_rationale: Quantified policy target and explicit price reform mechanism; clearly an implemented/underway policy initiative under NEEAP.
mechanism_description: The initiative increases retail gasoline prices to reduce light-duty vehicle fuel use, lowering transport emissions.

policy_statement: Bahrain implemented fuel economy labels for light-duty vehicles on all new vehicles of model year 2018 to increase consumer awareness as part of vehicle efficiency standards and labeling.
parent_statement: Implement the National Energy Efficiency Action Plan (NEEAP) launched in 2017 with 22 initiatives across sectors to reduce energy use by 6% by 2025 relative to average energy use over 2009-2013.
verbatim_text: Vehicle Efficiency Standards & Labeling: This initiative aims to improve efficiency in the transport sector by  setting  minimum  fuel  economy  standards  for light  duty  vehicles;  and  by  making  vehicle  fuel economy an explicit part of  the purchase decision by ensuring that labels are appropriately displayed thereby increasing consumer awareness. The fuel  economy  labels  for  light  duty  vehicles  were implemented on all new vehicles of model 2018.
extraction_rationale: Concrete regulatory/information policy action with an explicit implementation milestone (model year 2018 labels).
mechanism_description: The initiative implements fuel economy labels on new light-duty vehicles to increase consumer awareness and make fuel economy part of purchase decisions, supporting uptake of more efficient vehicles and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Implement the National Renewable Energy Action Plan (NREAP) with targets of 5% renewable energy (255 MW installed capacity) by 2025 and 10% (710 MW) by 2035, using solar, wind, and waste-to-energy with public and private investment.
verbatim_text: NREAP has two primary targets: having 5% of energy (255 MW of installed capacity) from renewable sources by 2025, and 10% (710 MW of installed capacity) by 2035 (SEU, 2017a). The plan seeks to realize these targets by a mixture of solar, wind, and waste-to-energy technologies and involves a mixture of public and private investment.
extraction_rationale: Named national plan with quantified targets and a list of policy instruments established to realize targets.
mechanism_description: The plan sets targets to increase electricity from renewable sources (solar, wind, and waste-to-energy), supported by public and private investment, to displace fossil generation and reduce power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Adopt net metering to enable consumers to generate on-site, grid-connected renewable energy (e.g., rooftop solar panels).
parent_statement: Implement the National Renewable Energy Action Plan (NREAP) with targets of 5% renewable energy (255 MW installed capacity) by 2025 and 10% (710 MW) by 2035, using solar, wind, and waste-to-energy with public and private investment.
verbatim_text: Three main policies have been established to realize the targets, as follows: - Net metering: This will enable consumers to generate on-site, grid-connected, renewable energy, for example via the installation of solar panels on roofs;
extraction_rationale: Specific policy instrument explicitly named and described under NREAP.
mechanism_description: Net metering enables consumers to install on-site, grid-connected solar panels, increasing renewable electricity generation that displaces fossil-based power.

policy_statement: Use feed-in tariffs via a tender-based mechanism with long-term power purchase agreements to support renewable energy.
parent_statement: Implement the National Renewable Energy Action Plan (NREAP) with targets of 5% renewable energy (255 MW installed capacity) by 2025 and 10% (710 MW) by 2035, using solar, wind, and waste-to-energy with public and private investment.
verbatim_text: Three main policies have been established to realize the targets, as follows: - Feed-in tariffs: This is a tender-based mechanism to support renewable energy characterized by long-term power purchase agreements;
extraction_rationale: Concrete market/policy mechanism described with implementation approach.
mechanism_description: Using feed-in tariffs delivered through tenders and long-term power purchase agreements supports renewable energy projects, increasing renewable generation that displaces fossil-based electricity.

policy_statement: Apply renewable energy mandates for new buildings (NREAP policy instrument).
parent_statement: Implement the National Renewable Energy Action Plan (NREAP) with targets of 5% renewable energy (255 MW installed capacity) by 2025 and 10% (710 MW) by 2035, using solar, wind, and waste-to-energy with public and private investment.
verbatim_text: Three main policies have been established to realize the targets, as follows: - Mandates: This refers to renewable energy mandates for new buildings.
extraction_rationale: Explicit mandate requirement tied to new buildings under NREAP.
mechanism_description: The policy mandates renewable energy in new buildings, leading to more renewable energy use that displaces fossil-based generation and lowers emissions.

policy_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text:
## Adaptation interventions

Two strategic interventions were considered to ensure a stable equilibrium between future water supply and demand under climate change.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella intervention package explicitly introducing multiple specific sub-policies described in subsequent bullets.
mechanism_description: The plan considers reducing per-capita water demand and reducing leakage to help keep future municipal water supply and demand in stable balance under climate change.

policy_statement: Reduce per capita municipal water demand by 33% from 183 to 122 m3/person/year by 2035, with a policy start year of 2016, using demand-side management policies (awareness, strengthened block tariffs, and new legislation).
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: The first intervention aims to achieve a 33% reduction in per capita municipal water demand, from the current level of  183 to 122 m 3 per person per year by 2035, with a policy start year of  2016. To achieve this target, three complementary  demand  side  management  policies would be advanced, as outlined in the bullets below.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target, timeframe, and implementation approach clearly specified as a planned intervention.
mechanism_description: The policy advances demand-side management (e.g., awareness efforts, strengthened block tariffs, and legislation) to reduce per-capita municipal water demand.

policy_statement: Implement awareness raising campaigns and government incentives to promote municipal water efficiency and conservation.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Awareness raising : This would involve social campaigns and government incentives to emphasize the importance of water efficiency and conservation among the general population.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention mechanism (social campaigns and incentives) presented as part of the demand-side package.
mechanism_description: Awareness campaigns and government incentives emphasize water efficiency and conservation, encouraging reduced water use per person.

policy_statement: Strengthen block water tariffs for non-household users under EWA's tariff schedule effective 1 March 2016.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Strengthened  block  tariffs: Up  until  recently  water tariffs were heavily subsidized and did not recover  minimum  production  and  distribution costs.  Effective  1  March  2016,  water  tariffs  for commercial,  industrial,  touristic,  and  other  nonhousehold users are being gradually strengthened in accordance with the new tariff schedule established by EWA.
extraction_rationale: Explicit tariff reform with an effective date and defined target groups; constitutes a policy lever.
mechanism_description: It gradually strengthens block water tariffs for non-household users, encouraging lower water use and improved efficiency.

policy_statement: Develop new legislation to promote water use efficiency and conservation by strengthening building codes and promoting water-saving devices (with retrofits for existing buildings).
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § New legislation: To promote water use efficiency and conservation, new legislation would be needed to strengthen existing building codes, promote water saving  devices  such  as  low-flow  shower  heads, faucet  aerators,  automatic  shut-off   nozzles  and others for new buildings while existing buildings would  be  retrofitted.
extraction_rationale: Concrete proposed legislative action plus enumerated device standards/retrofit scope.
mechanism_description: New legislation would strengthen building codes and promote water-saving devices and retrofits in buildings to improve water efficiency and reduce water use.

policy_statement: Reduce leakage in the municipal water distribution network to achieve a 22% reduction in per capita municipal water demand from 183 to 142 m3/person/year by 2035, with a policy start year of 2016 (implemented starting 2020 per phase-in schedule).
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: The  second  intervention  aims  to  reduce  leakage  in the  water  distribution  network. ... Through investments in detection and repair, this second intervention aims to achieve a 22% reduction in per capita municipal water demand, from the current level of 183 to 142 m 3 per person per year  by  2035,  with  a  policy  start  year  of 2016.
extraction_rationale: Quantified leakage-related demand reduction target and deadline; framed as an intervention with investments.
mechanism_description: The policy invests in leak detection and repair in the water distribution network to reduce leakage and thereby reduce per-capita municipal water demand.

policy_statement: Strengthen EWA's leak detection programme to conduct more frequent surveys and expand the use of leak detection technologies.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Leak detection: This would involve the strengthening of  the  leak  detection  programme  within  EWA's Leakage Detection and Control Group to perform more frequent leak detection surveys and expand the  use  of   leak  detection  technologies  (e.g.,  leak noise correlators, thermography free-floating inline  leak  detectors,  etc).
extraction_rationale: Specific programmatic action to strengthen leak detection with examples of technologies.
mechanism_description: Strengthening leak detection surveys and technologies helps find and fix leaks, reducing water losses and the energy used to treat and pump wasted water.

policy_statement: Implement leak control measures including Active Leakage Control, Pressure Management, Network Rehabilitation, and improved speed and quality of repair.
parent_statement: Bahrain considered two strategic adaptation interventions for the municipal water sector to stabilize future water supply-demand equilibrium under climate change (per-capita demand reduction and leakage reduction).
verbatim_text: § Leak control: This  would  involve  several  available currently  technologies  including  Active  Leakage Control, Pressure Management, Networks Rehabilitations  and  Speed  &  Quality  of   Repair (Lambert, 2003).
extraction_rationale: Clearly specified set of operational measures to control leakage; part of the leakage reduction intervention.
mechanism_description: Implementing leak detection, pressure management, network rehabilitation, and faster higher-quality repairs reduces leakage in the water distribution network and lowers overall water losses.

section_header: 3.3 Commitments in the application of the social pillar for updating and implementing the NDC
policy_statement: Contribution in Just Transition and Sustainable Development N°1-2-3 (SP1-2-3).
verbatim_text:
“## 3.3 Commitments in the application of the social pillar for updating and implementing the NDC

## Contribution in Just Transition and Sustainable Development N°1-2-3 (SP1-2-3)

## CONTRIBUTION

SP1) Ensure the application of the criteria mentioned in Section 3.2 in the processes of update, elaboration and implementation of the NDC.

SP2) Establish a mechanism to measure, report and verify the application of the criteria mentioned in section 3.2.

SP3) Develop by 2021 a 'Strategy for Just Transition' that protects the rights of the most vulnerable in the process of decarbonizing the energy matrix, ensuring active participation of citizens in its design and implementation.”

policy_statement: Contribución en Mitigación N°1 (M1).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribución en Mitigación N°1 (M1)

## CONTRIBUTION

M1) Chile commits to a GHG emission budget not exceeding 1,100 MtCO2eq between 2020 and 2030, with a GHG emissions maximum (peak) by 2025, and a GHG emissions level of 95 MtCO2eq by 2030.

... Therefore, at the national level, in 2020 we will establish a public-private dialogue roundtable to define specific policy for the use of markets, taking into account clear guidelines that preserve environmental integrity, avoid double-counting and promote sustainable development.”
extraction_rationale: Contains quantified, time-bound economy-wide mitigation targets plus an implementation action (market policy dialogue).
mechanism_description: Chile commits to an economy-wide GHG emissions budget and peak/level targets, creating an overall pathway to reduce national emissions.

mechanism_description: Chile commits to an economy-wide GHG emissions budget and peak/decline milestones to drive an overall emissions reduction pathway.

“## Contribution to Mitigation Nº2 (M2)

## CONTRIBUTION

M2) Reduce total black carbon emissions by at least 25% by 2030, with respect to 2016 levels. This commitment will be implemented primarily through national policies focused on air quality. In addition, it will be monitored through permanent and periodic work to improve information available in the black carbon inventory.”

section_header: 5.2.1 Climate change policies, strategies and plans
policy_statement: Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Adaptation N°2 (A2)

## CONTRIBUTION

A2) The coordination of climate action on adaptation at national level will be strengthened through the national adaptation plan, and the adaptation plans for 11 priority sectors, incorporating lessons learned from the implementation of earlier plans. Those documents will be used as inputs for the Adaptation Communication that will be sent to the UNFCCC in 2022 at the latest.

## The plans will be developed according to the following schedule:

a) 2022: The National Adaptation Plan shall be updated and in the first stages of implementation.

b) 2022: The first Adaptation Plans on water resources, coastal areas and the mining sector shall be developed and in the first stages of implementation.

c) 2021-2028: The Adaptation Plans for sectors in Agriculture and Forestry ... shall be updated and implemented.”
extraction_rationale: Umbrella adaptation planning commitment with a detailed schedule of sub-actions.
mechanism_description: It strengthens national coordination of adaptation by updating and implementing national and sector adaptation plans, using them to guide adaptation actions and support adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4)

## CONTRIBUTION

b) Sustainable yield parameters guaranteeing that extractions do not exceed forest growth will be applied.”

mechanism_description: It will identify peatland and other wetland areas in a national inventory to improve information for climate-related planning and decision-making.

policy_statement: By 2030, develop standardized metrics to evaluate wetlands’ (especially peatlands) adaptation/mitigation capacity and implement actions to enhance co-benefits in five pilot sites in protected areas (I8).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration- LULUCF Forests N°4 (I4).
verbatim_text: “I8) By 2030, standardized metrics will be developed to evaluate the capacity of wetlands (especially peatlands) for climate change adaptation or mitigation, implementing actions to enhance these co-benefits in five pilot sites...”
extraction_rationale: Explicit metric development and pilot implementation commitment with numeric scope.
mechanism_description: The policy will develop standardized metrics to evaluate wetlands’ (especially peatlands) climate adaptation/mitigation capacity and implement pilot actions that enhance co-benefits like ecosystem resilience and carbon storage.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration -Crosscutting to ecosystems N°9 (I9).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration -Crosscutting to ecosystems N°9 (I9)

## CONTRIBUTION

I9) By 2021, a National Plan for the Restoration of Landscapes will be developed, which will consider restoration of 1,000,000 hectares of ecosystems, prioritizing those facing greatest social, economic and environmental vulnerabilities.”
extraction_rationale: Plan development with deadline and quantified restoration scale.
mechanism_description: Developing a national plan to restore landscapes supports ecosystem recovery, which can increase carbon sequestration and improve resilience in vulnerable areas.

policy_statement: By 2021, develop a National Plan for the Restoration of Landscapes considering restoration of 1,000,000 hectares, prioritizing the most vulnerable ecosystems (I9).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration -Crosscutting to ecosystems N°9 (I9).
verbatim_text: “I9) By 2021, a National Plan for the Restoration of Landscapes will be developed, which will consider restoration of 1,000,000 hectares of ecosystems...”
extraction_rationale: Quantified hectares and deadline make it a concrete commitment.
mechanism_description: The government will develop a national plan to guide landscape and ecosystem restoration, supporting ecosystem recovery and increased carbon storage.

policy_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Integration - Ocean N ° 11 (I11)

## CONTRIBUTION

I11) All marine protected areas of Chile created up to 2020 will have a management or administration plan under implementation, taking into account actions for adaptation to climate change.

The following climate actions will be specifically developed:

a) By 2025: 100% of marine protected areas created up to 2020 will hold management or administration plans including actions for adaptation to climate change.

... e) By 2030: Implementation of a methodology for the evaluation of management effectiveness of 100% management or administration plans...”
extraction_rationale: Umbrella commitment with multiple quantified sub-actions and deadlines for MPA management plans.
mechanism_description: It implements management or administration plans for marine protected areas that include climate change adaptation actions, improving the resilience of marine ecosystems and protected-area management to climate impacts.

policy_statement: Establish new protected areas in under-represented marine ecoregions and in coastal wetland ecosystems to supplement the marine network (I10).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “I10) New protected areas will be established in under-represented marine ecoregions... In addition, protected areas will be established in coastal ecosystems for wetlands...”
extraction_rationale: Concrete establishment of protected areas as a policy action.
mechanism_description: The policy will establish new protected areas in marine and coastal wetland ecosystems to conserve habitats and preserve stored carbon over time.

policy_statement: By 2030, protect at least 10% of under-represented marine ecoregions (Humboldt, Central Chile, Araucanía and Chiloe) via participatory, science-based marine spatial planning including climate-change criteria (I10a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2030, protect at least 10% of under-represented marine eco-regions (Humboldt, Central Chile, Araucanía and Chiloe), in the framework of a participatory marine spatial planning, based on science and holding criteria to deal with the effects of climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified protection target with deadline and implementation approach.
mechanism_description: Protecting under-represented marine ecoregions through participatory, science-based marine spatial planning that includes climate-change criteria helps preserve marine ecosystems and strengthen climate resilience.

policy_statement: By 2025, protect at least 20 coastal wetlands as new protected areas (I10b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2025, protect at least 20 coastal wetlands as new protected areas.”
extraction_rationale: Clear numeric target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Protecting coastal wetlands as new protected areas helps prevent ecosystem degradation and preserves existing carbon stocks and ecological functions.

policy_statement: By 2030, protect at least 10 additional coastal wetlands as protected areas (I10c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2030, protect at least 10 additional coastal wetlands as protected areas.”
extraction_rationale: Clear numeric target with deadline.
mechanism_description: Protecting additional coastal wetlands as protected areas helps conserve ecosystems and preserve stored carbon while supporting coastal resilience.

policy_statement: Ensure all marine protected areas created up to 2020 have management/administration plans under implementation that include climate change adaptation actions (I11).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “I11) All marine protected areas of Chile created up to 2020 will have a management or administration plan under implementation, taking into account actions for adaptation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete requirement for MPA plans and implementation.
mechanism_description: Implementing management or administration plans for marine protected areas that include climate change adaptation actions strengthens ecosystem resilience to climate impacts.

policy_statement: By 2030, ensure 100% of MPAs created between 2020 and 2025 have management/administration plans including climate change adaptation actions (I11c).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “c) By 2030: 100% of marine protected areas created between 2020 and 2025 will have management or administration plans including actions for adaptation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified planning target with deadline.
mechanism_description: The policy sets a target for marine protected areas to have management or administration plans that include climate change adaptation actions, strengthening adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: By 2025, implement management/administration plans for at least 40% of MPAs created before 2020 through monitoring, control, community involvement, and threat control programs (I11b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2025: Management or administration plans of at least 40% of the marine protected areas created before 2020 will be implemented at least through monitoring, control, community involvement and threat control programs.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified implementation share with specified program elements.
mechanism_description: It will implement management or administration plans for marine protected areas through monitoring, control, community involvement, and threat control programs to reduce ecosystem threats and preserve marine ecosystems and blue-carbon storage.

policy_statement: By 2030, implement a methodology to evaluate management effectiveness for 100% of MPA management/administration plans to track mitigation and adaptation targets (I11e).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “e) By 2030: Implementation of a methodology for the evaluation of management effectiveness of 100% management or administration plans of marine protected areas in order to know the progress of targets proposed in said plans for mitigation and adaptation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Specific methodological implementation with quantified scope and deadline.
mechanism_description: Implement a methodology to evaluate the effectiveness of marine protected area management plans so progress toward climate mitigation and adaptation targets can be tracked and used to support adaptation planning.

policy_statement: Assess co-benefits of ecosystems in marine protected areas for mitigation and adaptation and implement actions to strengthen these co-benefits (I12).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “I12) The co-benefits of different ecosystems in marine protected areas will be assessed with respect to climate change mitigation and adaptation. Actions will also be implemented to strengthen these co-benefits.”
extraction_rationale: Specific assessment and action commitment.
mechanism_description: The policy will assess mitigation and adaptation co-benefits of ecosystems in marine protected areas and implement actions that strengthen ecosystem services such as carbon storage and climate resilience.

policy_statement: By 2025, develop standardized metrics in three MPAs to evaluate their adaptation/mitigation capacities (I12a).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “a) By 2025: Three marine protected areas of Chile will have standardized metrics for the evaluation of their capacities for adaptation or mitigation to climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline.
mechanism_description: It establishes standardized metrics to evaluate marine protected areas’ climate adaptation or mitigation capacity, supporting better-informed adaptation planning and management.

policy_statement: By 2030, apply monitoring and verification metrics in at least five MPAs and strengthen co-benefits in their management plans (I12b).
parent_statement: Contribution to Integration - Ocean N°11 (I11).
verbatim_text: “b) By 2030: metrics for monitoring and verification of adaptation or mitigation capacities will be applied in at least five marine protected areas, while strengthening co-benefits in their management plans.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified application commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Applies monitoring and verification metrics in marine protected areas and strengthens co-benefits in their management plans to support tracking and improving adaptation or mitigation capacity.

section_header: 7.3 Contribution in capacity building and strengthening
policy_statement: Contribución en Medios de Implementación Creación y fortalecimiento de capacidades N°1 (IM1).
verbatim_text:
“## 7.3 Contribution in capacity building and strengthening

Contribución en Medios de Implementación Creación y fortalecimiento de capacidades N°1 (IM1)

## CONTRIBUTION

IM1) In 2020, Chile will develop 'Strategy for 'Capacity Development and Climate Empowerment' and will begin its implementation in 2021, with the aim of strengthening the sectoral, national and subnational capacities...”
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with development and implementation start dates and defined objective.
mechanism_description: Chile will develop and begin implementing a capacity development and climate empowerment strategy to strengthen sectoral and multi-level capacities for climate action.

section_header: 7.3 Contribution in capacity building and strengthening
policy_statement: Develop in 2020 a Capacity Development and Climate Empowerment Strategy and begin implementation in 2021 to strengthen sectoral, national and subnational capacities to meet mitigation and adaptation targets (IM1).
parent_statement: Contribución en Medios de Implementación Creación y fortalecimiento de capacidades N°1 (IM1).
verbatim_text: “IM1) In 2020, Chile will develop 'Strategy for 'Capacity Development and Climate Empowerment' and will begin its implementation in 2021, with the aim of strengthening the sectoral, national and subnational capacities... to achieve national mitigation and adaptation targets.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete strategy and start date for implementation.
mechanism_description: Chile will develop and implement a capacity development and climate empowerment strategy to strengthen sectoral, national, and subnational capacities to achieve mitigation and adaptation targets.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text:
“## 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer

Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2)

## CONTRIBUTION

IM2) In 2020, Chile will present its 'Climate Change Technology Transfer and Development Strategy' (EDTTCC) and it will initiate the implementation of this strategy...”
extraction_rationale: Named technology strategy with timing and multiple dated sub-actions.
mechanism_description: Chile will present and begin implementing a climate change technology transfer and development strategy to support the development and uptake of climate-related technologies, enabling emissions reductions.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: In 2020, present and initiate implementation of the Climate Change Technology Transfer and Development Strategy (EDTTCC) to support sustainable, resilient and carbon neutral development by 2050 (IM2).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “IM2) In 2020, Chile will present its 'Climate Change Technology Transfer and Development Strategy' (EDTTCC) and it will initiate the implementation of this strategy... necessary to achieve sustainable, resilient and carbon neutral development by 2050.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete presentation and implementation initiation commitment with year.
mechanism_description: Chile will present and start implementing a climate change technology transfer and development strategy to support sustainable, resilient, and carbon-neutral development.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: By 2020, establish governance and an institutional framework for EDTTCC implementation to support mitigation and adaptation (IM2a-2020).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “By 2020, Chile will have established a governance and institutional framework for the implementation of the EDTTCC and will support the national contribution in mitigation and adaptation...”
extraction_rationale: Time-bound governance/institutionality commitment.
mechanism_description: Chile will establish a governance and institutional framework to implement the EDTTCC, supporting national mitigation and adaptation action through stronger institutional capacity.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: By 2025, review and evaluate the EDTTCC based on the Long-Term Climate Strategy and current climate plans (IM2a-2025).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “By 2025, Chile will have reviewed and evaluated its EDTTCC, based on the Long-Term Climate Strategy and the current climate plans.”
extraction_rationale: Specific review/evaluation milestone with date.
mechanism_description: Chile will review and evaluate its EDTTCC using the Long-Term Climate Strategy and current climate plans to support improved climate governance and planning capacity.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: By 2030, evaluate and update the EDTTCC based on implementation monitoring (IM2a-2030-update).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “By 2030, Chile will have evaluated and updated its EDTTCC, from the evaluation and monitoring done during the implementation in climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Time-bound update commitment.
mechanism_description: Chile will evaluate and update the EDTTCC based on monitoring during climate change implementation, using the results to support future adaptation planning.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: Between 2020 and 2025, implement the first cycle of EDTTCC Technological Needs Action Plan for at least three prioritized areas/sectors (IM2b-2020-2025).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “Between 2020 and 2025, Chile shall implement the first cycle of the EDTTCC Technological Needs Action Plan for at least three prioritized areas and/or sectors.”
extraction_rationale: Quantified scope (at least three areas) and timeframe.
mechanism_description: Chile shall implement a technological needs action plan cycle across prioritized sectors to strengthen the institutional and technical capacity to deliver climate actions.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: By 2021, establish a Climate Technology Inventory System to be transferred, including local generation and adoption of global technologies (IM2b-2021-inventory).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “By 2021, Chile will have a Climate Technology Inventory System to be transferred that includes the local generation, such as also, the adoption of existing technologies worldwide.”
extraction_rationale: Specific system deliverable with deadline.
mechanism_description: Chile will develop a climate technology inventory system to support the transfer, local generation, and adoption of climate-related technologies.

section_header: 7.4 Contribution in terms of Technology development and transfer
policy_statement: By 2022, produce an inventory of Research Centers and R&D+i initiatives/projects related to mitigation/adaptation and capabilities to coordinate technology development and transfer (IM2b-2022).
parent_statement: Contribution in Implementation Measures Development and transfer of technologies Nº2 (IM2).
verbatim_text: “By 2022, Chile will have an inventory of Research Centers, R&D+i initiatives and projects related with mitigation and adaptation, and skills to coordinate Technology Development and Transfer for climate change.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete inventory deliverable with deadline.
mechanism_description: Chile will develop an inventory of climate mitigation/adaptation research centers and R&D+i initiatives to support coordination of technology development and transfer for climate action.

policy_statement: Contribution to Implementation Measures Climate Finance N°3 (IM3).
verbatim_text:
“## Contribution to Implementation Measures Climate Finance N°3 (IM3)

## CONTRIBUTION

IM3) Implementation of the EFCC will commence in 2020 and it will be updated every 5 years, with the first revision taking place in 2021.”
extraction_rationale: Clear commitment to implement and periodically update a named finance strategy with dates.
mechanism_description: The policy will commence implementation of a climate finance framework and update it regularly to support financing and delivery of climate measures.

policy_statement: Commence implementation of the Financial Strategy on Climate Change (EFCC) in 2020 and update it every 5 years, with the first revision in 2021 (IM3).
parent_statement: Contribution to Implementation Measures Climate Finance N°3 (IM3).
verbatim_text: “IM3) Implementation of the EFCC will commence in 2020 and it will be updated every 5 years, with the first revision taking place in 2021.”
extraction_rationale: Time-bound and cyclical update commitment.
mechanism_description: It begins implementation of a financial strategy on climate change and updates it periodically to help steer resources and strengthen capacity to deliver climate actions.

policy_statement: During 2020, establish a work plan for determining direct and indirect climate expenditure (subject to resource availability and Budget Law approval) (IM3d).
parent_statement: Contribution to Implementation Measures Climate Finance N°3 (IM3).
verbatim_text: “d) Establish a work plan during 2020 to advance in a public framework for the determination of direct and indirect climate expenditure... The implementation of this plan is subject to resources availability as per the corresponding approval to the Budget Law.”
extraction_rationale: Specific work plan commitment with timing; notes conditionality on resources.
mechanism_description: Establishing a work plan to determine direct and indirect climate expenditure supports transparent climate budget tracking, which in turn helps inform and improve climate planning and investment decisions.

section_header: CHILE'S NATIONALLY DETERMINED CONTRIBUTION (NDC) UPDATE 2020 (Foreword message by the President of Chile)
policy_statement: Chile will close all coal-fired power generation plants nationwide no later than 2040, with closures continuing through 2024 and beyond.
verbatim_text: “Their commitment is materializing itself in specific results, such as the closure of coal-fired power generation plants. The process has already started in 3 plants, will continue with 7 more by 2024, and will conclude no later than 2040 with the closure of all coal plants nationwide.”
extraction_rationale: Concrete phase-out action with a clear deadline (2040) and interim milestone (by 2024).
mechanism_description: Chile commits to closing coal-fired power generation plants, reducing coal-based electricity generation and lowering power-sector emissions by displacing fossil generation.

section_header: 3.1 Goal 1: Achieving Sustainable Economic Growth and Low-Emission Development in Various Sectors
policy_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text:
Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix

The Ministry of Electricity and Renewable Energy has taken several measures aimed at increasing the contribution of new and renewable energy, which is planned to reach 42% of the total electrical energy produced in 2035.
extraction_rationale: This objective sets a quantified target (42% renewables in electricity by 2035) and is followed by a list of implementation directions, making it an umbrella policy.
mechanism_description: The ministry plans to increase the share of renewable and alternative sources in the electricity mix, which displaces fossil-based generation and lowers power-sector emissions.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Deploy centralized renewable energy systems at large scale, including wind farms, concentrated solar power (CSP), solar PV plants, and bioenergy plants.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text:
The following directions contribute to achieving this objective:

-  Deployment of centralized systems of renewable energy on a large scale, such as wind farms, concentrated solar power (CSP), solar photovoltaic plants, as well as bioenergy plants
extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation direction listed under Objective (1.a).
mechanism_description: Deploying large-scale centralized renewable energy systems increases renewable generation and displaces fossil electricity generation, lowering power-sector emissions.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Promote small-scale decentralized renewable systems including waste-to-energy, bioenergy, rooftop PV, and solar water heaters.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Promoting small-scale decentralized systems including waste, bioenergy, rooftop photovoltaic solar cells and solar water heaters
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention to scale decentralized renewables.
mechanism_description: Promoting small-scale decentralized renewable and solar thermal systems increases low-carbon energy supply and displaces fossil-based generation and fuel use.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Develop technologies to accommodate renewable energy use, such as intelligent control systems.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Developing  new  technologies  to  accommodate  the  use  of  renewable  energy sources,  such  as  intelligent  control  systems
extraction_rationale: Specific technology development direction under Objective (1.a).
mechanism_description: Developing intelligent control systems and other new technologies helps accommodate renewable energy sources on the grid, improving integration and enabling displacement of fossil-based generation.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Optimize renewable energy use in off-grid applications.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: - ‡ Optimizing the use of renewable energy in off-grid applications
extraction_rationale: Actionable direction supporting energy transition.
mechanism_description: Optimizing renewable energy use in off-grid applications increases reliance on renewables and displaces fossil energy use, reducing associated greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Use energy storage technologies such as batteries, molten salt, and pumped storage.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Take advantage of energy storage technologies, such as batteries, molten salt and pumped storage
extraction_rationale: Specific technology deployment direction.
mechanism_description: Using energy storage technologies increases grid flexibility, helping integrate more renewable electricity and reduce reliance on fossil generation.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Include alternative energy sources such as green hydrogen, blue hydrogen, and nuclear energy in the energy mix.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Inclusion of new alternative energy sources, such as green hydrogen, blue hydrogen and nuclear energy
extraction_rationale: Explicit intervention naming technologies to be included.
mechanism_description: Including green hydrogen, blue hydrogen, and nuclear energy in the energy mix increases low-carbon energy supply and helps reduce emissions from energy generation.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Increase renewable electricity generation within industrial facilities and use solar thermal energy in industrial processes.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Increasing  the  use  of  renewable  energy  to  generate  electricity  within  industrial facilities  and  the  applications  of  solar  thermal  energy  in  industrial  processes
extraction_rationale: Sector-specific deployment direction (industry).
mechanism_description: It increases renewable electricity generation within industrial facilities and uses solar thermal energy in industrial processes to displace fossil energy and reduce industrial emissions.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Phase out coal and switch to low-carbon fuels.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Phasing out coal and switching to low carbon fuels
extraction_rationale: Clear fuel-switching commitment.
mechanism_description: Phasing out coal and switching to low-carbon fuels reduces emissions by lowering the carbon intensity of energy generation.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Stimulate increased production and use of biofuels as an alternative to fossil fuels.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Stimulating the increased production and use of biofuels as an alternative to fossil fuels
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention to expand biofuels.
mechanism_description: The policy stimulates greater biofuel production and use so biofuels substitute for fossil fuels, lowering emissions from fossil energy consumption.

section_header: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix
policy_statement: Pursue an equitable transition that accounts for the workforce in fossil fuel-based facilities.
parent_statement: Objective (1.a): Energy transition by increasing the share of all renewable and alternative energy sources in the energy mix.
verbatim_text: -  Equitable transformation that takes into account the workforce in fossil fuel-based facilities
extraction_rationale: Explicit equity/just-transition direction linked to energy transition.
mechanism_description: Pursues an equitable transition by taking into account workers in fossil fuel-based facilities, helping enable a shift away from fossil-based operations.

section_header: 3.2 Goal 2: Enhancing Adaptive Capacity and Resilience to Climate Change and Alleviating the Associated Negative Impacts
policy_statement: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts.
verbatim_text:
Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts

The following directions contribute to achieving this objective:
extraction_rationale: Objective includes a list of concrete DRR measures (early warning, monitoring, awareness, social protection).
mechanism_description: Implementing disaster risk reduction concepts helps reduce the impacts of climate-related hazards by improving preparedness, risk management, and resilience planning.

section_header: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts
policy_statement: Establish early warning systems for all sectors by developing a unified database, activating climate change information centers in ministries, and issuing sector-specific recommendations (e.g., for farmers).
parent_statement: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts.
verbatim_text: -  Establishing early warning systems for all sectors by developing a unified database. The database can be established by activating the role of climate change information centers  affiliated  with  various  ministries.  These  centers  have  information  and maps related to climate change and its impacts on the sector.
extraction_rationale: Specific DRR system-building action with institutional mechanism described.
mechanism_description: Developing a unified climate-impact database and activating climate change information centers strengthens early warning systems and improves decision-making for climate risk preparedness across sectors.

policy_statement: Activate and use interactive maps to study expected climate impacts on regions/projects and update interactive maps every five years as part of adaptation measures.
parent_statement: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts.
verbatim_text: -  Activating the project of creating and using interactive maps to study the expected impacts of climate change on regions and new projects and hence various sectors and updating interactive maps every five years as part of climate change adaptation measures, according to new data and methodologies
extraction_rationale: Contains a clear action and update frequency (every five years).
mechanism_description: The policy activates the creation, use, and periodic updating of interactive maps to assess expected climate change impacts using new data and methods, supporting adaptation planning across regions, projects, and sectors.

policy_statement: Ministry of Health and Population to study expected health impacts of climate change and develop and adopt a plan to address these impacts effectively.
parent_statement: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts.
verbatim_text: -  The Ministry of Health and Population will study the expected health impacts as a result of climate change, and then develop and adopt a plan to address these impacts  effectively.
extraction_rationale: Assigns responsibility to a ministry to produce and adopt a plan.
mechanism_description: The ministry will study expected climate-change health impacts and then develop and adopt a plan to address those impacts, strengthening capacity to manage climate-related health risks.

policy_statement: Use climate risk insurance as a financial mechanism to increase societies’ ability to overcome climate risks.
parent_statement: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts.
verbatim_text: -  Using  the  climate  risk  insurance  tool  as  one  of  the  financial  mechanisms  that increase  the  ability  of  societies  to  overcome  the  risks  of  climate  change.
extraction_rationale: Specific financial instrument proposed for adaptation resilience.
mechanism_description: Using climate risk insurance as a financial mechanism increases societies’ ability to overcome climate change risks by providing financial protection and supporting recovery after climate impacts.

policy_statement: Establish a coordination mechanism with civil society to implement pilot projects and coordinate among NGOs to disseminate applied models.
parent_statement: Objective (2.e): Implementation of disaster risk reduction concepts.
verbatim_text: -  Establishing  a  mechanism  for  coordination  with  civil  society  to  implement  pilot projects, and coordination between NGOs to benefit from experiences and applied models for dissemination in society
extraction_rationale: Concrete governance mechanism proposal involving civil society.
mechanism_description: Establishes a coordination mechanism with civil society and NGOs to run pilot projects and disseminate applied models, strengthening institutional capacity to implement climate-related actions.

section_header: 3.3 Goal 3: Enhancing Climate Change Action Governance
policy_statement: Objective (3.d): Enhancing institutional, procedural and legal arrangements such as the Monitoring, Reporting and Verification (MRV) system.
verbatim_text:
Objective  (3.d):  Enhancing  institutional,  procedural  and  legal  arrangements  such  as Monitoring,  Reporting  and  Verification  (MRV)  system

The following directions contribute to achieving this objective:
extraction_rationale: Objective is explicitly about MRV and is followed by specific actions (operationalize MRV, update CAPMAS data, create institutions).
mechanism_description: Enhancing institutional, procedural, and legal arrangements for an MRV system improves emissions monitoring and reporting capacity, supporting better-informed climate planning and oversight.

section_header: Objective (3.d): Enhancing institutional, procedural and legal arrangements such as Monitoring, Reporting and Verification (MRV) system
policy_statement: Operationalize the climate change MRV system approved by the NCCC and included in Egypt’s 2018 updated biennial report.
parent_statement: Objective (3.d): Enhancing institutional, procedural and legal arrangements such as the Monitoring, Reporting and Verification (MRV) system.
verbatim_text: -  Operationalizing the climate change MRV system, which was approved by the NCCC and included in the updated biennial report for Egypt (2018)
extraction_rationale: Clear action to operationalize an approved system.

section_header: PART 3-IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PARIS AGREEMENT — Implementation of the Paris Agreement
policy_statement: Minister must take all reasonable steps to prepare, communicate, maintain and update successive NDCs, pursue domestic mitigation measures, and communicate an NDC every 5 years with progression and highest possible ambition, including specified accounting and reporting information.
extraction_rationale: Creates a binding umbrella obligation with multiple lettered sub-requirements under section 7(2).
mechanism_description: The Minister must take reasonable steps to prepare, communicate, maintain, and periodically update NDCs and to pursue domestic mitigation measures to achieve those NDC objectives, supporting an ongoing pathway for emissions reductions.
