section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions
policy_statement: Minister may introduce and implement economy-wide mitigation regulations/measures including carbon pricing/ETS, emissions fees, fiscal incentives/levies, sink protection measures, sustainable building regulations, and awareness programmes.
verbatim_text:
The Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions with the purpose of limiting or reducing Fiji's greenhouse gas emissions across the economy...

Without limiting subsection (1), the Minister may- (a) introduce and implement a carbon pricing mechanism, including an emissions trading scheme; ...
extraction_rationale: Umbrella enabling provision with a lettered list of specific instruments, requiring parent/sub extraction.
mechanism_description: The Minister can introduce and implement economy-wide measures such as carbon pricing or an emissions trading scheme to limit or reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions

mechanism_description: Introduce and implement a carbon pricing mechanism, including an emissions trading scheme, to create a cost on emissions that incentivizes emissions reductions across the economy.

extraction_rationale: Specific economic instrument explicitly described.
mechanism_description: It prescribes fees and other charges on greenhouse gas emissions above a prescribed level, encouraging emitters to reduce emissions to avoid those costs.

section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions

parent_statement: Minister may introduce and implement economy-wide mitigation regulations/measures including carbon pricing/ETS, emissions fees, fiscal incentives/levies, sink protection measures, sustainable building regulations, and awareness programmes.

(ii) enhancing the conservation of natural sinks... and (iii) encouraging public and private investment in renewable energy and efficient cogeneration technologies, energy efficient infrastructure and zero-waste infrastructure and processes;
extraction_rationale: Concrete fiscal policy levers with specified purposes.
mechanism_description: Introduce and implement fiscal incentives and national levies to limit or reduce greenhouse gas emissions by encouraging investment in renewable energy, efficient cogeneration, energy-efficient and zero-waste infrastructure/processes, while enhancing conservation of natural sinks.

section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Minister has the power to introduce and implement regulations, measures and actions

extraction_rationale: Specific nature-based mitigation/sink protection actions listed in the Act.
mechanism_description: The policy enhances protection and restoration of forests, mangroves, reefs/coasts, and more sustainable agriculture to preserve existing carbon stocks and increase carbon sequestration in land and ocean sinks.

section_header: PART 2-DECLARATION OF CLIMATE EMERGENCY — Declaration of climate emergency
policy_statement: Fiji declares a climate emergency and commits to contribute to a net zero emissions global economy by achieving net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050.
verbatim_text:
This Act recognises and declares that Fiji and the Earth are facing a climate emergency.

A rapid and ambitious transformation to a net zero emissions global economy is necessary to address and mitigate the climate emergency, and Fiji will contribute to this transformation by achieving net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Binding legal declaration and an explicit, time-bound mitigation target (net zero by 2050).
mechanism_description: The Act declares a climate emergency and states Fiji will contribute to a net zero emissions global economy by achieving net zero greenhouse gas emissions, driving an economy-wide emissions reduction pathway.

section_header: PART 9-CLIMATE CHANGE MITIGATION — Long term emissions reduction target
policy_statement: Long-term emissions reduction target is net zero GHG emissions by 2050; Minister must promote achievement via carbon budgets and publish a 5-yearly emissions/progress statement with recommendations.
verbatim_text:
The long term emissions reduction target for Fiji is net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050.

Every 5 years the Minister must... publish a statement of- (a) Fiji's greenhouse gas emissions over the 5-year period... (b) an assessment of the progress...; and (c) recommendations on measures needed...
extraction_rationale: Quantified national target plus recurring mandatory reporting and recommendations.
mechanism_description: It sets a net-zero greenhouse gas target and set the Minister to publish five-year emissions and progress statements with recommendations, supporting an economy-wide pathway to reduce emissions.

policy_statement: AFOLU-related policies and measures supporting REDD+ and forest governance include a peatland license moratorium, mandatory sustainable forest management certification, strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) via landscape approach, environmental economic instruments, and establishment of the Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
verbatim_text: This successful endeavour has been supported by a number of PaMs including: (i) Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on termination of new license issuance on peatland, (ii) implementation of mandatory certification for sustainable forest management, (iii) Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs) through landscape approach aiming at securing food, water, and energy security based on sound ecosystem management, (iv) Government Regulation No. 46/2017 on Environmental Economic Instrument, and (v) Presidential Regulation No. 77/2018 on the establishment of Environmental Fund Management Agency (BPDLH).
extraction_rationale: Introduces a list of specific named policies/measures; functions as an umbrella set of PaMs.
mechanism_description: These policies terminate new peatland licenses, implement sustainable forest management certification, apply landscape-based strategic environmental assessments, use environmental economic instruments, and set up an environmental fund agency to support forest and peatland governance that protects ecosystems and preserves carbon stocks.

policy_statement: In forestry and other land uses, Indonesia identifies actions required to reduce emissions and achieve net sink (2050 under CPOS; 2030 under LCCP), including reducing deforestation/peat emissions, increasing sequestration via reduced degradation and enhanced regeneration, peatland restoration, forest restoration/sink enhancement, sustainable forest management, and maximizing use of unproductive lands.
verbatim_text: Efforts to significantly reduce the emission of this sector and turn it into net sink by 2050 under CPOS and by 2030 under LCCP depends primarily on the success of the following actions: (i) reducing emission from deforestation and peatland (peat decomposition and peat fire); (ii) increasing the capacity of natural forest in sequestering carbon (by reducing degradation and enhancing the regeneration); (iii) restoring peatland, (iv) implementing forest restoration (enrichment planting/sink enhancement), (v) adopting sustainable forest management practices; and (vi) maximizing the use of unproductive lands (idle lands) for the establishment of forest and agriculture plantations.
extraction_rationale: Clear umbrella list of required actions with CPOS/LCCP timelines.
mechanism_description: Indonesia plans to reduce emissions from deforestation and peat decomposition/fires and increase carbon sequestration through reduced forest degradation, enhanced regeneration, peatland and forest restoration, sustainable forest management, and using idle lands for forest/plantation establishment.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text:
To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia has issued a number of regulations. Some of key regulations are:

1. Government Regulation No.104/2015 on the changes of forest land use and function...
2. Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on Termination of New Permit and Improvement of Primary Natural Forest and Peatland Governance; and
3. Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs)...
extraction_rationale: Umbrella list of ‘key regulations’ explicitly issued to avoid forest conversion.
mechanism_description: Indonesia has issued regulations and an instruction to terminate new permits and improve governance for primary natural forest and peatland, helping avoid forest conversion and preserve ecosystem carbon stocks.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: Ban conversion of forested land (productive production forest) in forest areas for APL, except where non-forested lands in production forest are not available, under Government Regulation No. 104/2015.
parent_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text: Government Regulation No.104/2015 on the changes of forest land use and function, which ban the conversion of forested land (productive production forest) in forest area for APL, except in the province where the non-forested lands in the production forest are not available;
extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory ban with conditional exception.
mechanism_description: The regulation bans conversion of forested production forest land to other land uses, helping avoid deforestation and preserve ecosystem carbon storage.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: Terminate new permits and improve primary natural forest and peatland governance under Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019.
parent_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text: Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019 on Termination of New Permit and Improvement of Primary Natural Forest and Peatland Governance; and
extraction_rationale: Named presidential instruction with clear governance/permit objective.
mechanism_description: It terminates new permits and improves governance for primary natural forests and peatlands to reduce conversion and degradation, preserving ecosystem carbon stores.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: Use Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs to guide integrated, comprehensive, spatially explicit land-use planning aimed at food, water, and energy security based on ecosystem management.
parent_statement: To avoid further conversion of forest, Indonesia establishes key regulations including Government Regulation No. 104/2015, Presidential Instruction No. 5/2019, and Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on SEAs.
verbatim_text: Government Regulation No. 46/2016 on strategic environmental assessments (SEAs), which provide guidance on the integrated, comprehensive, spatially explicit land use planning at national and subnational level aiming at food, water, and energy security based on sound ecosystem management.
extraction_rationale: Explicit planning guidance and purpose.
mechanism_description: Strategic environmental assessments provide guidance for integrated, spatially explicit land-use planning based on ecosystem management to support food, water, and energy security.

policy_statement: Indonesia commits to an updated First NDC target of 29% (unconditional) and up to 41% (conditional) GHG reduction versus BAU by 2030.
verbatim_text: In the First NDC, Indonesia has unconditional target of 29% and conditional target up to 41% compared to business as usual in 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified national mitigation targets with a 2030 timeline.
mechanism_description: Indonesia sets an unconditional and conditional national GHG reduction target versus business-as-usual to guide an overall emissions-reduction pathway.

policy_statement: Indonesia sets an adaptation pathways goal to reduce climate change impacts on national GDP loss by 3.45% in 2050 by increasing resilience in food, water, energy, and environmental health across economic, social/livelihood, and ecosystem/landscape resilience areas.
verbatim_text: The LTS-LCCR 2050 sets the goal of adaptation pathways to reduce the impact of climate change on national GDP loss by 3.45% in 2050, through increasing resilience in four basic necessities (food, water, energy, and environmental health), with three target areas of resilience (economy, social and livelihood, ecosystem and landscape).
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified adaptation objective with timeframe and defined resilience domains.
mechanism_description: Sets an adaptation pathways goal to increase resilience in food, water, energy, and environmental health, strengthening adaptive capacity to reduce climate-change impacts on the economy.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – a. Avoiding Deforestation
policy_statement: In RPJMN 2020–2024, Indonesia expands forest areas that need to be protected based on ecosystem services from 51.8 Mha to 65.3 Mha (inside and outside forest areas).
verbatim_text: In the National Medium-Term Development Plan (RPJMN) 2020-2024, forest area that need to be protected based on the value of ecosystem services of the forest, are expanded from 51.8 Mha to 65.3 Mha, which are located inside and outside forest area.
extraction_rationale: Quantified protection-area expansion target embedded in a national plan.
mechanism_description: Indonesia expands the forest area that needs to be protected based on ecosystem services, helping preserve ecosystems and forest carbon stocks by limiting forest loss and degradation.

section_header: 4.2.1.1. Forestry and Other Land Uses – c. Protection and Restoration of Peatland
policy_statement: Indonesia sets peatland restoration targets: CPOS 1.03 Mha by 2030 and 1.7 Mha by 2050; LCCP 2.7 Mha by 2030 and 4.2 Mha by 2050.
verbatim_text: For peatland restoration, CPOS figures the area of degraded peatland being restored should reach 1.03 Mha by 2030 and 1.7 Mha by 2050. While under LCCP, the target will be increased to 2.7 Mha by 2030 and 4.2 Mha by 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified restoration targets with timelines under two scenarios.
mechanism_description: It sets targets to restore degraded peatlands, which helps re-wet peat soils and reduces emissions from peat degradation and fires while supporting carbon storage.

section_header: 4.2.1.3. Achieving the Goals – b. Integrated Farming System
policy_statement: Develop integrated oil palm–livestock farming with targets: CPOS 11.70% (1.68 Mha) by 2030 and 22.72% (3.25 Mha) by 2050; LCCP 21.11% (3.02 Mha) by 2030 and 34.33% (4.91 Mha) by 2050.
verbatim_text: Under CPOS, integrated farming of oil palm-livestock is targeted to reach 11.70% (1.68 Mha) in 2030 and 22.72% (3.25 Mha) in 2050. Under LCCP, the target is increased to 21.11% (3.02 Mha) in 2030 and to 34.33% (  4.91 Mha) in 2050.
extraction_rationale: Quantified adoption targets for a specific integrated farming mitigation approach.
mechanism_description: It targets expanding integrated oil palm–livestock farming, which can lower agricultural emissions by improving nutrient cycling and reducing reliance on external inputs.

section_header: 3.3. Role of Ocean and Integrated Terrestrial and Marine Ecosystem Management and Conservation
policy_statement: Implement COVID-19 recovery coastal/marine actions including 15,000 hectares of mangrove planting and 50 hectares of coral reef restoration in Bali in 2020.
verbatim_text: Coastal and marine based activities have also been part of COVID-19 recovery programme, including 15 thousands hectare mangrove planting and 50 hectare coral reef restoration in marine tourism area in Bali in 2020.
extraction_rationale: Specific implemented actions with quantified areas and date, framed as part of a government recovery programme.
mechanism_description: The programme implements mangrove planting and coral reef restoration to enhance coastal ecosystems, increasing carbon sequestration and strengthening coastal resilience.

policy_statement: By 2030, Italy expects renewables shares of 55.0% in electricity, 33.9% in heating and cooling, and 22.0% in transport (RED II accounting).
verbatim_text: the contribution of renewables... by 2030 (30%) is expected to have the following distribution... 55.0% renewables share in the electricity sector; 33.9%... in the heating sector...; 22.0%... in the transport sector (calculated using... RED II).
extraction_rationale: Explicit sectoral targets with numbers.
mechanism_description: Italy expects higher renewable energy shares across electricity, heating/cooling, and transport, increasing renewable use and reducing reliance on fossil energy.

policy_statement: Italy targets installed renewable electricity capacities by 2030 including 52,000 MW solar and 19,300 MW wind (including 900 MW offshore), alongside other RES capacity targets.
verbatim_text: Table 10 - Growth targets for power (MW) from renewable sources for 2030... Wind... 19,300... of which off-shore... 900... Solar... 52,000...
extraction_rationale: Quantified installed capacity targets by technology.
mechanism_description: It sets targets for expanding renewable power capacity (e.g., solar and wind), supporting a cleaner electricity mix that displaces fossil generation and reduces grid emissions.

policy_statement: Italy confirms a target of at least 1.1 billion m³ of advanced biomethane from agricultural waste and OFMSW by 2030 for transport.
verbatim_text: For advanced biomethane originating from agricultural waste and the organic fraction of municipal solid waste (OFMSW), the target of at least 1.1 billion m 3 by 2030 is confirmed.
extraction_rationale: Quantified biomethane target with deadline and feedstock scope.
mechanism_description: It confirms a target to produce advanced biomethane from agricultural waste and the organic fraction of municipal solid waste for transport use, displacing fossil transport fuels and reducing emissions.

section_header: (2) the cumulative amount of end-use energy savings to be achieved over the period 2021-2030
policy_statement: Italy will achieve cumulative end-use energy savings of about 50.98 Mtoe over 2021-2030 under EED Article 7, corresponding to annual incremental savings of 0.927 Mtoe/year and nearly 9.3 Mtoe/year by 2030, and will not use EED Article 7(2) options for 2021-2030.
verbatim_text:
Therefore... a 50.98 Mtoe saving of final energy to be attained through active policies during the period 2021-2030.

Therefore, in order to comply with the obligation, a reduction... of nearly 9.3 Mtoe/year by 2030... is required...

Italy has decided not avail itself of the options referred to in Article 7(2) of the EED for 20212030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified savings obligation and explicit decision on compliance options.
mechanism_description: Italy complies with an obligation to attain final end-use energy savings through active policies, reducing final energy consumption over time.

section_header: (4) the total floor area to be renovated... under Article 5 of Directive 2012/27/EU
policy_statement: Italy will continue and enhance the Energy Renovation Programme for the Central Public Administration (PREPAC) for 2021-2030 and plans to renovate 3.2 million m² of central public administration buildings over 2021-2030 to meet the 3% annual renovation obligation.
verbatim_text:
Provision is also made to continue with the Energy Renovation Programme for the Central Public Administration (PREPAC) during the period 2021-2030...

...during the period 2021-2030, 3.2 million m 2 of floor area of buildings associated with the central public administration will be subjected to energy renovation.
extraction_rationale: Named program with continuation period and quantified renovation area commitment.
mechanism_description: Italy plans to continue the PREPAC programme to carry out energy renovations in central public administration buildings, reducing building energy use.

policy_statement: Italy's PNIRE (National Plan for Electric Vehicle Charging Infrastructure) targets by 2020 include up to 13,000 slow/accelerated public charging stations and 6,000 fast charging stations, a 1:8 public-to-private ratio, and 130,000 electric vehicles in circulation, supported by a €72.2 million programme including €28.7 million government co-financing.
verbatim_text: The targets of the Plan to be achieved by 2020 are to create up to 13,000 slow/accelerated charging stations and 6,000 fast charging stations... and to have 130,000 electric vehicles in circulation... Provision is made for a total budget of €72.2 million, in respect of government co-financing totalling €28.7 million.
extraction_rationale: Named plan with quantified infrastructure/vehicle targets and committed budget.
mechanism_description: The plan sets targets and funding to create public EV charging stations, which supports wider electric vehicle adoption by making charging more available.

section_header: Replacement of the fleet of local public transport vehicles / Expected development trends
policy_statement: Italy proposes to accelerate implementation of Article 18(10) of Legislative Decree 257/2016 requiring that, in provinces with high PM10 pollution, when public administrations and related entities replace fleets, 30% of new vehicles purchased are low-emission vehicles by 2022, at least 50% by 2025, and 85% by 2030; non-compliant public tenders remain void.
verbatim_text: It  is  proposed to accelerate the provisions set out in paragraph 10 of  Article 18 of Italian Legislative Decree 257/2016... must ensure that 30% by 2022, at least 50% by 2025 and 85% by 2030 of new vehicles purchased are electric vehicles and hybrid vehicles with off-vehicle charging, powered by methane and hydrogen... For the law to be effective, additionally, it will remain the case that public tenders which do not comply with this provision are void.
extraction_rationale: Binding regulatory requirement with quantified targets and deadlines, plus enforcement via void tenders.
mechanism_description: It requires public administrations in high-PM10 provinces to procure a minimum share of low-emission vehicles when replacing fleets, increasing low-emission fleet uptake and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: Replacement of private vehicles used for the transport of persons
policy_statement: Italy introduced in 2019 a subsidy of 30% of purchase price up to €3,000 for buyers of new electric or hybrid vehicles in categories L1e–L7e who scrap an older EURO 0–3 vehicle (owned/held at least 12 months), with implementing rules set by Ministerial Decree of 20 March 2019.
verbatim_text: Law No 145 of 30 December 2018... established that anyone who, in 2019, bought... a new electric or hybrid vehicle in category L1e... or L7e and who took for scrapping... was entitled to a subsidy of 30% of the purchase price, up to a maximum of €3,000... The  rules  for  applying  the  incentive  were  established... in a Ministerial Decree of 20 March 2019.
extraction_rationale: Specific incentive with eligibility conditions, percentage cap, year, and implementing decree.
mechanism_description: A subsidy for buying new electric or hybrid vehicles, tied to scrapping an older vehicle, accelerates uptake of lower-emitting vehicles and speeds replacement of higher-emitting vehicles, reducing transport emissions.

section_header: Replacement of private vehicles used for the transport of persons
policy_statement: From January 2020, when public administrations purchase or hire at least two vehicles to replace fleets, at least 50% must be electric, hybrid, or hydrogen-powered vehicles (within earmarked budget limits).
verbatim_text: The Budget Law for 2020 establishes that from January 2020, when public administrations purchase or hire at least two vehicles to replace their vehicle fleets,  at  least 50% of that purchase or hire must be for road transport electric, hybrid or hydrogen-powered vehicles...
extraction_rationale: Binding procurement rule with start date and minimum share.
mechanism_description: It establishes that when public administrations purchase or hire replacement vehicles, at least 50% are electric, hybrid, or hydrogen-powered, shifting fleets to lower-emission vehicles and reducing transport emissions.

section_header: Replacement of private vehicles used for the transport of persons
policy_statement: From July 2020, Italy tightens fringe benefit taxation on newly registered company cars based on CO2 emissions, including higher tax rates for high-emitting vehicles and lower rates for vehicles below 60 gCO2/km.
verbatim_text: The Budget Law for 2020 also envisages tighter rules, from July 2020, on fringe benefit taxes on newly registered highly polluting company cars. The tax decreases to 25% for company cars with CO2 emissions of below 60 g/km... whereas for all cars with emissions over 190 g/km it rises to 50% (and 60% from 2021).
extraction_rationale: Concrete fiscal policy with thresholds, rates, and effective dates.
mechanism_description: It tightens fringe benefit taxes on highly polluting company cars and lowers the tax for low-CO2 cars, encouraging a shift to lower-emission vehicles and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Italy’s Budget Law for 2018 obliges public bodies to redevelop public lighting systems by 31 December 2023, achieving at least a 50% reduction in electricity consumption relative to average 2015–2016.
verbatim_text: In  this  connection, the Budget Law for 2018 established that public bodies are obliged to redevelop  public  lighting  systems  by  31 December  2023,  guaranteeing  a  reduction  in electricity consumption of at least 50% in relation to the average consumption 2015-2016.
extraction_rationale: Binding obligation with deadline and quantified reduction target.
mechanism_description: Public bodies are obliged to redevelop public lighting systems to reduce electricity consumption, lowering energy demand and associated power-sector emissions.

section_header: 3.10.1 Electrical interconnectivity — ii. Projections of interconnector expansion requirements (including for the year 2030)
policy_statement: Italy will complete the interconnection projects currently underway (Italy–France and Italy–Montenegro, first centre) by 2020 to contribute to achieving the 10% electricity interconnection target by 2020.
verbatim_text: The interconnection projects currently underway are the Italy-France and Italy-Montenegro (first centre) connections, which will be up and running by 2020 and will contribute to the achievement of the target of 10% by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure commitment with a timeline (up and running by 2020) and a quantified policy target (10% interconnection).
mechanism_description: Italy will complete cross-border electricity interconnection projects, increasing interconnection capacity to improve grid flexibility and support renewable energy integration.

policy_statement: Japan has legally positioned net-zero (carbon neutrality) by 2050 as a basic principle through the Act Partially Amending the Act on Promotion of Global Warming Countermeasures (Act No. 54 of 2021).
verbatim_text: By the Act Partially Amending the Act on Promotion of Global Warming Countermeasures (Act No. 54 of 2021) which was enacted in 204th session of the Diet, net-zero by 2050 became a Basic Principle of the Act.
extraction_rationale: States a binding mechanism: a legislative amendment that enshrines net-zero by 2050 as a basic principle.
mechanism_description: The Act positions net-zero by 2050 as a basic principle, providing an economy-wide direction for emissions reductions toward carbon neutrality.

section_header: (g) The intention to use voluntary cooperation under Article 6 of the Paris Agreement, if applicable.
policy_statement: Japan will establish and implement the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM) to quantitatively evaluate and use international emission reductions/removals toward its NDC, aiming to secure approximately 100 million t-CO2 of accumulated emission reductions and removals by fiscal year 2030 through public-private collaborations, and will appropriately count acquired credits while ensuring environmental integrity and avoiding double-counting.
verbatim_text: Japan will establish and implement the Joint Crediting Mechanism (JCM) in order to quantitatively evaluate contributions of Japan to greenhouse gas emission reductions and removals... and in order to use such contributions to achieve Japan's NDC. By doing so, through public-private collaborations, Japan aims to secure accumulated emission reductions and removals at the level of approximately 100 million t-CO2 by fiscal year 2030. Japan will appropriately count the acquired credits to achieve its NDC. With regards to the JCM which Japan has initiated to establish, Japan secures environmental integrity and the avoidance of double-counting in line with the international rules including the Paris Agreement.
extraction_rationale: Named mechanism with a quantified cumulative target (100 million t-CO2 by FY2030), plus explicit commitments to establish/implement, count credits, and ensure integrity/avoid double counting.
mechanism_description: Japan will establish and implement the Joint Crediting Mechanism to quantify and secure international emission reductions and removals, count the acquired credits toward its NDC, and do so with environmental integrity and avoidance of double-counting.

policy_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Accordingly, the NAP process is divided into six strategies. The strategies discuss each sector's principal vulnerabilities and management from a climate change perspective and present current guidelines for implementing adaptation measures to increase climate resilience.

## 5.2. Proposed sectors adaptation strategies
extraction_rationale: Introduces a structured set of sector strategies with multiple detailed actions underneath, functioning as an umbrella (parent) for the sector-specific sub-policies extracted next.
mechanism_description: The NAP process develops sector-specific strategies and guidelines to implement adaptation measures that increase climate resilience across priority and cross-cutting sectors.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: strengthen Ministry of Agriculture capacity; improve integrated pest/disease/weed management and resistant varieties; assess crop vulnerability/suitability by agro-ecological zones; and enhance climate-proof agro-infrastructure to raise farmer productivity.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the Ministry of Agriculture, including training of experts, logistics, and use of technology for the management of the sector.
• Improve the effectiveness of pest, disease, and weed management practices through the broader use of integrated pest and pathogen management...
• Assess crops vulnerability and suitability (cropping pattern) for different Agroecological zones.
• Enhance climate-proof agro-infrastructural systems that strengthen the capacity of local farmers to increase productivity.
extraction_rationale: Bullet-listed concrete interventions under the agriculture adaptation strategy table; clearly actionable capacity/infrastructure and management measures.
mechanism_description: The strategy strengthens agricultural institutions, improves pest/disease/weed management and crop suitability assessment, and enhances climate-proof farm infrastructure to reduce climate-related production risks and improve farmer productivity.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: support communities via indigenous knowledge dissemination, insurance schemes, early warning/early action systems, vaccination campaigns and disease control to cope with climate variability.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Support communities in livestock and crop sectors by inventorying and disseminating indigenous knowledge, establishing and/or strengthening insurance schemes, early warning, early action system, vaccination campaign, disease control, etc., to cope with the stress based on climate variability.
extraction_rationale: Specifies multiple concrete support mechanisms (insurance, EWS/EAS, vaccination, disease control) for adaptation.
mechanism_description: Support communities with indigenous knowledge dissemination, insurance schemes, early warning/early action systems, and vaccination and disease control to help crops and livestock cope with climate variability.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: develop and introduce integrated soil fertility management, water harvesting, and conservation techniques to improve soil fertility and cope with aridity, waterlogging and floods.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Develop and introduce a diverse range of integrated Soil fertility management (SFM), water harvesting, and conservation techniques to farmers as a sustainable means of improving soil fertility, intensifying agricultural production, and coping with extreme conditions (aridity, waterlogging, flood, etc.)
extraction_rationale: Concrete practice/technology package to be developed and introduced to farmers.
mechanism_description: Developing and introducing integrated soil fertility management, water harvesting, and conservation techniques improves soil fertility and helps farmers cope with extreme conditions such as aridity, waterlogging, and floods.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: strengthen CARI capacity for climate-smart agriculture, including establishing seed banks and improving soil management, crop diversification, and livestock breeds; and develop climate-resilient crops including drought-resistant and flood-tolerant varieties and gene banks.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Strengthen the Central Agricultural Research Institute (CARI) capacity for research development of climate smart agriculture initiates in Liberia, including the setting up of seed banks and soil management, crop diversification... improved livestock breeds, etc.
• Develop climate-resilient crops, promote drought - resistant, flood-tolerant, and establishing gene bank of climate-resilient varieties of indigenous food crops (NDC, 2015)
extraction_rationale: Institutional strengthening plus specific deliverables (seed banks/gene banks; resilient varieties) are clear adaptation actions.
mechanism_description: Strengthening agricultural research and developing climate-resilient seeds and practices (e.g., seed/gene banks, improved soil management, crop diversification, and hardier livestock/crop varieties) increases resilience to drought and flooding impacts.

policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: develop and implement coastal zone policy/design/management plan; develop an integrated coastal management plan and early warning system with training; promote disaster risk management and preparedness; rehabilitate and protect wetlands and mangroves; and climate-proof new coastal infrastructure investments.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 8: Adaptation strategies of the coastal sector

• develop and implement coastal zone policy, design, and management plan(NDC, 2015)
• Develop an integrated management plan for coastal zone management as well as an early warning system that includes training and capacity development for coastal management and monitoring
• Promote and implement disaster risk management in general (especially disaster preparedness).
• Support the rehabilitation and protection of wetlands and mangroves...
• Develop and implement a program for climate-proofing new investments in infrastructure (roads, sewers, water supplies, and other infrastructure) in coastal settlements and rural areas...
extraction_rationale: A suite of concrete coastal adaptation measures, including planning instruments, EWS, DRM, ecosystem protection, and infrastructure climate-proofing.
mechanism_description: Develops and implements coastal management planning, early warning and preparedness activities, rehabilitates and protects wetlands and mangroves, and climate-proofs new coastal infrastructure to reduce coastal climate hazard risks and impacts.

policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: design and implement a strategic communication action plan to inform and educate the public on climate change-related coastal changes, challenges, and adaptation options.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 8: Adaptation strategies of the coastal sector

• Design and implement a strategic communication action plan to inform and educate people about changes and challenges associated with coastal areas related to climate change and how they can adapt to cope with these changes and challenges.
extraction_rationale: Specific planned intervention (communication action plan) with clear purpose and scope.
mechanism_description: Designing and implementing a strategic communication action plan informs and educates people about climate change impacts on coastal areas and available adaptation options, supporting preparedness and adaptation planning.

policy_statement: Energy adaptation strategy: establish and promote a national solar energy program and other energy-efficient lighting technologies, including provision of energy-efficient technologies for schools, public institutions, and households.

verbatim_text:

• Establish and promote a robust national program on solar energy (e.g., hybrid systems, installation of solar panels, promotion of solar street lighting, etc.) and other energy-efficient lighting technologies.
• Support the provision of energy-efficient technologies such as energy-efficient bulbs to provide power and lighting for schools and other public institutions and households...
extraction_rationale: Concrete energy adaptation/transition actions (solar program, efficiency tech deployment) explicitly proposed as strategy measures.
mechanism_description: It establishes and promotes a national solar energy program and supports provision of energy-efficient lighting technologies, increasing solar-based electricity and lowering reliance on fossil electricity generation.

policy_statement: Energy adaptation strategy: promote energy plantations to reduce pressure on natural forests, regulate sustainable biomass energy use, support community-based off-grid/mini-grids, conserve/protect water catchments for hydropower and municipal supply, and prevent sedimentation affecting hydropower generation.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 9: Adaptation strategies of the energy sector

• Support the promotion and implementation of energy plantation schemes to minimize natural forest pressure and reduce energy stress.
• Develop a system to regulate the sustainable use of biomass energy.
• Promote and support the development and utilization of community-based off- grid/mini-grids.
• Conservation and protection of water catchments, including around hydro- power and municipal water supply sources.
• Prevent sedimentation that could hinder the production of energy in hydroelectric facilities
extraction_rationale: Multiple specific interventions for resilience and sustainability in the energy sector are explicitly listed.
mechanism_description: Conservation and protection of water catchments and efforts to prevent sedimentation help maintain hydroelectric generation and support reliable municipal water supply.

policy_statement: Fisheries adaptation strategy: strengthen capacity of fisheries authorities for research/monitoring/enforcement; support artisanal fishing communities with training, gear and alternative livelihoods; establish monitoring/reporting/verification and adaptive management; support research on climate pressures; identify/map/protect valuable fisheries areas including marine protected areas; reduce external stressors via regulatory measures; integrate fisheries into national adaptation and food security policies; establish early warning and information/communication networks; and regulate fishing to prevent overexploitation.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 10: Adaptation strategies of the fisheries sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the Bureau of National Fisheries... for research monitoring and enforcement.
• Invest in and support artisanal fishing communities, including training, fishing gear, and alternative livelihoods.
• Set up robust monitoring, reporting, and verification system...
• Identify, map, and protect areas valuable for fisheries... including the setting up of marine protected areas.
• ...instituting actions and regulatory measures to reduce land-based sources of pollution... and destructive fishing practices...
• Integrate fisheries fully into climate change adaptation and food security policies at the national level...
• Support the establishment of early warning systems...
• ...Regulate fishing practices to prevent overexploitation and fishing in restricted areas (NAPA, 2008)
extraction_rationale: Comprehensive list of concrete governance, capacity, protection, and regulatory measures presented as sector adaptation strategies.
mechanism_description: The strategy strengthens fisheries governance (research/monitoring, early warning, protected areas, pollution controls, and regulated fishing practices) to reduce climate and non-climate stresses on marine ecosystems and sustain fish stocks and livelihoods under changing conditions.

policy_statement: Forestry adaptation strategy: strengthen FDA capacity and logistics; implement sustainable/alternative livelihoods for forest-dependent communities; promote community forest activities beyond timber extraction; establish comprehensive forest (including NTFPs) monitoring; implement reforestation/afforestation in degraded areas; map and manage forest water catchments; consolidate protected area networks with landscape connectivity; strengthen coordination with other agencies; enforce anti-poaching regulations and a wildlife code of ethics; and implement communication strategies to raise awareness on climate impacts and adaptation.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 31: Adaptation strategies of the forestry sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the FDA, including training of experts and logistics for forest management
• Implement sustainable and, where applicable alternative livelihood initiatives for forest-dependent communities...
• Promote community forest activities beyond timber extraction...
• Establish a comprehensive monitoring system for forest resources...
• Implement reforestation and afforestation activities in degraded areas...
• Identify and map for proper management of water catchment areas in forests...
• Promote the consolidation of protected area network...
• Enforce regulations related to illegal hunting...
• Develop and implement a communication strategy to increase the awareness...
extraction_rationale: Explicit sector strategy with multiple specific interventions including capacity, monitoring, restoration, protected areas, enforcement, and outreach.
mechanism_description: Implementing reforestation and afforestation in degraded areas restores forest ecosystems and increases carbon storage while improving resilience to climate impacts.

policy_statement: Waste management adaptation/mitigation strategy: strengthen community and institutional capacity for integrated waste management; develop an integrated waste management strategy prioritizing waste prevention and low-GHG technologies compatible with methane capture for electricity; promote PPP financing for waste infrastructure; implement composting to convert urban waste into inputs for agriculture; and develop landfills for major cities including methane recovery and power generation using CDM and NAMAs.
parent_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Table 42: Adaptation strategies of the waste management sector

• Strengthen capacity at the community and institutional level for integrated waste management.
• Develop an integrated waste management strategy and system for all types of waste, assigning priority to prevent waste generation with nationally appropriate low greenhouse gas emission technologies... compatible with methane capture and use for electricity generation.
• Promote private-public partnership (PPP)...
• Design and implement a system to run urban waste into input for agricultural production through composting...
• Develop landfills for all major cities and use the Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) and Nationally Appropriate Mitigation Actions (NAMAs)to develop methane recovery and power generation projects in landfills.
extraction_rationale: Lists concrete policy/program actions with specific mechanisms (PPP, composting, landfills, methane capture, CDM/NAMAs).
mechanism_description: The strategy develops landfills with methane recovery and electricity generation so landfill methane is captured and used for power, reducing methane emissions and displacing fossil electricity.

policy_statement: Namibia commits to reduce its GHG emissions conditionally by at least 91% of BAU scenario emissions by 2030.
verbatim_text: Namibia  commits  to  reduce  its GHG emissions conditionally  by at least 91% of its BAU scenario emissions by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Explicit, quantified economy-wide mitigation target with a deadline (91% below BAU by 2030).
mechanism_description: Namibia commits to reduce economy-wide greenhouse gas emissions relative to a business-as-usual scenario, creating an emissions reduction pathway.

policy_statement: Namibia will implement economy-wide targets to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 91% below 2015 levels by 2030.
verbatim_text: Namibia continues to play a part in an effective global response to climate change. Namibia is to implement an economy-wide target to reduce greenhouse gas emissions from 91 per cent below 2015 levels by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Stated economy-wide target with a quantified percentage and a deadline.
mechanism_description: Namibia will implement an economy-wide target to reduce greenhouse gas emissions, guiding an economy-wide emissions reduction pathway.

policy_statement: Namibia commits to reduce its GHG emissions by 14% (limited support) and towards 77% (substantial international support) in 2030 compared to BAU, corresponding to a total reduction of 21.996 MtCO2e.
verbatim_text: Namibia commits to reduce its GHG emissions conditionally by 14% (under limited domestic and international support) and towards 77% (with substantial international support) in 2030 compared to the Business As Usual levels,  corresponding  to  a  total  reduction  by  21.996  MtCO2e.
extraction_rationale: Quantified conditional/unconditional mitigation levels (percentages and MtCO2e) with a target year.
mechanism_description: Namibia commits to an economy-wide reduction in greenhouse gas emissions relative to business-as-usual levels, creating an overall pathway for emissions reductions that scales with available support.

policy_statement: Namibia will reduce the deforestation rate by 75% from 0.9% per year to below 0.25% per year to drive achievement of the 2030 target.
verbatim_text: reducing the deforestation rate by 75% from 0.9% per year to below 0.25% will be the major drive to achieving the 2030 target
extraction_rationale: Quantified land-use mitigation target with clear baseline and threshold.
mechanism_description: Namibia will reduce the deforestation rate, preserving forest carbon stocks and ecosystem functions that help avoid emissions from land-use change.

section_header: TABLE 2.5. SUMMARY OF ESTIMATED GHG MITIGATION POTENTIAL FROM ALL MEASURES.
policy_statement: Namibia targets zero waste by 2050 through re-use and recycling, achieving 75% of the target by 2030.
verbatim_text: 26) Zero waste by 2050 through re-use and recycling (75% of target achieved by 2030)

mechanism_description: It targets zero waste by increasing re-use and recycling to divert waste from landfills, avoiding methane emissions.

section_header: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance
policy_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance

The Government will roll out a programme of non-statutory guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks. This could also support regulatory requirements to be developed through the National Planning Framework.
extraction_rationale: Umbrella programme explicitly introducing multiple specific guidance products listed as sub-items (3.7.1–3.7.8).
mechanism_description: The government will roll out a programme of non-statutory adaptation guidance to help decision makers assess and manage climate risks, improving institutional capacity for climate adaptation.

section_header: 3.7.1: Promote the use of the Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance
policy_statement: Promote the use of updated Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance highlighting changes from AR6 to support updated risk management plans.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.1: Promote the use of the Aotearoa New Zealand Climate Change Projections guidance

An update for Aotearoa that highlights changes from the 2021 AR6 IPCC.12 report. The update will enable stakeholders to quickly assess where they may need to adapt their risk management plans to account for the latest science.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance deliverable under the rolling programme.
mechanism_description: Promoting updated climate projections guidance helps stakeholders use the latest science to revise risk management plans and strengthen adaptation planning.

section_header: 3.7.2: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers
policy_statement: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers, including a methodology to ensure adaptation is considered in new policy and services to avoid increased exposure or vulnerability.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.2: Produce adaptation guidance for central government policy makers

A methodology for central government to ensure it considers adaptation in new policy and services. This will avoid increased exposure or vulnerability to climate impacts.
extraction_rationale: Specific methodology commitment under the guidance programme.
mechanism_description: Produce adaptation guidance and a methodology for central government policy makers so adaptation is considered in new policies and services, helping avoid increased exposure or vulnerability to climate impacts.

section_header: 3.7.3: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP)
policy_statement: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP) for central and local government to plan adaptation under uncertain climate futures.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.3: Produce guidance for dynamic adaptive pathways planning (DAPP)

A guide for central and local government on how to plan for adaptation in a context of uncertain climate futures.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance product with a defined purpose.
mechanism_description: Produce guidance for central and local government on dynamic adaptive pathways planning to support adaptation planning under uncertain climate futures.

section_header: 3.7.4: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning
policy_statement: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning to help governments and businesses assess climate risks and plan responses.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.4: Produce guidance on using different socio-economic scenarios for adaptation planning

Assists central and local government and businesses to consider future socio-economic scenarios when assessing climate risks, and planning how to manage them.
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance output under an identified action sub-item.
mechanism_description: It produces guidance that assists governments and businesses to use socio-economic scenarios in climate risk assessments and to plan how to manage those risks, strengthening adaptive capacity.

section_header: 3.7.5: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government
policy_statement: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government to support adaptation in planning and decisions, including communicating technical information and making climate decisions.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.5: Regularly update adaptation guidance for local government

Supports local government to consider adaptation in planning and decisions. This will include guidance on communicating scientific and technical information to communities, and on making climate decisions.
extraction_rationale: Ongoing guidance update commitment for a specific user group (local government).
mechanism_description: Regularly updating adaptation guidance helps local governments incorporate adaptation into planning and decisions by improving access to scientific and technical information and supporting climate decision-making.

section_header: 3.7.6: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua
policy_statement: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.6: Produce guidance on integrating mātauranga Māori into adaptive planning and working with mana whenua

Helps central and local government effectively engage with hapū, iwi and Māori when managing risk and planning for adaptation.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance deliverable focused on Māori knowledge and engagement.
mechanism_description: Produces guidance to help central and local government engage with hapū, iwi and Māori and integrate mātauranga Māori into risk management and adaptation planning, strengthening institutional capacity to adapt.

section_header: 3.7.7: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans
policy_statement: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans using a standard approach under uncertain climate projections.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.7: Produce guidance for preparing adaptation plans

Enables communities and different audiences, sectors and levels of government to produce their own adaptation plans, using a standard approach under conditions of uncertainty in climate projections.
extraction_rationale: Concrete guidance product intended to standardise adaptation planning.
mechanism_description: Produces guidance that enables communities and different levels of government to develop adaptation plans using a standard approach under uncertain climate projections, strengthening institutional adaptive capacity.

section_header: 3.7.8: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments
policy_statement: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments on a six-yearly cycle after each national risk assessment.
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
verbatim_text:
## 3.7.8: Regularly update the guide to local climate change risk assessments

Sets out a step-by-step process for local risk assessments. It will be updated on a six-yearly cycle after the release of each national climate change risk assessment.
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment with a defined update cycle linked to risk assessments.
mechanism_description: It will update guidance for local climate change risk assessments on a regular cycle, improving the information base used to plan and prioritize adaptation actions.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas

| Main objectives: • Reducing biomass fuel • Reducing the speed at which fires spread • Increasing the forest's resilience to fire • Ensuring the safety of people and property
extraction_rationale: A named line of action with an objective and multiple concrete measures listed beneath it, fitting a parent policy.
mechanism_description: Structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas reduce biomass fuel and slow fire spread, improving forest resilience to fire and reducing risks to people and property.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
policy_statement: Introduce discontinuity bands/patches to prevent rural fires, including (re)afforestation with fire-resistant species, controlling excessive natural regeneration after fire, creating agricultural mosaics, and creating grazing areas (including use of goats) to control spontaneous vegetation.
parent_statement: Line of Action #1: Rural fire prevention through structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas to reduce biomass fuel, slow fire spread, increase forest resilience to fire, and ensure safety of people and property.
verbatim_text:
• Introduction of discontinuity bands or patches by:
- (re)afforestation with other forest species resistant to the risk of fire
- control of excessive densities of natural regeneration after fire
- introduction of agricultural mosaic
- creation of areas for grazing and use of goats to control spontaneous vegetation
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention measures listed under the named Line of Action #1.
mechanism_description: It introduces vegetation discontinuity bands using fire-resistant (re)afforestation, control of dense post-fire regrowth, agricultural mosaics, and grazing (including goats) to reduce fuel continuity and help reduce rural fire risk and impacts.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #1 - Rural fire prevention - structural interventions in agricultural and forestry areas
