mechanism_description: Implementing soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques reduces erosion and runoff and increases soil organic matter, water retention, and biodiversity, strengthening ecosystem resilience and supporting soil carbon gains.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Recover areas of degraded soils and vegetation, prioritising areas susceptible to desertification, including areas affected by rural/forest fires, eroded/salted/degraded areas, areas with invasive exotic woody species, over-exploited soils, and inert extraction areas.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text: Recovering areas of degraded soils and vegetation, with priority in the areas most susceptible to desertification, namely: i) areas affected by rural fires, including forest fires; ii) eroded, salted, and other degraded areas; iii) areas affected by invasive exotic woody species; iv) areas with soils degraded by over- exploitation...; and v) areas of inert extraction
extraction_rationale: Concrete restoration action listed under Measures/concretization for Line of Action #2.
mechanism_description: The policy plans to recover degraded soils and vegetation in vulnerable areas to restore land cover and soil function, helping reduce desertification and erosion while improving ecosystem condition.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Apply soil conservation practices including afforestation with soil-improving species, incorporation of manures/compost and rotations with legumes/cover crops/by-products, direct seeding or in-line tillage and crop rotations, intercropping, and setting up overgrazing and biodiverse pastures.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization:
• Afforestation with soil-improving species - long revolutions
• Incorporation of manures and compost, rotations with legumes, cover and with agricultural or forestry by-products...
• Direct seeding or in-line tillage; crop rotation with different types of systems
• Intercropping in permanent crops
• Setting up overgrazing and biodiverse pastures
extraction_rationale: Specific list of techniques to be implemented; directly stated measures.
mechanism_description: Implements soil conservation practices such as afforestation, compost/manure incorporation, cover-crop and legume rotations, reduced tillage/direct seeding, intercropping, and biodiverse pastures to build soil carbon and restore ecosystem function.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Reposition/rehabilitate riparian galleries (planting/sowing indigenous species) and consolidate/recover slopes and riverbanks using natural engineering techniques for erosion control.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text:
• Reposition/rehabilitation of the riparian gallery (planting and/or sowing of indigenous species)
• Consolidation and recovery of slopes and riverbanks using natural engineering techniques
extraction_rationale: Concrete nature-based interventions listed under Line of Action #2 measures.
mechanism_description: It rehabilitates riparian vegetation with indigenous species and uses natural engineering to stabilize slopes and riverbanks, reducing erosion and strengthening river corridor resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: Create infiltration gardens and other aquifer recharge solutions (e.g., trenches and infiltration channels) as part of soil conservation and fertility improvement.
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text: Creation of infiltration gardens and other aquifer recharge solutions (e. trenches and infiltration channels)
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (aquifer recharge solutions) explicitly listed.
mechanism_description: Creating infiltration gardens, trenches, and infiltration channels increases infiltration to recharge aquifers and improve local water availability and resilience during dry periods.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #2 - Implementation of soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques
policy_statement: For Line of Action #2, make an additional €1.4 million in public investment in 2020 (PDR 2020: €1.4 million).
parent_statement: Line of Action #2: Implement soil conservation and fertility improvement techniques to minimise soil erosion, increase soil organic matter and water retention, reduce runoff, and increase soil biodiversity and ecosystem resilience.
verbatim_text:
## Goals:

- 2020: make an additional €1.4 million in public investment ( PDR 2020: €1.4 million)
extraction_rationale: Quantified public investment commitment for this line of action.
mechanism_description: The policy plans additional public investment, but the text does not indicate which actions the funding supports or how it leads to a specific climate effect.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts resulting from drought and scarcity phenomena

| Main objectives: • Reduce water consumption in the various sectors • Increasing water efficiency in the various sectors ... • Increasing the resilience of water supply infrastructure
extraction_rationale: Named action with multiple concrete measures listed under Measures/concretization.
mechanism_description: Implements improved water management practices across sectors to reduce water consumption, increase water efficiency, and improve the resilience of water supply infrastructure to drought and water scarcity.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Adopt water-saving practices in agriculture, including efficient irrigation (sprinkler/localised systems and meters), monitoring crop water needs, remodelling irrigation infrastructure to reduce losses and evaporation, and using rainwater and reuse of treated wastewater.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization: • Adoption of good water management practices in agriculture to reduce consumption:
- Irrigation practices that promote the more efficient and sustainable use of water resources [installation of sprinkler irrigation systems, localised (micro-sprinklers, drop-by-drop) and installation of meters at the water catchments]...
- Improved systems for monitoring the actual water needs of crops...
- Remodelling of irrigation infrastructures to reduce losses...
- Use of rainwater and re-use of treated wastewater in agriculture
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions enumerated under Line of Action #3.
mechanism_description: It supports improved agricultural irrigation and water management (efficient systems, metering, crop-need monitoring, reduced conveyance losses, and use of rainwater/treated wastewater) to reduce agricultural water consumption.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Promote installation of species/varieties better adapted to climate change, including reconversion of crops to less water-demanding species/varieties/cultivars with high water productivity and resistance to water stress.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
- Installation of species and varieties better adapted to changes in climate and extreme events:
- - Reconversion of crops to less water-demanding species, varieties, and cultivars (adapting crops and varieties to water availability, favouring the installation of species with high water productivity and resistance to water stress)
extraction_rationale: Concrete adaptation action (crop reconversion) explicitly listed.
mechanism_description: It promotes switching to crop species and varieties that need less water and tolerate water stress, improving agricultural resilience to climate change and extreme events.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Adopt water management practices in industry to reduce consumption, including wastewater reuse and installation of rainwater harvesting systems.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
• Adoption of good water management practices in the industry to reduce consumption:
- Wastewater reuse in industry
- Installation of rainwater harvesting systems
extraction_rationale: Specific industrial water efficiency interventions listed under Line of Action #3.
mechanism_description: Industry adopts wastewater reuse and rainwater harvesting systems to reduce water consumption.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Remodel aquaculture infrastructures to optimise water consumption and reduce losses.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text: • Remodelling of aquaculture infrastructures to optimise water consumption and reduce losses.
extraction_rationale: Explicit measure for aquaculture under this line of action.
mechanism_description: Remodelling aquaculture infrastructure improves water-use efficiency and reduces water losses, lowering overall water consumption.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: Adopt water management practices in the urban sector, including rehabilitating distribution systems, installing leakage monitoring, and implementing differentiated supply systems to diversify water sources; also requalify green spaces with native species and use treated rainwater/wastewater for urban cleaning and irrigation.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
• Adoption of good water management practices in the urban sector to reduce consumption:
- Rehabilitation of water distribution systems and installation of leakage monitoring systems
- Implementation of differentiated supply systems for the reinforcement and diversification of water sources

- -Requalification of green spaces with native species adapted to local soil and climate conditions
- -Use of treated rainwater or wastewater for urban cleaning, irrigation of green spaces
extraction_rationale: Concrete urban water efficiency and reuse actions listed in the measures.
mechanism_description: It adopts urban water management actions such as rehabilitating distribution systems, monitoring leakage, diversifying supply systems, and using treated rainwater/wastewater for cleaning and irrigation to reduce overall water consumption.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #3 - Implementation of good water management practices...
policy_statement: For Line of Action #3, make 124 million of public investment in 2020 (PDR 2020: €21 million; OP SEUR: EUR 100 million; other funds: EUR 3) and indicate at least EUR 350 million in the AUC by 2030.
parent_statement: Line of Action #3: Implement good water management practices in agriculture, aquaculture, industry and the urban sector to prevent impacts from drought and water scarcity, including reducing water consumption and increasing water efficiency and infrastructure resilience.
verbatim_text:
## Goals:

- 2020: 124 million of public investment (PDR 2020: €21 million; OP SEUR: EUR 100 million; other funds: EUR 3
- 2030: ...; at least EUR 350 million in the AUC
extraction_rationale: Contains explicit quantified public investment goals for 2020 and a minimum amount for 2030.
mechanism_description: The plan sets public investment levels to help finance implementation of climate-related actions.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change

| Main objectives: • Maintain or restore riparian galleries • Minimise barrier effects and reduce obstacles in the hydrographic network • Implementing environmental flow regimes ... • Encourage the creation of refuges and ecological corridors for vulnerable fauna species
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple measures beneath it.
mechanism_description: Restore riparian galleries, improve river continuity, implement environmental flow regimes, and encourage refuges/ecological corridors to strengthen species and habitat resilience to climate change impacts.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Conserve and rehabilitate watercourses to maintain environmental flows and river continuity in endemic species distribution areas; regulate irrigation abstractions from catchments serving as summer refuges; install/adapt fish transposition devices on weirs.
parent_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text:
• Conservation and rehabilitation of watercourses to maintain environmental flows and the river continuity in the distribution areas of endemic species...
• Regulating the volumes of water abstracted for irrigation from catchments that serve as summer refuges for vulnerable freshwater and migratory fish species.
• Installation or adaptation of transposition devices on weirs for vulnerable freshwater and migratory fish.
extraction_rationale: Concrete ecosystem/water management actions explicitly listed as measures.
mechanism_description: It conserves and rehabilitates watercourses, regulates irrigation abstraction volumes in refuge catchments, and installs or adapts fish transposition devices on weirs to maintain environmental flows and river continuity for vulnerable freshwater and migratory fish.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Install or adapt wildlife passages/crossings on highway/railway and conserve temporary pond systems in specified priority conditions/sites.
parent_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text:
• Installation or adaptation of wildlife passages/crossings on the highway/railway.
Conservation of temporary pond systems: i) sites with important populations of the striped terrapin ... and the Mediterranean terrapin ...; and ii) preferably oriented N -> S in open areas, away from roads and maintaining 10-70% forest cover
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure/ecological connectivity measures under the line of action.
mechanism_description: Installs or adapts wildlife passages at highway/railway barriers and conserves temporary pond habitats to improve wildlife movement, reduce barrier effects, and support ecosystem resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Create refuges and ecological corridors for vulnerable fauna species (including safeguarding native ichthyofauna and creating microhabitats with native species in areas occupied by monocultures).
parent_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text: • Creation of refuges and ecological corridors for vulnerable fauna species: i) in small intermittent coastal basins ... to safeguard native ichthyofauna; ii) from low to high altitude in forest areas ...; and iii) in agricultural and forest ecosystems ... creating small areas of microhabitats with native species in areas currently occupied by monocultures
extraction_rationale: Explicit habitat/corridor creation measures listed.
mechanism_description: Create refuges and ecological corridors and establish native-species microhabitats to safeguard vulnerable fauna (including native ichthyofauna) by improving habitat quality and connectivity in areas currently dominated by monocultures.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Promote, conserve and value animal, plant and forest genetic heritage to increase resilience to climate change (including resistance to drought, diseases and pests).
parent_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text: • Promote, conserve and value animal, plant and forest genetic heritage to increase resilience to climate change (resistance to drought, diseases and pests)
extraction_rationale: Concrete action (genetic heritage conservation/valorisation) stated as a measure.
mechanism_description: Promote, conserve and value animal, plant and forest genetic heritage so ecosystems and production systems are more resilient to climate change impacts such as drought, diseases and pests.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #4 - Increasing the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to the effects of climate change
policy_statement: Adapt fishing practices to promote non-waste and minimise by-catches of vulnerable species, and redirect aquaculture activity towards native species with greater climate resilience.
parent_statement: Line of Action #4: Increase the resilience of ecosystems, species, and habitats to climate change through measures such as riparian gallery interventions, maintaining environmental flows and river continuity, and creating refuges/ecological corridors for vulnerable species.
verbatim_text: • Adapt fishing practices by promoting non-waste and minimising by-catches of more vulnerable species in the new climate framework; Redirect aquaculture activity towards native species with greater climate resilience
extraction_rationale: Specific sector practice changes explicitly committed as measures.
mechanism_description: The policy adapts fishing to reduce waste and by-catch of vulnerable species and redirects aquaculture toward native, more climate-resilient species to strengthen ecosystem and food system resilience under climate change.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #5 - Reducing the vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures
policy_statement: Line of Action #5: Reduce vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures by countering the urban heat island effect and reducing adverse health effects.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #5 - Reducing the vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures

| Main objectives: • Regulating temperature in urban spaces • Minimising the adverse effects of intense heat on the health of populations
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple measures listed under Measures/concretization.
mechanism_description: The city plans to cool urban spaces to counter the urban heat island effect, which helps reduce heat exposure and related health harms during heatwaves.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #5 - Reducing the vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures
policy_statement: Implement measures to counter the heat island effect during heatwaves, including green infrastructure (green roofs/facades, renaturalisation, restoring pavement permeability), water retention basins, shaded areas (tree planting/canopies), ventilation corridors, misting and public drinking fountains, and creating green areas with drought-resistant vegetation cover.
parent_statement: Line of Action #5: Reduce vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures by countering the urban heat island effect and reducing adverse health effects.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization • Measures that counter the heat island effect, particularly during heatwave events:
• Implementation of green infrastructure ... (e.g. green roofs and facades) and renaturalisation and restoration of pavement permeability
• Implementation of water retention basins
• Creation of shaded areas (including tree planting and the installation of canopies between buildings)
• Creating ventilation corridors
• Thermoregulation of air by misting and installation of public drinking fountains
• ... Creating green areas with drought-resistant vegetation cover
extraction_rationale: Specific urban adaptation interventions enumerated under this line of action.
mechanism_description: Implements green infrastructure, shade, ventilation corridors, water-retention features, and cooling amenities (misting and public drinking fountains) to counter the heat island effect during heatwaves and reduce heat exposure.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #5 - Reducing the vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures
policy_statement: Carry out awareness-raising actions for the general population and vulnerable groups to deal with heatwaves.
parent_statement: Line of Action #5: Reduce vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures by countering the urban heat island effect and reducing adverse health effects.
verbatim_text: • Awareness-raising actions for the general population and the most vulnerable groups to deal with heatwaves
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to awareness-raising actions under Line of Action #5.
mechanism_description: Awareness-raising actions help the general population and vulnerable groups prepare for heatwaves and take protective steps that reduce heat exposure and related harms.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #5 - Reducing the vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures
policy_statement: Install or retrofit shading/cooling equipment in urban transport infrastructure/rolling stock, public buildings (schools, hospitals, health centres) and IPSS supporting children and the elderly; and implement alert systems to warn the general population and urban transport users.
parent_statement: Line of Action #5: Reduce vulnerability of urban areas to heatwaves and increasing maximum temperatures by countering the urban heat island effect and reducing adverse health effects.
verbatim_text:
• Installation or retrofit of shading/cooling equipment in:
- Urban transport infrastructure and rolling stock (vehicles, stops, stations, car parks)
- Public buildings (primarily schools, hospitals and health centres)
- IPSS supporting children and the elderly
• Alert systems to provide warnings for the general population and users of urban transport systems
extraction_rationale: Concrete equipment installations and warning systems are specific interventions.
mechanism_description: It installs or retrofits shading and cooling equipment in transport infrastructure and public/service buildings and implements alert systems to warn people, reducing heat exposure and heat-related risks during extreme heat events.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species...
policy_statement: Line of Action #6: Prevent the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests through surveillance, mapping, warning systems, and control/eradication actions.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests

Measures/concretization: • Strengthening entomological surveillance... • Identification of risk areas through mapping and geo-referencing... • Control of invasive alien species, and, where possible, eradication
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple concrete measures.
mechanism_description: The policy strengthens surveillance, mapping, and control/eradication actions to prevent invasive species and disease vectors from spreading, reducing ecosystem disruption and related disease risks.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species...
policy_statement: Strengthen entomological surveillance by expanding the Programme for Surveillance of Culicidal Vectors (REVIVE) and identify risk areas via mapping and geo-referencing on an electronic platform.
parent_statement: Line of Action #6: Prevent the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests through surveillance, mapping, warning systems, and control/eradication actions.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization: • Strengthening entomological surveillance ... through the expansion Programme for Surveillance of Culicidal Vectors (REVIVE)
• Identification of risk areas through mapping and geo-referencing the information available on an electronic platform
extraction_rationale: Specific surveillance and risk-mapping interventions listed.
mechanism_description: Expanding entomological surveillance and mapping/geo-referencing of vector data helps identify risk areas and supports better-targeted adaptation and public health response planning.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species...
policy_statement: Set up a centralised warning system for abiotic agents to support prevention, control and eradication of emerging agricultural and forest ecosystem pests and diseases.
parent_statement: Line of Action #6: Prevent the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests through surveillance, mapping, warning systems, and control/eradication actions.
verbatim_text: • Prevention, control and eradication of emerging agricultural and forest ecosystems enhanced by • Setting up a centralised warning system for abiotic agents)
extraction_rationale: Explicit measure: establishment of a centralized warning system.
mechanism_description: Setting up a centralised warning system for abiotic agents supports earlier detection and response to emerging agricultural and forest pests and diseases, improving prevention, control and eradication.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species...
policy_statement: Control invasive alien species and, where possible, eradicate them in priority sites for vulnerable species (e.g., amphibians/reptiles and Saramugo distribution areas).
parent_statement: Line of Action #6: Prevent the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests through surveillance, mapping, warning systems, and control/eradication actions.
verbatim_text: • Control of invasive alien species, and, where possible, eradication in priority sites for the most vulnerable species, such as amphibians and reptiles, and the distribution areas of Saramugo ( Anaecypris hispanica ).
extraction_rationale: Concrete control/eradication action explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Control and, where possible, eradication of invasive alien species in priority sites helps protect vulnerable native species and preserve ecosystem integrity.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species...
policy_statement: Valorise genetic material of agricultural and forestry varieties and species to reduce susceptibility to emerging diseases and pests.
parent_statement: Line of Action #6: Prevent the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests through surveillance, mapping, warning systems, and control/eradication actions.
verbatim_text: • Valorisation of the genetic material of agricultural and forestry varieties and species to reduce susceptibility to emerging diseases and pests
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention listed under the line of action.
mechanism_description: It valorises genetic material in agricultural and forestry varieties and species to reduce susceptibility to emerging diseases and pests, improving climate-related resilience of crops and forests.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #6 - Preventing the establishment and spread of invasive alien species...
policy_statement: For Line of Action #6, allocate 21 million of public investment in 2020 (PDR 2020: 20 M€; SEUR OP: 1 M€).
parent_statement: Line of Action #6: Prevent the establishment and spread of invasive alien species, vector-borne diseases, and agricultural and forestry diseases and pests through surveillance, mapping, warning systems, and control/eradication actions.
verbatim_text: Goals: • 2020: 21 million of public investment (PDR 2020: 20 M€; SEUR OP: 1 M€)
extraction_rationale: Quantified public investment goal for this line of action.
mechanism_description: It allocates public investment to support an action line, but the text does not state what intervention the funding supports or how it affects emissions or climate resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #7 - Reducing or minimising the risks associated with flooding phenomena
policy_statement: Line of Action #7: Reduce or minimise risks associated with flooding through flood risk management planning and measures such as infiltration areas, river profile restoration, protective infrastructure, monitoring/modelling, warning systems, and stormwater drainage upgrades.
verbatim_text:
Line of Action #7 - Reducing or minimising the risks associated with flooding phenomena

Main objectives: • Reduce the risk of river and urban flooding • Increasing the resilience of infrastructure • Implementing Flood Risk Management Plans
Measures/concretization: • Creating infiltration areas... • Implementation of forecasting and warning systems ... • Remodelling of urban stormwater drainage networks
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple listed measures for flood risk reduction.
mechanism_description: Implements flood risk management plans and measures (infiltration areas, drainage upgrades, monitoring/modelling, and forecasting/warning systems) to reduce river and urban flood risk and improve infrastructure resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #7 - Reducing or minimising the risks associated with flooding phenomena
policy_statement: Create infiltration areas for flood risk reduction, including constructing/rehabilitating retention basins, reconverting impermeable surfaces (e.g., drainage paving/renaturalisation), and building retention ditches on slopes to retain precipitation.
parent_statement: Line of Action #7: Reduce or minimise risks associated with flooding through flood risk management planning and measures such as infiltration areas, river profile restoration, protective infrastructure, monitoring/modelling, warning systems, and stormwater drainage upgrades.
verbatim_text:
Measures/concretization: • Creating infiltration areas through:
- Construction and/or rehabilitation of retention basins (excavation and dyke)
- Reconversion of impermeable surface areas ... (installation of drainage paving)
- Building retention ditches parallel to the ground level on slopes to retain precipitation
extraction_rationale: Concrete flood mitigation interventions listed under this line of action.
mechanism_description: It creates infiltration and retention areas (e.g., retention basins, drainage paving, and retention ditches) to retain precipitation and reduce runoff and flooding risk.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #7 - Reducing or minimising the risks associated with flooding phenomena
policy_statement: Protect water lines and restore natural profiles of river sections and floodplains (e.g., cleaning/regularisation, riparian vegetation restoration, floodplain clearance, sediment removal, restoring hydraulic crossings, removing obsolete structures, rehabilitating weirs, and small flood-correction works).
parent_statement: Line of Action #7: Reduce or minimise risks associated with flooding through flood risk management planning and measures such as infiltration areas, river profile restoration, protective infrastructure, monitoring/modelling, warning systems, and stormwater drainage upgrades.
verbatim_text:
• Protecting water lines and restoring the natural profiles of river sections and floodplains:
- Cleaning and regularisation of water lines
- Ecological restoration and maintenance of riparian vegetation
- Clearance of flood plains
- Removal of sediments ...
- Restoration of the flow section of hydraulic crossings and pontoons
- Removal of obsolete structures ...
- Rehabilitation of existing weirs ...
- Construction of small flood-correction works
extraction_rationale: Specific river/floodplain restoration actions enumerated as measures.
mechanism_description: It protects and restores river channels and floodplains (e.g., sediment removal, restoring crossings, and riparian vegetation work) to improve flow and floodplain function, reducing flood impacts and strengthening local resilience.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #7 - Reducing or minimising the risks associated with flooding phenomena
policy_statement: Implement flood risk monitoring and decision-support systems, including hydrometric stations, updated flow curves, hydrological/hydraulic modelling, and forecasting and warning systems for the population and responsible entities.
parent_statement: Line of Action #7: Reduce or minimise risks associated with flooding through flood risk management planning and measures such as infiltration areas, river profile restoration, protective infrastructure, monitoring/modelling, warning systems, and stormwater drainage upgrades.
verbatim_text:
• Maintenance/installation of hydrometric stations and updating of flow curves in the context of flood risk prevention
• Implementation of hydrological and hydraulic modelling as a decision support system ...
• Implementation of forecasting and warning systems for the population and responsible entities
extraction_rationale: Concrete monitoring/modelling/warning measures for floods.
mechanism_description: Implements hydrometric monitoring, modelling, and flood forecasting and warning systems to improve decision support and preparedness for flood risk.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #7 - Reducing or minimising the risks associated with flooding phenomena
policy_statement: Remodel urban stormwater drainage networks to hydraulically adjust to heavy rainfall flows, particularly in critical bottlenecks (bridges, aqueducts, etc.).
parent_statement: Line of Action #7: Reduce or minimise risks associated with flooding through flood risk management planning and measures such as infiltration areas, river profile restoration, protective infrastructure, monitoring/modelling, warning systems, and stormwater drainage upgrades.
verbatim_text: • Remodelling of urban stormwater drainage networks to hydraulically adjust to flows in heavy rainfall events, particularly in critical areas such as bridges, aqueducts and other bottlenecks
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure upgrade measure explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Remodelling stormwater drainage networks increases hydraulic capacity at bottlenecks so heavy rainfall flows are conveyed more safely, reducing urban flooding risk.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding

Measures/concretization:
• Operations promoting the natural re-establishment of sediment traffic from river basins...
• Interventions in dune systems...
• Interventions in coastal defence structures...
• Planned retreat with the removal of structures or buildings in high-risk areas
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple measures for coastal risk reduction.
mechanism_description: Sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat reduce exposure and damage in high-risk coastal areas from erosion, overtopping, and flooding.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: Promote the natural re-establishment of sediment transport from river basins and undertake dredging of lagoons to replenish beaches to safeguard priority species and habitats.
parent_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
• Operations promoting the natural re-establishment of sediment traffic from river basins...
• Dredging of lagoons to replenish beaches, to safeguard the conservation of priority species and habitats
extraction_rationale: Concrete sediment management measures explicitly listed.
mechanism_description: It promotes restoring sediment movement from river basins and uses lagoon dredging to replenish beaches, supporting coastal habitat conservation and reducing erosion impacts on priority species and habitats.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: Intervene in dune systems (including renaturalisation with native species and restoration/maintenance of secondary dunes important for vulnerable amphibian and reptile species) and replenish sediment in coastal systems; protect and rehabilitate coastal systems (including cliff work).
parent_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
• Interventions in dune systems (including renaturalisation with native species and maintenance and restoration of secondary dunes important for vulnerable amphibian and reptile species
• Interventions to replenish sediment in coastal systems
• Protection and rehabilitation of coastal systems
• Cliff work
extraction_rationale: Specific coastal ecosystem/geomorphology interventions listed as measures.
mechanism_description: Restore dunes and coastal/cliff systems and replenish sediment to strengthen natural coastal defenses and reduce erosion and storm-surge impacts.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: Upgrade coastal defence structures (including raising grades and riprap) and conduct artificial feeding operations (including high magnitude operations).
parent_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
• Interventions in coastal defence structures (including raising of grades and riprap)
• Artificial feeding operations, including high magnitude (shots)
extraction_rationale: Concrete engineered and sediment nourishment measures explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Upgrading coastal defense structures and carrying out artificial feeding operations helps reduce coastal flooding and erosion risks from storms and sea-level rise.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: Implement planned retreat by removing structures/buildings in high-risk coastal areas (including renaturalisation) and reconstructing in non-existent or low-risk areas; undertake land acquisition/expropriation for risk mitigation.
parent_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
• Planned retreat with the removal of structures or buildings in high-risk areas (including renaturalisation) and reconstruction in non-existent or low-risk areas
• Land acquisition/expropriation operations for risk mitigation
extraction_rationale: Explicit risk mitigation actions involving relocation and land measures.
mechanism_description: The policy pursues planned retreat by removing buildings from high-risk coastal areas, renaturalising those sites, and supporting relocation through land acquisition/expropriation to reduce exposure to coastal climate hazards.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: Strengthen or relocate transport and communications infrastructures located in coastal areas and install/reinforce appropriate signage.
parent_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
• Interventions in transport and communication infrastructures located in coastal areas (strengthening, relocation)
• Installation and reinforcement of appropriate signage
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure adaptation measures listed.
mechanism_description: Strengthening or relocating coastal transport and communications infrastructure and improving signage reduces disruption and safety risks from coastal hazards.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #8 - Increasing resilience and coastal protection...
policy_statement: For Line of Action #8, make an additional €47 million of public investment in 2020 (POSEUR: €47 million) and make €560 million of public investment in coastal protection by 2030.
parent_statement: Line of Action #8: Increase resilience and coastal protection in areas at high risk from coastal erosion, overtopping and flooding, including sediment management, dune and coastal defence interventions, and planned retreat in high-risk areas.
verbatim_text:
Goals:
• 2020: make an additional €47 million of public investment (POSEUR: €47 million)
• 2030: make €560 million of public investment in coastal protection
extraction_rationale: Explicit, quantified public investment commitments for 2020 and 2030.
mechanism_description: Increase public investment in coastal protection to reduce coastal flooding and related climate risks.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text:
## Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions

Measures/concretization: • Communication, dissemination, education and awareness-raising actions ... • Development of decision support tools ... reporting and developing a National Adaptation Platform. • ... drawing up of climate risk mapping • Development of Climate Change Adaptation Plans • Setting up forecasting, warning and response systems
extraction_rationale: Named line of action with multiple specific measures under it.
mechanism_description: Developing decision-support tools such as an adaptation platform, climate risk maps, adaptation plans, and forecasting/warning/response systems helps institutions plan for and respond to climate risks more effectively.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Develop decision support tools and information systems (including modelling and scenarisation, reporting) and develop a National Adaptation Platform to systematise and facilitate access to climate change information.
parent_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text: • Development of decision support tools and dissemination of good including information systems, modelling and scenarisation, reporting and developing a National Adaptation Platform.
extraction_rationale: Specific tool/platform development action explicitly listed.
mechanism_description: Developing decision-support tools, reporting, and a national adaptation platform improves access to climate information, which supports better adaptation planning and decision-making.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Implement capacity building actions for technicians and decision-makers in climate vulnerability assessment and adaptive management.
parent_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text: • Capacity building actions for technicians and decision-makers in climate vulnerability assessment and adaptive management
extraction_rationale: Concrete capacity-building measure.
mechanism_description: It implements capacity building for technicians and decision-makers to improve climate vulnerability assessment and adaptive management, strengthening institutional adaptive capacity.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Produce information and knowledge by implementing systems to monitor climate change impacts and drawing up climate risk mapping; create systems to identify risk areas using mapping and geo-referencing and make information available on an electronic platform.
parent_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text:
• Production of information and knowledge, in particular through the implementation of systems for monitoring the impacts of climate change drawing up of climate risk mapping
• Creation of a system to identify risk areas, using mapping and geo-referencing and making the information available on an electronic platform
extraction_rationale: Specific monitoring/mapping/platform actions explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Implement systems to monitor climate change impacts and develop geo-referenced risk mapping, then publish the information on an electronic platform to support climate risk planning and decisions.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Develop Climate Change Adaptation Plans and set up forecasting, warning and response systems, including climate prediction models for extreme phenomena and population warning.
parent_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text:
• Development of Climate Change Adaptation Plans
• Setting up forecasting, warning and response systems, including climate prediction models for extreme phenomena and population warning
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to plan development and early warning/response systems.
mechanism_description: Developing climate change adaptation plans and setting up forecasting, warning, and response systems improves preparedness and response to extreme climate phenomena, reducing associated impacts on people and assets.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Implement communication circuits to transmit information to health authorities following forecasting and warning systems.
parent_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text: • Implementation of communication circuits for the transmission of information to health authorities, following forecasting and warning systems
extraction_rationale: Concrete operational communication measure explicitly stated.
mechanism_description: Implements communication circuits so forecasting and warning information is transmitted to health authorities, improving preparedness and response capacity for climate-related health risks.

section_header: Annex I -Lines of action and measures / Line of Action #9 - Development of decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions
policy_statement: Set up monitoring and evaluation arrangements at national, regional, inter-municipal and municipal level to support the Lines of Action.
parent_statement: Line of Action #9: Develop decision support tools, capacity building and awareness-raising actions, including developing a National Adaptation Platform, producing climate risk mapping, developing adaptation plans, and setting up forecasting/warning/response systems.
verbatim_text:
Setting up monitoring and evaluation arrangements at the national, regional, inter-municipal and municipal level
development in support of the Lines of Action

mechanism_description: Sets up monitoring and evaluation arrangements across levels of government to track progress and support implementation of the lines of action.

policy_statement: Qatar intends to reduce 25% of its GHG emissions by 2030 relative to a Business-As-Usual (BAU) baseline scenario.
verbatim_text:
Qatar intends to reduce 25% of its GHG emissions by the year 2030, relative to baseline scenario {BusinessAs-Usual (BAU)}.

The reduction of 25% in GHG emissions for the year 2030, relative to BAU scenario is consistent with provisions stipulated in Articles 4.3, 4.4 and 4.7 of PA.
extraction_rationale: Quantifiable national mitigation target with a deadline (25% by 2030 relative to BAU).
mechanism_description: Qatar intends to reduce economy-wide greenhouse gas emissions relative to a business-as-usual baseline, creating an overall pathway to lower emissions.

section_header: 3. Economic Diversification with Mitigation Co-benefits — a) Oil & Gas
policy_statement: Qatar Petroleum (QP) is committed to zero routine flaring by 2030 and to reducing onshore flaring to the absolute minimum, including technically feasible non-routine flaring.
verbatim_text: The national oil company, Qatar Petroleum (QP), is committed to zero routine flaring by 2030, with a long-term goal to reduce flaring in onshore facilities to the absolute minimum. Technically feasible non-routine flaring is also covered.
extraction_rationale: Specific, time-bound operational commitment in the oil & gas sector (zero routine flaring by 2030).
mechanism_description: QP commits to eliminating routine flaring and minimizing other onshore flaring where technically feasible, reducing emissions from oil and gas facility operations.

section_header: 3. Economic Diversification with Mitigation Co-benefits — a) Oil & Gas
policy_statement: Qatar commissioned a CO2 recovery and sequestration facility at Ras Laffan in 2019 with a design capacity of 2.2 MTPA of CO2 capture and storage.
verbatim_text: Qatar commissioned the largest CO2 recovery and sequestration facility in the MENA region at Ras Laffan in 2019 with a design capacity of 2.2 MTPA of CO2 capture and storage.
extraction_rationale: Concrete implemented mitigation infrastructure with quantified capacity.
mechanism_description: Commissioning a CO2 recovery and sequestration facility captures CO2 from operations and stores it, reducing point-source CO2 emissions released to the atmosphere.

section_header: SINGAPORE'S COMMITMENTS TO ADDRESS CLIMATE CHANGE
policy_statement: Singapore pledged to reduce emissions intensity by 36% from 2005 levels by 2030 and stabilise emissions with the aim of peaking around 2030.
verbatim_text: In 2015, building on our earlier commitment, Singapore pledged to reduce our Emissions Intensity (EI) by 36 per cent from 2005 levels by 2030, and stabilise emissions with the aim of peaking around 2030.
extraction_rationale: Formal pledge with numeric EI target and timeframe; reiterated national commitment.
mechanism_description: Singapore pledged to reduce emissions intensity and stabilise emissions so that emissions peak around 2030, creating an economy-wide pathway for emissions reductions.

section_header: SINGAPORE'S COMMITMENTS TO ADDRESS CLIMATE CHANGE
policy_statement: Singapore pledged to reduce emissions by 16% from business-as-usual levels by 2020.
verbatim_text: In 2009, Singapore pledged to reduce emissions by 16 per cent from business-as-usual (BAU) levels by 2020.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with a deadline (2020).
mechanism_description: Singapore pledged to reduce economy-wide emissions relative to a business-as-usual baseline, creating an overall pathway for emissions reductions.

section_header: Increasing Energy and Carbon Efficiency in the Waste Management Process
policy_statement: NEA aims to increase Singapore’s overall recycling rate from 61% (2015) to 70% in 2030.
verbatim_text: NEA aims to increase the overall recycling rate from 61 per cent in 2015 to 70 per cent in 2030, by increasing awareness and implementing other policy initiatives (see feature story).
extraction_rationale: Quantified national target with baseline and deadline.
mechanism_description: NEA aims to increase recycling through awareness and other initiatives, diverting more waste from landfill and thereby avoiding landfill methane emissions.

section_header: INCREASING RENEWABLE ENERGY: SOLAR PHOTOVOLTAIC (PV) SYSTEMS
policy_statement: Singapore plans to raise solar power adoption to 350 MWp by 2020 (about 5% of peak electricity demand).
verbatim_text: Singapore plans to raise the adoption of solar power in our system to 350MWp by 2020, representing about 5 per cent of peak electricity demand.
extraction_rationale: Quantified renewable energy deployment target with a deadline (2020).
mechanism_description: Singapore plans to raise solar power adoption so more electricity is generated from solar, displacing fossil-based generation and reducing associated emissions.

policy_statement: Land Transport Master Plan: Singapore will expand rail network to 360 km by 2030 and add more than 120 trains, increasing total fleet size by more than 40%.
verbatim_text: By 2030, the rail network will be 360km, and will be comparable in rail density with developed cities like London, New York and Tokyo. More than 120 trains will also be added to existing train lines, increasing the total fleet size by more than 40 per cent.
extraction_rationale: Quantified infrastructure expansion commitments with timeframe (2030) and fleet increase.
mechanism_description: The plan will expand the rail network and add trains, increasing public transit capacity and encouraging travel by rail instead of private vehicles, which reduces transport emissions.

policy_statement: Singapore will quadruple sheltered walkways from 56 km to 200 km by 2018; expand cycling paths to more than 700 km by 2030; and require private developers (from July 2016) to consider pedestrians’ and cyclists’ needs in development plans.
verbatim_text:
To facilitate walking and cycling, our sheltered walkway network will be quadrupled from the existing 56km to 200km by 2018. Cycling paths will double from 355km in 2015 to more than 700km by 2030.

From July 2016, LTA and the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) will require private developers to consider the safety, convenience, and accessibility needs of pedestrians and cyclists in their development plans.
extraction_rationale: Multiple quantified infrastructure targets plus a dated regulatory requirement (from July 2016).
mechanism_description: By quadrupling sheltered walkways, expanding cycling paths, and requiring developers to consider pedestrian and cyclist needs, the policy facilitates more walking and cycling in place of motorized trips, reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Seven new integrated transport hubs will be built by 2023.
verbatim_text: Seven new integrated transport hubs will be built by 2023 to make it convenient for people to access different transport modes and to enjoy shopping and lifestyle services at the same location.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure commitment with deadline (2023).
mechanism_description: Building integrated transport hubs makes transfers between transport modes more convenient, helping people shift toward lower-emitting travel options and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: These measures aim to increase public transport mode share during morning peak hours to 75% by 2030 and 85% by 2050.
verbatim_text: These measures collectively aim to increase the use of public transport as a share of overall transport during morning peak hours from 66 per cent in 2015 to 75 per cent by 2030. By 2050, the aim is to further increase this share to 85 per cent.
extraction_rationale: Quantified mode share targets with time horizons (2030, 2050).
mechanism_description: The measures aim to increase public transport use as a share of peak-hour travel, shifting trips away from private vehicles and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, every HDB town will have a dedicated cycling network.
verbatim_text: By 2030, every HDB town will have a dedicated cycling network.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound infrastructure commitment.
mechanism_description: The policy will build dedicated cycling networks to encourage more trips by bicycle instead of cars, lowering transport emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing

## Components of the Strategy

- Integrate energy requirements and resources into affordable housing deals...
- Integrate energy performance into underwriting...
- Expand access to financing...
- Expand energy savings performance contracting for public sector buildings...
- Create a revolving loan fund...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple specific bullet actions beneath it, suitable as a parent policy.
mechanism_description: The strategy expands access to low-cost financing and energy savings performance contracting so building owners and the public sector can carry out energy improvements that reduce building energy use.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Require that by no later than 2023, all new construction projects receiving HCR Tax Credit funding be high-performance and all-electric buildings per HCR Sustainability Guidelines for New Construction, and streamline access to incentives through housing agencies for regulated affordable housing decarbonization.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Integrate energy requirements and resources into affordable housing deals: ... For example, by no later than 2023, all new construction projects that receive Tax Credit funding through HCR will be required to be high-performance and all-electric buildings, as required in HCR's Sustainability Guidelines for New Construction. The State should also continue to streamline access to all incentives and resources for regulated affordable housing building decarbonization to go through the housing agencies...
extraction_rationale: Quantified deadline (no later than 2023) and explicit requirement; clearly a bullet under B5.
mechanism_description: It requires tax-credit-funded new affordable housing projects to be high-performance and all-electric and streamlines access to incentives and resources through housing agencies, reducing on-site fossil fuel use and associated emissions from building operations.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Provide support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards; consider updating HCR underwriting standards to account for reduced operational costs from high-performance building standards informed by data from existing projects.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Integrate energy performance into underwriting: The State should provide support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards and applicable regulatory requirements. HCR should consider updating its underwriting standards... to take into account potential reduced operational costs stemming from the new high performance buildings standards.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (underwriting support/updates) under B5.
mechanism_description: The state provides support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards and updates underwriting assumptions to reflect lower operating costs, which helps enable high-performance buildings and reduces building energy use.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Provide greater access to low-cost financing products for upgrades (including for low-income homeowners and buildings in Disadvantaged Communities), including financing for non-energy improvements needed for code compliance and electrification; explore low-interest financing coupled with credit enhancement/insurance; prioritize CDFIs and credit unions.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand access to financing: The State should provide greater access to low-cost financing products for upgrades, including for low-income homeowners and buildings located in Disadvantaged Communities. This financing also should be available for non-energy improvements needed for code compliance and to enable electrification... The State should also explore new mechanisms... coupled with credit enhancement or insurance. The State should prioritize support for financing products made available by community development financial institutions and credit unions...
extraction_rationale: Concrete expansion of financing access with target groups and mechanisms; listed under B5.
mechanism_description: The state expands access to low-cost financing (including through CDFIs/credit unions and credit enhancement) to help pay for building upgrades and electrification-enabling improvements, leading to lower building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Enact enabling legislation to expand energy savings performance contracting for State and municipal buildings, schools, and other public facilities, including expanding eligible measures and payback terms and allowing retention of some cost savings.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand energy savings performance contracting for public sector buildings: The State should enact enabling legislation to expand the use of energy savings performance contracting to support implementation of emissions reduction upgrades in State and municipal buildings, P-12 schools, and other public facilities... Changes to existing statute should expand the eligible list of measures... expand the allowable payback term... allow... deferred maintenance needs, and allow a State agency or authority to request to keep a portion of cost savings...
extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism via proposed legislation; detailed statutory changes.
mechanism_description: The State enacts enabling legislation to expand energy savings performance contracting so public buildings can implement efficiency and emissions-reduction upgrades that lower building energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
policy_statement: Create a revolving loan fund for building decarbonization and reuse of buildings and building materials, implemented through a bond-issuing government authority.
parent_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Create a revolving loan fund: The State should create a revolving loan fund for building decarbonization and the reuse of buildings and building materials... This would be implemented through a bond-issuing government authority.
extraction_rationale: Specific financial mechanism and implementation approach; bullet under B5.
mechanism_description: The state plans to create a revolving loan fund, via a bond-issuing authority, to finance building decarbonization and reuse of buildings and building materials, which helps cut building energy emissions and reduce emissions from producing new construction materials.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks

## Components of the Strategy

- Workforce training for gas sector workers...
- Develop appropriate regulations and permit fees...
- Streamline access to public and utility rights of way...
- Support public-private partnerships...
- Prioritize public sector support...
- Explore thermal energy networks as part of the gas system transition...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple listed component actions; appropriate parent.
mechanism_description: The strategy supports development of thermal energy networks (including regulations, permitting, access to rights of way, and partnerships) to help shift building heating away from fossil gas and reduce associated emissions.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Provide workforce training and transition support for gas sector workers to install/operate thermal energy networks, coordinating PSC/DPS, DOL, Office of Just Transition, utilities and unions.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Workforce training for gas sector workers to operate thermal energy networks: ... The PSC and DPS, DOL, the Office of Just Transition, and other State agencies should work together with utilities and unions... to help connect trained gas workers with new thermal energy network projects, on training and skills development...
extraction_rationale: Specific training/transition action under B6.
mechanism_description: State agencies plan to support training and skills development for gas-sector workers so they can move into thermal energy network projects, helping enable lower-carbon heating and associated emissions reductions.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Develop geothermal well regulations and revise statutory financial security and permit fee provisions to reduce burden for geothermal wells >500 feet, including allowing DEC to set requirements/fees in regulation and developing geothermal/stratigraphic well regulations.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
- Develop appropriate regulations and permit fees for geothermal wells greater than 500 feet deep: ... The following actions should be taken... 
- DEC should develop regulations for geothermal and stratigraphic wells...
- Remove the dollar amounts from ECL §§ 23-0305... and allow DEC to establish financial security requirements in regulation...
- Remove the fixed dollar amount in ECL § 23-1903... and allow DEC to develop an appropriate permit fee schedule in regulation.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory/statutory change actions are explicitly enumerated.
mechanism_description: It develops geothermal and stratigraphic well regulations and revises financial security and permit fee provisions to lower permitting burden, helping enable more geothermal well development and use of geothermal energy instead of fossil energy.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Develop clear processes/rules for thermal energy networks to access public and utility rights-of-way and establish default compensation levels for affected property owners; provide transparent access to heat sources/sinks.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Streamline access to public and utility rights of way as well as heat sources/sinks: The State, in partnership with local governments, should develop clear processes and rules for thermal energy network projects to access already established public and utility rights of way. In addition, the State should establish appropriate default compensation levels intended to compensate property owners in these rights of way... State and local governments also should provide simple and transparent access to heat sources and sinks...
extraction_rationale: Specific administrative rules/compensation commitments under B6.
mechanism_description: The state and local governments develop clearer, simpler rules and compensation defaults for rights-of-way and provide transparent access to heat sources and sinks, making it easier to build thermal energy networks that can supply lower-emission heating and cooling.

section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
policy_statement: Emphasize public sector support for thermal energy networks serving LMI/affordable/public housing and Disadvantaged Communities, including requiring at least one pilot per utility territory be located in a Disadvantaged Community per the Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act.
parent_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Prioritize public sector support for thermal energy networks that serve LMI housing and buildings in Disadvantaged Communities: ... The Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act... requir[es] that at least one thermal energy network pilot project per utility service territory must be located in a Disadvantaged Community.
extraction_rationale: Contains an explicit requirement (at least one pilot per territory in a DAC) tied to statute.
mechanism_description: The Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act requires at least one thermal energy network pilot per utility territory be located in a Disadvantaged Community, and public sector support helps deploy these networks to serve LMI/affordable/public housing, reducing building heating-related emissions by shifting to more efficient and lower-carbon thermal supply.

policy_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text:
## B8. Invest in Workforce Development

## Components of the Strategy

- Expand training...
- Curricula and career services...
- Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and other priority populations...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets; extract as parent.
mechanism_description: Expanding training, curricula, and career services grows a skilled workforce that can deliver building efficiency and electrification work, improving the capacity to reduce building-related emissions.

policy_statement: Increase training investments for incumbent and new clean energy workers via training infrastructure, career pathways, apprenticeships, on-the-job training, and industry partnerships in specified priority areas.
parent_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Expand training: Training for incumbent and new clean energy workers and adjacent industries needs to be increased dramatically, through investments in training infrastructure/delivery, career pathways, direct-entry Pre-apprenticeship and Registered Apprenticeship programs, on-the-job-training, and industry partnerships.
extraction_rationale: Specific workforce intervention and delivery mechanisms listed; sub-bullet under B8.
mechanism_description: The policy expands training, apprenticeships, and industry partnerships to grow the clean energy workforce, helping speed up deployment of clean energy and related emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services in State-funded and private education, including P-12, technical schools, apprenticeships, and university programs.
parent_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
verbatim_text: - Curricula and career services: The State should encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services in State-funded and private education including P-12, technical schools, apprenticeships, and engineering and architecture programs at universities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit education-system action under B8.
mechanism_description: The State should encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services to grow a trained workforce that supports implementation of building decarbonization and lowers building emissions.
