verbatim_text: - Support new agricultural products: The State, led by AGM, should help expand supply chain development for new agricultural products in New York such as agroforestry and perennial plant system products.
extraction_rationale: Explicit supply chain development support under AF16.
mechanism_description: The state supports supply-chain development for agroforestry and perennial plant products, helping expand these land-use practices and thereby increasing carbon storage in biomass/soils while improving ecosystem resilience.

section_header: AF17. Develop Forestry Training Programs to Support Expanding Workforce and Climate Knowledge
policy_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: There needs to be information on forest carbon management and climate resilience incorporated into existing forestry trainings and education programs to help encourage such an expansion.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components listing specific training, policy, and outreach actions.
mechanism_description: It incorporates forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry trainings and education programs to encourage workforce expansion and support better forest management for carbon and resilience outcomes.

policy_statement: Develop and support workforce development and training programs for the forest sector, including incorporating forest carbon management into high school and college curricula and supporting apprenticeships.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Support workforce development and training: DEC should work with partners such as the WPDC to develop and support workforce development and training programs for the forest sector, including incorporating forest carbon management into curriculums at the high school (such as the Boards of Cooperative Education Services) and college level and supporting existing training apprenticeship programs for careers in forestry and the forest product supply chain.
extraction_rationale: Concrete training program development action with examples under AF17.
mechanism_description: The policy supports forestry workforce training and education (including forest carbon management curricula and apprenticeships) to build capacity for improved forest carbon management outcomes.

policy_statement: Integrate and support forest carbon and climate resilience content within existing forestry training and continuing education programs.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Integrate forest carbon into education and outreach: DEC should integrate and support forest carbon, forest carbon management, climate change, and climate resilience into existing forestry training and continuing education programs.
extraction_rationale: Explicit integration action under AF17.
mechanism_description: DEC will integrate and support forest carbon, climate change, and climate resilience content in existing forestry training and continuing education programs to strengthen forest carbon management and climate resilience outcomes.

policy_statement: Add a continuing education requirement in forest carbon/forest carbon management to the Cooperating Consulting Forest Policy (CP-36) and approve program rigor/accountability.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Enhance cooperating consulting forester policy: DEC should add a requirement for continuing education in forest carbon or forest carbon management to Cooperating Consulting Forest Policy, CP-36, and approve rigor and accountability of the program.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy change (continuing education requirement) under AF17.
mechanism_description: DEC should add a requirement for continuing education in forest carbon or forest carbon management for cooperating consulting foresters and approve program rigor/accountability to improve forest carbon management practices.

policy_statement: Integrate forest carbon and forest carbon management into urban forestry and utility forestry training programs.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Integrate forest carbon management: DEC should integrate forest carbon and forest carbon management into urban forestry and utility forestry training programs.
extraction_rationale: Training integration action under AF17.
mechanism_description: It integrates forest carbon and forest carbon management into urban and utility forestry training programs to improve forest management practices that increase carbon sequestration and related climate benefits.

policy_statement: Bolster support for the Trained Logger Certification Program and implement new training modules including forest carbon BMPs and increasing carbon sequestration.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Bolster logger training: DEC and New York Logger Training should bolster support for the Trained Logger Certification Program and implement new training modules including forest carbon BMPs into the Trained Logger Certification program, including increasing carbon sequestration.
extraction_rationale: Specific training program expansion and module implementation under AF17.
mechanism_description: It bolsters the Trained Logger Certification Program by adding forest carbon BMP training modules to improve forest practices and increase carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: Provide outreach and education to construction industry and the public on mass timber and harvested wood products, including workforce training for use/installation of wood in buildings and infrastructure.
parent_statement: AF17 develops forestry training programs to expand the workforce and incorporate forest carbon management and climate resilience into forestry education and continuing education.
verbatim_text: - Provide outreach and education: SUNY ESF and Cornell CALS should provide outreach and education to the construction industry, architects, engineers, and the public on mass timber construction and use of harvested wood products (fire safety, high-rise applications). Education and outreach should include workforce training on the use and installation of wood in buildings and infrastructure.
extraction_rationale: Explicit outreach and workforce training actions under AF17 with target audiences.
mechanism_description: Provide outreach, education, and workforce training on mass timber and harvested wood products to enable greater use of wood in buildings and infrastructure, reducing construction-related embodied emissions.

section_header: AF18. Expand Markets for Sustainably Harvested Wood Products
policy_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: Use of durable, long-lasting wood products, especially as a substitute for fossil fuel-intensive products like steel, concrete, brick, or vinyl, can reduce overall emissions for construction projects.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component actions; serves as umbrella policy.
mechanism_description: Promoting long-lasting wood products as substitutes for emissions-intensive materials in construction reduces overall construction-related emissions.

policy_statement: Promote long-term durable wood products that store carbon and substitute for fossil-fuel-based materials, and incentivize longer-lived products while avoiding disincentives for residue/low-grade markets.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Promote carbon storing materials: SUNY ESF, WPDC, ESD, and DEC should promote longterm, durable wood products that store carbon and are substitutes for energy efficiency materials that are fossil fuel-based (such as cellulosic fiber insulation and hemp insulation replacing foams and research potential on uses for residues from wood and hemp product creation). In general, more long-term durable products should be incentivized over production of shorter-term and shorter-lived products; however, the market for wood residues and low-grade wood should not be disincentivized to prevent the degradation of existing forests and to allow for improved forest management practices that enhance sequestration and climate resiliency.
extraction_rationale: Concrete promotion and incentive guidance under AF18.
mechanism_description: It promotes and incentivizes long-lived carbon-storing wood and other bio-based products to replace fossil-fuel-based materials, lowering lifecycle emissions by storing carbon in products and reducing emissions from conventional material production.

policy_statement: Advance building code changes to adopt the International Code Council 2021 International Building Code.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Advance building code changes: DOS and the New York City Department of Buildings should advance building code changes to adopt the International Code Council 2021 International Building Code.
extraction_rationale: Specific code adoption commitment under AF18.
mechanism_description: Advancing building code changes to adopt the 2021 International Building Code updates construction standards in ways that reduce building energy use and related emissions over time.

policy_statement: Set standards/specifications for a minimum portion of harvested wood products (e.g., mass timber/wood flooring) in certain State-funded/supported new construction and infrastructure when supply chain can meet demand cost-effectively.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Develop standards: NYSERDA should work with SUNY ESF, Cornell CALS, and DEC to set standards and specifications for a minimum portion of harvested wood products, such as mass timber or wood flooring, in new construction in certain State-funded/supported buildings and infrastructure projects when New York's supply chain can cost-effectively meet the demand.
extraction_rationale: Specific standards-setting with minimum portion requirement under AF18.
mechanism_description: Develop standards and specifications to increase the share of harvested wood products in certain state-funded/supported construction and infrastructure projects, lowering embodied emissions by shifting material use and storing carbon in wood products.

policy_statement: Revise State procurement specifications to expand eligibility for qualifying wood products and set minimum standards for wood use in new State-funded construction and infrastructure projects as feasible.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Revise State procurement standards: OGS, DEC, GreenNY, and DASNY should revise State procurement specifications that limit the eligibility of wood products that meet the technical performance standards and set minimum standards for use of wood products in new State-funded construction and infrastructure projects as feasible.
extraction_rationale: Procurement rule revisions and minimum standards under AF18.
mechanism_description: Revising State procurement specifications and setting minimum standards for wood use in State-funded construction increases adoption of qualifying wood products, which can reduce embodied emissions from construction materials.

policy_statement: Remove barriers and create incentives for wood use in infrastructure applications (e.g., bridges, sound barriers, transportation hubs, utility poles, marine pilings, retaining walls, solar infrastructure, docks, piers).
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Remove barriers: DOT, DEC, NYSERDA, PANYNJ, and EFC should remove barriers and create incentives for using wood for infrastructure applications, including bridges, sound barriers, transportation hubs, utility poles, marine and foundation pilings, retaining walls, solar energy infrastructure, docks, and piers.
extraction_rationale: Specific barrier removal and incentive creation with example uses under AF18.
mechanism_description: Removing barriers and creating incentives for wood use in infrastructure shifts projects toward lower-embodied-carbon materials, reducing construction-related emissions (and storing carbon in long-lived wood products).

policy_statement: Enhance New York's harvested wood products supply chain and fund innovation to develop mass timber applications using northern hardwoods.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Enhance supply chain: NYSERDA, SUNY ESF, and Cornell CALS, with consultation from DEC, should enhance New York's supply chain for harvested wood products and fund innovation to develop mass timber applications using northern hardwoods.
extraction_rationale: Supply chain enhancement and innovation funding under AF18.
mechanism_description: Enhancing the harvested wood products supply chain and funding innovation for mass timber applications increases use of wood in construction, which can displace higher-embodied-carbon materials and reduce construction-related emissions.

policy_statement: Support RD&D demonstration and technology transfer for wood utilization/innovations to scale use and climate benefits of wood in the built environment.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Support research: NYSERDA, DEC, AGM, SUNY ESF, and Cornell CALS should support research and development demonstration, and technology transfer of wood utilization and wood innovations to scale the use and climate benefits of wood in the built environment.
extraction_rationale: Explicit RD&D and technology transfer support under AF18.
mechanism_description: Support research, development demonstration, and technology transfer to scale wood use and innovations in the built environment, helping substitute for more emissions-intensive materials and reduce construction-related emissions.

policy_statement: Conduct research on emerging forest products and markets related to bioeconomy and harvested wood product initiatives.
parent_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
verbatim_text: - Conduct research: DEC should work with partners to conduct research on emerging forest products and markets related to bioeconomy and harvested wood product initiatives.
extraction_rationale: Research action explicitly listed under AF18.
mechanism_description: Conducting research on emerging forest products and bioeconomy markets supports harvested wood product initiatives that can increase long-lived carbon storage in wood products and substitute for more emissions-intensive materials.

section_header: AF19. Develop a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for Bioenergy and Low-Carbon Products
policy_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: A Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan will identify feedstock volumes and production methods that utilize New York biomass resources (wood and wood processing wastes, agricultural crops and waste materials, organics in municipal solid waste, animal manure, and wastewater treatment byproducts) in a sustainable, emissions-reducing and substitution-maximizing manner.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components specifying guidelines, definitions, incentives, and system development actions.
mechanism_description: The action plan identifies sustainable biomass feedstocks, volumes, and production methods to maximize substitution and reduce emissions from energy and product pathways.

policy_statement: Establish rigorous sustainability guidelines and metrics for bioenergy products using life cycle analyses, including air quality/health impact analysis and best practices to minimize impacts (especially in Disadvantaged Communities).
parent_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: - Develop sustainability guidelines: NYSERDA, AGM, and DEC should establish rigorous energy, GHG, and environmental sustainability guidelines and metrics for bioenergy products using life cycle analyses. Sustainability guidelines should also include the analysis of potential air quality and health impacts of producing and utilizing bioenergy products and include best practices to minimize these impacts, especially for Disadvantaged Communities.
extraction_rationale: Specific guideline-setting requirement under AF19.
mechanism_description: It develops life-cycle-based sustainability guidelines and metrics for bioenergy products, including air quality and health impact analysis and best practices, to steer production and use toward lower lifecycle GHG emissions and fewer local pollution impacts.

policy_statement: Define sustainable feedstock production for bio-based processing to determine volumes and practices that minimize emissions, including cost-effectiveness and assessment of using former agricultural/underused lands.
parent_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: - Define sustainable feedstocks: NYSERDA should define sustainable feedstock production for bio-based processing to determine feedstock volume and practices that minimize total emissions as part of a biomass action plan. This should include an evaluation of cost-effectiveness and an assessment of activating former agricultural and underused lands (including former industrial lands) for more productive uses.
extraction_rationale: Clear definitional and evaluative action under AF19.
mechanism_description: NYSERDA will define sustainable biomass feedstock production to identify volumes and land-use practices (including use of underused lands) that minimize total lifecycle emissions from bio-based processing.

policy_statement: Establish preferential pricing to prioritize residue feedstocks (e.g., low-grade wood, residues, waste materials) and prioritize lowest life-cycle GHG emissions feedstocks/products for incentives, considering impacts on other sectors.
parent_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: - Incentivize residue feedstock use: NYSERDA, AGM, and DEC should work with SUNY ESF and Cornell CALS to establish preferential pricing to prioritize use of feedstocks that are residues from existing agricultural, forest, and waste systems (e.g., low-grade wood, wood residues, waste materials, and processing wastes). Feedstocks and products with the lowest GHG emissions following life cycle GHG accounting and analysis should be prioritized for incentives.
extraction_rationale: Specific incentive/pricing mechanism under AF19.
mechanism_description: Establishes preferential pricing and prioritizes incentives for residue-based and lowest life-cycle GHG feedstocks/products, shifting sourcing toward lower-emissions pathways and reducing lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Develop energy systems supporting a net zero emissions economy, including programs leveraging private capital to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstocks into bio-based products.
parent_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: - Develop net zero energy systems: NYSERDA and DPS should develop energy systems that can best support a net zero emissions economy in New York, including programs that leverage private capital to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstock into bio-based products.
extraction_rationale: Program development and investment leverage action under AF19.
mechanism_description: The policy develops programs that leverage private capital to invest in technology that converts bio-based feedstocks into bio-based products, helping displace fossil-based products and reduce lifecycle emissions.

policy_statement: Identify bioenergy pathways with high life-cycle energy efficiency and emissions reductions that complement electrification, and develop alternative fuel strategies for hard-to-electrify applications.
parent_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: - Identify efficient bioenergy pathways: NYSERDA should work with Cornell CALS and SUNY ESF to identify bioenergy pathways with high life cycle energy efficiency and high emissions reductions (from feedstock production, conversion, and delivery to the end user) that complement and support wide-scale electrification. NYSERDA and DEC should develop alternative fuel strategies for challenging to electrify applications.
extraction_rationale: Specific pathway identification and alternative fuel strategy development under AF19.
mechanism_description: NYSERDA and DEC plan to identify high-efficiency, low-emissions bioenergy pathways and develop alternative fuel strategies for hard-to-electrify uses, so that cleaner fuels can displace fossil fuels and reduce life-cycle emissions.

policy_statement: Identify 2050 hard-to-decarbonize fuel needs (e.g., jet fuels) and potential bioenergy development to meet these needs, tracking federal developments.
parent_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
verbatim_text: - Address hard to decarbonize fuel needs: NYSERDA and DPS should work with SUNY ESF and Cornell CALS to identify 2050 hard-to-decarbonize fuel needs (such as high-quality distillate jet fuels) and potential bioenergy development (feedstock supply chain, conversion systems, and end use markets) to meet these needs. The State should continue to follow developments on the federal level and be positioned to capture opportunities related to these future needs.
extraction_rationale: Explicit planning/analysis commitment with timeframe reference (2050) under AF19.
mechanism_description: The state plans to identify future hard-to-decarbonize fuel needs and bioenergy supply-chain options so bio-based fuels can substitute for higher-emissions fuels in those end uses.

section_header: AF20. Increase Market Access for New York Low-Carbon Products
policy_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: In the emerging field of low-carbon products, agricultural and forest residues are converted into biochemicals and biomaterials that are replacements for the petrochemicals used to create packaging, cosmetics, binders, absorbents, concrete strengtheners, and other products.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a component list specifying concrete actions, including procurement requirements, incentives, standards, and financing.
mechanism_description: By supporting procurement and market connections for biochemicals and biomaterials made from agricultural and forest residues, the policy increases substitution for petrochemical-based products and reduces associated fossil-derived emissions.

policy_statement: Begin tracking and reporting on the low-carbon product market to identify trends and growth opportunities to guide development.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Track low-carbon product market: ESD and NYSERDA should begin tracking and reporting on this market to spot emerging trends, innovative applications, external market opportunities, growth opportunities to guide the development.
extraction_rationale: Explicit monitoring/reporting action under AF20.
mechanism_description: Track and report on the low-carbon product market to identify trends and opportunities that help guide low-carbon product development.

policy_statement: Spur innovation through lead-by-example low-carbon procurement requirements for State government (e.g., bio-based products and low-carbon concrete).
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Incentivize innovation: OGS should spur innovation through lead-by-example in low-carbon procurement requirements for State government (such as bio-based products and low-carbon concrete).
extraction_rationale: Procurement requirement commitment under AF20.
mechanism_description: The state plans to use lead-by-example low-carbon procurement requirements to shift purchasing toward lower-carbon materials (e.g., bio-based products and low-carbon concrete), reducing embodied greenhouse gas emissions in the goods and construction materials it buys.

policy_statement: Conduct a technology readiness level analysis to identify low-carbon substitutes and high-value bio-based products from New York feedstocks and invest in production facilities.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Identify substitutes that can be used now: NYSERDA should commence a technology readiness level analysis of low-carbon substitutes for fossil fuel-based products and fuels and identify the high value products from bio-based processing of New York grown feedstocks and invest in production facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific analysis plus investment in facilities under AF20.
mechanism_description: Conducting a technology readiness analysis and investing in bio-based production facilities supports substituting fossil fuel-based products and fuels with lower-carbon alternatives, reducing emissions from industrial production.

policy_statement: Provide strategic incentives to scale up high-demand low-carbon product alternatives when not yet cost-competitive with fossil-based options.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Incentivize low-carbon products: NYSERDA should provide strategic use of incentives to drive the scale-up of high-demand products when the low-carbon product alternative is not yet costcompetitive with the fossil fuel-based option.
extraction_rationale: Explicit incentive mechanism under AF20.
mechanism_description: It provides incentives to help low-carbon product alternatives scale up when they are not yet cost-competitive with fossil-based options, reducing emissions from producing and using those products.

policy_statement: Develop standards and guidelines defining a low-carbon product, including sustainable feedstock production aligned with the biomass action plan.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Define standards for low-carbon products: DFS, the NY Green Bank, and the GreenNY Council should develop standards and guidelines for defining a low-carbon product, including ensuring sustainable feedstock production as defined by the biomass action plan (see AF19 ).
extraction_rationale: Standards/guidelines development with linkage to AF19 under AF20.
mechanism_description: Developing standards and guidelines for what counts as a low-carbon product, including sustainably produced biomass feedstocks, helps steer markets toward lower-carbon products and associated emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Expand access to low-interest loans or grants for existing NYS businesses to develop low-carbon product lines by educating local banks and offering NY Green Bank loan guarantees.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Incentivize existing businesses: NYSERDA, ESD, and DFS should expand access to low-interest loans or grants for existing New York State businesses to develop new low-carbon products lines by educating local banks on emerging biotechnologies and offering NY Green Bank loan guarantees.
extraction_rationale: Financing expansion and bank education with loan guarantees under AF20.
mechanism_description: Expanding low-interest loans, grants, and loan guarantees helps existing businesses invest in developing low-carbon product lines, leading to lower emissions from industrial production and products.

policy_statement: Create a low-carbon products portal to connect NY producers with corporate buyers and expand the NY Grown program to cover more products and add a low-carbon aspect.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Connect suppliers to corporations: NYSERDA should create a low-carbon products portal to facilitate connecting New York producers to corporations and other buyers that have made GHG emissions reduction commitments. The State should expand the NY Grown program to cover more products and add a low-carbon aspect.
extraction_rationale: Specific portal creation and program expansion under AF20.
mechanism_description: The state should create a low-carbon products portal and expand the NY Grown program with a low-carbon aspect to connect producers with corporate buyers, helping buyers choose lower-carbon products and reduce supply-chain emissions.

policy_statement: Expand production of high-value agroforestry products that help maintain healthy forests (e.g., sap/syrup, nuts, mushrooms, ginseng).
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Expand agroforestry forest products: AGM should help to expand production of high-value agroforestry products that contribute to maintaining healthy forests (sap/syrup production, nuts, mushroom cultivation, and ginseng production).
extraction_rationale: Specific product expansion support under AF20.
mechanism_description: The policy aims to expand production of high-value agroforestry products in ways that contribute to maintaining healthy forests, supporting ecosystem condition and carbon preservation.

policy_statement: Enhance public understanding of the bioeconomy and its role in reducing GHG emissions through education and outreach.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Provide education and outreach: DEC and AGM along with SUNY ESF should enhance the public's understanding of the bioeconomy and its role in reducing GHG emissions.
extraction_rationale: Outreach/education commitment under AF20.
mechanism_description: Provide education and outreach to enhance public understanding of the bioeconomy, supporting greater uptake of bioeconomy approaches that reduce GHG emissions.

policy_statement: Provide consumer and business-to-business education on bio-based and low-carbon products to build buyer confidence.
parent_statement: AF20 increases market access for New York low-carbon products by tracking markets, adopting low-carbon procurement, defining standards, incentivizing scale-up, financing assistance, and buyer/supplier connections.
verbatim_text: - Build buyer confidence: NYSERDA should provide consumer and business-to-business education on bio-based products and low-carbon products in order to build buyer confidence.
extraction_rationale: Education action aimed at market uptake under AF20.
mechanism_description: NYSERDA should provide consumer and business-to-business education on bio-based and low-carbon products to build buyer confidence and support greater uptake of lower-carbon products.

section_header: AF21. Provide Financial and Technical Assistance for Low-Carbon Product Development
policy_statement: AF21 provides financial and technical assistance for low-carbon product development, including criteria for investments, economic development attraction, preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks, and conversion technology investment programs.
verbatim_text: Providing this assistance will ensure this strategy mitigates GHG emissions without unintentionally emitting co-pollutants.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components that commit to criteria, initiatives, preferential pricing, and investment programs.
mechanism_description: Financial and technical assistance supports low-carbon product and conversion technology investments that reduce GHG emissions while avoiding unintended co-pollutant emissions.

policy_statement: Develop criteria for qualifying near-term bioprocessing capacity investments.
parent_statement: AF21 provides financial and technical assistance for low-carbon product development, including criteria for investments, economic development attraction, preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks, and conversion technology investment programs.
verbatim_text: - Quantify bioprocessing investments: NYSERDA should develop criteria for qualifying nearterm bioprocessing capacity investments.
extraction_rationale: Concrete criteria-setting action under AF21.
mechanism_description: Developing criteria to qualify near-term bioprocessing capacity investments helps steer funding toward bioprocessing projects, supporting scale-up of bioprocessing as a lower-emissions production pathway.

policy_statement: Provide financial and technical initiatives to identify and promote high-value outputs from New York bioprocessing inputs.
parent_statement: AF21 provides financial and technical assistance for low-carbon product development, including criteria for investments, economic development attraction, preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks, and conversion technology investment programs.
verbatim_text: - Promote high value outputs: NYSERDA, the Department of Taxation and Finance, and ESD should provide financial and technical initiatives to identify and promote the high value outputs from New York bioprocessing inputs.
extraction_rationale: Specific financial/technical initiatives under AF21.
mechanism_description: The state provides financial and technical initiatives to identify and promote high-value bioprocessing outputs, supporting market uptake of biobased products that can displace more emissions-intensive fossil-based alternatives.

policy_statement: Create an economic development initiative to attract bioprocessing and bio-based product businesses to New York.
parent_statement: AF21 provides financial and technical assistance for low-carbon product development, including criteria for investments, economic development attraction, preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks, and conversion technology investment programs.
verbatim_text: - Attract bioprocessing and bio-based products to the State: ESD should create an economic development initiative focused on attracting bioprocessing/bio-based product businesses to New York.
extraction_rationale: Explicit economic development initiative under AF21.
mechanism_description: Create an economic development initiative to attract bioprocessing and bio-based product businesses, increasing bio-based production that can displace more fossil-intensive products and reduce lifecycle emissions.

policy_statement: Use preferential pricing for in-State low-grade feedstocks that maximize carbon sequestration to create market value and incentivize forest management that increases sequestration.
parent_statement: AF21 provides financial and technical assistance for low-carbon product development, including criteria for investments, economic development attraction, preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks, and conversion technology investment programs.
verbatim_text: - Incentivize low grade feedstocks: DEC and AGM should have preferential pricing for in-State low grade feedstocks that maximize carbon sequestration (organic waste streams, wood residues, marginal land). Creating market value for low-grade wood products can incentivize forest owners to manage their forests in a way that increases sequestration.
extraction_rationale: Specific pricing mechanism tied to sequestration outcomes under AF21.
mechanism_description: Preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks is used to create market value that incentivizes forest owners to manage forests in ways that increase carbon sequestration.

policy_statement: Develop programs leveraging private capital (via NYSERDA and NY Green Bank) to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstocks into bio-based products.
parent_statement: AF21 provides financial and technical assistance for low-carbon product development, including criteria for investments, economic development attraction, preferential pricing for low-grade feedstocks, and conversion technology investment programs.
verbatim_text: - Invest in conversion technology: NYSERDA and the NY Green Bank should develop programs that leverage private capital to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstock into biobased products.
extraction_rationale: Program development for investment leveraging under AF21.
mechanism_description: Developing programs that leverage private capital to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstocks increases production of bio-based products, helping displace fossil-based products and reduce associated industrial emissions.

section_header: AF23. Advance Deployment of Net Negative Carbon Dioxide Removal
policy_statement: AF23 advances deployment of net negative carbon dioxide removal (CDR) by setting goals, identifying verifiable CDR pathways, developing an RD&D agenda, and funding demonstrations.
verbatim_text: DEC and NYSERDA, with assistance from SUNY ESF and Cornell, would need several years to begin to set up standards, identify CDR technologies and pathways, and identify research and development priorities.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a component list of concrete actions including goal-setting and funding demonstrations.
mechanism_description: DEC and NYSERDA plan to set up standards, identify CDR technologies and pathways, and prioritize research and development to support scaling carbon dioxide removal toward net-negative outcomes.

policy_statement: Set clear goals for CO2 removal technologies, evaluate solutions viable today, and monitor future solutions.
parent_statement: AF23 advances deployment of net negative carbon dioxide removal (CDR) by setting goals, identifying verifiable CDR pathways, developing an RD&D agenda, and funding demonstrations.
verbatim_text: - Set goals for CO2 removal: NYSERDA and DEC should set clear goals regarding the use of net negative removal technologies, evaluate solutions viable today and monitor solutions that could be viable in the future.
extraction_rationale: Explicit goals and evaluation action under AF23.
mechanism_description: NYSERDA and DEC set goals for CO2 removal technologies and evaluate and monitor viable solutions to support future net CO2 removal.

policy_statement: Support identification of verifiable and maintainable CDR technologies and pathways (e.g., direct air capture, biomass CDR options like biochar, capture and storage).
parent_statement: AF23 advances deployment of net negative carbon dioxide removal (CDR) by setting goals, identifying verifiable CDR pathways, developing an RD&D agenda, and funding demonstrations.
verbatim_text: - Identify CDR technologies: NYSERDA, with SUNY ESF, should support the identification of verifiable and maintainable CDR technologies and pathways, such as direct chemical carbon capture and CDR options for biomass energy generation (biochar, capture, and storage).
extraction_rationale: Specific identification support with examples under AF23.
mechanism_description: The policy supports identifying verifiable and maintainable carbon dioxide removal technologies and pathways so they can be used to remove CO2 from the atmosphere.

policy_statement: Develop an RD&D agenda and priorities for CDR, focusing initially on nature-based pathways while examining technology-based pathways.
parent_statement: AF23 advances deployment of net negative carbon dioxide removal (CDR) by setting goals, identifying verifiable CDR pathways, developing an RD&D agenda, and funding demonstrations.
verbatim_text: - Prioritize research needs: NYSERDA, with SUNY ESF, DEC, and Cornell, should develop RD&D agenda and priorities with initial work to focus on nature-based CDR pathways while examining the role of technology-based pathways in the future.
extraction_rationale: Explicit RD&D agenda commitment under AF23.
mechanism_description: The policy supports developing a research, development, and demonstration agenda that prioritizes nature-based carbon dioxide removal pathways and examines technology-based options to enable future scaling of CO2 removal.

policy_statement: Fund demonstration projects of CDR technologies to show life-cycle GHG benefits (e.g., direct chemical carbon capture; biomass CDR options like biochar, capture and storage).
parent_statement: AF23 advances deployment of net negative carbon dioxide removal (CDR) by setting goals, identifying verifiable CDR pathways, developing an RD&D agenda, and funding demonstrations.
verbatim_text: - Fund demonstration projects: NYSERDA should fund demonstration projects of CDR technologies, such as such as direct chemical carbon capture and CDR options for biomass energy generation (biochar, capture and storage) to show the GHG benefits of these techniques over the life cycle of projects.
extraction_rationale: Direct funding commitment for demonstrations under AF23.
mechanism_description: It funds demonstration projects for carbon dioxide removal technologies to demonstrate life-cycle GHG benefits and help enable broader carbon removal deployment.

section_header: W1. Organic Waste Reduction and Recycling
policy_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: Reducing the disposal of organics, including food scraps... is key to reducing the methane and CO2 emissions at landfills and combustion facilities.
extraction_rationale: Named waste strategy with an explicit 'Components of the Strategy' list of actions including legislation, funding, and program expansion.
mechanism_description: The policy expands food donation and organics recycling/composting so less organic material is disposed in landfills or combustion facilities, reducing methane and CO2 emissions while recovering nutrients via compost/digestate.

policy_statement: Enact legislation to amend/expand the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law to cover generators of 1 ton/week in 2024, 1/2 ton/week in 2025, and all generators by 2028; eliminate the 25-mile requirement and exemptions.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text:
- Significantly reduce the disposal of organics: The State should enact legislation to amend and expand the existing Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law (2019) to include generators of one ton per week or more beginning in 2024, one-half ton per week in 2025, and all generators
- by 2028. Also, the law should be amended immediately to eliminate the 25-mile requirement and the exemptions.
extraction_rationale: Specific legislative change with thresholds and dates under W1.
mechanism_description: The state should enact legislation to expand food donation and food scraps recycling so more organics are diverted from disposal, reducing landfill methane while recovering nutrients through composting/recycling.

policy_statement: Provide additional funds for emergency food relief organizations to expand food donation.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Financial assistance for food donation: The State should provide additional funds for emergency food relief organizations to expand food donation.
extraction_rationale: Direct funding commitment under W1.
mechanism_description: The state provides additional funds to emergency food relief organizations so they can expand food donation, reducing food waste sent for disposal and avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Fund research on food loss across the supply chain and financially support measures to reduce waste from farm to table.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Reduction of food supply chain losses: The State should provide funding to research food loss throughout the food supply chain, from farm to table, and financially support means to reduce waste at all points in the system.
extraction_rationale: Funding for research and interventions explicitly stated under W1.
mechanism_description: The State should fund research on food loss and financially support actions that reduce food waste across the supply chain, which lowers waste sent to landfills and avoids methane emissions.

policy_statement: Expand financial assistance programs for organics recycling facility infrastructure (collection and processing) for small- and large-scale operations.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Financial assistance for organics recycling infrastructure: The State should expand existing financial assistance programs for organics recycling facility infrastructure, including collection and processing, for small-scale and larger-scale operations.
extraction_rationale: Infrastructure funding expansion under W1.
mechanism_description: The state expands financial assistance for organics collection and processing infrastructure, increasing composting and diverting organics from landfills to avoid methane emissions and recover nutrients.

policy_statement: Expand and replicate organics collection and recycling models for multifamily buildings and public housing.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Expand food scraps collection and recycling at multi-family buildings: DEC and appropriate housing authorities should expand and replicate successful models of organics collection and recycling programs inclusive of multifamily buildings and public housing.
extraction_rationale: Program expansion with target building types under W1.
mechanism_description: Expand food-scraps (organic) collection and recycling programs for multifamily and public housing to divert organics from landfill, reducing methane emissions and recovering nutrients.

policy_statement: Facilitate research and other actions to increase markets for compost, digestate, and other organics recycling products.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Markets for compost, digestate, and similar products: DEC should facilitate research and other means to increase markets for compost, digestate, and other products derived from organic waste.
extraction_rationale: Market development facilitation under W1.
mechanism_description: Facilitate research and other actions to expand markets for compost and digestate, supporting more organic waste recycling that avoids landfill methane and recovers nutrients.

policy_statement: Continue developing food waste reduction education and outreach for businesses and encourage greater food donation.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Food waste reduction and donation for businesses: DEC should continue to develop food waste reduction education and outreach specific to the business sector and encourage greater food donation.
extraction_rationale: Ongoing outreach/education commitment under W1.
mechanism_description: The agency will continue education and outreach for businesses to reduce food waste and encourage food donation, lowering organic waste sent to landfills and avoiding methane emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation allowing composting facility operation on public parks.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Composting on public park land: The State should enact legislation that will allow composting facility operation on public parks.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative action under W1.
mechanism_description: The State should enact legislation that will allow composting facility operation on public parks, enabling organic waste composting that reduces landfill methane and recovers nutrients.

policy_statement: Continue and expand outreach and technical assistance to expand organics recycling (drop-off programs, household reduction, school programs, facilities).
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Outreach and education to promote organics recycling: DEC should continue and expand outreach and technical assistance to expand all aspects of organics recycling (drop-off programs, household food waste reduction, school programs, facilities, etc.).
extraction_rationale: Specific outreach/TA expansion under W1.
mechanism_description: The agency will expand outreach, education, and technical assistance to increase organics recycling, diverting organic waste from landfills and reducing methane emissions while recovering nutrients.

policy_statement: Work with the farming community to increase use of organic products, explore organics recycling facilities on farms, increase use of food scraps for animal feed, and explore increased food donation from farms.
parent_statement: W1 reduces organic waste disposal by expanding the Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law, providing financial assistance and infrastructure support, expanding collection programs, market development for compost/digestate, and enabling composting on public park land.
verbatim_text: - Engagement with the farming community: The State should work with the farming community to increase the use of organic products, explore the potential for organics recycling facilities on farms, increase the use of food scraps for animal feed, and explore the potential for increased food donation from farms.
extraction_rationale: Specified collaboration and exploration actions under W1.
mechanism_description: The State will work with farms to expand on-farm organics recycling and redirect food scraps to animal feed and donation, reducing organic waste sent to landfill and avoiding methane emissions.

section_header: W2. Waste Reduction, Reuse, and Recycling
policy_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: Waste reduction, reuse, and recycling initiatives significantly reduce the methane and CO2 emissions at landfills and combustion facilities by avoiding disposing of waste in the first place.
extraction_rationale: Named strategy with an extensive 'Components of the Strategy' list including multiple legislative and programmatic measures.
mechanism_description: Waste reduction, reuse, and recycling efforts reduce disposal to landfills and combustion facilities, avoiding methane and CO2 emissions from those waste management pathways.

policy_statement: Enact legislation in 2023 to establish a disposal disincentive (fee per ton) on all waste generated in New York to fund reduction, reuse, and recycling.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Fee per ton on waste: As stated in Strategy W1, the State should enact legislation in 2023 to establish a disposal disincentive (fee per ton) on all waste generated in New York to provide financial support for reduction, reuse, and recycling.
extraction_rationale: Legislative action with date and mechanism (fee per ton) under W2.
mechanism_description: The state should enact a per-ton waste disposal fee to discourage disposal and provide financial support for reduction, reuse, and recycling, increasing diversion from landfill and avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation requiring 'By Request Only' policies for single-use products and requiring reusable/refillable options in retail stores.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Enact 'By Request Only' legislation: The State should enact legislation requiring 'By Request Only' policies for single-use products and require reusable/refillable options for consumer goods in retail stores.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative requirement under W2.
mechanism_description: The legislation requires “by request only” distribution for single-use products and reusable/refillable options in retail stores, reducing single-use consumption and the amount of waste sent to landfill, which helps avoid landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation to reduce and eventually eliminate single-use packaged items in stores.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Phase out single use packaging: The State should enact legislation that supports the reduction and eventual elimination of single-use packaged items for use in stores.
extraction_rationale: Legislative commitment with phase-out intent under W2.
mechanism_description: The state enacts legislation to support reducing and eventually eliminating single-use packaged items in stores, which lowers packaging waste and the emissions associated with producing and disposing of it.

policy_statement: Enact legislation to implement expanded deposit container programs where feasible and needed.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Container deposits: The State should enact legislation to implement expanded deposit container programs where feasible and needed (if not covered by Extended Producer Responsibility [EPR] programs).
extraction_rationale: Specific legislative expansion under W2.
mechanism_description: The state should enact legislation to expand container deposit programs so more containers are returned and recycled instead of disposed, reducing landfill-related emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation supporting 'Right to Repair' by requiring manufacturers to provide repair information to consumers and third-party technicians.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Right to Repair: The State should enact legislation to support 'Right to Repair' and other legislation that requires manufacturers of products sold in New York to provide information to consumers and third-party technicians about how to repair damaged products.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative requirement under W2.
mechanism_description: The State should enact legislation that requires manufacturers to provide repair information, enabling more repairs and longer product lifetimes that reduce emissions associated with producing replacement goods.

policy_statement: Encourage materials exchanges and sharing platforms by developing resources and facilitating avenues for material reuse and product sharing.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Materials exchanges: The State should encourage the use of materials exchanges and sharing platforms through development of resources and facilitate the development of avenues for material reuse and product sharing opportunities for used goods.
extraction_rationale: Programmatic encouragement with specified means under W2.
mechanism_description: The policy encourages materials exchanges and sharing platforms to increase material reuse and product sharing, reducing the need for new goods and the emissions associated with producing and disposing of them.

policy_statement: Develop and promote sharing table and donation guidance for P-12 schools through a DEC partnership with the State Education Department and DOH.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Elementary school education: DEC should partner with the State Education Department and New York State Department of Health (DOH) to develop and promote sharing table and donation guidance for P-12 schools.
extraction_rationale: Specific guidance development and promotion under W2.
mechanism_description: DEC plans to partner with state agencies to develop and promote sharing-table and donation guidance in schools to increase redistribution of edible food, reducing food waste disposal and avoiding landfill methane.

policy_statement: Develop and use school curriculum educating students on connections between waste and the environment.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - School curriculum: The State should educate students on the connections between waste and the environment through curriculum development and use.
extraction_rationale: Curriculum development action under W2.
mechanism_description: The State educates students through school curriculum about how waste affects the environment, aiming to shift behaviors toward reducing waste and increasing diversion to avoid landfill emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation incentivizing reusable/refillable solutions across packaged goods sectors (refill/return at home and on-the-go).
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Reusable/Refillable containers: The State should enact legislation that incentivize reusable and refillable solutions across the full spectrum of the packaged goods sectors, such as refill at home, return from home, refill on the go, and return on the go.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislation to incentivize reuse/refill under W2.
mechanism_description: The State should enact legislation that incentivize reusable and refillable packaging (refill/return models), reducing single-use packaging waste and the emissions associated with producing and disposing of that packaging.

policy_statement: Support university research on the viability of reusable shipping and packaging materials.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Research on reusable packaging: The State should support colleges and universities to research the viability of reusable shipping and packaging materials.
extraction_rationale: Research support commitment under W2.
mechanism_description: The state supports university research into reusable shipping and packaging materials, helping enable wider reuse that lowers single-use packaging waste and associated lifecycle emissions.

policy_statement: Establish a targeted grant funding program to support reuse.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Assistance for reuse: The State should establish a targeted grant funding program to support reuse.
extraction_rationale: Creation of a grant program is a concrete resource allocation under W2.
mechanism_description: The State should establish a targeted grant funding program that supports reuse, increasing reuse and reducing disposal, which helps avoid landfill-related emissions.

policy_statement: Support policy approaches and potential government requirements to increase capture and use of deconstruction materials and recovered aggregate (including procurement/bid specifications).
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Reuse of construction materials: The State should support policy approaches that increase the capture and use of building deconstruction materials and recovered aggregate for a variety of applications. This may include government requirements (procurement standards, bid specifications, etc.) to include recycled or reused deconstruction materials.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy and procurement mechanisms under W2.
mechanism_description: The state should support procurement standards and bid specifications that increase use of recycled or reused deconstruction materials, lowering embodied emissions from producing new construction materials.

policy_statement: Provide financial support for local reuse centers, material exchanges, and repair shops using new Climate Act funding and other sources.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Support for local facilities: The State should provide financial support from new funding provided to implement the Climate Act and other sources, for local reuse centers, material exchanges, and repair shops to move beyond volunteer-run operations.
extraction_rationale: Direct financial support action under W2.
mechanism_description: The State plans to provide financial support for reuse centers, material exchanges, and repair shops to expand reuse and repair and reduce waste generation and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Support workforce development and job training in repair, refurbishment, remanufacturing, recycling, and innovative materials reuse.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Workforce development: The State should support workforce development, job training and trade skills in repair, refurbishment, remanufacturing, recycling, and innovative materials reuse.
extraction_rationale: Workforce program commitment under W2.
mechanism_description: The State should support job training and trade skills for repair, remanufacturing, recycling, and materials reuse to expand circular-economy activity and reduce waste and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Implement comprehensive textile waste reduction and recycling programs.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Textile recycling: The State should implement comprehensive textile waste reduction and recycling programs.
extraction_rationale: Clear implementation commitment under W2.
mechanism_description: Implementing textile waste reduction and recycling programs diverts textiles from landfill disposal, helping avoid landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Expand and replicate recyclables collection and outreach in multifamily buildings/public housing and fund infrastructure (e.g., eco-hubs) for multifamily housing and campuses.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Buildings solutions: The State should expand and replicate successful models of recyclables collection and outreach programs inclusive of multifamily buildings and public housing and fund infrastructure development (such as eco-hubs) to increase access to reuse and recycling opportunities for multifamily housing and campuses.
extraction_rationale: Specific program replication and infrastructure funding under W2.
mechanism_description: The State plans to expand recyclables collection, outreach programs, and reuse/recycling infrastructure so more material is diverted from disposal, avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: Implement and expand statewide campaigns and peer-to-peer outreach for reduction, reuse, and recycling, including targeted efforts in underperforming and Disadvantaged Communities.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Outreach: The State should implement new and expand existing statewide campaigns for reduction, reuse, and recycling targeting New York residents and businesses and increase research collaborations and expand upon existing partnerships to improve outreach and education efforts. The State should also support peer-to-peer education and outreach campaigns in underperforming and Disadvantaged Communities around reduction, reuse, and recycling.
extraction_rationale: Explicit campaigns and targeted outreach under W2.
mechanism_description: The State plans to expand reduction, reuse, and recycling outreach and peer-to-peer education to increase waste diversion, lowering landfill disposal and avoiding methane emissions.

policy_statement: Support coordination among municipalities for regional recycling initiatives and fund hiring local enforcement officers for municipal recycling programs.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Support municipalities: The State should support coordination between local and regional municipalities to enhance regional recycling initiatives and provide funding to hire local enforcement officers for municipal recycling programs, encouraging cross-jurisdiction and multiplanning unit collaboration.
extraction_rationale: Funding and coordination measures under W2.
mechanism_description: The state supports regional coordination and funds local enforcement officers to strengthen municipal recycling programs, increasing waste diversion and avoiding methane emissions from landfills.

policy_statement: Codify the GreenNY procurement program in statute to ensure long-term success and continued issuance of green procurement standards.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - State procurement standards: The State should codify its GreenNY procurement program in statute to ensure the long-term success of the program and continued progress on issuing new green procurement standards for products that reduce GHG emissions, are energy-efficient, produce less waste and are made with recycled content, and reduce the usage of toxic chemicals in State operations.
extraction_rationale: Statutory codification is a binding mechanism under W2.
mechanism_description: Codifying the GreenNY procurement program supports continued issuance of green purchasing standards that shift state buying toward lower-GHG, energy-efficient, lower-waste, recycled-content products, reducing emissions associated with state operations and their supply chains.

policy_statement: Develop a life cycle analysis model and solid waste management decision-making tool.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Tool development: The State should develop a life cycle analysis model and solid waste management decision-making tool.
extraction_rationale: Explicit tool development commitment under W2.
mechanism_description: The State should develop a life cycle analysis model and solid waste management decision-making tool to support choosing waste strategies that reduce lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Enact legislation requiring municipalities to develop and implement local solid waste management plans or affiliate with planning units with approved plans.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Enhanced local solid waste management plans: The State should enact legislation that would require municipalities to develop and implement local solid waste management plans or to become affiliated with planning units with approved local solid waste management plans.
extraction_rationale: Legislative mandate for municipal planning under W2.
mechanism_description: The legislation would require municipalities to develop and implement local solid waste management plans (or affiliate with approved planning units), improving waste handling and diversion from landfills to avoid methane emissions.

policy_statement: Enact additional legislation to ban materials and chemicals of concern in products and support research toward less toxic alternatives.
parent_statement: W2 advances waste reduction, reuse, and recycling through legislation (fees, by-request-only, packaging reduction, deposits, right-to-repair), grants and support for reuse/recycling infrastructure, workforce development, outreach, tools, and municipal planning requirements.
verbatim_text: - Reduce toxics in products: The State should enact additional legislation to ban materials and chemicals that may be found in products that are of concern for human health or environmental impacts. DEC should support research and activities that will lead to less toxic alternatives.
extraction_rationale: Explicit legislative action plus research support under W2.
mechanism_description: The State enacts additional legislation to ban chemicals of concern in products and supports research toward less toxic alternatives, reducing the use and release of hazardous substances into the environment.

policy_statement: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
