verbatim_text: Art. 29 'Rules and regulations on geother - mal energy': users who connect a remote-heating network that operates geothermal energy or is fuelled by - masses receive a subsidy (tax credit) of 40,000 Lire for each kW of power used.
extraction_rationale: Quantified incentive (tax credit per kW) linked to geothermal/biomass district heating.
mechanism_description: Provides a tax credit to encourage users to connect to geothermal- or biomass-fuelled district heating networks, shifting heating supply toward lower-carbon sources and reducing heating-related emissions.

section_header: Table of laws and measures (2000-12-23 Law 388)
policy_statement: Law 388/2000 establishes a fund for the reduction of atmospheric emissions and for promotion of energy efficiency and renewable energy sources, financing national and regional programs and including incentives such as 80% support for solar panels (as described).
parent_statement: Law 388/2000 (2001 Budget Law) introduces multiple sustainable development measures, including tax relief for SMEs’ environmental investments, excise tax exemptions/reductions for certain biofuels and environmentally defensive fuel products, tax credits for geothermal/biomass district heating connections, incentives for sustainable development measures, and establishment of a fund to reduce atmospheric emissions and promote energy efficiency and renewables.
verbatim_text: Art. 110 Fund for the reduction of - sions in the atmosphere and for - motion of energy efficiency and - able sources of energy. Financing national and regional programs for reduction of atmospheric emissions, - motion of energy efficiency... Plan for the installation of - els... calling for: incentives of 80% panels.
extraction_rationale: Creates a named fund with explicit purposes and financing scope; includes described incentive level for solar panels.
mechanism_description: The fund provides incentives for installing solar panels, increasing solar generation and displacing fossil-based electricity generation to reduce atmospheric emissions.

section_header: 2.5.4.5 Indicators for the generation of electricity
policy_statement: ENEL signed a voluntary agreement (20 July 2000) with relevant ministries committing to reduce its CO2 emissions by 20% by 2006.
verbatim_text: The most important step has been the signing of a voluntary agreement on 20 July 2000 by the Ministry of the Environment, the Ministry of Production Activities and ENEL, with the latter, Italy's leading producer of electricity, voluntarily  committing  itself  to  reduce  its  emissions  of CO2 (by  -20%  within  2006).
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified emissions reduction commitment with deadline under a voluntary agreement.
mechanism_description: A voluntary agreement has the electricity producer committing itself to reduce its CO2 emissions, leading to lower emissions from electricity production.

section_header: 2.5.4.5 Indicators for the generation of electricity
policy_statement: Legislative Decree No. 79 of 16 March 1999 requires that starting in 2002 producers and importers supply at least 2% of prior-year thermoelectricity as electricity from new renewable plants, with compliance possible via purchasing GRTN-issued green certificates.
verbatim_text: Article 11 of Legislative Decree no. 79 of 16 March 1999 on the liberalisation  of  the  electricity  industry  stipulates that,  starting  in  2002,  producers  and  importers  are required  to  supply  to  the  national  electricity  system quantities of energy produced by new plants operated with renewable sources and equal to at least 2% of the thermoelectricity produced or imported in the course of the previous year. This requirement... can  also  be  met  by  purchasing from  other  operators  the  'green  certificates' issued by the Manager of the National Transmission  Network  (GRTN)  to  certify  production from renewable sources.
extraction_rationale: Binding renewable portfolio requirement with start date and compliance mechanism (green certificates).
mechanism_description: It requires producers and importers to supply a minimum share of electricity from new renewable plants (or meet the requirement via green certificate purchases), increasing renewable generation and displacing fossil thermoelectricity to reduce power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: Italy will implement additional expansion of renewable sources requiring an increase of 0.3% each year of the mandatory 2% renewable electricity obligation from 2005 to 2012.
verbatim_text: This additional expansion requires an increase of 0.3% each year of the mandatory 2% obligation, from 2005 to 2012..
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified policy change (annual percentage increase) with timeframe.
mechanism_description: It increases a mandatory renewable electricity obligation over time to expand renewable generation and reduce the carbon intensity of grid electricity.

section_header: 4.3.3.2 Increase in the efficiency of the transformers stock
policy_statement: From 2008, Italy plans to ban the sale of transformers whose losses exceed the standard in CEI 14.13 to increase average efficiency of the transformer stock.
verbatim_text: it is expected that, as from 2008, all transformers with leaks exceeding the standard indicated in the rules CEI 14.13. will not be allowed to be sold.
extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory prohibition with an effective date and referenced technical standard.
mechanism_description: It will not allow the sale of transformers that exceed the CEI 14.13 loss standard, increasing average transformer efficiency and reducing electricity losses and related power-sector emissions.

section_header: 4.4.4 Additional initiatives planned for the transport sector
policy_statement: Italy plans biodiesel blending in diesel up to 5%, with blending forecast to begin in 2006 at 1–1.5% and reach 4–5% by 2010, supported by a voluntary agreement and rules on diesel formulation (and possible tax reductions).
verbatim_text:
- -Bio-fuels. There are plans for the blending of diesel fuel used in engines with bio-diesel... up to a level of 5%...
...it can be forecast that blending of the commercial product will begin in 2006 at levels of 1÷1.5%, reaching rates of 4÷5% in the year 2010.
Plans call for a voluntary agreement... together with rules and regulations on the formulation of diesel oils sold to the public. There is the added possibility of a partial or total tax reduction for the bio-diesel fuel utilised.
extraction_rationale: Quantified blending targets with timeline and stated implementation instruments.
mechanism_description: The policy plans to increase biodiesel blending in diesel (via a voluntary agreement, diesel formulation rules, and possible tax reductions), which displaces some fossil diesel use and reduces transport greenhouse-gas emissions.

section_header: 4.5.1 Measures implemented in the residential and tertiary sector
policy_statement: Italy will implement a National Program for Introducing Solar Energy in Italian Penitentiaries, installing 3,000 m² of solar collectors within 5 years.
verbatim_text:
June 2001 Inter-Ministerial Agreement Ministries of the Environment and Justice
National Program for Introducing Solar Energy in Italian Penitentiaries: shall result in the installation of 3,000 m2 of collectors in 5 years.
extraction_rationale: Concrete program with quantified deployment target and timeframe.
mechanism_description: The program shall result in installing solar energy collectors at penitentiaries, increasing solar energy supply and displacing fossil-based electricity generation to reduce emissions.

policy_statement: Italy plans to reduce SF6 leakage from electrical equipment by 2010 through an agreement between manufacturers and the Ministry for the Environment (including users for disposal), under assumptions including reducing installed-equipment leakage rate to 0.5% and end-of-life leakage rate to 1% by 2010.
verbatim_text: Electrical equipment... assumptions have been made: 1) stabilisation of manifacturing emissions at 2000 levels; 2) reduction of the leakage rate from installed equipment up to 0,5% by 2010; 3) reduction of the leakage rate from end-of-life equiment up to 1% by 2010. This will require the negotiation of an agreement between manifacturers and the Ministry for the Environment (also users shall be included as concerns the disposal of the systems).
extraction_rationale: Concrete target rates and deadline; specifies governance mechanism (negotiated agreement).
mechanism_description: It will negotiate an agreement with manufacturers (and include users for disposal) to cut SF6 leakage from electrical equipment in use and at end of life, reducing fugitive greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Italy plans reforestation in hydrogeologically unstable areas under Law No. 183/1989 at about 6,000 hectares per year for 2003–2012 (total 60,000 hectares).
verbatim_text: The operating capacity of the Basin Authorities is approximately 6,000 hectares of reforestation per year for the period of 2003-2012, making for a total of 60,000 hectares.
extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation rate and total area tied to a named legal framework (Law 183/89).
mechanism_description: It plans reforestation to increase vegetation cover, which helps stabilize soils in hydrogeologically unstable areas and increases carbon sequestration.

section_header: Table of laws and measures (2002-10-08 National plan to reduce greenhouse-gas emissions)
policy_statement: A national plan to reduce greenhouse-gas emissions targets a reduction of 93 million tonnes by 2008–2012 and identifies interventions across energy, agriculture/forestry sinks, transport, and cooperation, with financing via fuel excise tax re-modulation, use of an emissions-reduction/energy-efficiency fund, and revenues from energy-sector reform.
verbatim_text: National plan to reduce greenhouse-gas emissions ... Reduction of 93 million tonnes of emissions by 2008-2012. Identifies modalities of intervention to comply the emission reduction objectives... The financing of the plan will focus on three main interventions: a)Internal re-modulation of excise taxes on fuels... b)Utilization of the 'Fund for reducing atmospheric emissions and promoting energy efficiency... c)Use of revenues generated by the appli - cation of art. 21 of the Reformation decree for the energy sector
extraction_rationale: Contains a quantified target (93 Mt by 2008–2012) and describes financing instruments and intervention modalities.
mechanism_description: The national plan sets an overall greenhouse-gas emissions reduction objective and finances interventions (including fuel excise tax re-modulation and use of an emissions-reduction/energy-efficiency fund) to drive emissions reductions across sectors.

policy_statement: Adopt a building performance policy requiring large commercial buildings to fully transition to efficient all-electric equipment by 2035, begin regular disclosure in 2025, and allow annual fees in lieu of electrification dedicated to decarbonizing low-income and affordable housing (BO.2-7).

b) in 2025, begin regular disclosure of progress toward goal c) allow payment of annual fees in lieu of electrification, which must be invested into decarbonization of low-income and affordable housing.

mechanism_description: The policy requires a residential time-of-sale electrification plan (prioritizing water and space heating) and records gas service status, supporting a shift from gas equipment to electric systems and reducing on-site fossil fuel use in homes.

policy_statement: By 2024, implement prescriptive criteria and permit/inspection processes for residential heat pump water heaters using a single integrated permit (BO.2-11).

mechanism_description: The policy explores developing a virtual power plant or other district-scale solutions that monetize efficiency, demand response, and storage to provide grid flexibility and support decarbonization.

policy_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN

TLU.1
Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.

TLU.1-2 ... fund State of Good Repair with at least $300 million annually.

extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple detailed supporting actions and quantified funding/mileage commitments.
mechanism_description: It plans to fund and implement transit improvements to make service faster and more reliable, encouraging more people to use transit instead of driving and lowering transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Fund and implement recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan, including frequent corridors (5-minute service), transit priority measures, signal retiming, stop spacing optimization, and major transit capital projects (TLU.1-1).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text:
TLU.1-1 Fund and implement the recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan, including taking steps to:
- a) Identify and implement key transit corridors for service every 5 minutes or better all day long.

b) Ensure transit on frequent corridors is not delayed by recurring congestion by investing in transit-only lanes, signal management, queue-jump lanes and other transit priority treatments.

- c) Retime traffic lights to minimize signal delay for frequent lines.
- d) Optimize stop spacing on frequent lines to maximize transit ridership.
- e) Advance major transit capital projects, including a new Westside Subway along 19th Avenue and Geary, the Caltrain Downtown Extension, Central Subway extension, and the Link21 new transbay tube.
extraction_rationale: Concrete package of transit service and capital project actions.
mechanism_description: Funds and implements transit corridor frequency, priority, and capital improvements to make transit faster and more reliable, supporting ridership growth and reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: Improve transit reliability by bringing infrastructure into a state of good repair and fund State of Good Repair with at least $300 million annually (TLU.1-2).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: TLU.1-2 Improve transit reliability by bringing infrastructure into a state of good repair. Adequately fund State of Good Repair with at least $300 million annually.
extraction_rationale: Specific funding commitment with quantified annual amount.
mechanism_description: Bring transit infrastructure into a state of good repair and fund it to improve transit reliability, supporting transit use and reducing transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Greatly improve rider comfort, safety, and experience on transit across demographics and ability, including data collection/reporting, sensitivity training of fare inspectors, and expanding the Muni Transit Assistance Program (TLU.1-3).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: TLU.1-3 Greatly improve rider comfort, safety, and experience on transit across age, gender, race, and ability to encourage more people to ride transit. Example activities include data collection, reporting, sensitivity training of fare inspectors, and expanding the Muni Transit Assistance Program.
extraction_rationale: Specified intervention set to improve transit experience and equity.
mechanism_description: The policy improves comfort, safety, and accessibility on public transit (including through data collection/reporting, sensitivity training, and assistance program expansion) to encourage more people to ride transit, supporting a shift away from car travel and lowering transport emissions.

policy_statement: Implement Phase One of SFMTA's Racial Equity Action Plan and develop Phase Two in 2021 and implement Phase Two in 2022 (TLU.1-4).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: - TLU.1-4 Implement Phase One of SFMTA's Racial Equity Action Plan to improve working conditions and initiate the development of Phase Two in 2021 and then implement Phase Two in 2022 to improve safety, access, and opportunities for the public.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound implementation steps for a named equity plan.
mechanism_description: Implementing the agency’s Racial Equity Action Plan improves safety and access for the public, which can support greater use of public transit and reduce transportation emissions.

policy_statement: By 2025, implement 50 miles of Muni Forward transit priority improvements including 30 miles of new transit-only lanes (TLU.1-6).

verbatim_text: - TLU.1-6 By 2025, implement 50 miles of Muni Forward transit priority improvements, including 30 miles of new transit-only lanes. to increase reliability, frequency and safety for riders.
extraction_rationale: Quantified infrastructure delivery commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Implementing transit priority improvements such as transit-only lanes increases transit reliability, frequency, and safety, supporting a shift away from car travel and reducing transportation emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, study the role of Muni fare programs on equity, climate, and mobility goals and adopt recommendations (TLU.1-7).
parent_statement: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
verbatim_text: - TLU.1-7 By 2022, study the role of Muni fare programs on equity, climate, and mobility goals and adopt recommendations.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound study with commitment to adopt recommendations.
mechanism_description: The city will study Muni fare programs and adopt recommendations intended to improve equity and mobility outcomes and support lower transportation emissions through increased public transit use.

policy_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.

extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple time-bound pricing actions (congestion pricing, fees, tolling).
mechanism_description: Developing and implementing mobility pricing and financing (including tolling) that reflects carbon costs and mode efficiency shifts travel choices and investment toward lower-carbon transportation, reducing transport emissions.

policy_statement: By 2022, develop recommendations for equitable pricing programs (discounts/exemptions), reduce vehicle traffic, and increase transit service downtown; by 2026, study and implement appropriate pricing policies (TLU.3-1).
parent_statement: TLU.3: Develop pricing and financing of mobility that reflect carbon cost/efficiency and correct inequities of past investments.

For example, complete the Downtown San Francisco Congestion Pricing Study recommendations, and by 2026, study and implement the appropriate pricing policies.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound congestion pricing pathway with explicit equity features and implementation timeline.
mechanism_description: The city will study and implement pricing policies (with discounts/exemptions) intended to reduce vehicle traffic and support increased downtown transit service, which lowers transportation emissions.

extraction_rationale: Concrete fee/tax policy and advocacy actions with deadlines.
mechanism_description: The policy plans to identify local pricing options and advocate for higher fees to drive, which can discourage driving and reduce transportation emissions.

extraction_rationale: Explicit zoning policy exploration/commitment.
mechanism_description: Examining rezoning to allow multi-family housing can increase housing density, which supports lower car dependence and reduced transport emissions while limiting land-use expansion.

policy_statement: By 2023, increase the types of home-based businesses allowed in residential districts (TLU.6-3).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-3 By 2023, increase the types of home-based businesses allowed in residential districts.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound land use regulatory change.
mechanism_description: Increase the types of home-based businesses allowed in residential districts to enable more work-from-home/local work and reduce commuting travel and related emissions.

policy_statement: Identify and reimagine under-utilized publicly owned land and roadways for transformation/repurposing (TLU.6-4).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-4 Identify and reimagine under-utilized publicly owned land and roadways that could be transformed or repurposed.
extraction_rationale: Specific action to repurpose public assets.
mechanism_description: The policy plans to identify and rethink under-used public land and road space for transformation or repurposing, enabling land-use changes that can reduce car travel and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Co-develop plans/projects with BIPOC community members to design public space and the transportation system to advance racial and social equity (TLU.6-5).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-5 Design public space and the transportation system (including roadways) to advance racial and social equity by co-developing plans and projects with BIPOC community members and understanding their needs before designing the space.
extraction_rationale: Explicit participatory design requirement tied to equity outcomes.
mechanism_description: The policy co-develops transportation system and public space plans with BIPOC community members so designs better reflect community needs and can support more walking, biking, and transit use, lowering transportation emissions.

policy_statement: Update the Transportation Element of the City's General Plan (TLU.6-6).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-6 Update the Transportation Element of the City's General Plan.
extraction_rationale: Formal planning document update action.
mechanism_description: Update the transportation element of the general plan to guide future transportation decisions that can support emissions reductions.

policy_statement: Co-develop plans/projects with the disability community to design public space and the transportation system to advance disability justice (TLU.6-7).
parent_statement: TLU.6: Strengthen and reconnect communities by increasing density, diversity of land uses, and location efficiency.
verbatim_text: TLU.6-7 Design public space and the transportation system to advance disability justice by codeveloping plans and projects with diverse elements of the disability community and understanding their needs before designs are complete.
extraction_rationale: Explicit accessibility/disability justice co-development requirement.
mechanism_description: The city will co-develop public-space and transportation plans and projects with diverse disability community members so designs better reflect accessibility needs and strengthen equitable access and adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- TLU 7 Where motor vehicle use or travel is necessary, accelerate the adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEV's) and other electric mobility options.

Where motor vehicle use or travel is necessary, accelerate the adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
TLU.7-2 Expand publicly available EV charging...
TLU.7-6 By 2030, create incentives... to reduce emissions from construction equipment at least 50% from 2020 levels.
extraction_rationale: Named ZEV strategy with multiple supporting actions including charging targets and construction equipment incentives.
mechanism_description: The plan aims to accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles and other electric mobility options to reduce emissions from motor vehicle travel.

policy_statement: By 2023, launch a community-tailored public awareness campaign on benefits of transit, active transportation, and electric vehicles (TLU.7-1).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: TLU.7-1 By 2023, launch a public awareness campaign, including messaging tailored to specific communities, with the goal of educating residents about the health, economic, and environmental benefits of transit, active transportation, and electric vehicles.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound outreach/education intervention.
mechanism_description: Launch a community-tailored public awareness campaign to educate residents on the benefits of transit, active transportation, and electric vehicles, supporting shifts to lower-emission travel and vehicle choices that reduce transport emissions.

policy_statement: Expand publicly available EV charging that is financially and geographically accessible to low-income households and renters, including: 2022 evaluation framework for curbside pilots; by 2023 charging in 10% of municipal lot spaces and 10% of private large commercial garage spaces and creation of three fast-charging hubs (one in a disadvantaged community); by 2025 charging in 10% of SFO-owned parking stalls (TLU.7-2).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text:
TLU.7-2 Expand publicly available EV charging across the city that is financially and geographically accessible to low-income households and renters.

a) By 2022, complete an evaluation framework to develop curbside charging pilots b) By 2023, expand charging to 10% of spaces in municipally owned parking lots

c) By 2023, expand charging to 10% of spaces within privately owned large commercial garages d) By 2023, create three 'fast-charging hubs' with one serving a disadvantaged community within San Francisco.

e) By 2025, install charging to 10% of SFOowned parking stalls supported by load management software.
extraction_rationale: Quantified charging deployment targets with multiple deadlines and equity condition.
mechanism_description: The city plans to expand publicly available EV charging in curbside locations, parking lots, garages, and fast-charging hubs to improve charging access, which helps enable greater EV adoption.

policy_statement: By 2024, develop a plan to help the City's non-revenue fleet and small/local businesses build infrastructure for zero-emission delivery, drayage, and longer-haul trucks (TLU.7-3).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: TLU.7-3 By 2024, develop a plan to help the City's nonrevenue fleet and small and locally owned businesses build infrastructure that allows for zero emission delivery, drayage, and longer haul trucks.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound infrastructure planning for fleets and small businesses.
mechanism_description: Develop a plan to help the city fleet and small/local businesses build infrastructure that enables zero-emission trucks, supporting adoption of lower-emitting freight vehicles.

policy_statement: By 2023, establish a pathway to incentivize ZEVs for passenger service vehicles operating at the airport (TLU.7-4).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: TLU.7-4 By 2023, establish a pathway to incentivize ZEVs for passenger service vehicles operating at the airport.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound incentive pathway for airport ground transport.
mechanism_description: Establishes incentives to increase zero-emission passenger service vehicles, reducing transportation emissions from those trips.

policy_statement: By 2024, launch a pilot to advance ZEVs, e-bikes, and other low-carbon modes for door-to-door goods and meal delivery services (TLU.7-5).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: - TLU.7-5 By 2024, launch a pilot to advance the use of ZEVs, e-bikes, and other low-carbon modes for door-to-door goods and meal delivery services.
extraction_rationale: Pilot program commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Launch a pilot to advance the use of ZEVs, e-bikes, and other low-carbon modes for door-to-door deliveries, reducing emissions from delivery transportation.

policy_statement: By 2030, create incentives for renewable diesel and emerging zero-emission technologies to cut emissions from construction equipment at least 50% from 2020 levels (TLU.7-6).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: - TLU.7-6 By 2030, create incentives for the use of renewable diesel and emerging zero-emission technologies to reduce emissions from construction equipment at least 50% from 2020 levels.
extraction_rationale: Quantified emissions reduction target for construction equipment with deadline and policy tool (incentives).
mechanism_description: Creates incentives to increase use of renewable diesel and zero-emission technologies in construction equipment, reducing emissions from that equipment.

policy_statement: Design by 2023 and launch by 2024 a pilot to test accessible bicycles, e-bikes, and e-scooters for commuting and recreation (TLU.7-7).
parent_statement: TLU.7: Where motor vehicle use is necessary, accelerate adoption of zero-emissions vehicles (ZEVs) and other electric mobility options.
verbatim_text: - TLU.7-7 Design by 2023 and launch by 2024 a pilot project to test the use of accessible bicycles, e-bicycles and e-scooters for commuting, as well as recreation.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound accessibility-focused mobility pilot.
mechanism_description: The policy designs and launches a pilot to test accessible bikes, e-bikes, and e-scooters, supporting micromobility use and shifting some trips away from higher-emitting travel, which reduces transport emissions.

policy_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
## HOUSING (H)
- H 1 Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.

H.1
## Supporting Actions
H.1-1 Leverage every housing action and investment...
H.1-2 Prioritize affordable housing in cultural districts...
H.1-3 Expand tenant services...
H.1-4 Initiate steps to increase housing production... in higher opportunity neighborhoods...
extraction_rationale: Named housing equity strategy with multiple supporting actions.
mechanism_description: The city plans to use housing and stabilization programs (including affordable housing actions and tenant services) to support household stability, which can strengthen community resilience.

policy_statement: Leverage every housing action and investment to reverse historic dispossession and enable housing security for affected communities (H.1-1).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: H.1-1 Leverage every housing action and investment to help reverse historic dispossession based on race, ethnicity, disability, or socio-economic status, and enable housing security for affected communities.
extraction_rationale: Explicit directive for housing investments/actions tied to equity outcomes.
mechanism_description: The policy commits to using housing actions and investments to help reverse historic dispossession and improve housing security for affected communities, strengthening social resilience.

policy_statement: Prioritize affordable housing in cultural districts and areas with historically marginalized racial/ethnic communities to encourage stabilization and return (H.1-2).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: - H.1-2 Prioritize affordable housing in cultural districts and areas with historically marginalized racial or ethnic communities to encourage their stabilization and return.
extraction_rationale: Targeting policy for affordable housing siting/prioritization.
mechanism_description: Prioritizing affordable housing in cultural districts and historically marginalized communities supports resident stabilization and return, strengthening community resilience to climate-related stresses.

policy_statement: Expand tenant services (education, outreach, counseling, legal and rent assistance) to keep residents and workers housed in San Francisco (H.1-3).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: - H.1-3 Expand tenant services including education, outreach, counseling, and legal and rent assistance to keep local residents and workers housed in San Francisco.
extraction_rationale: Specific service expansion commitments.
mechanism_description: Expanding tenant services such as education, outreach, counseling, and legal and rent assistance helps keep residents and workers housed, supporting housing stability and community resilience.

policy_statement: Initiate steps to increase housing production—particularly affordable and accessible housing—in higher-opportunity neighborhoods historically racially/economically exclusive (H.1-4).
parent_statement: H.1: Anchor BIPOC families and advance their return to San Francisco through robust housing and stabilization programs.
verbatim_text: - H.1-4 Initiate steps to increase housing production, particularly affordable and accessible housing, in higher opportunity neighborhoods that historically have been racially and economically exclusive.
extraction_rationale: Explicit housing production commitment with equity siting focus.
mechanism_description: Initiate steps to increase housing production in higher-opportunity neighborhoods, which can shift growth toward infill locations and reduce car travel, lowering transportation emissions and land-use impacts.

policy_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.

extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple supporting actions including an annual unit goal.
mechanism_description: The plan supports preservation and rehabilitation of housing, including energy efficiency and decarbonization upgrades, which lowers residential energy use and related emissions.

policy_statement: Provide funding and resources to help unhoused or unstably housed people become and stay safely housed (H.2-1).

policy_statement: Subsidize and incentivize housing for vulnerable populations in high-resource areas, especially along transit-rich commercial and social-service corridors (H.2-2).
parent_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text: - H.2-2 Subsidize and develop incentives for building housing targeted towards vulnerable populations in high resource areas, especially along transit-rich, commercial, and social service corridors.

policy_statement: Initiate steps to fund acquisition and preservation of existing affordable multifamily housing with a goal of at least 400 units annually (H.2-3).

policy_statement: Secure federal, state, and local resources for accessibility, energy efficiency, decarbonization, and resilience upgrades in existing and new housing (H.2-4).
parent_statement: H.2: Support vulnerable populations and underserved communities through preservation/rehabilitation of existing housing and new housing development serving their needs.
verbatim_text: - H.2-4 Secure federal, state, and local resources for accessibility, energy efficiency, decarbonization, and resilience upgrades in existing and new housing.
extraction_rationale: Resource mobilization commitment for specified upgrade types.
mechanism_description: The policy seeks federal, state, and local resources to support energy efficiency and decarbonization upgrades in housing, which lowers building energy use and associated emissions.

policy_statement: Develop additional approval and permit streamlining for new housing that exceeds inclusionary and sustainability requirements (H.3-2).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.

policy_statement: Address barriers for lower-income small property owners to add housing (e.g., ADUs) and rehabilitate healthy, resource-efficient existing units (H.3-3).

mechanism_description: The policy addresses financial and educational barriers so lower-income small property owners can rehabilitate existing homes to be more resource efficient, reducing household energy use.

policy_statement: By 2025, establish codes and regulations to enable new materials (e.g., cross-laminated timber) and technologies (e.g., modular housing) to lower costs and increase construction resource efficiency (H.3-4).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.

extraction_rationale: Time-bound code/regulatory change with specific examples.
mechanism_description: Establish codes and regulations that facilitate new construction materials and technologies to improve construction resource efficiency, reducing emissions from construction materials and processes.

policy_statement: Expand green construction training and apprenticeship programs to grow skilled labor and reduce construction costs (H.3-5).
parent_statement: H.3: Advance zoning and implementation improvements that support new housing production sufficient to meet goals, especially sustainable small/mid-sized/family/workforce housing in lower-density neighborhoods.

extraction_rationale: Workforce program expansion linked to housing delivery.
mechanism_description: Expanding green construction training and apprenticeships grows the skilled labor pool and lowers costs, which helps enable wider delivery of low-carbon building construction and upgrades.

policy_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
## RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION (RPC)
- RPC 1 Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.

RPC.1
## Supporting Actions
RPC.1-1 Between 2024-2026, phase in policies...
RPC.1-3 By 2025, establish a maximum allowance for embodied carbon...
RPC.1-4 By 2025, amend existing policies to require deconstruction...
extraction_rationale: Named strategy focused on embodied carbon with multiple time-bound supporting actions.
mechanism_description: The plan aims to achieve carbon balance in buildings and infrastructure by establishing embodied-carbon allowances and related policies that reduce emissions from construction materials and projects.

policy_statement: Between 2024-2026, phase in policies to reduce embodied carbon more than 10% per project by addressing at least three product categories or building assembly types (RPC.1-1).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-1 Between 2024-2026, phase in policies to reduce embodied carbon more than 10% per project by addressing at least three product categories or building assembly types.
extraction_rationale: Quantified embodied carbon reduction requirement with timeframe and scope.
mechanism_description: The policy phases in policies that reduce embodied carbon in construction projects by targeting multiple material product categories or building assemblies, lowering construction-related emissions.

policy_statement: By 2025, develop incentives/policies/guidelines for adaptive reuse of existing buildings and for design/procurement of low-carbon structural materials for new construction (RPC.1-2).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-2 By 2025, develop a suite of incentives, policies, and/or guidelines for adaptive reuse of existing buildings, as well as the design and procurement of low-carbon structural materials for new construction.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound development of policy/incentive tools for embodied carbon reduction.
mechanism_description: Develop incentives, policies, and guidelines that support adaptive reuse of existing buildings and the design/procurement of low-carbon structural materials, reducing embodied emissions from new construction.

policy_statement: By 2025, establish a maximum allowance for embodied carbon of buildings, adjusted at regular intervals (RPC.1-3).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-3 By 2025, establish a maximum allowance for embodied carbon of buildings, to be adjusted at regular intervals.
extraction_rationale: Regulatory standard commitment with deadline.
mechanism_description: Establishing a maximum embodied-carbon allowance for buildings encourages lower-carbon materials and construction choices, reducing construction-related emissions.

policy_statement: By 2025, amend policies to require deconstruction of buildings and increase source separation of specific materials (RPC.1-4).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-4 By 2025, amend existing policies to require deconstruction of buildings and increase the source separation of specific materials.
extraction_rationale: Policy amendment requirement with deadline.
mechanism_description: Amend policies to require building deconstruction and increased source separation of materials, diverting more waste from landfills and reducing landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2030, advance 'Design for Disassembly' and 'Buildings As Material Banks' best practices and pilot at least one municipal project (RPC.1-7).
parent_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
verbatim_text: RPC.1-7 By 2030, advance best practices for 'Design for Disassembly' and 'Buildings As Material Banks' by creating implementation resources in partnership with global cities, and pilot at least one municipal project to maximize the value of carbon already invested in buildings.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound best-practice adoption and municipal pilot commitment.
mechanism_description: The city plans to develop and pilot design-for-disassembly and material-banking practices so building components can be reused, maximizing existing embodied carbon and reducing emissions from new material production.

policy_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text:
## TABLE 1: STRATEGIES IN 2021 CLIMATE ACTION PLAN
- RPC 2 Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.

RPC.2
Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system...
## Supporting Actions
RPC.2-1 By 2030, reduce food waste by 50%...
RPC.2-3 By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy...
RPC.2-6 By 2025, ... hospitals meet a 20% reduction in carbon and water footprints...
extraction_rationale: Named food system strategy with multiple quantified and time-bound actions.
mechanism_description: The plan aims to reduce food waste and increase edible food recovery so less food is discarded, lowering emissions by avoiding landfill methane and the emissions embedded in wasted food.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce food waste by 50% in alignment with the Pacific Coast Collaborative voluntary commitment by implementing guidelines and recommendations with food retailers/distributors/manufacturers (RPC.2-1).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-1 By 2030, reduce food waste by 50% in alignment with the City's voluntary commitment to the Pacific Coast Collaborative initiative by implementing food waste reduction guidelines and recommendations in partnership with food retail, distributors, and manufacturers.
extraction_rationale: Quantified food waste reduction target with deadline and implementation approach.
mechanism_description: The city plans to implement food waste reduction guidelines and recommendations with retailers, distributors, and manufacturers to cut food waste and avoid methane emissions from landfilled organic material.

policy_statement: By 2022, continue and scale the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program to track over-purchasing and redirect wasted food to communities in need, including recovered fresh produce for communities with limited access (RPC.2-2).
parent_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
verbatim_text: RPC.2-2 By 2022, continue implementing and scale the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program, which reduces food waste by tracking over-purchasing by food generators, and redirects otherwise wasted food to communities in need, including providing recovered fresh produce to communities with limited access.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound scale-up of named pilot with equity intent.
mechanism_description: It scales the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program to track over-purchasing and redirect edible surplus food to communities, reducing food waste sent to disposal and avoiding landfill methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy and develop compliance/monitoring program and incentives aligned with California SB 1383 (RPC.2-3).

verbatim_text: RPC.2-3 By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy and develop a program and incentives structure for compliance and monitoring in alignment with California's State Bill 1383 food recovery regulations.
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy adoption with deadline and compliance mechanism.
mechanism_description: The city plans to adopt a food waste prevention and edible food recovery policy and develop compliance/monitoring and incentives to increase diversion of food from landfills, reducing methane emissions.

policy_statement: By 2024, develop guidance for City food procurement aligned with Good Food Purchasing Program core values (including environmental sustainability and regenerative agriculture) (RPC.2-5).

verbatim_text: RPC.2-5 By 2024, develop guidance in partnership with other municipal agencies to implement city procurement of food in alignment with the five core values put forth by the Good Food Purchasing Program (GFPP): developing local economies, improving health, valuing the workforce, considering animal welfare, and environmental sustainability, including regenerative agriculture.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound procurement guidance tied to named program values.
mechanism_description: Developing guidance to align city food procurement with environmental sustainability (including regenerative agriculture) shifts purchasing toward producers using regenerative practices, supporting soil carbon gains and ecosystem restoration.

policy_statement: By 2025, SFDPH will ensure Zuckerberg San Francisco General and Laguna Honda Hospitals achieve a 20% reduction in carbon and water footprints via sustainable food purchasing standards aligned with GFPP values (RPC.2-6).

extraction_rationale: Quantified institutional target with deadline and implementation mechanism.
mechanism_description: SFDPH will implement sustainable food purchasing standards so hospital food procurement aligns with GFPP values, reducing the carbon and water footprints associated with the food they buy.

policy_statement: By 2030, SF Unified School District will build on its GFPP resolution to procure food locally and from minority-owned businesses/farms, switch entrees to lower-emissions alternatives, reduce over-purchasing, and donate meals to communities in need (RPC.2-7).

verbatim_text: RPC.2-7 By 2030, San Francisco Unified School District will continue to build upon its adopted resolution to participate in the GFPP, aiming to procure food locally and from minority owned businesses and farms, switch entrees to lower-emissions alternatives, reduce over-purchasing of food, and donate meals to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound school district procurement/diet/waste actions with equity components.
mechanism_description: The school district plans to procure more local food, shift entrees to lower-emissions alternatives, reduce over-purchasing, and donate surplus meals, lowering food-related lifecycle emissions and reducing emissions associated with wasted food.

section_header: THE PATH TO REACH NET-ZERO BY 2040 / EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS
policy_statement: Reduce San Francisco sector-based greenhouse gas emissions by at least 61% below 1990 levels by 2030 and achieve net-zero sector-based emissions by 2040 (at least a 90% reduction below 1990), sequestering any residual emissions through nature-based solutions.
verbatim_text:
By integrating these four climate justice commitments, the CAP proposes two ambitious and achievable climate emission reduction targets:
- An interim target of cutting sector-based emissions 61% below 1990 levels by 2030; and
- Net-zero sector-based emissions by 2040, a 90% reduction from 1990 levels

TABLE 5: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION TARGETS
By 2040, achieve net-zero emissions by reducing emissions at least 90%compared to 1990 levels and sequester any residual emissions through nature- based solutions
extraction_rationale: Citywide quantified emissions targets with deadlines and defined net-zero mechanism (90% reduction plus sequestration).
mechanism_description: The CAP proposes interim and net-zero sector-based emissions targets and plans to sequester any remaining emissions through nature-based solutions, driving an overall pathway to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: THE PATH TO REACH NET-ZERO BY 2040 / EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS
policy_statement: Reduce consumption-based emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030 and by 80% below 1990 levels by 2050 (expressed as less than 30 mtCO2e per household by 2030 and less than 10 mtCO2e per household by 2050).
verbatim_text:
San Francisco is determined to reduce the impacts of these outsourced emissions and has set two targets:
- A 40% reduction in consumption-based emissions by 2030
- An 80% reduction in consumption-based emissions by 2050

TABLE 5: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION TARGETS
By 2030, reduce consumption-based emissions to less than 30 mtCO2e per household, equivalent to a 40% reduction compared to 1990 levels
By 2050, reduce consumption-based emissions to less than 10 mtCO2e per household, equivalent to an 80% reduction compared to 1990 levels
extraction_rationale: Quantified consumption-based emissions reduction targets with deadlines and per-household metrics.
mechanism_description: The city has set targets to reduce consumption-based (outsourced) emissions over time, guiding actions that lower supply-chain and household-associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: Supply 100% renewable electricity in San Francisco by 2025 and supply 100% renewable energy (no more fossil fuels) by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
ENERGY  By 2025, supplying 100% renewable electricity, and by 2040, supplying 100% renewable energy (no more fossil fuels).
extraction_rationale: Clear, dated renewable energy goals stated in the CAP goals table.
mechanism_description: It sets a goal to supply electricity and overall energy from renewable sources instead of fossil fuels, lowering emissions by decarbonizing the energy supply.

section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / BUILDING OPERATIONS GOALS
policy_statement: Require zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all new buildings by 2021; require zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all large existing commercial buildings by 2035; and achieve zero emissions for all buildings by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
BUILDINGS  By 2021, requiring zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all new buildings, and by 2035, requiring zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all large existing commercial buildings.

## BUILDING OPERATIONS
## GOALS:
1) Zero emisisons new construction by 2021
2) All large commerical buildings are zero emissions by 2035
3) All buildings zero emissions by 2040
extraction_rationale: Mandate-style requirements with deadlines for new buildings, large commercial buildings, and all buildings.
mechanism_description: The city is requiring new buildings and large existing commercial buildings (and ultimately all buildings) to have zero onsite fossil fuel emissions, eliminating onsite combustion emissions from building energy use.

section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / TRANSPORTATION & LAND USE GOALS
policy_statement: By 2030, achieve at least 80% of trips by low-carbon modes; electrify at least 25% of registered private vehicles by 2030 and 100% by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
TRANSPORTATION  By 2030, an increase in low-carbon trips to at least 80%of all trips measured and an increase in the level of electrification of vehicles to at least 25%of all private vehicles registered, and by 2040, an increase in the level of electrification of vehicles to 100% of all private vehicles registered.

## TRANSPORTATION & LAND USE
## GOALS:
1) By 2030, 80% of trips taken by low-carbon modes
2) By 2030, at least 25% of all  vehicles registered in SF are electric, reaching 100% by 2040
extraction_rationale: Quantified transport mode-share and EV adoption targets with explicit deadlines.
mechanism_description: The policy sets goals to increase trips taken by low-carbon modes and to increase private vehicle electrification, which together reduce transportation greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION GOALS
policy_statement: By 2030, reduce solid waste generation at least 15% below 2015 levels and reduce waste disposed by incineration or landfill at least 50% below 2015 levels.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
ZEROWASTE  By 2030, a reduction in the generation of solid waste of at least 15% below 2015 levels and a reduction in the amount of solid waste disposed of by incineration or deposited in landfill of at least 50%below 2015 levels.

RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION
## GOALS:
By 2030:
1) Reduce solid waste generation 15% below 2015 levels
2) Reduce disposal to landfill 50% below 2015 levels
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste reduction and landfill disposal targets with deadline.
mechanism_description: The plan sets goals to reduce solid waste generation and reduce disposal to landfill or incineration, which lowers landfill-related greenhouse gas emissions, especially methane.

policy_statement: To meet San Francisco's goal of zero emissions by 2040, all existing buildings must be renovated or replaced by 2040.
verbatim_text: As buildings are retrofitted and renovated, the stock of existing buildings decreases in this model. To meet San Francisco's goal of zero emissions by 2040, 100% of existing buildings must be renovated or replaced by 2040.
extraction_rationale: This is a quantified, deadline-based requirement explicitly linked to achieving the City's 2040 zero-emissions goal; it functions as a policy target/condition.
mechanism_description: Existing buildings are renovated or replaced over time, improving building performance and reducing energy use and associated emissions.

section_header: Finance Ministries' Roles and Capacities in Climate Policy (Helsinki Principle 2)
policy_statement: Implement the Santiago Action Plan 2020 workstream to strengthen finance ministries’ roles and capacities in climate policy (Helsinki Principle 2), including sharing practices, reviewing governance/competency models, and collaborating with partners.
verbatim_text:
## Finance Ministries' Roles and Capacities in Climate Policy (Helsinki Principle 2)

Members will be invited to prepare written descriptions about the design, organization and exercise of climate policy (mitigation and adaptation/resilience), and the role of Finance Ministries in these arrangements. Key actions and deliverables under Helsinki Principle 2 include:

- Sharing national approaches and good practices to help Members develop their capacities and roles in policy-making, including in the Nationally Determined Contributions (NDC) processes.
- Reviewing practices, resources, and competencies representing different governance and organizational models.
- Working in collaboration with partners to leverage expertise and bridge the gap between Finance Ministries and those leading on climate action.
extraction_rationale: Named Principle 2 workstream with multiple specified actions/deliverables listed.
mechanism_description: Members are invited to share practices, review governance and competency models, and collaborate with partners to strengthen finance ministries’ capacity and role in climate policy-making.

section_header: Finance Ministries' Roles and Capacities in Climate Policy (Helsinki Principle 2)
policy_statement: Invite members to prepare written descriptions on the design, organization, and exercise of climate policy and the role of finance ministries in those arrangements.
parent_statement: Implement the Santiago Action Plan 2020 workstream to strengthen finance ministries’ roles and capacities in climate policy (Helsinki Principle 2), including sharing practices, reviewing governance/competency models, and collaborating with partners.
verbatim_text: Members will be invited to prepare written descriptions about the design, organization and exercise of climate policy (mitigation and adaptation/resilience), and the role of Finance Ministries in these arrangements.
extraction_rationale: A specific action the plan states will be undertaken (member invitations for written descriptions).

section_header: Climate Change in Macroeconomic Management and Public Finance (Helsinki Principle 4)
policy_statement: Implement the Santiago Action Plan 2020 workstream to mainstream climate change in macroeconomic management and public finance (Helsinki Principle 4), including tool development, guides for green budgeting/procurement/investment management, and assessments of policies including NDCs.
verbatim_text:
## Climate Change in Macroeconomic Management and Public Finance (Helsinki Principle 4)

Key actions and deliverables under Helsinki Principle 4 include:

- Developing tools to address knowledge and expertise gaps in macroeconomic forecasting and fiscal planning for climate change impacts, as well as disaster risk management, among others.
- Preparing toolkits and guides on whole of government approaches that integrate climate in the policy and budget process, such as green budgeting where appropriate, green procurement and climate-informed public investment management.
- Supporting macroeconomic and fiscal assessements of adaptation, resilience and mitigation policies, including NDCs.
extraction_rationale: Named Principle 4 workstream with multiple specific deliverables listed.
mechanism_description: Develops tools, toolkits, and guidance and supports assessments so climate risks and mitigation/adaptation considerations are integrated into forecasting, budgeting, procurement, and public investment decisions.

policy_statement: Work with regional and state partners to adopt a transportation funding strategy, including mechanisms such as a city development authority, a tax on unpaid off-street parking in commercial areas, and comprehensive congestion pricing on limited access highways (and potentially arterials).
parent_statement: Funding (Transportation Choices): secure adequate funding to implement the Transit, Bicycle, Pedestrian and Freight Master Plans and meet maintenance needs.

## Actions to Implement by 2015

*State legislative action required.

policy_statement: Adopt a citywide climate protection vision to reach zero net greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 and prepare for likely climate change impacts (Resolution 31312).
verbatim_text:
In 2011, recognizing that the Kyoto Protocol goal is just the first step forward in reducing climate pollution, the Mayor and City Council adopted a bold climate protection vision for our community (through Resolution 31312):
- Reach Zero Net Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions by 2050
- Prepare for the likely impacts of climate change
extraction_rationale: Formal, adopted City vision (via resolution) with a quantified long-term target (zero net GHG by 2050) and a defined adaptation objective.
mechanism_description: The city adopted a climate protection vision to reach zero net GHG emissions and prepare for climate change impacts, guiding an economy-wide pathway for emissions reduction and adaptation planning.

section_header: Transportation + Land Use — How much will these actions reduce GHG emissions?
policy_statement: Set 2030 transportation outcome targets including: 82% reduction in passenger vehicle emissions, 20% reduction in vehicle miles traveled, and 75% reduction in GHG emissions intensity per mile, from a 2008 baseline.
verbatim_text: The City's 2030 goals are to reduce emissions from passenger vehicle transportation by 82%, vehicle miles traveled by 20%, and emissions per mile traveled by 75% from a 2008 baseline.
extraction_rationale: Explicit quantified targets with baseline year, meeting the quantifiable target criterion.
mechanism_description: The City sets 2030 goals to reduce passenger-vehicle emissions, vehicle miles traveled, and emissions intensity per mile, leading to a transport emissions reduction pathway.

policy_statement: Set waste outcome targets including achieving a 70% diversion rate by 2022 and reducing methane emissions from landfill by 50% by 2020.
verbatim_text:
| Recycling & Composting             | Waste diverted from landfill to recycling and composting | 70% diversion rate by 2022                 |
| Collection, Processing, & Disposal | Methane emissions from landfill                          | 50% reduction in methane emissions by 2020 |

mechanism_description: Set targets to divert waste from landfill to recycling and composting, reducing landfill methane emissions.
