policy_statement: Limit emissions within Stockholm’s geographical area to a maximum of 0.6 tonnes CO2e per resident by 2030 and ensure negative emissions are greater than remaining emissions, as part of being climate-positive by 2030 and fossil-free by 2040.
verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.1 A Stockholm that is climate-positive by 2030and fossil-freeby2040
area shall not exceed 0.6 tonnes carbon dioxide equivalents (CO 2 e) per resident. Negative emissions must be greater than the remaining emissions.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline and defined condition on negative emissions.
mechanism_description: Sets a per-resident emissions ceiling and aims for negative emissions to exceed remaining emissions, driving an economy-wide emissions reduction pathway toward climate-positive status.

policy_statement: Reduce transport-sector emissions within Stockholm’s geographical area by 80% by 2030 compared with 2010.
verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.2 Reduced climate impact from the transport sector
area shall be reduced by 80% by 2030 (compared with 2010).
extraction_rationale: Quantified reduction target with baseline year and deadline.
mechanism_description: Sets a milestone to reduce the transport sector’s climate impact, guiding actions that lower transport-related greenhouse gas emissions.

section_header: Climate impact from consumption a different perspective
policy_statement: Halve consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm by 2030 compared with 2019 (from 10.7 to 5.4 tonnes CO2e per resident).
verbatim_text:
Milestone 2.4 Ahalving of emissions from consumption
Consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm shall be halved by 2030 compared with 2019.

According to the City's milestone 2.4, A halving of emissions from consumption, these emissions must be halved to 5.4 tonnes per resident by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with baseline and per-capita numeric endpoint.
mechanism_description: The City sets a target that consumption-based greenhouse gas emissions in Stockholm shall be halved, creating an overall pathway to reduce emissions associated with residents’ consumption.

section_header: Emission budget sets the pace in reducing emissions
policy_statement: Establish a local emissions budget for Stockholm for 2024–2040 with a maximum of 9 million tonnes CO2e emitted over the period to complement Milestone 2.1.
verbatim_text:
This action plan defines a local emissions budget for Stockholm as a complement to milestone 2.1 A Stockholm that is climate-positive by 2030 and fossil-free by 2040 .
Stockholm's emissions budget 2024-2040 states that a maximum of 9 million tonnes CO2e may be emitted for the period 2024-2040.
extraction_rationale: Quantified carbon budget over a defined period; explicit policy instrument.
mechanism_description: The city defines a local emissions budget that sets a maximum amount of CO2e that may be emitted over a multi-year period, supporting an economy-wide pathway to reduce emissions.

policy_statement: Reduce car traffic by 30% by 2030 from the 2017 level.
verbatim_text: By 2030, the City aims to reduce car traffic by 30% from the 2017 level, which requires strong measures.
extraction_rationale: Clear quantified target with baseline year and deadline.
mechanism_description: The city aims to reduce car traffic, which lowers driving-related fuel use and greenhouse gas emissions from transport.

policy_statement: Install and commission a bio-CCS (BECCS) plant in Stockholm to enable capture and permanent storage of 800,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide annually (commissioning planned for 2027).
verbatim_text:
Building and commissioning a bio-CCS plant in Stockholm will enable 800,000 tonnes of carbon dioxide to be captured and stored annually.

Negative emissions in the emissions budget assume that a bioCCS/BECCS plant in connection with the biofuel-fired CHP KVV8 at the Värtaverket plant will be built and taken into operation in 2027.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure intervention with quantified annual capture and an operational start-year assumption.
mechanism_description: Building and commissioning a BECCS/bio-CCS plant captures carbon dioxide from bioenergy operations and stores it permanently to deliver negative emissions.

policy_statement: Cease all firing using fossil oil by 2025.
verbatim_text: All firing using fossil oil must have ceased by 2025.
extraction_rationale: Clear phase-out requirement with a deadline.
mechanism_description: Ceasing all firing using fossil oil reduces emissions from stationary fossil fuel combustion.

section_header: ## Natural Disasters — ## Efforts for the Future
policy_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Efforts for the Future

In response to natural threats, such as floods, inland floods, storm surges, and landslides due to intensified heavy rains and typhoons, TMG will promote the utilization of state-of-the-art technologies and the development of urban facilities in both structural and non-structural aspects.

To cope with increasingly larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains in recent years, TMG will further improve our initiatives.
extraction_rationale: Introduces an umbrella set of measures with multiple structured subcomponents (structural and non-structural) detailed below.
mechanism_description: TMG will promote advanced technologies and develop urban facilities using structural and non-structural measures to better cope with larger typhoons and heavier rains, reducing disaster impacts such as flooding, storm surges, and landslides.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will develop regulating reservoirs (including extending the Wide-Area Regulating Reservoir under Loop Road No. 7 as an underground river), improve river discharge capacity in the Tama area, and implement preventive river maintenance using ICT/AI for inspections.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Measures for rivers against heavy rains

- ' Promote the development of regulating reservoirs under construction, such as the Wide-Area Regulating Reservoir under Loop Road No. 7, in parallel with that of river revetment.
- ' Proceed with the study to operationalize new regulating reservoirs and extend the Wide-Area Regulating Reservoir under Loop Road No. 7 by making it an underground river.
- ' In the Tama area damaged by Typhoon Hagibis in 2019, TMG will improve the discharge capacity of rivers through local improvements.

## Promotion of strategic maintenance of rivers

- ' Steadily implement maintenance based on a preventive maintenance plan...
- ' Ensure more efficient and sophisticated inspections through the introduction of ICT/AI...
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure and maintenance actions for rivers with clear verbs ('promote,' 'proceed,' 'will improve,' 'introduce ICT/AI').
mechanism_description: TMG will develop and extend regulating reservoirs, improve river discharge capacity, and use ICT/AI-enabled preventive maintenance to help reduce flooding impacts during heavy rains.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will develop sewerage trunk lines and storage to handle 75 mm/hour rainfall in high-risk areas, verify sewerage capacity via runoff simulation for 75 mm/hour rainfall, and strengthen countermeasures in newly identified high-risk districts.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Development of sewerage facilities

- ' Develop large trunk lines and storage facilities that can handle precipitation of 75 mm/hour...
- ' By utilizing runoff analysis simulation technology, TMG will verify the capacity of sewerage facilities in case of precipitation of 75 mm/hour, add new districts at high risk of inundation, and strengthen the relevant countermeasures.
extraction_rationale: Contains a quantifiable design standard (75 mm/hour) and concrete infrastructure and analytical actions.
mechanism_description: TMG will develop and verify sewerage trunk lines and storage capacity for heavy rainfall and strengthen countermeasures in high-risk districts to reduce inundation and flood impacts.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will set non-binding rainwater runoff control targets (equivalent to 10 mm/hour rainfall) for municipalities, post progress information, consider expanding subsidized prioritized basins, conduct model projects with municipalities, and promote greening to reduce flood damage.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of measures for river basins

- ' To encourage municipalities to make voluntary and systematic efforts to control rainwater runoff equivalent to precipitation of 10 mm/hour, TMG will set non-binding targets for the amount to be controlled and post information on progress made towards achieving such targets.
- ' Consider adding prioritized basins that will be subsidized...
- ' Carry out model projects in cooperation with municipalities...
- ' Promote efforts to create and conserve greenery and reduce flood damage through a rainwater drainage function.
extraction_rationale: Specific interventions include target-setting (10 mm/hour), subsidies, model projects, and greening for drainage.
mechanism_description: TMG sets non-binding rainwater runoff control targets, shares progress information, supports projects and greening, and these actions increase runoff capture/drainage to reduce flood damage.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will use digital technology to improve disaster preparedness functions of agricultural canal reservoirs and intake gates.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Enhancement of agricultural infrastructure

- ' Utilize digital technology to improve the disaster preparedness function of reservoirs and intake gates of agricultural canals.
extraction_rationale: Concrete technology-enabled adaptation action for agricultural water infrastructure.
mechanism_description: Using digital technology improves the disaster preparedness of agricultural canal reservoirs and intake gates, strengthening resilience of water infrastructure during extreme events.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will develop storm surge protection facilities for the eastern lowlands, formulate the next Port of Tokyo Coastal Conservation Facilities Development Plan, develop coastal conservation facilities considering climate impacts, and enable remote floodgate operations and monitoring.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Measures for storm surges

- ' Promote the development of storm surge protection facilities to protect the lives and livelihoods of approximately 3 million people living in the eastern lowlands.
- ' Formulate the next Port of Tokyo Coastal Conservation Facilities Development Plan and promote the development of coastal conservation facilities taking into account the impacts of climate change.
- ' Prepare for emergencies by remotely opening and closing floodgates whenever possible and monitoring camera videos and information signals sent from facilities.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure planning and operational measures; includes an affected population figure (~3 million).
mechanism_description: It promotes storm surge and coastal conservation facilities and supports remote floodgate operation and monitoring to reduce storm-surge flooding impacts and improve coastal resilience.

section_header: ## (1) Structural measures / ## i Development of rivers, sewerage systems, and coastal conservation facilities
policy_statement: TMG will construct coastal conservation facilities (e.g., detached breakwaters) on islands to protect coastal villages from high tidal waves caused by typhoons and other disasters.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Construction of coastal conservation facilities on the islands

- ' Promote the construction of coastal conservation facilities, such as detached breakwaters, to protect villages etc. along the coast from high tidal waves caused by typhoons and other natural disasters.
extraction_rationale: Concrete coastal infrastructure commitment for island protection.
mechanism_description: Promote the construction of coastal conservation facilities such as detached breakwaters to protect coastal villages from high tidal waves caused by typhoons and other natural disasters.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will promote removal of utility poles and strengthen support for municipality roads under the Strategy for Accelerating the Removal of Utility Poles.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promoting the removal of utility poles

- ' Promote the removal of utility poles to prevent their collapse during earthquakes, storms and flooding, and facilitate a smooth response to disasters.
- ' Work to strengthen support for municipality roads in addition to Tokyo metropolitan roads and efforts for urban development based on the Strategy for Accelerating the Removal of Utility Poles.
extraction_rationale: Specific infrastructure resilience action (undergrounding/removal of poles) tied to a named strategy.
mechanism_description: It promotes removing utility poles and strengthening road support to reduce collapse risks during earthquakes, storms, and flooding and to facilitate a smoother disaster response.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will build redundancy in roads/bridges for disasters by building a highway network, constructing new bridges (including along borders with Chiba), improving access roads to the Tachikawa Wide-Area Disaster Management Base, and upgrading emergency transport routes (bridges, alternative routes, widening).
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Development of roads and bridges

- ' Build a highway network to ensure redundancy 1 in the event of a disaster.
- ' Promote the construction of new bridges along sections that border Chiba Prefecture...
- ' Improve accessibility to the Tachikawa Wide-Area Disaster Management Base...
- ' Improve the disaster preparedness of Tokyo... through the construction or replacement of bridges along emergency transport routes, the development of roads as alternative routes..., and the widening of emergency transport roads.
extraction_rationale: Multiple concrete transport infrastructure commitments to maintain evacuation/logistics during disasters.
mechanism_description: Build and improve highways, bridges, and emergency transport routes to add redundancy and improve access for disaster response, strengthening resilience during disasters.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will diagnose and systematically renew roadside trees in areas with many fallen trees to strengthen their disaster preparedness function.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Strengthening the disaster preparedness function of roadside trees

- ' To strengthen the disaster preparedness function of roadside trees, TMG will focus on the diagnosis of roadside trees in areas with many fallen trees due to typhoons for their systematic renewal.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (diagnosis and renewal) targeting typhoon-related treefall risk.
mechanism_description: TMG will diagnose roadside trees and carry out systematic renewal to strengthen their disaster preparedness function, with co-benefits from healthier urban tree cover that can reduce heat and store carbon.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will improve stability of water supply by doubling water conveyance facilities, building wide-area transmission pipe networks with a dual system, and burying pipelines that cross rivers.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## More stable water supply

- ' Double water conveyance facilities to improve the stability of water supply.
- ' Construct wide-area water transmission pipe networks and promote a dual system for water transmission to water supply stations...
- ' Bury pipelines that cross rivers, as surface pipes may be affected by river flooding...
extraction_rationale: Concrete water infrastructure resilience measures to prevent service disruption during floods.
mechanism_description: It improves water-supply stability by expanding and making water conveyance and transmission pipelines more redundant and less vulnerable to flooding damage.

section_header: ## ii Protection of functions of urban facilities
policy_statement: TMG will add and strengthen flood control measures for the Toei Subway based on newly announced expected inundation areas and examine measures for early restoration from large-scale floods.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Flood control in subways

- ' Take necessary additional measures for the Toei Subway in light of the announcement of new expected inundation areas that may assume the heaviest possible rainfall.
- ' Strengthen measures against inundation by examining more effective measures for early restoration from large-scale floods, such as overflowing of the Arakawa River.
extraction_rationale: Specific public transport flood resilience actions tied to inundation projections.
mechanism_description: The city will add and strengthen flood control and early-restoration measures for the subway to reduce inundation damage and shorten service disruptions during large-scale floods.

section_header: ## iii Promotion of urban development with disaster preparedness
policy_statement: TMG will promote urban development with elevation in model districts to avoid catastrophic flood damage and continue coordination with the national government and wards.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promotion of urban development with elevation

- ' Promote urban development with elevation to avoid catastrophic damage in the event of large-scale floods by continuing liaison meetings with the national government and promoting examination and operationalization in model districts in cooperation with the national government and wards.
extraction_rationale: Concrete planning and operationalization commitment for elevated urban development.
mechanism_description: TMG will promote urban development with elevation in model districts, in coordination with the national government and wards, to help avoid catastrophic damage during large-scale floods.

section_header: ## iii Promotion of urban development with disaster preparedness
policy_statement: TMG will elevate Shinozaki Park to secure evacuation routes to the Edogawa Levee.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:

- ' Elevate Shinozaki Park and secure evacuation routes to the Edogawa Levee.
extraction_rationale: Specific named project (Shinozaki Park elevation) for evacuation resilience.
mechanism_description: Elevating the park helps secure evacuation routes to the levee, improving flood response and evacuation resilience.

section_header: ## iii Promotion of urban development with disaster preparedness
policy_statement: TMG will support municipalities' risk assessment efforts to prevent damage from broken residential land retaining walls.

mechanism_description: Provide support for municipalities’ risk assessment work on residential land retaining walls to help prevent damage from retaining wall failures.

policy_statement: TMG will prioritize erosion control work in landslide-prone areas with evacuation centers and where landslides have occurred, and manage slope protection facilities (e.g., ground anchors) with preventive maintenance; it will also manage forests for disaster preparedness and encourage relocation to safer areas via consolidated regional structure efforts.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Implementation of erosion control work

- ' Prioritize erosion control work in landslide prone areas where evacuation centers are located and locations where landslides have actually occurred.

## Preventive maintenance management of existing ground anchors

- ' Promote preventive maintenance management of various slope protection facilities...

## Strengthening the disaster preparedness function of forests

- ' Improve benefits... by thinning and pruning...
- ' Develop disaster-resistant forests...

## Making regional structure consolidated

- ' Promote efforts for making regional structure consolidated... TMG will encourage relocation to safer areas...
extraction_rationale: Multiple specific landslide-risk actions including erosion control prioritization, preventive maintenance, forest management, and relocation encouragement.
mechanism_description: It prioritizes erosion control and preventive maintenance of slope protection facilities, improves forests for disaster preparedness, and encourages relocation to safer areas to reduce landslide hazard and exposure.

policy_statement: TMG will strengthen flood response by developing personal flood-disaster materials and equipment.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Strengthening disaster response in rescue activities

- ' Strengthen the flood prevention activity system by developing personal materials and equipment for flood disasters.
extraction_rationale: Specific preparedness investment/action: develop equipment and materials.
mechanism_description: The plan strengthens flood response by developing personal flood-disaster materials and equipment, improving readiness and reducing harm from flooding events.

policy_statement: TMG will promote self-consumed renewable generation (solar, storage batteries) and fuel cells for emergency power availability, and install emergency power supplies at TMG and municipal facilities and other important bases.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Expansion and installation of emergency power supplies

- ' To ensure electricity is available... even in the event of a disaster, TMG will promote the spread of selfconsumed renewable generation, such as solar power generation and storage batteries, as well as fuel cells, for better regional disaster preparedness.
- ' Promote the installation of emergency power supplies at TMG facilities, municipalities' buildings, and other important bases.
extraction_rationale: Concrete actions to deploy distributed energy resources and emergency power supplies for resilience.
mechanism_description: TMG will promote self-consumed renewable generation (solar, storage batteries) and fuel cells and promote installing emergency power supplies at key facilities so electricity remains available during disasters.

policy_statement: TMG will promote the spread of ZEVs for disaster power supply (V2H/V2B), including installing public chargers and hydrogen stations and introducing portable V2L and V2H equipment.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Promoting the spread of ZEVs

- ' Promote the spread of zero-emission vehicles (ZEVs 2 ) as "moving storage batteries"...
- ' Promote the installation of public chargers and hydrogen stations necessary for the spread, as well as the introduction of portable vehicle-to-load systems and V2H equipment necessary for the supply of power.
extraction_rationale: Specific technology and infrastructure actions supporting resilience and low-emission transport.
mechanism_description: It promotes installation of public chargers and hydrogen stations and introduces V2L/V2H equipment so more zero-emission vehicles can be adopted and used as mobile power sources during disasters.

policy_statement: TMG will improve island resilience by installing solar generation and storage on islands and considering regional renewable energy sharing.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## Better resilience in regions

- ' Consider the sharing of regional renewable energy and aim to strengthen regional disaster preparedness.
extraction_rationale: Concrete resilience measures for islands including renewable deployment and energy sharing consideration.
mechanism_description: Promoting installation of solar generation and storage batteries on islands, along with considering regional renewable energy sharing, supports more flexible local power supply and stronger disaster preparedness.

policy_statement: TMG will raise awareness of disaster preparedness through targeted communications and tools (Trial Examination, booklet, app, My Timeline), distribute flood risk maps and VR content, continue drills for foreigners with plain Japanese, and provide condominium-focused information tools.
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## i Improvement of awareness of disaster preparedness

- ' Raise awareness of disaster preparedness fine-tuned to different generations... through the Tokyo Disaster Preparedness Trial Examination... and distribution of the Tokyo Disaster Preparedness App.
- ' Distribute Tokyo My Timeline... and introduce a reminder...
- ' Raise public awareness of floods by distributing a flood risk map... and a VR video...
- ' Continue disaster preparedness drills for foreigners...
- ' Promote awareness raising by providing information through the Condominium Portal Site...
extraction_rationale: Specific non-structural interventions: apps, timelines, maps/VR, drills, and information portals.
mechanism_description: The city will raise disaster-preparedness and flood-risk awareness by distributing maps and digital tools and continuing preparedness drills, supporting stronger community readiness and adaptive capacity.

policy_statement: TMG will strengthen evacuation support systems (including guidelines considering emerging infectious diseases and vulnerable groups), consider wide-area evacuation for large floods, expand shelter options including hotels/inns, update hazard maps, enhance flood measures for underground spaces, review/designate landslide-risk areas and support municipal alert/evacuation systems, and digitize disaster preparedness (digital signage, smart poles, open APIs).
parent_statement: TMG will promote structural and non-structural measures for natural disasters using state-of-the-art technologies and urban facility development, and further improve initiatives to cope with larger typhoons and more frequent heavy rains.
verbatim_text:
## ii Development of the system

- ' Strengthen the support system for welfare specialists... through the timely and appropriate revision of the evacuation center management and operation guidelines...
- ' Consider wide-area evacuation measures in the event of a large-scale flood...
- ' Support coordination for using TMG facilities and the utilization of hotels and inns...

## Flood inundation area maps

- ' Promote the update of hazard maps...

## Flood control in large underground malls

- ' Promote further enhancement of measures against inundation in underground spaces...

## Landslide measures

- ' Systematically review the designation of specific areas... and provide technical support...

## Digitization of disaster measures

- ' Work to provide information... by utilizing digital signage... and smart poles...
- ' Promote the digitization of disaster preparedness... through open APIs 1 etc.
extraction_rationale: Bundle of concrete system-building commitments across evacuation, mapping, underground flood control, landslides, and digitization.

policy_statement: TMG will strengthen health adaptation by implementing preventive and ex-post measures to minimize heatstroke, infectious disease risks, and air-pollution health hazards.
verbatim_text:
## Efforts for the Future

extraction_rationale: Umbrella commitment introducing multiple sub-areas (heat, infectious diseases, air pollution) detailed below.

policy_statement: TMG will promote public heat countermeasures by fostering momentum for Uchimizu, creating cool spots with misting devices, constructing about 245 km of heat-blocking/water-retaining pavements by FY2030, promoting urban greening, increasing Tokyo Zero Emission Houses and supporting municipal heat-shielding/efficient appliance upgrades, and using smart poles to collect temperature/humidity data.

- ' Construct a total of approximately 245 km of heat blocking and water-retaining pavements... by FY 2030.
- ' Steadily operate the Greenery Program...
- ' Increase the number of Tokyo Zero Emission Houses...
- ' Collect temperature and humidity data through smart poles...
extraction_rationale: Contains multiple concrete interventions and a quantified pavement target (245 km by FY2030).
mechanism_description: TMG plans to expand measures like water-sprinkling activities, misting devices, heat-blocking/water-retaining pavements, and urban greening to lower urban heat and reduce heat exposure, supported by temperature/humidity data collection via smart poles.

policy_statement: Commit (through ClimateActionWR) to an 80% greenhouse gas reduction target by 2050 for Waterloo Region partners.

policy_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: The Kingdom is taking a number of steps in deploying desalination technologies to minimize the GHG emissions in the form of co-benefits from this sector leading to saving of energy from this sector. These steps include:
extraction_rationale: Introduces a structured set of concrete steps (a–g) under a named subsection; suitable as a parent for sub-policies.
mechanism_description: The Kingdom is taking steps to deploy desalination technologies, improving water supply resilience while also aiming to reduce energy use and associated GHG emissions from the desalination sector.

policy_statement: Gradually replace conventional MSF and MED desalination with lower-energy reverse osmosis (RO) technology to reduce energy consumption in desalination.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: - a. Reverse Osmosis (RO) technology: The Kingdom is gradually replacing the conventional energyintensive multi-stage flash (MSF) and multiple effect distillation (MED) technologies by lower energy consuming RO technology, reducing the energy consumption in this sector. RO is approximately 30% less energy-intensive than the conventional MSF and MED technologies.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention with comparative performance detail (RO ~30% less energy-intensive).
mechanism_description: The Kingdom is gradually replacing MSF and MED desalination with lower-energy reverse osmosis, reducing energy consumption in the desalination sector.

policy_statement: Use renewable energy (solar, wind, green hydrogen) for seawater desalination in NEOM to meet potable water needs.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: - b. Renewable Energy for Seawater Desalination: NEOM city will be using renewable energy resources such as solar, wind and green hydrogen for the seawater desalination for their potable water needs.
extraction_rationale: Specific commitment for NEOM desalination energy source.
mechanism_description: The city will use renewable energy (solar, wind, green hydrogen) to run seawater desalination, reducing the emissions associated with producing potable water.

policy_statement: Replace old potable water distribution networks with new networks to minimize leakages and avoid increased desalination capacity needs.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: - c. Minimizing Leaks in the Potable Water Distribution System: Old water distribution network will be replaced with a new water distribution network to minimize the leakages in order to save desalinated potable water avoiding increasing the production capacity of the existing plants or avoiding the need for new desalination plants.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure replacement action with stated purpose.
mechanism_description: Replacing old potable water distribution networks with new networks reduces leaks, saving desalinated potable water and avoiding the need to increase or add desalination plant capacity.

policy_statement: Expand wastewater collection, treatment, and reuse to reduce the need for desalinated potable water for city greening and groundwater for agriculture.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text:
- d. Increasing the use of treated wastewater: The Kingdom is working on expanding the wastewater collection, treatment and reuse to augment the need for potable and groundwater in city greening

and in agriculture. This will help reducing the need for potable water (desalinated water) for city greening and groundwater for agriculture.
extraction_rationale: Specific expansion commitment in wastewater sector.
mechanism_description: Expanding wastewater collection, treatment, and reuse increases treated wastewater available for city greening and agriculture, helping reduce the need for desalinated potable water and groundwater.

policy_statement: Encourage rainwater harvesting to augment potable and irrigation water needs.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: - e. Rain Water Harvesting: Encouraging rainwater harvesting to augment the need for potable and irrigation water.
extraction_rationale: Specific policy action (encouraging rainwater harvesting).
mechanism_description: Encouraging rainwater harvesting increases use of captured rainfall for irrigation and other needs, reducing demand on potable water supplies and improving water supply resilience.

policy_statement: Increase storage of surface water runoff by constructing new dams.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: - f. Storage of Surface Water Runoff: Increasing the storage of surface water runoff through constructing new dams in the country.
extraction_rationale: Concrete infrastructure commitment (constructing new dams).
mechanism_description: Constructing new dams increases storage of surface water runoff, improving water supply reliability and resilience to drought and variable rainfall.

policy_statement: Employ new irrigation techniques (including drip, sprinkler, subsurface irrigation) to save groundwater used for agriculture.
parent_statement: Implement adaptation actions related to water and wastewater management to reduce exposure and vulnerability, including the listed steps (a–g) to reduce emissions/energy use and improve water efficiency.
verbatim_text: - g. New Irrigation Techniques: Employing new irrigation techniques such as surface irrigation, drip irrigation, sprinkler irrigation, subsurface irrigation, and other techniques will minimize the use of and save a substantial amount of precious groundwater used for agriculture.
extraction_rationale: Specific technology/practice change with clear intended resource-saving outcome.
mechanism_description: Employing more efficient irrigation techniques reduces groundwater used for agriculture by minimizing irrigation water use.

policy_statement: Saudi Arabia aims to reduce, avoid, and remove greenhouse gas emissions by 278 million tons of CO2e annually by 2030, using 2019 as the base year.

extraction_rationale: Contains a quantified mitigation target (278 MtCO2e annually) with a deadline (2030) and a specified base year (2019).
mechanism_description: The Kingdom will implement actions, projects, and plans to reduce, avoid, and remove greenhouse gas emissions, creating an economy-wide pathway toward lower emissions.

policy_statement: Saudi Arabia aims to reach around 50% renewable energy in the electricity energy mix by 2030.
verbatim_text: Thus, the renewable energy ambition aims to reach around 50% of the energy mix by 2030.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline for renewable energy share.
mechanism_description: The policy sets an ambition to raise the share of renewable electricity in the energy mix, displacing fossil generation and lowering power-sector emissions.

policy_statement: NEOM will build one of the world’s largest green hydrogen facilities powered by over 4 GW of renewable energy; once onstream by 2025 it is expected to produce 650 tons/day of green hydrogen and 1.2 million tons/year of green ammonia.
verbatim_text: The Kingdom's flagship giga -project, NEOM, will be building one of the world's largest green hydrogen facilities. The plant will be powered by over four gigawatts of renewable energy from solar and wind. Once onstream by 2025, it is expected to produce 650 tons per day of green hydrogen by electrolysis and 1.2 million tons per year of green ammonia.
extraction_rationale: Concrete project commitment with capacity and production targets and a timeline (by 2025).
mechanism_description: NEOM will build a renewable-powered green hydrogen and ammonia facility that produces hydrogen via electrolysis, displacing fossil-derived hydrogen/ammonia and lowering associated greenhouse gas emissions.

policy_statement: As part of domestic energy sector reforms, Saudi Arabia aims to increase the utilization of natural gas in its energy mix, with up to 50% of electricity generation expected to be met by natural gas by 2030.
verbatim_text: As part of reforms of its domestic energy sector, the Kingdom aims to increase the utilization of natural gas in its energy mix. By 2030, the Kingdom aims that up to 50% of its electricity generation is expected to be met by natural gas significantly lowering the carbon intensity of domestic energy generation.
extraction_rationale: Quantified target with timeline for fuel switching in electricity generation.
mechanism_description: The Kingdom aims to increase natural gas use in electricity generation to displace more carbon-intensive fuels and lower the carbon intensity of domestic power generation.
